solutions.team_name: Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Blue_Phoenix
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 10:12 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement processes today rely heavily on manual evaluation of tender documents and bidder submissions. These documents are often lengthy, unstructured, and heterogeneous — including typed PDFs, scanned files, tables, and images. As a result, evaluation is time-consuming, inconsistent across officers, and difficult to audit.We propose an Explainable AI Procurement Assistant "Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)" — a modular, end-to-end platform that automates tender evaluation , ranks bidders intelligently while keeping humans in the loop to ensure full transparency.Core Idea: The system ingests,A tender document that contains eligibility criteria.Multiple bidder submissions with documents in various formats.Work of the AI assistant,Read Tender document and extract key eligibility criterions.Read Bidder Documents, the bidder's documents can be in multiple formats (like pdf's/photos/scaned images/word files, etc...). The assistant should be able to identify the file type and parse the necessary information correctly.Assistant should review and evaluate each bidder against extracted criteria of tender document.After evaluation the assistant should give output as "Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Manual Review", along with the explanation for that decision.As an extra we want to develop an ranking system that assigns bidders a composite score based on multiple evaluation criteria such as financial strength, past experience, compliance, and certifications. Then the system generates a ranked list of eligible bidders, enabling officers to focus on top candidates first.Finally, all these processes are to be done and displayed visually on a clean web interface.System Architecture Overview:Document Ingestion Layer (PDF, image, DOC support) OCR + Parsing Engine NLP/LLM-based Criteria Extractor Structured Data Store (criteria + bidder data) Rule Engine + Evaluation Module Scoring & Ranking EngineExplainability Layer (evidence mapping + audit logs + score justification) Reviewer Dashboard (human-in-the-loop interface) System Architecture:1. Tender Understanding (Criteria Extraction):Using NLP and LLM-based parsing, the system extracts:Technical requirements Financial thresholds (e.g., turnover ≥ ₹5 Cr) Compliance conditions (GST, ISO, certifications) Each criterion is structured into machine-readable rules and classified as mandatory or optional.﻿2. Bidder Document Parsing:The system processes bidder submissions using:OCR pipelines for scanned and image-based documents Document parsing for PDFs and structured files Information extraction models to identify relevant values It normalizes diverse formats into a unified data structure, handling inconsistencies in how bidders present information.﻿3.Bidders Evaluation Engine:A hybrid rule-based + AI engine evaluates each bidder against extracted criteria:Outputs: Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Manual Review Applies configurable thresholds and logical rules Handles ambiguity and incomplete data by routing to human review﻿4. Intelligent ranking and prioritization layer Beyond eligibility evaluation, the system introduces a scoring and ranking mechanism to assist procurement officers in prioritizing bidders efficiently.Each bidder is assigned a composite score based on weighted evaluation criteria such as financial strength, experience, and compliance. Mandatory criteria act as hard filters (pass/fail), while performance-based criteria contribute to ranking. The system generates a ranked list of eligible bidders, enabling officers to focus on top candidates first.﻿5. Explainability & Audit Layer (Key Differentiator)Every decision is fully transparent and traceable:Links each verdict to specific criteria, document, and extracted value Highlights evidence (e.g., “₹6.2 Cr found in balance sheet, page 3”) Maintains a complete audit trail for compliance and review No bidder is silently rejected — uncertain cases are explicitly flagged.﻿6. Reviewer Dashboard (Human-in-the-Loop Interface)The platform includes an intuitive Reviewer Dashboard that enables procurement officers to efficiently review and validate AI-generated decisions. The dashboard presents:Eligibility status, scores, and rankings for all bidders Criterion-wise evaluation with highlighted evidence from source documents Flagged cases (“Needs Manual Review”) with reasons for ambiguity Reviewers can:Approve, override, or request clarification on decisions View complete audit trails of how each decision was made Provide feedback, which can be used to continuously improve system accuracy This ensures that the system remains transparent, controllable, and aligned with real-world procurement workflows, while significantly reducing manual effort. In conclusion the proposed Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP) aims to transform government tender evaluation by combining AI-driven automation with human oversight. By extracting, evaluating, and explaining decisions from complex documents, and further enhancing them with intelligent ranking, the system reduces manual effort, improves consistency, and ensures transparency. Rather than replacing human judgment, PDIP acts as a decision-support system, enabling CPRF officers to make faster, more informed, and auditable decisions — ultimately leading to a more efficient, fair, and scalable procurement process.

It reads the RFP and every bidder submission — typed PDFs, scanned certificates, phone photos of stamps — and returns a PASS / FAIL / NEEDS REVIEW verdict per criterion, with a clickable evidence trail (page + bounding box) that survives CVC, CAG and RTI review.How it worksTender understanding — Docling + PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 parse the RFP; Qwen2.5-VL with Outlines constrained decoding extracts eligibility criteria (technical / financial / compliance, mandatory / optional), each anchored to its exact clause.Bidder understanding — multi-engine OCR (PaddleOCR-VL → olmOCR 2 fallback), YOLOv9s stamp/signature detection with LaMa inpaint + re-OCR, TrOCR-Indic for handwriting, TableFormer for tables, Bhashini NMT for Indic text.Evaluation — the LLM decides nothing. PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


P patelsanskrati05_6347
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Sutradhara: AI-Powered Two-Way Interoperability Layer Between Karnataka Single Window System and Department Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 10:50 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Sutradhara is an AI-powered middleware interoperability platform designed to solve one of Karnataka’s most important digital governance challenges: enabling seamless two-way synchronization between the Single Window System (SWS) and 40+ independent department systems such as Factories, Labour, Pollution Control Board, BESCOM, BWSSB, Fire, Food Safety, Urban Local Bodies, and sector regulators. Today, businesses interact with multiple disconnected government systems, where updates made in one portal often remain outdated in others. This creates fragmented records, operational delays, repeated document verification, inconsistent decisions, and poor citizen experience. Sutradhara solves this problem by acting as a secure orchestration layer between systems without requiring any migration, replacement, or modification of existing departmental software. Using the Unique Business Identifier (UBID) as the common identity key, the platform intelligently links records across systems and propagates verified updates in both directions. Changes originating in SWS automatically flow to relevant departments, while updates initiated in department systems are reflected back into SWS. The platform is designed for reliability with retry-safe delivery, conflict detection, explainable resolution policies, and complete auditability of every action. If two systems update the same business field differently, Sutradhara detects the conflict and applies transparent rules such as trusted-source priority, timestamp precedence, or human review. Every propagation is traceable end-to-end through an evidence graph, and historical events can be replayed safely to correct issues or validate policy changes. Sutradhara uses AI responsibly only as an assistance layer for onboarding new departments, suggesting schema mappings, recommending conflict rules, and explaining anomalies. No raw sensitive data is exposed, as all records pass through a privacy-preserving tokenizer before any AI processing. This solution enables faster approvals, consistent business records across departments, lower reconciliation workload, stronger compliance monitoring, better investor confidence, and future-ready interoperable governance infrastructure for Karnataka. Unlike theoretical concepts, Sutradhara is based on production-tested integration patterns already implemented by the team, making it practical, scalable, and deployment-ready for government systems. For complete architecture, technical design, and supporting documents, please refer to:   https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iZdwiRqO7MpV6XeUpVV6kwxSAhXqqIfB/view?usp=sharing

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE

K kvsabhiram
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NIRNAYAK — AI-Powered Tender Bid Evaluation Agent for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 10:38 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
What is NIRNAYAK?NIRNAYAK (Neural Intelligence for Review, Normalization & Assessment of Yields in Automated Knowledge-driven procurement) is an AI-powered bid evaluation agent that automates the eligibility screening of government tender submissions — making the process faster, consistent, explainable, and legally defensible."Har Bid. Har Shart. Poora Sachcha Nirnay."﻿The Problem We Are SolvingManual tender evaluation at organisations like CRPF is broken in four ways:Slow — committees spend days reading hundreds of pages across 10+ biddersInconsistent — two evaluators on the same documents may reach different verdictsError-prone — a scanned certificate buried on page 47 of a 200-page bid gets missedHard to audit — disqualified vendors can challenge decisions; post-hoc proof is nearly impossible﻿How NIRNAYAK Works — 5-Layer PipelineLayer 1 — Document Ingestion Every document format is handled: typed PDFs (PyMuPDF), scanned PDFs and photographs (AWS Textract with word-level confidence scores), Word files (python-docx), and financial tables (Camelot/pdfplumber). All output is normalised to clean text with page references and confidence scores attached.Layer 2 — AI Extraction (LLM Core) Two separate pipelines powered by Claude claude-sonnet-4-20250514:Tender Criteria Extractor — two-pass pipeline: first segments eligibility clauses, then uses chain-of-thought reasoning to extract structured criteria (type, threshold, time window, mandatory/optional flag) as JSONBidder Value Extractor — criterion-targeted prompts search each bidder's documents using a curated evidence pattern library (12+ known patterns per criterion type), returning extracted value + confidence scoreLayer 3 — Matching & Verdict Engine A fully deterministic rules engine (separate from the AI) applies the extracted rules:Financial criteria → numeric comparisonTechnical criteria → count + category + date window checkCompliance/certification → document presence + expiry validationThree and only three possible verdicts per criterion:✅ ELIGIBLE — confidence ≥ 0.85 and rule passes❌ NOT ELIGIBLE — confidence ≥ 0.85 and rule fails⚠️ MANUAL REVIEW — confidence < 0.85 or document not foundLayer 4 — Human-in-the-Loop Every MANUAL REVIEW case is surfaced to the procurement officer with full context: the criterion being checked, the document searched, the value found (if any), the confidence score, and the plain-English reason for uncertainty. The system never silently disqualifies a bidder — ambiguity is always a handoff to a human.Layer 5 — Tamper-Evident Audit Trail Every extraction, match, verdict, and human decision is recorded as an immutable append-only event with SHA-256 hash chaining. Every automated decision traces back to a specific document page, a specific extracted value, and a specific rule — suitable for CVC scrutiny and formal procurement hearings.﻿Sample OutputFor a construction services tender (turnover ≥ ₹5 Cr, GST, ISO 9001, 3 similar projects):CriterionRequiredFoundVerdictAnnual Turnover₹5 Cr₹6.2 Cr✅ ELIGIBLEGST RegistrationValid29ABCDE1234F1Z5✅ ELIGIBLEISO 9001Valid certNot found❌ NOT ELIGIBLESimilar Projects x33 / 5 yrs2 confirmed, 1 unreadable⚠️ MANUAL REVIEW﻿What Makes NIRNAYAK DifferentZero silent disqualifications — architecturally impossible; every uncertain case goes to officer reviewDeterministic rules engine separate from AI — the matching logic is fully readable and auditable without AI expertiseEvidence pattern library — handles the 12+ ways a bidder might prove annual turnover across different document typesChain-of-thought audit record — the LLM's reasoning scratchpad is preserved alongside every extractionOn-premise deployment option — classified tenders never leave the network (Tesseract OCR fallback)Indian procurement context built-in — GFR 2017 aligned, handles GSTIN formats, Form 3CB/3CD, CA certificates, and Indian government document conventions﻿Technology StackComponentTechnologyLLMClaude claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Anthropic)OCRAWS Textract (primary) / Tesseract (on-premise fallback)Document parsingPyMuPDF, python-docx, CamelotRules enginePython + Pydantic (fully deterministic)Audit storePostgreSQL append-only with SHA-256 hash chainAPI / UIFastAPI + React﻿Round 2 Plan5 sprints across 14 days: document ingestion pipeline → tender criteria extractor → bidder value extractor + rules engine → report generation + audit trail → end-to-end testing on sandbox tenders.Success metrics: ≥95% criterion extraction accuracy, ≥90% verdict accuracy on clear cases, 100% MANUAL REVIEW routing on uncertain cases, <3 minutes processing time per bidder.﻿NIRNAYAK is not designed to replace procurement officers. It is designed to make every decision rigorous, consistent, and legally defensible — so officers can focus their expertise where it matters most.

Project Description
TenderMind AI is an end-to-end, explainable artificial intelligence platform designed to automate and standardize the evaluation of government tenders by accurately interpreting complex tender documents and analyzing diverse bidder submissions. The system begins by processing tender documents using advanced natural language processing techniques to extract and structure eligibility criteria, including technical requirements, financial thresholds, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications, while distinguishing between compulsory and optional rules. Ambiguous or low-confidence cases are automatically flagged for human review, preventing incorrect or unfair disqualification. The platform maintains a complete audit trail of all processing steps, making it suitable for formal government decision-making and compliance requirements. With an intuitive user interface that allows procurement officers to upload documents, review evaluations, and export consolidated reports, and a scalable cloud-based architecture that can be deployed across departments, TenderMind AI significantly reduces evaluation time, improves consistency, enhances fairness, and brings accountability to the public procurement process.

solutions.team_name: NyayaCheck — Explainable AI Procurement Auditor for Government Tenders
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N NyayaCheck — Explainable AI Procurement Auditor for Government Tenders
TEAM SUBMISSION [5]
NyayaCheck — Explainable AI Procurement Auditor for Government Tenders
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 08:35 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A CRPF procurement committee evaluating 20 bidders manually spends 4 to 6 days cross-checking hundreds of pages. Two evaluators reading the same document will often reach different conclusions. One missed criterion means a wrong award. One untraceable decision means a court challenge. NyayaCheck eliminates both problems. NyayaCheck is a three-engine AI platform that takes a tender document and any number of bidder submissions and delivers a fully explainable, criterion-level eligibility report in under 30 minutes — regardless of whether documents are typed PDFs, scanned certificates, photographs, or Word files. ENGINE 1 — CriteriaLens: Reads the tender document and converts every eligibility rule into a structured schema — criterion text, mandatory vs optional, numeric threshold, reference period, and acceptable evidence documents. Legal markers like "shall", "must", "essential" are detected automatically. Ambiguous mandatory status is flagged for officer confirmation before evaluation begins. The system never assumes. ENGINE 2 — DocProbe: Processes every bidder submission regardless of format. For digital PDFs: direct text extraction. For scanned documents and photographs: a preprocessing pipeline (deskew, contrast enhancement, noise removal) before OCR. For each criterion, DocProbe finds the exact page, paragraph, and value — or returns an explicit "not found" signal. It never invents evidence. ENGINE 3 — VerdictCore: Scores each match with a four-factor confidence rating: OCR quality, evidence-criterion alignment, document authenticity signals, and value parseability. Above 85% and threshold clearly met: Eligible. Above 85% and threshold clearly missed: Not Eligible. Anything below 85%, borderline values within 10% of threshold, conflicting evidence, or missing evidence: Needs Officer Review — with full context surfaced. Nothing is ever silently rejected. AUDITABILITY: Every verdict cites criterion ID, document name, page number, extracted value, and plain-English reason. Immutable audit log: append-only, SHA-256 chained, tamper-evident. Consolidated report exportable as signed PDF — ready to endorse and archive. Officer review decisions feed back as labelled examples to improve future evaluations. RISKS ADDRESSED: Badly scanned documents may still defeat OCR — mitigated by confidence flagging and the review queue. LLMs can hallucinate evidence — mitigated by requiring source citation for every extraction and officer confirmation before the report is finalised. Forged documents cannot be verified by AI — the system audits but final responsibility stays with the officer, as it should. ROUND 2 PLAN: Week 1: tender parser + criterion schema + OCR pipeline. Week 2: verdict engine + confidence scoring + review queue UI. Week 3: report generator + audit trail + end-to-end test on 10 synthetic bidders. Deliverable: working web prototype, GitHub repo, signed sample report, 5-minute demo video. NON-NEGOTIABLES MET: No silent disqualification. Criterion-level explainability. Scanned document support. End-to-end audit trail for government compliance.

Setu AI — Intelligent Bidirectional Interoperability Layer for Karnataka Government Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 08:37 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A business in Karnataka updates its registered address on the Single Window System and walks away thinking it is done. It is not. The Factories system still has the old address. The Labour system still has the old address. Three months later, an inspector arrives at the wrong location. The citizen did everything right — the system failed them. Setu AI ends this.
Setu AI is a stateless middleware layer that propagates service requests bidirectionally between Karnataka's SWS and its 40+ legacy department systems — with zero modifications to either side. UBID is the only join key. Every propagation is idempotent, auditable, and conflict-aware.
DIRECTION 1 — SWS TO DEPARTMENTS: When a service request is raised on SWS, Setu AI identifies every department record for the same UBID and translates the SWS payload into each department's native schema using a declarative mapping engine. Mappings are defined per department as configuration — not code — so onboarding a new department takes days, not months. Each write is confirmed. Failed writes enter an exponential backoff retry queue with idempotency keys that prevent duplicate writes on retry. The SWS event is not marked complete until all department writes are confirmed or escalated.
DIRECTION 2 — DEPARTMENTS TO SWS: Legacy systems do not all emit events. Setu AI uses a three-tier detection strategy: webhook subscription where supported, REST polling where an API exists, and scheduled snapshot diffing for systems that expose only bulk exports. No change goes undetected regardless of how primitive the source system is.
CONFLICT RESOLUTION: When updates to the same UBID arrive from two sources within a configurable window, Setu AI detects the collision and applies a declared policy — configurable per field type: timestamp priority for factual fields, source trust ranking for operational data, or officer escalation for high-stakes fields. The policy, inputs, and resolution are all recorded. No silent overwrites. Ever.
AUDIT TRAIL: Every propagation generates an immutable record: source system, destination system, UBID, field changed, old value, new value, timestamp, idempotency key, retry count, and conflict resolution if applied. Any service request traced end-to-end in under 10 seconds.
RISKS ADDRESSED: Department system goes offline mid-propagation — retry queue handles this with at-least-once delivery and idempotency on the receiving end. Schema changes in a department system break the mapping — the engine validates against a known schema version and alerts rather than silently corrupting data. Clock skew between systems causes false conflict detection — timestamps normalised to IST at ingestion with a configurable grace window.
ROUND 2 PLAN: Week 1: mock SWS and 3 department endpoints + bidirectional propagation engine. Week 2: conflict detection + resolution policies + audit trail store. Week 3: snapshot diff engine for event-less systems + monitoring dashboard + integration test. Deliverable: working prototype handling the exact address-change and signatory-update scenarios from the problem statement, full audit trail, GitHub repo, 5-minute demo video.
NON-NEGOTIABLES MET: Zero modifications to SWS or a

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Adaptive Screen Interaction System Using AI-Based Gesture and Voice Control
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 01:09 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
ASIS (Adaptive Screen Interaction System) is an AI-based multimodal human-computer interaction system that enables users to control a computer without using traditional input devices like a mouse or keyboard. The system uses computer vision, machine learning, and speech processing to interpret user inputs through hand gestures, eye movements, and voice commands.A webcam captures real-time video, and MediaPipe is used to detect and track hand landmarks. These landmarks are processed and classified using machine learning algorithms such as K-Nearest Neighbors to recognize user-defined gestures. Based on the detected gesture, actions like cursor movement, clicking, dragging, and scrolling are performed using PyAutoGUI.The system also integrates a voice assistant using VOSK, which allows users to activate the system with a wake word (“Jarvis”) and execute commands such as clicking, scrolling, and opening applications. Additionally, eye-blink detection is used as an alternative method for clicking, improving accessibility.A key feature of this system is its adaptive learning capability, where users can train their own gestures, making the interaction personalized and more intuitive. The system operates in real-time with optimized performance and minimal latency.ASIS demonstrates the potential of AI-driven, touchless interfaces and can be applied in assistive technologies, smart environments, gaming, and next-generation human-computer interaction systems.

solutions.team_name: ai-tender-evaluation-solution
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N NyayaAI
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ai-tender-evaluation-solution
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 04:51 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
For a detailed version of this solution with full technical explanations and diagrams, please refer to the complete document here:  here  (https://drive.google.com/file/d/1tzu3A-VbMSoJV-skJA2NA_NuCWsA0_LH/view?usp=sharing)1. Understanding the ProblemGovernment procurement, especially in organisations like CRPF, involves evaluating large volumes of documents submitted by different companies. Each bidder provides multiple documents such as financial statements, project certificates, tax registrations, and sometimes scanned or photographed copies of physical records.Currently, procurement officers manually review these documents to check whether each bidder meets the eligibility criteria defined in the tender. This process is time-consuming and has several limitations:Different evaluators may interpret the same documents differently Important details can be missed when dealing with large document sets Decisions are difficult to track and audit later The core challenge is to reliably extract useful information from unstructured documents and apply eligibility rules consistently, while keeping every decision transparent and traceable.﻿2. Overview of the SystemThe proposed system is designed as a structured pipeline with five main components:Extracting and understanding the tender requirements Building a searchable knowledge base from documents Processing bidder submissions in different formats Evaluating each bidder against the criteria Generating reports and visual outputs Each component works independently but connects smoothly with the others. This makes the system easier to test, maintain, and improve over time. The system is also designed to work in secure environments, with the option to run locally when handling sensitive data.﻿3. Understanding the TenderTender documents are first converted into machine-readable text. If the document is already digital, it can be processed directly. If it is scanned, image preprocessing and OCR techniques are used to extract the text.From this text, the system identifies eligibility criteria such as financial thresholds, technical requirements, and mandatory certifications. Each criterion is stored in a structured format that includes:A unique identifier The type of requirement (financial, technical, compliance, etc.) Whether it is mandatory Any threshold values (for example, minimum turnover) Keywords that help locate supporting evidence in bidder documents Special attention is given to identifying mandatory conditions using words like “shall” and “must”. Before proceeding further, a procurement officer reviews and confirms the extracted criteria to ensure correctness.﻿4. Understanding the Documents (RAG Approach)To improve accuracy, the system uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach. All documents are broken into smaller sections and converted into embeddings, which are stored in a vector database. When evaluating a specific criterion, the system retrieves only the most relevant parts of the documents instead of processing everything at once.These retrieved sections are then passed to the language model, which is instructed to extract information strictly from the provided content. If the required information is not present, the system clearly reports that it was not found. This approach ensures that all outputs are grounded in actual document content and helps avoid incorrect assumptions.﻿5. Processing Bidder DocumentsThe system is designed to handle multiple types of documents, including :Digital PDFs Scanned PDFs Images and photographs Word documents Tables and spreadsheets Digital files are processed directly, while scanned documents and images go through OCR. Image preprocessing techniques such as noise removal and contrast enhancement are applied to improve accuracy .Each extracted value is stored along with The source text The page number A confidence score If the quality of a document is too low or the confidence score is below a defined threshold, the case is flagged for manual review instead of making an uncertain decision.﻿6. Evaluating EligibilityThe evaluation process combines rule-based logic with AI-based understanding. Rule-based logic is used for clear checks such as numeric comparisons (e.g., turnover ≥ ₹5 crore), date validation, and boolean conditions AI-based methods help interpret variations in language, such as different terms used for the same concept For example, terms like “net sales” and “annual turnover” are treated as equivalent using semantic similarity techniques .Each criterion is evaluated and assigned one of three outcomes :Eligible — when the requirement is clearly satisfied Not Eligible — when the requirement is clearly not met Manual Review — when the system is unsure due to low confidence, missing data, or conflicting information The system never rejects a bidder without providing a clear reason.﻿7. Human-in-the-Loop and Continuous Improvement Whenever the system is uncertain, it flags the case for human review.Procurement officers can:View the extracted values along with the original document context Correct any incorrect values Override system decisions with a justification Request additional or clearer documents All actions are recorded. Corrections made by officers are stored and can be used to improve the system’s performance over time.﻿8. Explainability , Audit, and SecurityEvery decision made by the system is supported by clear evidence, including The criterion being evaluated The value extracted The source document and page The reasoning behind the decision The confidence level An audit log records every step in the process, from document upload to final decision. The log is designed to be tamper-resistant and suitable for official review. The system also supports secure deployment options On-premise or air-gapped environments Controlled user access Encrypted data storage and communication ﻿9. Dashboard and ReportingThe system provides a simple visual dashboard that displays results in a grid format, with bidders on one axis and criteria on the other. Green indicates that the requirement is satisfied Red indicates that it is not satisfied Yellow indicates that manual review is required Users can click on any result to see detailed explanations and supporting evidence. A final report is generated that includes A summary of all bidders Detailed evaluation for each criterion A list of cases that require manual review References to supporting documents ﻿10. Measuring System PerformanceThe effectiveness of the system is evaluated using Accuracy of criterion extraction Accuracy of data extraction from documents OCR performance on scanned documents Precision and recall of eligibility decisions Number of cases incorrectly rejected (target: zero) Percentage of cases requiring manual review These metrics help ensure that the system remains reliable and continues to improve.﻿11. Technology StackThe prototype uses a practical and lightweight stackBackend: Python with FastAPI Frontend: React with Tailwind CSS OCR: Tesseract with image preprocessing AI Model: GPT-4o or equivalent Retrieval: FAISS with embedding models Database: PostgreSQL Storage: Local or object storage The system can also be adapted to run with local models in secure environments.﻿12. Risks and MitigationSome challenges and their solutions include Poor document quality: handled using preprocessing and fallback methods OCR errors: managed through confidence thresholds and manual review Variation in terminology: handled using semantic matching Data privacy concerns: addressed through secure and local deployment Model limitations: mitigated through human validation and audit logging This system is designed to support procurement officers, not replace them. Every decision is based on actual document evidence, clearly explained, and fully traceable. Any uncertain case is brought to human attention rather than being automatically rejected. The goal is to make the procurement process faster, more consistent, and easier to audit, while maintaining fairness and transparency.

solutions.team_name: TenderLens AI — Explainable AI Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform for CRPF
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T TenderLens AI
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
TenderLens AI — Explainable AI Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform for CRPF
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 09:34 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderLens AI — Explainable AI Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform for CRPFThe Problem Government organisations like the CRPF issue tenders with detailed eligibility requirements technical specifications, financial thresholds, compliance rules, mandatory certifications, and document checklists written in formal legal language and spread across hundreds of pages. Private bidders respond with heterogeneous submissions: typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word files, tables, and even photographs of physical certificates. Today, evaluation committees spend days manually cross-checking these documents against criteria. The process is slow, inconsistent across evaluators, prone to oversight, and hard to audit. Two officers reviewing the same bid can reach different conclusions, and there is no traceable trail to justify final decisions.The Solution TenderLens AI is an explainable AI platform that automates tender evaluation end-to-end while keeping a human officer firmly in control of every consequential decision.How It WorksTender Understanding: The system ingests a tender document and uses a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline combined with structured LLM extraction to identify every eligibility criterion — separating technical, financial, and compliance conditions, and distinguishing mandatory from optional requirements. Extracted criteria are stored in a structured, machine-matchable form.Bidder Document Parsing: Each bidder submission is processed through a heterogeneous document pipeline pdfplumber for typed PDFs, Tesseract and AWS Textract for scanned documents and photographs, and table extraction for financial statements. The system handles format variation, partial information, and inconsistent presentation across bidders.Criterion-Level Matching: For each bidder, the engine matches extracted evidence against every criterion. It produces a verdict Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review along with a confidence score. Thresholds are calibrated so that clearly passing or failing cases are auto-decided, but ambiguous ones are routed to a human reviewer instead of being silently disqualified.Explainability Layer: Every verdict is traceable end-to-end. The officer can click a verdict and see the exact criterion, the specific document, the page, and the extracted value that drove the decision. No black-box judgments.Human-in-the-Loop Review: Ambiguous cases low-confidence matches, unreadable scans, conflicting values surface in a reviewer workflow with the reason for ambiguity. Reviewer decisions are captured and feed back into the system to improve future evaluations.Consolidated Audit Report: The platform generates a signed, exportable evaluation report suitable for formal government procurement, with a complete audit trail of every automated decision, every document reference, and every human intervention.Architecture & Tech Stack Frontend: React with an officer-friendly dashboard for tender upload, bidder management, verdict review, and report export. Backend: FastAPI (Python) with modular services for document ingestion, criterion extraction, matching, and audit logging. Document Processing: pdfplumber (typed PDFs) + Tesseract OCR + AWS Textract (scanned docs and photos) + table-structure recognition. AI Core: RAG pipeline with sentence-transformers embeddings + FAISS vector search for criterion retrieval; AWS Bedrock (Claude / Titan) for structured criterion extraction and evidence matching. Explainability: Every decision stores criterion ID, source document, page number, extracted snippet, confidence score, and rule trace. Audit & Compliance: Immutable audit log (append-only) with cryptographic hashing for every decision; reversible by reviewer action. Security: No hosted-LLM calls on raw PII all sensitive data is redacted or processed on-premise/VPC-isolated inference as per non-negotiables.Why It Wins Built for the real constraints procurement officers need to SIGN decisions, not rubber-stamp them. Every verdict is explainable at the criterion level, every ambiguous case surfaces for review, and every decision is auditable end-to-end. The platform never silently disqualifies a bidder. It turns days of manual evaluation into minutes of reviewer-assisted decision-making, with full traceability for any future audit or legal challenge.Impact For CRPF and similar government procurement bodies, TenderLens AI reduces evaluation time by 80%+, eliminates inconsistency across evaluators, creates defensible audit trails for every tender, and levels the playing field for bidders by ensuring uniform, rule-based evaluation. The architecture is domain-agnostic and can be extended to other government procurement, grants, and eligibility-driven workflows.Non-Negotiables Respected ✓ Every verdict is criterion-level explainable ✓ No silent disqualification ambiguous cases go to human review ✓ Handles scanned documents and photographs, not just digital text ✓ End-to-end auditable, suitable for formal government procurement ✓ No hosted-LLM calls on raw PII; compliant with data-sovereignty requirementsOne tender. Ten bidders. One defensible, auditable decision in minutes, not days.

solutions.team_name: "UBID Bridge" — A Stateful Interoperability Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A avanish.23mim10017_9b44
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
"UBID Bridge" — A Stateful Interoperability Engine
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 10:06 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
"UBID Bridge" — A Stateful Interoperability EngineThe core idea is a standalone middleware service we call the UBID Bridge. It sits entirely outside SWS and all department systems, integrates with each using only their existing APIs and data surfaces, and acts as the single orchestrator of all cross-system propagation.﻿﻿DIRECTION 1 — SWS to DepartmentsWhen SWS receives a service request (e.g., change of registered address), it publishes an event. The Bridge subscribes to this via SWS's existing API or webhook surface. On receipt, the Bridge looks up the UBID from the event, consults a pre-built department registry to determine which department systems carry a record for that UBID, and fans out translation+write tasks to each relevant system.Each write task is idempotent by design: before writing, the Bridge checks a Redis-backed deduplication store keyed on (UBID, event_id, target_system). If the key exists, the write is skipped. If the write fails, the task is retried with exponential back-off using a message queue (Kafka). The retry carries the same event ID, so even if an earlier partial write succeeded, the idempotency check prevents a duplicate. A confirmed write produces an audit record: what changed, from which source, to which target, timestamp, and the final state written.Schema translation is handled by a per-department Translator module — a small, stateless function that maps SWS's canonical field names to a target department's schema. New departments are onboarded by writing one new translator, registered in a config file. No core engine changes are needed.﻿﻿DIRECTION 2 — Departments to SWSThis direction is harder because department systems are heterogeneous in their event surfaces. Our Bridge handles three tiers:Systems that support webhooks or push APIs register with the Bridge directly. Systems with polling APIs are covered by a scheduled poller that fetches state at configurable intervals and diffs against the last known snapshot (stored in the Bridge's own state store). Systems with no API at all are handled by a snapshot comparator that reads database exports or flat-file dumps and detects row-level changes using a hash comparison.In all three cases, once a change is detected, it is translated into SWS's canonical format and written to SWS via its existing API, again with full idempotency and audit trail.Conflict Detection and ResolutionWhen two updates for the same UBID arrive within a configurable time window from different sources, the Bridge flags a conflict rather than silently applying one. Three resolution policies are configurable per field type: Last-Write-Wins (default for low-stakes fields), Source-Priority (SWS wins over department systems by default, configurable per department), and Manual-Escalation (the conflict is held in a review queue and neither write is applied until a human resolves it). Every resolved conflict writes a special audit record explaining which policy was applied, what both conflicting values were, and what the final accepted state is. This makes every resolution explainable and reversible.﻿IDEMPOTENCY AND AUDITEvery propagation carries a globally unique event ID. The Bridge never processes the same event ID twice for the same target. All audit records are written to a PostgreSQL append-only table — no row is ever updated or deleted. An officer can query any UBID and see its complete change history across all systems, including retries, conflicts, and resolutions.

solutions.team_name: "TenderLens" — An Explainable AI Procurement Evaluator
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A avanish.23mim10017_9b44
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
"TenderLens" — An Explainable AI Procurement Evaluator
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 14, 2026 10:08 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
"TenderLens" — An Explainable AI Procurement EvaluatorStep 1 — Criterion Extraction from the TenderThe tender document is parsed using a vision-capable LLM (for scanned pages) combined with a structured text extractor (for digital PDFs). We prompt the LLM to extract each eligibility criterion and classify it as mandatory or optional, labelling its type (financial, technical, compliance, certification) and its exact threshold or condition. The output is a structured JSON list of criteria, each with a source page reference. A procurement officer reviews and confirms this list before any bidder evaluation begins. This review step is non-negotiable — it prevents AI hallucination from corrupting the evaluation basis.Step 2 — Bidder Document ParsingEach bidder's submission is run through a multi-stage document understanding pipeline. Digital PDFs are parsed with a standard text extractor. Scanned documents and photographs are processed with an OCR engine (Tesseract or a cloud vision API) before LLM extraction. Tables are extracted using a table structure detection model. The LLM is prompted to extract, for each criterion, the specific value or evidence the bidder has provided — along with the source document name, page, and verbatim excerpt.Critically, if the LLM cannot extract a value with reasonable confidence, it does not guess — it returns a "not found / low confidence" flag instead. This is the mechanism that prevents silent disqualification.Step 3 — Matching and VerdictFor each criterion and each bidder, the Bridge compares the extracted evidence value against the criterion threshold. Numeric comparisons (turnover ≥ ₹5 crore) are handled deterministically. Fuzzy matches (project descriptions that may or may not meet a "similar project" requirement) are scored by the LLM with a confidence value. Cases where confidence is below a threshold, where the OCR quality was flagged as poor, or where the extracted value is ambiguous are automatically routed to a Human Review Queue — they are never silently failed.Each verdict (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review) is accompanied by a full evidence chain: which criterion, which document, which page, what value was found, and what rule was applied.Step 4 — Consolidated Report and AuditThe system generates a per-bidder criterion matrix and a summary report. Every cell in the matrix is traceable to its source document and LLM extraction call. The report is exportable as a PDF suitable for a procurement committee sign-off. The entire evaluation — every extraction, every comparison, every routing decision — is logged to an immutable audit table. An officer can later reconstruct exactly how any verdict was reached.Technical Stack and ArchitectureA message queue (Kafka) for reliable async delivery, Redis for idempotency deduplication, PostgreSQL for append-only audit storage, and an LLM gateway that operates exclusively on synthetic or scrambled data in compliance with the PII constraint. The interoperability engine and the tender platform are independently deployable services that share only this infrastructure layer.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE

LEADERBOARD


C Code Breakers
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Sutra-K: The Interoperability Fabric for Karnataka SWS
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 15, 2026 12:10 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Executive Summary: Karnataka's digital infrastructure is currently operating with a massive "split-brain" problem. We know that forcing a big-bang migration of 40+ legacy department systems is practically impossible and historically prone to failure. Our solution is an event-driven, bidirectional interoperability layer that sits between the Single Window System (SWS) and existing department databases. We achieve strict data synchronization without modifying a single line of legacy source code. By utilizing the Unique Business Identifier (UBID) as our absolute join key, we act as an invisible traffic controller, ensuring that whether a business updates their profile on a modern SWS portal or a legacy endpoint, the state remains consistent state-wide.1. Problem Relevance & The "Split-Brain" Resolution: The core bottleneck is that departments operate in isolated silos. A manufacturing business might update their registered address in the SWS, but the State Pollution Control Board still queries the outdated address from their legacy database, leading to compliance failures. Instead of attempting to replace these legacy systems, our middleware simply translates schemas and routes service requests bidirectionally. It treats both the SWS and the legacy departments as clients, ensuring 100% data parity across the state's ecosystem while respecting the boundaries of the existing infrastructure.﻿2. Technical Implementation & The Interoperability Engine: We are engineering a robust middleware architecture designed specifically for environments where endpoints are unreliable or lack modern webhook capabilities:SWS to Legacy (The Push): When a service request is generated in SWS, our message broker intercepts the payload. It passes the data through a modular JSON mapping layer that translates the SWS schema into the specific format required by the target department's API. We utilize robust retry queues with exponential backoff to handle legacy system downtime without dropping payloads.Legacy to SWS (State Inference via Polling): This is the hardest constraint to solve, as many legacy systems do not natively emit events. To counter this, we deploy an asynchronous High-Speed Polling Engine. It periodically fetches records for active UBIDs, computes a cryptographic hash of the current state, and compares it to our last known hash. If a delta is detected, we extract the updated fields, reverse-translate the schema, and push the update to SWS.Conflict Resolution: In a distributed system, simultaneous updates will inevitably collide. If updates happen on both sides at the same time, our Deterministic Timestamp Triage kicks in. We configure strict, explainable rules (e.g., SWS owns global profile data, legacy systems own historical compliance data) to resolve the merge automatically, ensuring no data is ever silently overwritten.﻿3. Real-World Deplorability & The Cryptographic Audit Ledger Government systems require absolute accountability. Every single merge, schema translation, and conflict resolution needs to be verifiable by a human reviewer.Zero-Touch Integration: We strictly adhere to the non-negotiable rule of not altering source systems. We integrate purely via the APIs they already expose.Immutable Audit Ledger & Reversibility: Every propagation, conflict resolution, and schema translation is cryptographically logged. Because this ledger is append-only, any incorrect merge or resolution is 100% reversible. A procurement officer can trace any service request end-to-end and instantly roll back to a previous known state, guaranteeing complete accountability.4. Technology Stack, Risks & Trade-OffsThe Tech Stack: The core middleware and routing logic will utilize a high-performance message broker (e.g., Redis Streams/RabbitMQ). The high-speed Polling Engine will be written in C to ensure minimal memory overhead while calculating state hashes. The visual Observer Dashboard will be built using a modern frontend framework to render real-time telemetry.Identified Risk - API Rate Limiting & Legacy Overload: Polling legacy systems that lack webhooks risks overwhelming their servers.The Trade-Off & Mitigation: We trade real-time sync speed for system stability. We will implement Adaptive Polling—systems with high transaction volumes are polled frequently, while dormant UBIDs are polled asynchronously during off-peak hours, protecting legacy uptime.﻿5. Scalability & Long-Term Impact To scale this across 40+ departments, we designed the schema translator to be highly modular. Adding the 42nd department system does not require rewriting the core engine; it is as simple as writing a new JSON mapping configuration for that specific department. This keeps integration complexity at O(1), allowing for the rapid, cost-effective onboarding of future municipal bodies﻿6. Grand Finale Demo Strategy If selected for the Bengaluru finale, our team will deploy a locally simulated, end-to-end environment:The Backend: A functional message broker managing the bidirectional data flow between mocked SWS and legacy endpoints.The Frontend Observer Dashboard: Because the middleware itself is invisible, we will deploy a high-fidelity visualizer to act as the system's "nerve center." This custom dashboard will visually demonstrate the exact flow of data, highlight webhook intercepts, flag simulated conflicts, and display the immutable audit ledger in real-time, proving to the jury that the synchronization works flawlessly under failure conditions.

C Code Breakers
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Sentinel-AI: Multi-Modal & Explainable Procurement Evaluation Engine
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 15, 2026 12:20 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Executive Summary: Government procurement evaluation is currently a slow, manual bottleneck prone to human fatigue. We approach this not as a simple generative AI task, but as a rigid data-forensics operation. Sentinel-AI is a multi-modal, Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) pipeline designed for the CRPF. It ingests wildly heterogeneous bidder documents (clean PDFs, skewed scans, and photographs), extracts criteria into strict schemas, and produces a deterministic, 100% auditable eligibility report. We do not just provide an AI verdict; we provide the mathematical confidence score and physically point to the exact evidence in the document.1. Problem Understanding & The Realities of Procurement Government tenders are legally binding frameworks, not simple Q&A documents. Bidders submit evidence in chaotic formats—a ₹5 crore turnover requirement might be buried in a blurry photograph of a chartered accountant's certificate. We recognize that an AI hallucinating a number here has massive legal and financial ramifications.2. Tender Criteria Extraction Layer To understand the tender, we utilize an LLM pipeline heavily constrained by JSON output parsing. It breaks the tender down into a strict evaluation schema, categorizing every extracted rule into Technical, Financial, or Compliance buckets, and explicitly flagging each as Mandatory or Optional using keyword heuristics (e.g., "shall" vs "may").3. Multi-Modal Parsing & Value Mapping We handle heterogeneous documents using a two-staged pipeline:Ingestion: Standard PDFs are parsed directly. Scans, tables, and photographs are passed through advanced Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and layout-aware Vision-Language Models to normalize the text while retaining spatial relationships.Extraction: We map bidder information to the criteria schema. To handle language variation, we use semantic vector search (RAG) rather than brittle keyword matching, allowing the system to understand that "Annual Revenue" satisfies the "Turnover" requirement.4. Explainability & The "No Silent Rejection" HITL ProtocolBounding-Box Evidence: Every extracted value is tied to its spatial coordinates in the source document. The final report explains verdicts by hyperlinking directly to the exact highlighted paragraph or table cell.Human-in-the-Loop (HITL): The system will never silently disqualify a bidder. We calculate a confidence score for every extraction. If a scan is too blurry or language is ambiguous (falling below an 85% confidence threshold), the bidder is flagged as Needs Manual Review, allowing a human officer to make the final call.5. Auditability for Government Context Every evaluation generates an immutable, cryptographic audit log. It records the exact AI prompt used, the model version, the confidence score, and the extracted data points. This ensures that years later, an auditor can mathematically prove exactly why a contractor was selected or rejected.6. Architecture, Tech Stack, & RisksTech Stack: Python backend orchestrating EasyOCR/Tesseract for image normalization, a vector database for semantic search, and an isolated, constrained LLM for data extraction.Risk & Mitigation: The primary risk is LLM math hallucinations (e.g., the AI incorrectly comparing ₹4.5Cr to ₹5Cr). Trade-off: We strip math away from the AI. The LLM only extracts the numbers; standard, deterministic Python logic evaluates the numerical thresholds to guarantee 100% mathematical accuracy.7. Round 2 Implementation & Demo Plan Our team of 4 will deploy a functional "Procurement Command Center" for the sandbox demo. Yadnesh and Yug will manage the backend OCR and extraction pipeline, while our frontend lead handles the presentation layer. We will upload an intentionally messy mock submission (including cell phone photos of tax certificates). The jury will watch the pipeline parse the noise, flag a blurry document for manual review, and generate a visually verifiable, consolidated PDF evaluation report.

A ayushi771_10eb
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
NullSync — Intelligent Bidirectional Interoperability Layer for Karnataka Government Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 15, 2026 12:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Karnataka's Single Window System and 40+ legacy department systems drift out of sync daily — a split-brain problem with no reliable fix today. NullSync is a stateless, non-invasive middleware layer that propagates service requests bidirectionally, without touching either SWS or any department system.SWS → Departments: When a change is raised in SWS (e.g., address update), NullSync identifies all department systems where the same UBID exists, translates the SWS payload into each target's schema using a schema mapping registry (stored in MongoDB), and writes via the target's existing API. Each write is idempotent — duplicate detection via a content hash + UBID + event-type composite key prevents double-writes even on retries.Departments → SWS: For systems that emit webhooks, NullSync subscribes directly. For systems with no native event emission, NullSync uses scheduled snapshot diffing — periodic lightweight API polls that compare current state to the last known snapshot and extract changes. All changes are translated into the SWS schema and written with UBID as the join key.Conflict Detection: When two updates for the same UBID arrive within a configurable time window, NullSync flags the conflict, applies a configurable resolution policy (e.g., SWS-authoritative, latest-timestamp, or escalate-to-reviewer), and records the resolution in a full audit log.Audit Trail: Every propagation event — source, target, payload hash, timestamp, retry count, conflict resolution decision — is written to an immutable audit log in MongoDB with a React-based audit explorer for officers.Non-negotiables respected: No SWS or department system modification. UBID is the only join key. All operations are idempotent and auditable. LLM used only for schema mapping suggestions on synthetic data.Stack: React, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, Redis (idempotency cache), Docker, Node-cron.

TenderLens — Explainable AI Procurement Auditor for Government Tenders
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 15, 2026 12:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tender evaluation today is slow, inconsistent, and hard to audit. TenderLens is an end-to-end AI platform that ingests a tender document and bidder submissions in any format — typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, photographs — and produces criterion-level, explainable eligibility verdicts for every bidder.Tender Understanding: The tender document is parsed using a hybrid pipeline — pdfplumber for digital PDFs, Tesseract OCR for scans. An LLM (Claude/GPT via API, on redacted data only) extracts eligibility criteria and classifies each as mandatory or optional, with financial thresholds, compliance conditions, and document requirements stored as structured JSON in MongoDB.Bidder Parsing: Each bidder submission undergoes OCR + layout analysis. Extracted text is chunked and mapped against each criterion using semantic similarity (sentence-transformers). The system handles variation in how bidders present the same information — e.g., "annual revenue" vs "yearly turnover" vs a financial table row.Evaluation Engine: For each bidder × criterion pair, the system produces: the criterion text, the document used, the extracted value, the match verdict (Pass / Fail / Review), and the reason. Confidence below a threshold routes to human review — never a silent disqualification.Output: A React dashboard shows a consolidated evaluation matrix. A one-click PDF export produces a formally structured report suitable for officer sign-off, with a complete audit trail of every automated decision.Non-negotiables respected: Every verdict is explainable at criterion level. No silent disqualification. Scanned documents and photographs are handled. Full audit trail. LLM operates only on redacted/synthetic data.Stack: React, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, Python (pdfplumber, Tesseract, sentence-transformers), Docker.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderMind AI - Explainable Procurement Intelligence Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 15, 2026 04:39 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Executive Summary: TenderMind AITenderMind AI automates the extraction and evaluation of eligibility criteria in government procurement. By combining Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with deterministic matching rules, the system transforms dense tender documents and varied bidder submissions into structured, auditable evaluation reports.﻿1. Core System CapabilitiesTender Parsing: Extracts mandatory/optional criteria into structured JSON.Multi-Format OCR: Processes typed PDFs, scanned images, and handwritten forms.Deterministic Matching: Uses code-based logic for financial/date thresholds to prevent "AI hallucinations."Human-in-the-Loop: Automatically flags low-confidence or ambiguous cases for officer review.Immutable Audit Trail: Cryptographically signs every extraction and verdict for legal defensibility.﻿2. Technical Architecture & Workflow2.1 Phase 1: Criterion Extraction (The "Rulebook")The system ingests the tender PDF and uses a fine-tuned LLM to identify eligibility sections. It breaks compound requirements into atomic conditions.Example: "3 projects in 5 years" becomes two rules: count >= 3 and date_range <= 5yrs.Officer Input: The procurement officer must confirm this list before bidder parsing begins, ensuring the "ground truth" is 100% accurate.2.2 Phase 2: Bidder Parsing (The "Evidence")Documents are routed based on quality:Digital PDFs: Direct text extraction via PyMuPDF.Scanned/Photos: Processed via Tesseract 5 or Google Cloud Vision.Extraction: The system performs a semantic search to find relevant passages (e.g., finding "Turnover" in a 200-page audit) and extracts the specific value.2.3 Phase 3: Matching & VerdictsVerdicts are assigned based on strict confidence thresholds:VerdictLogicEligibleValue meets threshold + Confidence $> 85\%$Not EligibleValue fails threshold + Confidence $> 85\%$Needs ReviewConfidence $< 85\%$, ambiguous language, or unreadable scan﻿3. Human-in-the-Loop & AuditabilityThe system acts as an Advisory Tool, not a Final Decision Maker.Explainability: Every "Eligible" verdict includes a deep link to the specific page and paragraph used as evidence.Conflict Resolution: If a bidder’s cover letter says "10 Cr" but their balance sheet says "8 Cr," the system detects the discrepancy and triggers a manual review.Non-Repudiation: All logs use SHA-256 hashing, ensuring evaluation records cannot be altered after the fact to favor a specific bidder.﻿4. Implementation Roadmap (4 Weeks)Week 1: Ingestion & OCR pipeline setup.Week 2: LLM-based criterion and evidence extraction.Week 3: Deterministic matching engine and Reviewer UI.Week 4: Audit logging, PDF report generation, and stress testing.﻿5. Risk MitigationHallucination Risk: Mitigated by using LLMs only for retrieval and extraction, while using Python code for mathematical comparison.Data Privacy: Hosted on Azure OpenAI with zero-data-retention; PII is stripped from chunks before processing.Legal Risk: The "Needs Review" default ensures no bidder is ever disqualified silently by an algorithm.Conclusion: TenderMind AI scales the expertise of procurement committees, reducing evaluation time from days to hours while providing a gold-standard audit trail for government transparency.

G gaganlkklr2006_d9c6
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Sentinel-Procure: An Automated Multimodal AI Framework for Explainable Tender Eligibility & Compliance Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 16, 2026 06:40 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Sentinel-Procure: An Automated Multimodal AI Framework for Explainable Tender Eligibility & Compliance Analysis
The Vision In the high-stakes world of government procurement for organizations like the CRPF, accuracy is not just a metric—it’s a legal necessity. Currently, procurement officers are buried under mountains of heterogeneous documents, manually cross-referencing "Clause A" in a tender with "Document B" in a bidder's scanned PDF. Sentinel-Procure transforms this manual, error-prone bottleneck into a streamlined, AI-assisted workflow that ensures every bid is evaluated with 100% consistency and total transparency.
The Solution: A Three-Pillar Approach
Intelligent Tender Deconstruction Our system uses Layout-Aware LLMs (like LayoutLMv3) to "read" tender documents like a human expert. It doesn't just look for keywords; it understands the intent.Mandatory vs. Optional: Automatically distinguishes between "Must" (Non-negotiable) and "May" (Preferential) requirements.Criteria Extraction: Breaks down complex eligibility into a structured checklist of technical specs, financial thresholds (e.g., ₹5Cr turnover), and mandatory certifications (e.g., ISO 9001).
Multimodal Bidder Synthesis Bidders submit everything from clean digital PDFs to blurry photos of physical certificates. Our pipeline handles the "dirty data":Advanced OCR & Image Restoration: Pre-processes scans and photos to sharpen text and remove noise.Contextual Extraction: Navigates complex tables in financial statements and extracts specific values even when the format varies wildly between bidders.Semantic Matching: Understands that a "Completion Certificate" and a "Work Experience Letter" often serve the same purpose, reducing false negatives.
The "Explainable AI" (XAI) Decision Engine The system acts as a Digital Co-Pilot, not a black box. For every bidder, it provides:Evidence-Backed Verdicts: Each "Eligible" or "Ineligible" status is hyperlinked to the specific page and paragraph in the bidder's submission.The "Human-in-the-Loop" Safety Net: If the AI is uncertain (e.g., a low-quality scan or ambiguous legal phrasing), it refuses to disqualify. Instead, it flags the item for manual review, highlighting the specific area of concern for the officer.
Key Technical Innovations
Multimodal Reasoning: Leveraging Gemini 1.5 Pro’s 1M+ context window to analyze the entire tender and all bidder documents simultaneously, ensuring no context is lost.
Audit-Ready Architecture: Every decision is logged with a cryptographic timestamp and a "Reasoning Path," creating an immutable audit trail suitable for formal government scrutiny.
Semantic Cross-Referencing: Moving beyond simple string matching to Vector Embeddings, allowing the system to understand synonymous technical requirements.
Impact and Success Metrics
Efficiency: Reduces the evaluation cycle from days to minutes.
Integrity: Eliminates "silent disqualifications" and ensures every bidder is judged against the exact same logic.
Trust: Provides a consolidated, sign-off-ready report that clearly justifies every decision with direct evidence.
Sentinel-Procure doesn't replace the procurement officer; it empowers them with the tools to make faster, fairer, and more defensible decisions in the service of national security.

SetuSync AI is a middleware-based interoperability platform that keeps Karnataka’s Single Window System (SWS) and legacy department systems synchronized using UBID as the shared business identity. It is designed for a real government environment where systems cannot be modified, schemas differ across departments, and service requests may originate from either side.The solution supports both directions of propagation. If a request is raised in SWS, it is translated and pushed to all relevant department systems linked to the same UBID. If a request is raised in a department system, the change is captured through available mechanisms such as APIs, polling, webhooks, or snapshot comparison, converted into SWS format, and written back to SWS.A key part of the platform is its ability to handle retries, duplicates, conflicts, and failures in a controlled and auditable way. Every propagation is idempotent, every action is logged, and conflicting updates are resolved through clear, configurable policies instead of silent overwrites. This ensures traceability, reliability, and reversibility.SetuSync AI is built as a scalable synchronization layer with configurable schema mappings, complete audit trails, and deployment readiness for sandbox-based synthetic data environments. It enables consistent service state across systems without requiring any big-bang migration or source-system changes.

solutions.team_name: ProcureSense: Explainable AI for Auditable Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S shivanshgautam135_59b4
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
ProcureSense: Explainable AI for Auditable Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 16, 2026 08:09 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
[1. Problem Understanding & Constraints] Government procurement is a high-stakes, low-trust environment. The core challenge is not simply extracting text, but standardizing highly heterogeneous, unstructured data (scans, varied formats, low-quality photos) and mapping it against rigid legal criteria. The system cannot act as a black box. Absolute explainability, comprehensive audit trails, and the strict prevention of silent disqualifications are non-negotiable for administrative and legal compliance. | [2. Tender Extraction Approach (The Ground Truth)] The system will first process the authoritative tender document to establish a deterministic ruleset: - Parsing: Utilize layout-aware NLP to segment the tender document. - Classification: Deploy a Large Language Model (LLM) to extract and categorize criteria into a strict JSON schema: Technical, Financial, and Compliance. - Tagging: Each extracted criterion is explicitly flagged as Mandatory or Optional. Target thresholds (e.g., "Turnover > 5 Crore") are converted into programmatic operators. | [3. Bidder Submission Parsing (The Intake Pipeline)] Bidder documents are highly variable. We will implement a multi-modal intake pipeline: - Routing: Documents are classified by type (Structured PDF, Scanned PDF, Image/Photo). - Extraction: Structured PDFs: PyMuPDF for direct text extraction. Scans/Photos: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) combined with Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to handle tabular data and read physical certificates. - Standardization: Extracted text is normalized into standard Key-Value pairs mapped to the tender's JSON schema. | [4. Matching & Evaluation Logic] - Quantitative Matching: Deterministic boolean logic is applied to numerical thresholds (e.g., verifying if the extracted financial figure is >= 50,000,000). - Qualitative Matching: Semantic search and LLM reasoning verify document intent (e.g., checking if an uploaded certificate fulfills the "ISO 9001" requirement). - Handling Ambiguity: If the OCR confidence score falls below a set threshold (e.g., 0.85), or if a required field is null, the system defaults the status to Manual Review Required. The system is hardcoded to never output a definitive "Fail" on low-confidence data. | [5. Explainability & Auditability] Every automated decision operates on a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) style citation principle: - The final report outputs an array for each bidder containing: [Criterion ID, Extracted Value, Verdict, Source Document Name, Page Number]. - The UI provides a side-by-side view: when an officer clicks a verdict, the system highlights the exact bounding box on the original document used to make that decision. | [6. Architecture Overview] - Frontend UI: Streamlit (allows for rapid iteration of data-heavy dashboards and side-by-side PDF rendering). - Backend: Python application layer orchestrating data flow. - OCR/Vision: Tesseract / EasyOCR for text, paired with layout parsers for tables. - Inference: Open-weights LLMs (e.g., Llama-3) hosted locally/in-sandbox to process raw sensitive data without violating the non-negotiable PII/sandbox constraints. | [7. Risks & Trade-Offs] - Risk: Poor quality scans leading to OCR hallucinations. Mitigation: Strict thresholding on OCR confidence. We trade automated completion rates for accuracy by aggressively routing blurry documents to human review. - Risk: Variations in financial terminology (e.g., "Gross Revenue" vs. "Turnover"). Mitigation: Utilizing semantic embeddings to map synonymous terms before applying deterministic value checks. | [8. Implementation Plan (Round 2)] - Phase 1: Pipeline Foundation (Days 1-5): Setup local Streamlit environment. Build the PDF/Image ingestion router and establish the baseline OCR extraction pipeline using synthetic bidder documents. - Phase 2: Extraction & Schema (Days 6-10): Develop the LLM prompt chains to accurately generate the JSON criteria schema from sample tender documents. Implement deterministic matching logic. - Phase 3: Integration & Explainability (Days 11-15): Connect extraction to the matching logic. Build the side-by-side evidence UI. Implement the confidence scoring system to flag Manual Review. - Phase 4: Testing & Demo Prep (Days 16-21): Stress-test the pipeline with edge-case documents (blurry photos, rotated scans). Record the 5-minute walkthrough video and finalize the GitHub repository.

solutions.team_name: OmniLink: Bi-Directional Legacy Sync Layer
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S shivanshgautam135_59b4
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
OmniLink: Bi-Directional Legacy Sync Layer
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 16, 2026 08:40 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
[1. Problem Understanding & Constraints] This is fundamentally a distributed systems data-sync problem, not a pure ML challenge. We must solve the split-brain state between the modern Single Window System (SWS) and 40+ legacy systems. The non-negotiables are strict idempotency, flawless auditability, and zero source-system modification, relying entirely on the UBID as the foundational join key. | [2. Two-Way Propagation Architecture] From SWS to Departments: An event-driven architecture using an event broker. SWS events are published, consumed by middleware, translated via department-specific schema maps, and pushed via API. Retries use exponential backoff. From Departments to SWS: Since legacy systems lack webhooks, we deploy a Snapshot Diffing worker. It periodically ingests data dumps, compares hashes of UBID records against the last known state, and generates synthetic events for any detected changes. | [3. Schema Translation & Management] We utilize an LLM-assisted schema mapping tool during setup, but execution relies on hardcoded, deterministic JSON-to-JSON transformers to ensure predictable, zero-hallucination runtime translations. As new departments are added, the translation layer acts as an adapter pattern, isolating legacy logic. | [4. Conflict Resolution & Idempotency] Conflicts occur when simultaneous updates hit the same UBID. Resolution policy: Timestamp-based Last Write Wins (LWW) with a human-in-the-loop override for critical fields. Idempotency is guaranteed by assigning a unique, deterministic hash to every distinct transaction payload; the receiving layer drops duplicate hashes. | [5. Audit Trail Design] Every data mutation generates an immutable audit log entry containing: [Timestamp, UBID, Source System, Original Payload, Translated Payload, Target System, Final Status]. This is stored in an append-only datastore, providing cryptographic proof of the system's state. | [6. Architecture Overview] Middleware: Python (FastAPI) for high-throughput API translation. Message Broker: Kafka or RabbitMQ for decoupled, durable event queuing. Storage: PostgreSQL for the append-only audit log and state tracking. | [7. Risks & Trade-Offs] Risk: Polling/Snapshotting legacy databases causes severe performance degradation on government infrastructure. Mitigation: Batching snapshot queries during off-peak hours and strictly limiting the query scope to high-velocity tables. | [8. Implementation Plan (Round 2)] Phase 1 (Days 1-5): Deploy the event broker and build the SWS-to-Department API publisher. Phase 2 (Days 6-10): Implement the Snapshot Diffing worker for legacy data ingestion. Phase 3 (Days 11-15): Build the translation adapters, idempotency locks, and conflict resolution engine. Phase 4 (Days 16-21): Stress-test conflict scenarios, finalize the audit log UI, and record the demo.

solutions.solution_text:

COMPETE
