S sivakukkuluri82_6cc5
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderIQ — Explainable AI & Multimodal Evaluation Engine for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 16, 2026 09:59 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderIQ is a highly secure, Human-in-the-Loop AI platform designed to evaluate complex government procurement pipelines without the risk of silent vendor disqualification.While our comprehensively researched architecture handles the theoretical integration of Air-Gapped NER and Multimodal LLMs (like Gemini 1.5 Pro) to bypass fragile OCR limitations, our submitted working prototype brings the most critical aspect of this system to life: The Human-AI Evaluation Workbench.What We Built (The Prototype)To prove the real-world deployability and ergonomic feasibility of this system for government officers, we engineered a high-performance, full-stack application from scratch during this hackathon.1. The "God-Tier" Frontend Interface We built a responsive, visually stunning web portal tailored for procurement officers. It abandons clunky legacy interfaces for a highly optimized, modern UX.Tech Stack: React (TypeScript), Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion, Lucide-React.Design System: Built using advanced CSS glassmorphism, glowing neon status indicators, and micro-animations to instantly draw the user's eye to systemic priorities without visual clutter.The Command Dashboard: A live overview tracking active tenders, daily processed bids, and a real-time counter immediately escalating cases that require manual review.The Evaluation Workbench (Core Feature): A highly engineered split-pane interface. On the left, it breaks down the extracted tender requirements (Financial, Technical, Compliance). On the right, it renders an Evidence Viewer that ties the AI’s decision directly to a quoted contextual snippet and specific page number from the bidder's document.2. The Fallback Mechanism ("Needs Review") The absolute non-negotiable of this hackathon was preventing silent algorithmic disqualification. We coded the prototype to demonstrate our "Manufacturing Inspection Line" analogy.If the backend recognizes a scanned document as blurry or the AI's confidence score drops below 90%, it intentionally halts automation.The UI aggressively flags the criteria with a yellow [NEEDS REVIEW]warning alert.The officer is presented with the illegible snippet and given Manual Override buttons to either force the verdict to Eligible or Not Eligible, ensuring a human mathematically owns the final decision.3. The API Backend Architecture To ensure a completely flawless, deterministic demo free from third-party API timeout risks during the hackathon, we built a fully-typed backend simulation layer.Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, Pydantic, Uvicorn.Data Integrity: All AI extraction constraints, criteria, and bidder evidence are strictly enforced through declarative Pydantic schemas (e.g., CriterionType, VerdictStatus).Live Overrides: The backend exposes RESTful endpoints (/override) that dynamically recalculate the overall status of a bidder the exact millisecond a procurement officer clicks an override command in the UI.Our prototype successfully proves that advanced Multimodal AI extraction can be securely and effectively merged with a fail-safe, human-audited web environment.

solutions.team_name: BidShield: Graph-Based Bid Ring Detection and Fraud Prevention in Government Procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Anshu Aditya
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
BidShield: Graph-Based Bid Ring Detection and Fraud Prevention in Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 04:52 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem====== Government procurement fraud costs India an estimated ₹1.5–3 lakh crore annually. The most damaging form is bid rigging — where coordinated groups of vendors submit seemingly competing bids that are actually controlled by the same entity. Current systems only detect fraud after audits, often years later. No tool exists that flags collusion before contract award.
The Gap No One Is Solving========Eligibility checkers verify documents. What they miss: a coordinated vendor group can have 5 fully eligible entities submitting "competing" bids — all controlled by the same party. This passes every rule-based check. It requires relational analysis, not document analysis.
Our Solution========= BidShield A Graph Neural Network-based procurement intelligence system that constructs a vendor relationship graph from tender submission metadata and detects coordinated bidding rings in real time — before contract award.
How It Works======= Phase 1 — Graph Construction Each vendor submission creates nodes (vendors, directors, addresses, bank accounts, registration numbers). Edges are drawn when vendors share directors, registered addresses, phone numbers, or exhibit suspiciously similar document formatting. This builds a Vendor Relationship Graph across all submissions for a given tender.
Phase 2 — Anomaly Detection Two parallel detection layers:GNN (Graph Attention Network): Learns structural patterns of known collusive network topologies — star graphs (one controller, many shell entities), clique patterns (mutual cross-ownership), and bridge nodes (intermediary companies). Flags vendor clusters with high coordination probability.Statistical Bid Analysis: Detects Benford's Law violations in submitted pricing, abnormal bid clustering (competing vendors quoting within 0.5% of each other), and cover bidding patterns (one low bid, rest artificially high).
Phase 3 — Risk Scoring & Audit Report Every tender gets a Collusion Risk Score (CRS) per vendor cluster. The system outputs an explainable audit report — which vendors are flagged, which shared attributes triggered the flag, and what the statistical anomaly was. Fully human-reviewable before any contract decision.
What Makes This Different========= Every other team will build a document eligibility checker. BidShield doesn't check documents — it checks relationships. It's the difference between verifying a passport and detecting a coordinated forgery operation.
Impact========= BidShield gives procurement officers a pre-award red flag system — not a post-audit one. Even flagging 10% of rigged contracts before award would save thousands of crores annually. The explainability layer ensures no vendor is disqualified by a black box — every flag is human-reviewable and legally defensible.

solutions.team_name: Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Blue_Phoenix
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 10:12 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement processes today rely heavily on manual evaluation of tender documents and bidder submissions. These documents are often lengthy, unstructured, and heterogeneous — including typed PDFs, scanned files, tables, and images. As a result, evaluation is time-consuming, inconsistent across officers, and difficult to audit.We propose an Explainable AI Procurement Assistant "Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP)" — a modular, end-to-end platform that automates tender evaluation , ranks bidders intelligently while keeping humans in the loop to ensure full transparency.Core Idea: The system ingests,A tender document that contains eligibility criteria.Multiple bidder submissions with documents in various formats.Work of the AI assistant,Read Tender document and extract key eligibility criterions.Read Bidder Documents, the bidder's documents can be in multiple formats (like pdf's/photos/scaned images/word files, etc...). The assistant should be able to identify the file type and parse the necessary information correctly.Assistant should review and evaluate each bidder against extracted criteria of tender document.After evaluation the assistant should give output as "Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Manual Review", along with the explanation for that decision.As an extra we want to develop an ranking system that assigns bidders a composite score based on multiple evaluation criteria such as financial strength, past experience, compliance, and certifications. Then the system generates a ranked list of eligible bidders, enabling officers to focus on top candidates first.Finally, all these processes are to be done and displayed visually on a clean web interface.System Architecture Overview:Document Ingestion Layer (PDF, image, DOC support) OCR + Parsing Engine NLP/LLM-based Criteria Extractor Structured Data Store (criteria + bidder data) Rule Engine + Evaluation Module Scoring & Ranking EngineExplainability Layer (evidence mapping + audit logs + score justification) Reviewer Dashboard (human-in-the-loop interface) System Architecture:1. Tender Understanding (Criteria Extraction):Using NLP and LLM-based parsing, the system extracts:Technical requirements Financial thresholds (e.g., turnover ≥ ₹5 Cr) Compliance conditions (GST, ISO, certifications) Each criterion is structured into machine-readable rules and classified as mandatory or optional.﻿2. Bidder Document Parsing:The system processes bidder submissions using:OCR pipelines for scanned and image-based documents Document parsing for PDFs and structured files Information extraction models to identify relevant values It normalizes diverse formats into a unified data structure, handling inconsistencies in how bidders present information.﻿3.Bidders Evaluation Engine:A hybrid rule-based + AI engine evaluates each bidder against extracted criteria:Outputs: Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Manual Review Applies configurable thresholds and logical rules Handles ambiguity and incomplete data by routing to human review﻿4. Intelligent ranking and prioritization layer Beyond eligibility evaluation, the system introduces a scoring and ranking mechanism to assist procurement officers in prioritizing bidders efficiently.Each bidder is assigned a composite score based on weighted evaluation criteria such as financial strength, experience, and compliance. Mandatory criteria act as hard filters (pass/fail), while performance-based criteria contribute to ranking. The system generates a ranked list of eligible bidders, enabling officers to focus on top candidates first.﻿5. Explainability & Audit Layer (Key Differentiator)Every decision is fully transparent and traceable:Links each verdict to specific criteria, document, and extracted value Highlights evidence (e.g., “₹6.2 Cr found in balance sheet, page 3”) Maintains a complete audit trail for compliance and review No bidder is silently rejected — uncertain cases are explicitly flagged.﻿6. Reviewer Dashboard (Human-in-the-Loop Interface)The platform includes an intuitive Reviewer Dashboard that enables procurement officers to efficiently review and validate AI-generated decisions. The dashboard presents:Eligibility status, scores, and rankings for all bidders Criterion-wise evaluation with highlighted evidence from source documents Flagged cases (“Needs Manual Review”) with reasons for ambiguity Reviewers can:Approve, override, or request clarification on decisions View complete audit trails of how each decision was made Provide feedback, which can be used to continuously improve system accuracy This ensures that the system remains transparent, controllable, and aligned with real-world procurement workflows, while significantly reducing manual effort. In conclusion the proposed Procurement Decision Intelligence Platform (PDIP) aims to transform government tender evaluation by combining AI-driven automation with human oversight. By extracting, evaluating, and explaining decisions from complex documents, and further enhancing them with intelligent ranking, the system reduces manual effort, improves consistency, and ensures transparency. Rather than replacing human judgment, PDIP acts as a decision-support system, enabling CPRF officers to make faster, more informed, and auditable decisions — ultimately leading to a more efficient, fair, and scalable procurement process.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Praman — Evidence-Grounded AI for CRPF Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 10:18 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Praman is an air-gapped, decision-support system for CRPF tender evaluation. It reads the RFP and every bidder submission — typed PDFs, scanned certificates, phone photos of stamps — and returns a PASS / FAIL / NEEDS REVIEW verdict per criterion, with a clickable evidence trail (page + bounding box) that survives CVC, CAG and RTI review.How it worksTender understanding — Docling + PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 parse the RFP; Qwen2.5-VL with Outlines constrained decoding extracts eligibility criteria (technical / financial / compliance, mandatory / optional), each anchored to its exact clause.Bidder understanding — multi-engine OCR (PaddleOCR-VL → olmOCR 2 fallback), YOLOv9s stamp/signature detection with LaMa inpaint + re-OCR, TrOCR-Indic for handwriting, TableFormer for tables, Bhashini NMT for Indic text.Evaluation — the LLM decides nothing. Extracted facts are evaluated by an Open Policy Agent (Rego) rule engine; one Rego rule per criterion, every rule naming its RFP clause. MAPIE conformal prediction + ensemble disagreement drive abstention.Audit — Merkle-hash-chained append-only log (RFC 9162 pattern) on immudb + WORM MinIO; every FAIL requires officer DSC sign-off against CCA India RCAI.Non-negotiables, mechanically enforcedCriterion-level explainability: every verdict = rule ID + bindings + cited spans.Never silently disqualify: missing data fails closed to ABSTAIN → human review, never FAIL.Scanned + photograph support: multi-engine OCR, super-resolution, char-conf<0.85 routes to human. End-to-end auditable: signed immutable log exportable as CVC / CAG / RTI evidence PDF.

COMPETE
JOBS

P patelsanskrati05_6347
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Sutradhara: AI-Powered Two-Way Interoperability Layer Between Karnataka Single Window System and Department Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 17, 2026 10:50 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Sutradhara is an AI-powered middleware interoperability platform designed to solve one of Karnataka’s most important digital governance challenges: enabling seamless two-way synchronization between the Single Window System (SWS) and 40+ independent department systems such as Factories, Labour, Pollution Control Board, BESCOM, BWSSB, Fire, Food Safety, Urban Local Bodies, and sector regulators. Today, businesses interact with multiple disconnected government systems, where updates made in one portal often remain outdated in others. This creates fragmented records, operational delays, repeated document verification, inconsistent decisions, and poor citizen experience. Sutradhara solves this problem by acting as a secure orchestration layer between systems without requiring any migration, replacement, or modification of existing departmental software. Using the Unique Business Identifier (UBID) as the common identity key, the platform intelligently links records across systems and propagates verified updates in both directions. Changes originating in SWS automatically flow to relevant departments, while updates initiated in department systems are reflected back into SWS. The platform is designed for reliability with retry-safe delivery, conflict detection, explainable resolution policies, and complete auditability of every action. If two systems update the same business field differently, Sutradhara detects the conflict and applies transparent rules such as trusted-source priority, timestamp precedence, or human review. Every propagation is traceable end-to-end through an evidence graph, and historical events can be replayed safely to correct issues or validate policy changes. Sutradhara uses AI responsibly only as an assistance layer for onboarding new departments, suggesting schema mappings, recommending conflict rules, and explaining anomalies. No raw sensitive data is exposed, as all records pass through a privacy-preserving tokenizer before any AI processing. This solution enables faster approvals, consistent business records across departments, lower reconciliation workload, stronger compliance monitoring, better investor confidence, and future-ready interoperable governance infrastructure for Karnataka. Unlike theoretical concepts, Sutradhara is based on production-tested integration patterns already implemented by the team, making it practical, scalable, and deployment-ready for government systems. For complete architecture, technical design, and supporting documents, please refer to:   https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iZdwiRqO7MpV6XeUpVV6kwxSAhXqqIfB/view?usp=sharing

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


K kvsabhiram
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NIRNAYAK — AI-Powered Tender Bid Evaluation Agent for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 10:38 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
What is NIRNAYAK?NIRNAYAK (Neural Intelligence for Review, Normalization & Assessment of Yields in Automated Knowledge-driven procurement) is an AI-powered bid evaluation agent that automates the eligibility screening of government tender submissions — making the process faster, consistent, explainable, and legally defensible."Har Bid. Har Shart. Poora Sachcha Nirnay."﻿The Problem We Are SolvingManual tender evaluation at organisations like CRPF is broken in four ways:Slow — committees spend days reading hundreds of pages across 10+ biddersInconsistent — two evaluators on the same documents may reach different verdictsError-prone — a scanned certificate buried on page 47 of a 200-page bid gets missedHard to audit — disqualified vendors can challenge decisions; post-hoc proof is nearly impossible﻿How NIRNAYAK Works — 5-Layer PipelineLayer 1 — Document Ingestion Every document format is handled: typed PDFs (PyMuPDF), scanned PDFs and photographs (AWS Textract with word-level confidence scores), Word files (python-docx), and financial tables (Camelot/pdfplumber). All output is normalised to clean text with page references and confidence scores attached.Layer 2 — AI Extraction (LLM Core) Two separate pipelines powered by Claude claude-sonnet-4-20250514:Tender Criteria Extractor — two-pass pipeline: first segments eligibility clauses, then uses chain-of-thought reasoning to extract structured criteria (type, threshold, time window, mandatory/optional flag) as JSONBidder Value Extractor — criterion-targeted prompts search each bidder's documents using a curated evidence pattern library (12+ known patterns per criterion type), returning extracted value + confidence scoreLayer 3 — Matching & Verdict Engine A fully deterministic rules engine (separate from the AI) applies the extracted rules:Financial criteria → numeric comparisonTechnical criteria → count + category + date window checkCompliance/certification → document presence + expiry validationThree and only three possible verdicts per criterion:✅ ELIGIBLE — confidence ≥ 0.85 and rule passes❌ NOT ELIGIBLE — confidence ≥ 0.85 and rule fails⚠️ MANUAL REVIEW — confidence < 0.85 or document not foundLayer 4 — Human-in-the-Loop Every MANUAL REVIEW case is surfaced to the procurement officer with full context: the criterion being checked, the document searched, the value found (if any), the confidence score, and the plain-English reason for uncertainty. The system never silently disqualifies a bidder — ambiguity is always a handoff to a human.Layer 5 — Tamper-Evident Audit Trail Every extraction, match, verdict, and human decision is recorded as an immutable append-only event with SHA-256 hash chaining. Every automated decision traces back to a specific document page, a specific extracted value, and a specific rule — suitable for CVC scrutiny and formal procurement hearings.﻿Sample OutputFor a construction services tender (turnover ≥ ₹5 Cr, GST, ISO 9001, 3 similar projects):CriterionRequiredFoundVerdictAnnual Turnover₹5 Cr₹6.2 Cr✅ ELIGIBLEGST RegistrationValid29ABCDE1234F1Z5✅ ELIGIBLEISO 9001Valid certNot found❌ NOT ELIGIBLESimilar Projects x33 / 5 yrs2 confirmed, 1 unreadable⚠️ MANUAL REVIEW﻿What Makes NIRNAYAK DifferentZero silent disqualifications — architecturally impossible; every uncertain case goes to officer reviewDeterministic rules engine separate from AI — the matching logic is fully readable and auditable without AI expertiseEvidence pattern library — handles the 12+ ways a bidder might prove annual turnover across different document typesChain-of-thought audit record — the LLM's reasoning scratchpad is preserved alongside every extractionOn-premise deployment option — classified tenders never leave the network (Tesseract OCR fallback)Indian procurement context built-in — GFR 2017 aligned, handles GSTIN formats, Form 3CB/3CD, CA certificates, and Indian government document conventions﻿Technology StackComponentTechnologyLLMClaude claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Anthropic)OCRAWS Textract (primary) / Tesseract (on-premise fallback)Document parsingPyMuPDF, python-docx, CamelotRules enginePython + Pydantic (fully deterministic)Audit storePostgreSQL append-only with SHA-256 hash chainAPI / UIFastAPI + React﻿Round 2 Plan5 sprints across 14 days: document ingestion pipeline → tender criteria extractor → bidder value extractor + rules engine → report generation + audit trail → end-to-end testing on sandbox tenders.Success metrics: ≥95% criterion extraction accuracy, ≥90% verdict accuracy on clear cases, 100% MANUAL REVIEW routing on uncertain cases, <3 minutes processing time per bidder.﻿NIRNAYAK is not designed to replace procurement officers. It is designed to make every decision rigorous, consistent, and legally defensible — so officers can focus their expertise where it matters most.

solutions.team_name: Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Binary Brains ITM
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 10:10 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender EvaluationA CRPF procurement committee evaluating 20 bidders manually spends 4 to 6 days cross-checking hundreds of pages of documents. Two evaluators reviewing the same submission often reach different conclusions. A single missed criterion can result in an incorrect award, and an untraceable decision can lead to audit failure or legal disputes.Veridict is designed to eliminate both inconsistency and untraceability.Veridict is not just an AI extraction system. It is a decision intelligence engine that reads tender documents, understands eligibility criteria, evaluates bidder submissions across multiple formats, and produces fully explainable, evidence-backed decisions with built-in consistency validation across all bidders.The system processes typed PDFs, scanned documents, images, and Word files. Using OCR and layout-aware models such as LayoutLMv3, it converts unstructured inputs into structured representations including tables, financial values, and key entities. Image preprocessing ensures robustness against low-quality scans.The evaluation pipeline is built on three core engines.CriteriaLens converts the tender document into a structured rule schema. Each criterion is extracted with its type, threshold, time constraints, mandatory status, and acceptable evidence sources. Legal indicators such as “shall”, “must”, and “essential” are used to detect mandatory conditions. Before evaluation begins, Veridict introduces a validation step where unclear or incomplete criteria (for example, vague terms like “adequate experience” or missing thresholds) are flagged for officer confirmation. This ensures that the system never evaluates against ambiguous rules.DocProbe processes bidder submissions using a hybrid extraction pipeline. For digital documents, it performs direct parsing. For scanned inputs, it applies preprocessing followed by OCR. Instead of extracting text blindly, the system uses retrieval-based grounding to locate only the most relevant sections for each criterion. Semantic normalization using models such as Sentence-BERT ensures that variations like “turnover”, “revenue”, and “gross receipts” are interpreted consistently. Every extracted value is tied to a document, page number, and source snippet. If evidence is not found, the system explicitly reports it rather than inferring.VerdictCore performs structured evaluation using a deterministic reasoning engine. Unlike systems that rely purely on AI matching, Veridict applies explicit reasoning logic for different types of criteria. Numeric conditions are evaluated using rule-based comparisons, temporal conditions such as “last 5 years” are validated using time-aware logic, and count-based requirements such as “at least 3 projects” are computed through aggregation. Logical conditions (AND/OR) are resolved systematically. AI is used only for interpretation, while final decisions are deterministic, ensuring consistent results for the same input every time.Each decision is assigned a multi-factor confidence score based on OCR quality, extraction reliability, semantic alignment, cross-document consistency, and anomaly detection signals. Veridict does not hide uncertainty. If confidence is low, the system clearly explains why — whether due to poor scan quality, missing data, conflicting values, or unclear evidence — and routes the case for manual review.Veridict introduces advanced intelligence layers that improve reliability beyond standard approaches.The Evidence Graph links every decision to its source: criterion → extracted value → document → page reference. This ensures that every output is fully traceable and auditable.The Consistency Engine evaluates all bidders collectively, not in isolation. It detects anomalies such as outlier values, inconsistent document formats, or unusual patterns across submissions. For example, if most bidders provide audited financial statements while one bidder provides unsupported values, the system flags this discrepancy automatically.The Cross-Validation Layer verifies information across multiple documents within the same submission. If a financial value appears in both a balance sheet and a tax document, the system checks consistency. Conflicting evidence reduces confidence and triggers a review.The Fraud Signal Layer identifies suspicious patterns such as repeated document reuse, abnormal value distributions, or inconsistencies across submissions. These signals are surfaced to officers as indicators, not final judgments.Each criterion is classified into one of three outcomes: Eligible when requirements are clearly satisfied, Not Eligible when requirements are clearly violated, Needs Manual Review when confidence is low, evidence is missing, or inconsistencies are detected.No bidder is ever silently disqualified. Every uncertain case is surfaced with explicit reasoning.Veridict also provides comparative intelligence across bidders. Instead of evaluating submissions in isolation, the system highlights top-performing candidates, detects outliers, and enables ranking based on criteria satisfaction. This gives decision-makers a clearer view of the overall competition.Every decision is accompanied by a complete explanation including the criterion evaluated, the value extracted, the document source, and the reasoning applied. A tamper-evident audit trail records every step of the process, making the system suitable for formal government use.System performance is measured using clear metrics such as extraction accuracy, decision precision, false rejection rate (targeting zero incorrect disqualifications), and percentage of cases requiring manual review. Reviewer corrections are captured as labeled data and used to continuously improve extraction accuracy, confidence calibration, and decision quality over time.In a real-world scenario, a procurement officer uploads a tender and multiple bidder submissions. Veridict extracts criteria, validates them, evaluates all bidders using deterministic reasoning, highlights anomalies and inconsistencies, and generates a consolidated report within minutes. Clearly eligible and ineligible bidders are identified, while ambiguous cases are flagged with full context for review.Veridict directly addresses all key evaluation requirements. It demonstrates a deep understanding of the problem by targeting inconsistency, ambiguity in criteria, auditability, and real-world document complexity. Its hybrid architecture combines AI-based understanding with deterministic reasoning, ensuring both flexibility and reliability. The system is deployable as a modular layer without requiring changes to existing infrastructure and can operate in secure environments with controlled data handling.Beyond procurement, the architecture is scalable to any domain involving document-heavy eligibility decisions, including compliance verification, grant evaluation, and regulatory audits. As more data and reviewer feedback are incorporated, the system continuously improves while maintaining decision stability.Veridict transforms tender evaluation from a manual, inconsistent process into a structured, transparent, and defensible decision system where every outcome is backed by verifiable evidence, consistent reasoning, and measurable confidence.

ParakhOps – AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis SystemParakhOps is designed to address inefficiencies in government tender evaluation processes, such as those followed by the CRPF. Traditional evaluation involves manual verification of complex eligibility criteria across multiple bidder documents, leading to delays, inconsistencies, and limited auditability.The system transforms this workflow into a structured, explainable, and auditable decision pipeline. These criteria are then used to evaluate bidder submissions, which may include PDFs, scanned documents, or images.How It WorksFor each bidder, ParakhOps performs criterion-level evaluation by extracting relevant values, comparing them against requirements, and assigning a status: Pass, Fail, or Needs Manual Review.Rather than producing "black-box" outputs, every decision is supported with:Clear reasoningExtracted valuesDirect document referencesAmbiguous or low-confidence cases are never auto-rejected; they are explicitly flagged for human review to ensure fairness and accuracy.Key FeaturesGranular Intelligence: Criterion-level analysis for precision.Evidence-Based Decisions: Every result is backed by specific document data.Conflict Detection: Identifies discrepancies across different bidder documents.Human-in-the-Loop: A robust review mechanism for high-stakes or ambiguous data.Audit Trail: Maintains a complete log of inputs, extracted data, results, and timestamps for total traceability.Technical ArchitectureThe architecture is built for speed and reliability:Interface: Streamlit for seamless control flow and UI.Reasoning Engine: Google Gemini 1.5 Pro for multimodal document understanding.Data Structure: JSON-based outputs to ensure consistency across the pipeline.Decision Layer: A lightweight rules engine that enforces mandatory criteria and handles ambiguity.ParakhOps significantly reduces evaluation time, improves consistency across decisions, and provides transparent, audit-ready outputs. CocoMind
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 11:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemGovernment procurement (like CRPF tenders) is slowed by manual document verification, prone to human error, and vulnerable to legal challenges (RTIs/CAG audits). Most AI tools are "black boxes" that lack the transparency and data security required for high-stakes government contracts.The Solution CocoMind automates the transition from messy PDF bids to a Verdict Traceability Matrix (VTM). It extracts data using LLMs but verifies it using a deterministic Python Rule Engine, ensuring every Pass/Fail decision is mathematically provable and linked to source evidence. Key Technical Features Traceable Logic: Every verdict includes the raw text, page number, bounding box, and the specific rule expression used for evaluation.Indian-Specific PII Redaction: Mandatory masking of Aadhaar, PAN, and GSTIN using Microsoft Presidio before any data hits the LLM.Hybrid Multilingual RAG: Combines BGE-M3 semantic search with BM25 lexical search to handle English, Hindi, and Marathi technical specifications.Integrity-First Architecture: Uses a SHA-256 hash-chained audit log and DSC (Digital Signature) integration to ensure non-repudiation of officer decisions.CRPF-Specific Compliance: Automated checkers for CVC debarment lists, EMD bank guarantee validity, and near-relation affidavits.The Impact:CocoMind slashes evaluation time by 90% while providing a "bulletproof" audit trail. It moves AI from a "creative assistant" to a formal verification tool that can survive a CAG audit and simplify RTI compliance.

solutions.team_name: ProcureCheck — AI-Assisted Tender Eligibility Evaluation for Government Procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S siddharthreddygogula123_8143
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ProcureCheck — AI-Assisted Tender Eligibility Evaluation for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 10:46 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemTender evaluation committees in government bodies like CRPF spend 7–15 working days manually cross-checking 30–50 documents per bidder across 10–20 bidders. One officer misses what another catches. Two evaluators reach different conclusions from the same file. Bidders describing the same qualification in different words confuse tired readers. When rejected bidders file RTI queries, committees often cannot produce a clean answer — because the process never created one.ProcureCheck does not replace the officer. It does all the reading and checking so the officer can focus entirely on judgment. Every decision stays human.﻿System FlowTender Upload |Tender Understanding EngineRule-based pattern extraction + LLM structured extractionClassify criteria: Financial / Technical / ComplianceFlag each condition: Mandatory or Optional |Officer Criteria Review LayerOfficer edits and confirms the extracted criteria listNo evaluation starts before officer approval |Bidder Document IngestionTyped PDFs / Word files / Scanned PDFs / Photographs |Document Processing Pipelinepdfplumber for typed PDFspython-docx for Word filesOpenCV preprocessing for scans and photos (deskew, denoise, contrast enhancement)Google Vision OCR for scanned textSeparate table extraction for financial figures |Evidence Extraction LayerExtract: turnover, project counts, GST, ISO, EPF/ESI, dates, document IDsEach field linked to: document name, page number, source passage |Evidence Reliability Scoring EngineOCR confidence of source pageDocument type trust level (audited balance sheet vs self-declaration)Consistency of the same value across multiple documentsStructured field vs free text |Retrieval and Matching EngineBM25 for exact values (amounts, numbers, dates)Semantic search for phrasing variationMulti-document aggregation when evidence is splitContradiction detection when documents disagreeMissing and unclear evidence detection |Rule-Aware Evaluation EngineNumeric threshold checksCount checksBoolean checksDocument validity checks |Verdict EngineELIGIBLENOT ELIGIBLEOFFICER REVIEW REQUIRED |Officer Review WorkflowLow-confidence and ambiguous cases surfaced to officerConflicting evidence shown side by sideManual override with mandatory written justification |Audit and Compliance LayerImmutable PostgreSQL audit logStores: extracted criteria, evidence used, reliability scores, verdicts, officer overrides, timestamps, officer IDs |Dashboard and Reporting LayerBidder x Criteria evaluation grid, colour-coded by verdictDrill-down evidence view per cellOfficer review queueExport: Tender Committee Minutes format under GFR 2017RTI-ready final signed report﻿How Each Stage WorksStage 1-3 — Reading the TenderThe officer uploads the tender. The system uses rule-based pattern matching combined with LLM extraction to pull out every eligibility condition. Mandatory conditions are spotted by phrases like "the bidder must" or "shall be rejected if." Optional ones say "preference will be given to." Where the language is genuinely ambiguous, the condition is flagged for the officer to classify. The officer sees the full extracted list, edits freely, and confirms. Evaluation only runs against what the officer approves.Stage 4-5 — Processing Bidder DocumentsGoogle Vision is used over Tesseract because it handles degraded government scans — blurry stamps, faded ink, phone camera photos of certificates — far more reliably, and returns per-word confidence scores. Pages below the confidence threshold are flagged and never used to generate a verdict on their own. Financial tables are parsed separately so rupee figures stay linked to correct row labels.Stage 6-7 — Evidence Reliability ScoringBefore any matching begins, every extracted piece of evidence gets a reliability score. Low-reliability evidence never drives an automatic verdict — it goes straight to the officer review queue with the score and reason shown clearly. The system does not just extract. It knows how much to trust what it extracted, and is honest about it.Stage 8-10 — Matching and Verdict GenerationKeyword search (BM25) finds exact values — rupee amounts, registration numbers, project counts. Semantic search finds meaning regardless of wording — "three works of similar nature costing not less than forty percent of estimated cost" and "three comparable contracts above the minimum threshold" correctly match the same condition. The engine aggregates evidence across documents, detects missing or contradicting information, and routes partial matches to the officer rather than auto-rejecting.Every bidder gets exactly one verdict per condition:ELIGIBLE — clearly met. Shows document name, page, extracted value, exact passage, and reliability score.NOT ELIGIBLE — clearly not met. Shows what was found and why it fails in plain language.OFFICER REVIEW REQUIRED — triggered by low reliability, ambiguous language, missing documents, contradictions, or evidence needing human judgment. The officer sees the exact reason and all evidence gathered so far.The system never silently disqualifies a bidder. Any doubt goes to the officer — always.Stage 11-13 — Officer Review, Audit, and ReportA grid shows every bidder against every condition, colour-coded by verdict. Clicking any cell opens the full evidence with reliability scores. Flagged cases appear in a review queue — the officer reads the source document, makes the call, and writes a short reason. Every override in either direction is permanently logged with timestamp and officer ID. On sign-off, ProcureCheck generates an evaluation report in the Tender Committee Minutes format under GFR 2017 — RTI-ready by default.﻿Technology ChoicesGoogle Vision API over Tesseract -> Handles degraded government scans far better. Returns per-word confidence scores which directly power the reliability scoring system.BM25 + Semantic Search together, not either alone -> BM25 catches exact values like rupee thresholds and registration numbers. Semantic embeddings catch phrasing variation — the same condition written in different legal language by different bidders. Both are needed.Qdrant Vector Store -> Open source, self-hostable on NIC servers. Sensitive bid documents never leave the local environment.Structured LLM Output -> Forces the model to return machine-readable verdicts with evidence references. No free-form text to parse or interpret.PostgreSQL Immutable Audit Log -> Append-only event log. Nothing can be deleted or edited after the fact — a hard requirement for government procurement accountability and RTI compliance.﻿Risks and How We Handle ThemPoor scan quality -> OCR confidence gate flags the page before any verdict runs from it.Ambiguous mandatory/optional conditions in the tender -> Officer confirms and classifies the full criteria list before evaluation begins.Evidence split across multiple documents -> Multi-document aggregation runs before verdict generation.Missing documents entirely -> Treated as unverified, not automatically failed — goes to the officer review queue.Contradictions within a single bidder's submission -> Both conflicting passages are shown to the officer side by side.LLM or API failure mid-evaluation -> All processing results are cached. Officer can enter verdicts manually through the dashboard.﻿Why This Will Actually Be UsedEvery existing tool — Minaions, ContraVault, TenderIntelligence — is built for bidders checking their own eligibility. Not one is built for the officer who must evaluate all bidders fairly and sign a legally defensible record.ProcureCheck is built for that officer. Evaluation time drops from 15 days to 2. Every rejection becomes documentable and defensible. The platform scales from CRPF to all central and state procurement — bringing consistency and auditability to India's ₹50–70 lakh crore annual procurement market.

Project Description
SATYA (Systemic Automated Tender Yield Analyzer) is a multi-layer AI system that extracts tender criteria, evaluates bidder eligibility with explainable evidence, detects fraud signals, and ensures every procurement decision is audit-ready and RTI-compliant.The system operates as a 4-module pipeline:Criteria Intelligence Engine — Extracts and classifies all eligibility criteria from tender documents into a structured schema (financial, technical, compliance).Multi-Modal Document Parser — Processes all bidder submission formats including clean PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, and photographs using PaddleOCR and Vision AI.Explainable Evaluation Engine — Produces per-criterion verdicts (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review) with exact evidence citations — document name, page number, and extracted value.Cross-Bidder Risk & Fraud Signal Detector — Analyzes all bidders collectively to detect cartel patterns, duplicate GST numbers, and document similarity — invisible to manual review.Unlike traditional systems, SATYA never silently disqualifies a bidder. Every decision generates an RTI-ready audit report that officers can sign and defend.SATYA does not replace procurement officers — it makes every decision they take defensible.

solutions.team_name: TenderKit — AI-powered eligibility evaluation for government procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T tenderkit
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderKit — AI-powered eligibility evaluation for government procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 01:48 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A document-intelligence platform that extracts tender requirements, parses bidder submissions in any format, evaluates eligibility with explainable evidence, detects fraud signals across bidders, and produces a fully auditable, RTI-ready procurement report — without silently disqualifying anyone.THE PROBLEMWhen a government body like CRPF issues a tender, evaluating whether each bidder actually meets the eligibility criteria is entirely manual. A committee reads through hundreds of pages per bidder — financial statements, tax certificates, experience letters, scanned copies of ISO certificates — and cross-checks each one against the tender requirements. It takes days, it is inconsistent across evaluators, and there is no structured audit trail. Two officers reviewing the same submission can reach different conclusions. Ambiguous cases either get passed through without scrutiny or rejected without explanation. And beyond individual bidder evaluation, there is a deeper problem that manual review cannot catch at all: coordinated fraud, cartel behaviour, duplicate registrations, and suspiciously similar documents submitted by bidders who appear unrelated. Neither inconsistency nor invisible fraud is acceptable in a formal procurement context.THE IDEATenderKit is built as a four-stage pipeline: extract and structure the tender criteria, parse every bidder document regardless of format, evaluate each bidder criterion by criterion with full evidence, and then analyse all bidders collectively to surface fraud signals that only become visible when you look across the entire submission pool at once.The process starts when the procurement officer uploads the tender document. TenderKit parses it — handling PDFs, Word files, and scanned copies through OCR — and passes the full text through an LLM to extract every eligibility criterion. Each criterion is tagged as mandatory or optional and classified by type: financial threshold, technical qualification, compliance document, or certification requirement. These structured criteria are stored in ChromaDB as vector embeddings, keyed to a unique tender ID, and serve as the ground truth for every evaluation that follows.When bidder submissions arrive, TenderKit handles every file format without treating any of them as a special case. Typed PDFs go through pdfplumber or pymupdf4llm, Word files through python-docx, and scanned images or photographs of physical certificates through PaddleOCR or AWS Textract. The output in every case is clean, readable text that the rest of the pipeline can work with. We then query ChromaDB using each bidder's extracted content to retrieve the most relevant criteria. Because this is vector-based semantic search rather than keyword matching, it handles natural variation in phrasing — a bidder writing "yearly revenue above five crore" and the tender saying "minimum annual turnover of ₹5 crore" correctly map to the same requirement.EVALUATION AND VERDICTSFor each criterion, the LLM receives the requirement from the tender alongside the relevant section from the bidder's documents. It returns one of three verdicts — eligible, not eligible, or needs human review — along with a confidence score and a short explanation citing the specific document, page number, and extracted value that drove the decision.When a bidder clearly meets a criterion — the turnover figure is present, readable, and above the threshold — the verdict is eligible with a confidence close to 1. When a required document is missing or a value clearly falls short, the verdict is not eligible, again with high confidence. The third case is the most important one: when the LLM cannot be sure — because a scanned certificate is too blurry to read reliably, because a figure is present but the context is ambiguous, or because a document only partially addresses the requirement — it returns a needs human review verdict with a low confidence score and an explicit explanation of what is uncertain. That case is never silently dropped. It is flagged, reasoned, and handed to the procurement officer with full context.This is the core design principle of TenderKit: the system is allowed to be uncertain, but it is never allowed to hide that uncertainty. Every borderline case surfaces with a reason.CROSS-BIDDER FRAUD AND RISK DETECTIONOnce individual evaluations are complete, TenderKit runs a second layer of analysis that looks across all bidders together — something a manual reviewer simply cannot do at scale. This layer checks for duplicate or shared GST numbers across different company submissions, unusually high document similarity between bidders who should be independent, pricing patterns consistent with cartel behaviour, and other signals that suggest coordination or fraud. These patterns are invisible when you review each submission in isolation but become clear the moment you compare them systematically.Any bidder or group of bidders that triggers a risk signal is flagged in the report with a clear description of what was detected and why it warrants attention. The system does not make accusations — it surfaces evidence and leaves the judgement to the officer.THE OUTPUTOnce all evaluations and risk checks are complete, TenderKit generates a consolidated Word report. It covers every bidder and every criterion — listing the verdict, the source document, the page number, the extracted value, and the LLM's reasoning for each one. Bidders flagged for human review are highlighted with the specific reason. Documents that were expected but not submitted are listed explicitly. Risk and fraud signals detected across bidders are summarised in a dedicated section. The report is structured to be RTI-compliant and ready to sign off — every automated decision traces back to something TenderKit actually read, and nothing in it is a black box. TenderKit does not replace the procurement officer. It makes every decision they take fully defensible.WHY THIS WORKS FOR GOVERNMENT PROCUREMENT SPECIFICALLYMost document automation tools assume clean, digital inputs. Real government procurement is the opposite — documents arrive scanned at odd angles, typed in inconsistent formats, and written in legal language that varies across bidders and regions. TenderKit is designed from the ground up for this messiness. The OCR layer handles poor quality scans, the semantic search layer handles language variation, the LLM is explicitly instructed to flag uncertainty rather than push through it, and the cross-bidder analysis layer catches coordinated fraud that no individual document review would ever surface. The audit trail is built in at every step — every verdict, every flag, and every risk signal is tied to a source, a page, and a value, making the entire procurement decision traceable and defensible long after the report is signed.TECH STACKChromaDBpdfplumber / pymupdf4llmPaddleOCR / AWS Textractpython-docxLLM — verdict and confidence scoringcross-bidder fraud signal detectionFastAPIRTI-compliant Word report generationFrontend- React

A aryan.rai.cse23_4e29
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Pramaan AI
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 02:28 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Pramaan AI (प्रमाण AI) — Bringing Trust, Transparency, and Determinism to Government Tender EvaluationGovernment procurement systems like CRPF face a critical bottleneck: manual verification of tender compliance. Officers must sift through dense legal documents and inconsistent bidder submissions—ranging from clean PDFs to blurry scanned images. This process is slow, error-prone, and lacks auditability.While Generative AI promises automation, it introduces a dangerous tradeoff: hallucinations and black-box decisions are unacceptable in legal workflows.Pramaan AI solves this by redefining AI’s role—from decision-maker to verifiable compliance assistant.What We BuiltPramaan AI is a deterministic, explainable AI-powered compliance engine that transforms tender evaluation into a structured, transparent, and auditable workflow. 1. Intelligent Tender UnderstandingParses complex tender documents into structured JSON criteriaClassifies requirements into mandatory vs optionalEliminates ambiguity in interpretation 2. Multi-Format Document IntelligenceHandles PDFs, scans, and images seamlesslyUses Vision-Language Models (VLMs) instead of unreliable OCRAccurately extracts even tables, stamps, and low-quality scans3. Deterministic Evaluation EngineAI extracts values → Rule engine evaluates complianceExample: Extracts “₹6 Crore turnover” → compares against “≥ ₹5 Crore”Ensures zero hallucinated approvals/rejections4. Explain ability & Auditability (Game-Changer)Every decision is backed by visual proofHighlights exact evidence in documents using bounding boxesGenerates tamper-proof audit logs (SHA-256 hashed)Key InnovationsOCR-Free Visual RAG (ColPali) Reads documents as visual patches → works even on distorted scansZero Silent Rejections Confidence-based system: If confidence < 85% → “Needs Manual Review”Visual Grounding UI Officers don’t just see results—they see the exact proof instantlyWhy This MattersReduces tender evaluation time from days → minutesEliminates human bias & inconsistencyEnsures legal-grade transparency and trustKeeps humans in control, AI as an assistant—not a black boxScalable & Production-Ready ArchitectureFrontend: React + Tailwind (intuitive split-screen review UI)Backend: Node.js + Express (secure API & logging)AI Engine: Python + FastAPI + LangGraphLLMs: Gemini 1.5 Pro (multimodal reasoning)Database: PostgreSQL + pgvectorRisk-Aware by DesignNo blind AI decisions → rule-based verificationPoor-quality inputs → auto-flagged for human reviewEvery action → fully auditable and traceableVisionPramaan AI is not just a tool—it is a trust infrastructure for public procurement. By combining AI + determinism + visual proof, we aim to make government tendering:✔ Faster ✔ Fairer ✔ Fully transparentWhy Pramaan AI Stands Out InnovationMoves beyond generic LLM automation to a deterministic AI + rule-engine hybrid, eliminating hallucination risks in legal workflows Introduces OCR-free Visual RAG (ColPali) to extract information from complex, low-quality documents where traditional systems fail Implements visual grounding with bounding boxes, turning AI decisions into verifiable evidence instead of opaque outputs First-principles design: AI extracts → Code decides, ensuring reliability in high-stakes governance Not just smarter AI—safer AI for real-world decision systems. ImpactReduces tender evaluation time by 80–90%, accelerating public procurement cycles Minimizes human bias, clerical errors, and corruption risks through consistent rule-based evaluation Enables audit-ready governance with cryptographic logs—critical for disputes and compliance Scalable across all government departments (not just CRPF) and even private procurement systems Transforms procurement from a bottleneck into a transparent, high-trust pipeline.FeasibilityBuilt using production-ready, widely adopted technologies (FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Gemini) Modular microservice architecture ensures easy scaling and integration with existing systems Clear implementation roadmap (4-week phased plan already defined) Human-in-the-loop design ensures practical deployability without regulatory resistance Not a concept—this is deployable within real government workflows.“While others use AI to automate decisions, Pramaan AI makes decisions provable.”In high-stakes systems like government procurement, accuracy is not enough—trust is mandatory. Pramaan AI is designed not just to be intelligent, but to be provably correct, auditable, and accountable.

solutions.solution_text:

S sneha7259
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderMind AI
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 05:26 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemKarnataka's government procurement involves thousands of tenders annually across departments — police, infrastructure, health, IT, and more. Every tender evaluation is governed by the KTPP Act, 1999 and GFR 2017. Yet the core of this process — "evaluating bidder eligibility" — is still done manually, by overwhelmed committees, on paper.A single tender can attract 40 bidders, each submitting 45+ pages of documents. That's 1,800+ pages for a committee to read, cross-check, score, and record — within 30–45 days. The result is chronic failure across six dimensions: 6 Critical Problems in Government Tender Work1. Evaluation Inconsistency — Two officers reading the same documents reach different verdicts. One accepts a CA-certified turnover letter; another demands an audited balance sheet. No standardization exists, and decisions depend on who's in the room, not what the law says.2. Document Overload — 1,000+ pages per tender, evaluated manually under strict legal deadlines that are routinely missed — triggering grievance challenges and project delays.3. Silent Disqualification — Bidders are rejected without documented, criterion-level reasons. When challenged at the Karnataka High Court, the government cannot defend its decisions. The outcome: costly legal reversals and mandatory re-tendering.4. Corruption & Bid Rigging — Manual evaluation enables criteria manipulation, document forgery, L1 manipulation, and insider leaks. Because evaluation records are narrative and unstructured, these manipulations leave no detectable trace.5. CAG Audit Failures — Every procurement decision must be traceable to a criterion, a document, and an officer. Manual narrative reports cannot meet this standard. Karnataka departments face repeated CAG audit objections for missing criterion-level evaluation records.
Solution: Upload a tender. Upload bidder documents. Get a ranked, scored, criterion-by-criterion evaluation report in minutes — not weeks.TenderMind AI is a purpose-built, AI-powered evaluation platform that understands the legal, technical, and financial language of Indian government tenders.Upload a tender. Upload bidder documents. Get a ranked, scored, criterion-by-criterion evaluation report in minutes — not weeks.How it works:Officer uploads the NIT/RFP (any format — PDF, Word, scanned)AI auto-extracts all eligibility criteria with mandatory/optional flagsOfficer confirms or edits criteria before evaluation beginsBidders upload documents; AI classifies each document on uploadOCR + LLM pipeline reads all documents — typed or scanned — and extracts criterion-relevant valuesAI scores every bidder against every criterion, flags ambiguous cases for human reviewGenerates a ranked bidder list and audit-ready report with source references and L1 identificationThree-verdict system per criterion: ELIGIBLE (AI confidence ≥85%) → auto-scored | MANUAL REVIEW (70–84%) → flagged to officer with evidence | NOT ELIGIBLE (≥85%, criterion failed) → auto-rejection report generated."Zero silent disqualifications". Every rejected bidder gets a structured report: criterion failed + document evaluated + value found vs. value required. This pre-empts litigation and satisfies KTPP Act transparency requirements.﻿Why AI — and Why Nothing Else WorksKarnataka's e-Procurement portal already digitizes bid submission. But digitization ≠ intelligence. The portal cannot read a scanned GST certificate and verify it matches the bidder's PAN. It cannot calculate whether a project value meets the minimum turnover threshold. It cannot cross-reference figures across two documents. Only AI can do this at scale.ProblemTenderMind AI SolutionInconsistent evaluationSame logic applied to every bidder, every timeDocument overload60-page submission processed in under 30 secondsSilent disqualificationAuto-generated criterion-level rejection reportsCorruption riskDeterministic scoring with tamper-evident audit logsCAG audit failuresEvery decision traceable to criterion + document + timestampGovernance delaysWeeks of evaluation compressed to hours﻿Tech StackBuilt on microservices, deployable on KSDC / NIC Cloud:Ingestion: FastAPI + MinIO + ClamAVDocument Intelligence: Tesseract OCR + Azure AI Doc Intelligence + IndicBERT (Kannada/Hindi/English)AI Reasoning: Fine-tuned LLM + LangChain + RAG pipelineEvaluation Engine: Python rules engine + PostgreSQL + RedisAudit Layer: React.js dashboard + DSC-signed PDF export + immutable audit logImpact and SecurityTenderMind AI doesn't just speed up procurement — it structurally fixes it. Evaluation time drops from weeks to hours. Litigation risk drops to near-zero. CAG compliance becomes automatic. Corruption loses its entry points. And public services get delivered on time. This is Smart Government Procurement for Karnataka — purpose-built, legally compliant, and ready to prototype. How the Public Can Trust TenderMind AI: Every evaluation decision is locked into an immutable, tamper-proof audit log — no one can alter it after the fact, not even system administrators. Every rejected bidder receives a written, criterion-level reason — no silent disqualifications, ever. All override decisions by officers are dual-approved and permanently recorded. CAG and Lokayukta auditors have independent read-only access to the entire evaluation trail at any time. And every final report is digitally signed, making it mathematically verifiable in court. The system doesn't ask the public to trust people — it makes the process itself trustworthy.

solutions.team_name: Pramana AI: Evidence-Grounded Eligibility Evaluator
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Boys
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Pramana AI: Evidence-Grounded Eligibility Evaluator
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 05:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Core Bottleneck & Security ImperativeGovernment procurement in defense and paramilitary organizations (CRPF) is a high-stakes, legally binding process. Procurement officers are tasked with evaluating massive volumes of heterogeneous bidder submissions—ranging from structured digital PDFs to blurry photographs of physical certificates—against strict tender criteria.The primary challenge is twofold: First, manual evaluation is prone to human fatigue, leading to inconsistencies and oversight. Second, deploying standard, cloud-based AI to automate this process introduces unacceptable data sovereignty risks (by transmitting sensitive financial/PII data to third-party servers) and legal risks (through "black-box" AI hallucinations). A compliant solution must be entirely air-gapped, explainable, and deterministic.2. Proposed Innovation: The "Extraction vs. Evaluation" ArchitecturePramana AI (derived from the Kannada word for Proof/Evidence) solves the hallucination problem by strictly separating text extraction from mathematical evaluation. Large Language Models are excellent at reading text but terrible at deterministic math.The AI Extracts: A locally hosted, open-weight LLM reads the unstructured bidder documents to extract raw values (e.g., retrieving the string "₹4.5 Crore" from a scanned tax document).The Code Evaluates: Hard-coded, deterministic Python logic handles the actual threshold comparisons (e.g., verifying if 4.5 >= 5.0).By ensuring the AI never calculates the final verdict itself, Pramana AI guarantees 100% mathematical accuracy and legal compliance, eliminating the risk of a model hallucinating a passing grade for an unqualified bidder.3. End-to-End WorkflowThe pipeline operates in four strict stages designed to process "dirty data" without relying on external APIs:Phase 1: Tender Deconstruction: The system ingests the authoritative CRPF tender document. It uses NLP to extract and categorize all eligibility criteria into a strict JSON schema (Technical, Financial, Compliance), explicitly flagging rules as Mandatory or Optional. A procurement officer confirms this baseline schema.Phase 2: Multi-Modal Bidder Ingestion: Bidder documents are dynamically routed based on format. Digital PDFs undergo direct text extraction (PyPDF), while physical scans, images, and tables are processed through local Optical Character Recognition (Tesseract OCR) to normalize the text.Phase 3: RAG-Grounded Evidence Matching: The system creates a local FAISS vector database for each bidder submission. When evaluating a criterion, it mathematically retrieves only the most relevant paragraphs. The LLM is forced to extract answers strictly from these retrieved chunks, ensuring all data is explicitly grounded in the bidder's own submission.Phase 4: The Explainability Dashboard: The backend generates a strict JSON array of verdicts ("Eligible," "Not Eligible," or "Needs Review") which is pushed to an interactive frontend UI.4. Non-Negotiables: Human-in-the-Loop & AuditabilityPramana AI acts as an evidence-gathering "Digital Co-Pilot," not an autonomous operator.No Silent Disqualification: If a scanned document is too blurry (OCR confidence Hyperlinked Audit Trails: Every automated "Eligible/Not Eligible" verdict is hyperlinked directly to the source document. When an officer clicks a verdict, the UI displays a side-by-side view highlighting the exact page, paragraph, and extracted text used to make that decision, ensuring complete administrative transparency.5. Technology Stack (Air-Gapped & Zero-Trust)This stack is specifically chosen to run entirely on local hardware, meeting strict government data privacy requirements:AI/LLM Engine: Llama 3.1 (8B) running locally via Ollama.Orchestration: LangChain for RAG workflows and Pydantic for strict JSON output parsing.Vector Store & Embeddings: FAISS and sentence-transformers for high-speed local semantic search.Document Processing: Tesseract OCR (Scans/Images) and PyPDF (Digital Text).Frontend UI: Streamlit for deploying the interactive Procurement Officer Dashboard and immutable audit reports.

solutions.team_name: NirnayAI : AI-powered tender evaluation for fast, fair, and auditable government decisions
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


V VSquare
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NirnayAI : AI-powered tender evaluation for fast, fair, and auditable government decisions
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 10:41 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Statement and Need Government procurement, such as tenders issued by organizations like Central Reserve Police Force, is heavily document-driven, where eligibility criteria are deeply embedded in lengthy, unstructured legal text. Evaluators must manually interpret these criteria—financial, experiential, and compliance-based—and verify them against highly inconsistent bidder submissions (scanned images, PDFs, varied formats), making the process slow and cognitively demanding. The lack of standardization forces subjective judgment calls, especially in ambiguous cases (e.g., unclear scans or mismatched financial formats), leading to inconsistencies across evaluators. This not only delays evaluations (often 3–5 days per tender) but also weakens auditability, increasing risks of disputes, complaints, and allegations of bias or corruption. At its core, the task is structured reasoning over unstructured data—reading, extracting, comparing, and justifying decisions—which is currently done manually despite being well-suited for systematic, traceable automation.

Solution Overview NirnayAI structures the evaluation process into a three-stage intelligent pipeline—understanding the tender, interpreting bidder submissions, and generating explainable decisions—each directly addressing a core inefficiency in traditional procurement workflows. Stage 1 — Tender Intelligence: The system uses an LLM to read the tender contextually and extract a structured criteria registry, classifying conditions (financial, technical, compliance, documentation) and distinguishing mandatory vs. optional based on legal language cues. Each criterion is stored with source text, category, and a formal rule, with a human-in-the-loop review to ensure control over rule definition. Stage 2 — Bidder Document Parsing: A multi-format ingestion pipeline processes PDFs, scans, and Word files using OCR and layout-aware parsing, followed by an AI layer that builds an evidence map linking extracted values to exact document locations. This enables consistent interpretation despite variations in format or presentation. Stage 3 — Criterion-Level Evaluation and Reporting: The system evaluates each bidder against every criterion, producing explainable outputs with evidence, extracted values, and reasoning. It supports three outcomes—Eligible, Not Eligible, and Needs Manual Review—to explicitly handle ambiguity, ensuring human oversight where needed while maintaining a complete, auditable decision trail.

Comprehensive Feature Set 1. Automated Criteria Extraction — structured registry, automatically converts unstructured tender text into clearly defined, editable eligibility rules with categories and thresholds. 2. Multi-Format Document Ingestion — all formats, processes PDFs, scans, images, Word, and Excel files seamlessly through format-specific pipelines. 3. OCR with Confidence Scoring — accuracy flags, assigns confidence levels to extracted text and flags low-quality or uncertain data for review. 4. Evidence Mapping — source traceability, links every extracted value to its exact document source (file, page, section) for easy verification. 5. Three-Tier Verdict System — clear outcomes, classifies results as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review to handle ambiguity safely. 6. Criterion-Level Explainability — granular insights, provides detailed reasoning for each criterion rather than just overall bidder results. 7. Human-in-the-Loop Review Interface — manual validation, allows officers to review flagged cases with full context and override decisions when needed. 8. Immutable Audit Log — full traceability, records every action and decision in a tamper-proof, append-only system for accountability. 9. Consolidated Procurement Report — exportable summary, generates a complete, structured, and audit-ready report for official use. 10. Role-Based Access Control — user roles, ensures secure access by assigning permissions based on user responsibilities.

System Architecture and Workflow Tier 1 — Document Ingestion Layer: Uploaded files are routed by format — typed PDFs through text extraction, scanned documents and photos through confidence-scored OCR, and Word/Excel files through structural parsers — with every output stored alongside its source file, page number, and confidence score. Tier 2 — Tender Intelligence Layer: The tender text is processed by an LLM that extracts a structured criteria registry, tagging each criterion by type, mandatory/optional status, threshold value, and source sentence, which an officer reviews and confirms before evaluation begins. Tier 3 — Bidder Evidence Extraction Layer: For each bidder, the LLM reads across all submitted documents and builds an evidence map recording what value was found for each criterion, in which document, on which page, and with what confidence — handling variation in language and presentation automatically. Tier 4 — Evaluation and Verdict Engine: Each criterion is matched against the bidder's evidence map to produce a three-tier verdict — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — with full reasoning recorded and any low-confidence, borderline, or contradictory cases automatically escalated rather than silently decided. Tier 5 — Human Review and Override Layer: Flagged cases are presented to the officer with the criterion, extracted evidence, confidence level, and source document side by side; every officer decision is recorded with timestamp and user identity as an immutable audit entry. Tier 6 — Reporting and Audit Layer: The consolidated report is assembled with all verdicts, evidence references, and officer decisions, backed by an append-only audit database that records every automated and human action and cannot be modified at any level.

Technology Stack 1. Document Ingestion & OCR (Tesseract OCR, AWS Textract / Google Vision) — layout-aware extraction, high accuracy. 2. Core AI / Language Understanding (Claude API / GPT-4 via Azure OpenAI) — contextual reasoning, legal text. 3. Backend (Python + FastAPI) — async, scalable APIs. 4. Document Processing Pipeline (LangChain, PyMuPDF, python-docx) — structured parsing, orchestration. 5. Database (PostgreSQL) — relational, audit integrity. 6. Frontend (React + TypeScript) — maintainable, structured UI. 7. Infrastructure (Docker, NIC MeghRaj / AWS GovCloud) — secure, portable deployment.

Unique Differentiators 1. Language-Aware Understanding — interprets meaning beyond rigid templates. 2. Three-Tier Verdict System — avoids risky binary-only automated decisions. 3. Built-in Immutable Audit Trail — tamper-proof logging at database level. 4. India-Specific Document Handling — optimized for local formats and scripts. 5. Transparent Explainability — shows reasoning, not black-box outputs.

Challenges and Impact Challenge 1 — Poor Quality Scanned Documents Strategy — Confidence-based OCR with manual escalation. Impact — Prevents errors and ensures reliable evaluations. Challenge 2 — Ambiguous Tender Language Strategy — Pre-evaluation human clarification of criteria. Impact — Improves consistency and reduces misinterpretation. Challenge 3 — Data Privacy & Security Strategy — On-premise or configurable AI deployment. Impact — Ensures data sovereignty and regulatory compliance. Challenge 4 — Resistance to Adoption Strategy — Human-in-the-loop decision support design. Impact — Builds trust and enhances officer productivity. Challenge 5 — Legal Admissibility Strategy — AI outputs as recommendations with audit logs. Impact — Enables legally defensible and transparent decisions.

Scenario After Project Adoption Before NirnayAI, tender evaluation involved multiple officers manually reviewing lengthy documents, leading to 3–5 day timelines, inconsistent judgments, repeated reviews, and weak audit trails with limited reasoning. After adoption, the process becomes streamlined—criteria are auto-extracted, bidder documents are analyzed within hours, and officers review only flagged cases with full evidence on a dashboard. Decision-making shifts from manual reading to structured validation, reducing effort to a few hours over 1–2 days. The system ensures consistency by evaluating all bidders uniformly against every criterion, eliminating missed checks and subjective variation. It produces audit-ready, explainable reports instantly, making decisions legally defensible. Overall, NirnayAI delivers faster evaluations, higher accuracy, scalability, and significantly improved transparency in procurement.

For More Understanding of the Idea and Visual Representation : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1didG6NjIW679qalxrmlF0kqkCHbykQEm/view A simple prototype to demonstrate the proposed solution : https://nirnay-clarity-aid.lovable.app
"From paper trails to proof trails — NirnayAI turns tender decisions into decisions you can defend."

solutions.solution_text:

COMPETE

R r9427173766_af96
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderLens — AI-Powered Tender Eligibility Evaluation & Audit Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 11:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemGovernment procurement evaluation is manual, inconsistent, and slow. For a single tender, a committee spends days cross-checking hundreds of pages of bidder documents against eligibility criteria — and two evaluators can reach different conclusions from the same submission. Scanned certificates, varied document formats, and legally dense language make the problem worse. There is no audit trail, no consistency guarantee, and no scalable path forward.﻿Our SolutionTenderLens is an AI-powered platform that ingests a tender document and a set of bidder submissions, automatically extracts eligibility criteria, evaluates each bidder against those criteria, and produces an explainable, auditable evaluation report — ready for a procurement officer to review and sign off.﻿How It WorksStep 1 — Tender Understanding The platform parses the tender document using an LLM-based extraction pipeline. It identifies and structures every eligibility criterion — financial thresholds (e.g. minimum turnover), technical requirements (e.g. past project experience), compliance conditions (e.g. GST registration), and certification requirements (e.g. ISO 9001). Each criterion is tagged as Mandatory or Optional and stored in a structured schema that drives the downstream evaluation.Step 2 — Bidder Document Ingestion Bidder submissions arrive in any format — typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word documents, tables, or photographs of physical certificates. The platform runs each document through an OCR layer (for scanned/image inputs) followed by a document understanding model that extracts the values and evidence relevant to each criterion. Variation in how bidders present the same information is handled through semantic matching rather than rigid templates.Step 3 — Criterion-Level Evaluation For each bidder, the platform evaluates every criterion independently. Each verdict — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — is backed by a structured evidence record: which criterion was checked, which document was used, what value was extracted, and why the verdict was reached. No bidder is silently disqualified. Any case where confidence falls below a defined threshold is automatically routed to a human reviewer with the specific reason flagged.Step 4 — Consolidated Report & Audit Trail The platform generates a consolidated evaluation report listing all bidders, their per-criterion verdicts, and the evidence behind each decision. Every automated decision is logged with a timestamp, confidence score, and source reference — making the full evaluation auditable end-to-end and suitable for formal government procurement decisions﻿What Makes This DifferentMost document AI tools extract information. TenderLens goes further — it evaluates, explains, and escalates. The human reviewer is not a fallback; they are a first-class participant. Every ambiguous case is surfaced with context, not buried. The output is not a data dump — it is a decision-ready report that a procurement officer can trust and defend.

It combines document intelligence, eligibility analysis, and user friendly guidance to help businesses make faster, smarter, and more confident bidding decisions.
