solutions.team_name: TenderVerify AI: Agentic Multi-Modal Framework for Explainable Procurement Audit
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


J Jai_Hind
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderVerify AI: Agentic Multi-Modal Framework for Explainable Procurement Audit
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 01:31 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding: The "Gov-Procure" RealityIn the context of the CRPF, tender evaluation is not just a data-matching task; it is a legal and administrative safeguard. The primary challenges are:Semantic Complexity: Eligibility criteria use "Legalese" where mandatory (shall/must) vs. directory (may/should) clauses are critical.Data Heterogeneity: Bidders often provide non-standardized evidence (e.g., a handwritten experience letter vs. a digital GST certificate).Accountability: An AI "black box" is unacceptable. Every decision must have a "paper trail" to prevent litigation and ensure transparency in government spending.2. Proposed Architecture: The Agentic WorkflowWe propose a multi-agent system built on a Vision-Language RAG pipeline. Instead of one large model doing everything, we use specialized AI agents.Agent A (The Analyst): Extracts and structures eligibility criteria into a dynamic JSON schema.Agent B (The Visionary): Uses Multi-modal LLMs to parse scanned images/PDFs and extract relevant entities.Agent C (The Auditor): Compares Agent B’s findings against Agent A’s criteria and flags discrepancies.3. Technical ApproachA. Tender Intelligence (Understanding the Rulebook)We use Hierarchical PDF Parsing to maintain the document structure (headings, sub-clauses).Model: Gemini 1.5 Pro or GPT-4o (due to high context windows).Logic: The system classifies criteria into:Technical: (e.g., "3 years experience in construction").Financial: (e.g., "Turnover > ₹5Cr").Compliance: (e.g., "Valid ISO 9001").Output: A Structured Criteria Map where each requirement is assigned a "Mandatory" flag based on linguistic cues.B. Heterogeneous Document Processing (The OCR+VLM Layer)To handle "photographs and scans," we bypass traditional OCR-to-text (which loses formatting) and use Layout-Aware Extraction.Tech Stack: DocTR (Document Text Recognition) or Azure Document Intelligence for spatial anchoring.VLM Integration: We use Vision-Language Models to "see" certificates. If a GST certificate is a photo, the VLM identifies the GSTIN and the validity date directly from the image pixels.C. Explainable Evaluation & Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)Evidence Mapping: For every "Eligible" verdict, the system generates a Source Citation. (e.g., "Criterion 4.1 met. Reference: 'Experience_Letter.pdf', Page 2, Paragraph 3").Ambiguity Trigger: We use Confidence Scoring. If the VLM's confidence in reading a blurry scanned figure is below 85%, the system does not disqualify. It generates a "Flag for Manual Review" with a reason: "Inconclusive reading of turnover figures due to document blur."4. Auditability and Risk ManagementEnd-to-End Audit Trail: Every step—from extraction to the final verdict—is logged in a version-controlled database. Any human override is recorded with a mandatory justification field.Risk Mitigation:Hallucination Risk: We use Self-Correction loops where Agent C cross-checks Agent B's extracted values against the raw text chunks to ensure factual alignment.Format Inconsistency: Using Few-shot prompting with diverse Indian government document templates (GST, ITR, MSME certificates) to improve extraction accuracy.5. Round 2 Implementation PlanPhase 1 (Data Pre-processing): Build a pipeline using PyMuPDF and EasyOCR/Tesseract for initial text/image segregation.Phase 2 (Schema Definition): Develop a system to convert "CRPF Tender Language" into a standardized JSON Eligibility Template.Phase 3 (Agent Development): Implement the multi-agent framework using LangGraph or CrewAI to manage the flow between extraction and verification.Phase 4 (UI/Dashboard): Create a Streamlit/React dashboard for procurement officers to view the "Consolidated Evaluation Report" with side-by-side document comparisons.6. Key Technology ChoicesLLM/VLM: Gemini 1.5 Pro (Native multi-modality for scans).Vector Database: ChromaDB or Pinecone for storing document embeddings for RAG.Framework: LangChain for orchestration.Document Analysis: LayoutLMv3 for understanding the spatial relationship in tables and forms.

solutions.team_name: TEVA 2.0
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


M mharikumar2007_54a4
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TEVA 2.0
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 10:35 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TEVA 2.0 ---> Truth-First Intelligent Vendor Shortlisting for CRPF ProcurementTheme 3 — AI-Based Tender Evaluation & Eligibility AnalysisPROTYPE LINK :    https://teva2.netlify.app/  LINK HERE ﻿﻿1. ProblemCRPF procurement committees manually review bids — slow, inconsistent, and easily manipulated. Forged certificates pass undetected. Cartel bids go unnoticed. Every competing team will build:PDF → OCR → NLP → Pass/Fail (This solves the wrong problem)﻿2. Core Insight“The PDF is not the answer. It is the KEY to an independent investigation.”TEVA extracts identifiers (CIN, GST No., project locations, equipment registration) from submitted documents, then uses these as keys to independently verify reality from sources the bidder cannot control or fabricate.﻿3. Truth Stack Architecture — Four Evidence Layers﻿LayerNameWeightData SourcesLayer 1 — Most TrustedGround Truth25%Satellite imagery · IoT/FASTag · Public recordsLayer 2Behavioural30%Financial DNA · Narrative intelligence · PatternsLayer 3Registry30%MCA21 · GSTN · VAHAN · CPPP · ROC · EPFOLayer 4. — Least TrustedDocuments15%PDFs · Scans · Photos · Word files﻿﻿4. System FlowStepAction1Upload Tender PDF → GPT-4o extracts mandatory & desirable criteria2Upload Bidder Submissions (any format: typed PDF, scan, photo, Word)3Extract identifiers: CIN, GST No., Project Sites, Equipment Reg. Numbers4Truth Stack Agents fire in parallel across all 4 layers per bidder5Neo4j Cartel Detection — director network mapped across ALL bidders6Composite Score 0–100 per bidder → Ranked Shortlist generated7Officer Dashboard: review flags, acknowledge risks, sign-off8Decision anchored to Hyperledger Fabric — immutable blockchain audit﻿﻿5. Five Unique Innovations5.1 Reverse Verification EngineBidder claims a highway project (Rajasthan, 2022). TEVA queries Google Earth Engine + ISRO Bhuvan — before/after satellite imagery confirms whether construction actually occurred. A forged letter cannot fake a satellite image.5.2 Financial DNA FingerprintingClaimed turnover vs actual GST e-invoice volume. TDS deductions reveal real payroll. ROC filing gaps signal stress. Example: Bidder claims Rs.7.2 Cr; GST invoices show Rs.2.1 Cr → Claim-Reality Ratio 3.4x → FRAUD FLAG.5.3 Cartel & Fraud Graph DetectionMCA21 API extracts all directors for every bidder. Neo4j maps the complete ownership network across all bidders simultaneously. Shared director + shared address + shared CA firm = Cartel Flag raised automatically.5.4 IoT Asset VerificationBidder claims 10 JCBs. VAHAN confirms registration. FASTag transaction history confirms whether those machines actually moved on Indian highways in the last 24 months. Paper ownership ≠ operational capacity.5.5 Predictive Delivery Risk ModelXGBoost classifier trained on historical GeM contract outcomes. Predicts delivery failure before contract is awarded. Features: company age, project/company size ratio, simultaneous contract load, geographic distance, past penalty clauses﻿﻿8. TEVA 2.0 vs Other ApproachesManual procurement takes 3–5 days per tender and outputs a pass/fail decision based entirely on submitted paperwork — no fraud detection, no delivery prediction, no cartel awareness. Every other AI team will produce the same outcome faster using OCR and NLP. TEVA 2.0 is categorically different: it delivers a ranked composite score for every bidder, validates turnover via GST DNA instead of reading a balance sheet, confirms project existence via satellite instead of accepting an experience letter, detects cartel manipulation via a director ownership graph, predicts delivery risk via an ML model trained on real contract outcomes, scores human factors via EPF and Glassdoor, and produces a blockchain-anchored audit trail. Time per tender: minutes.9. Technology StackGPT-4o with function calling handles structured criteria extraction and grounded explanation — function calling eliminates parsing errors and grounding checks prevent hallucination. Google Earth Engine and ISRO Bhuvan provide satellite imagery with change detection; ISRO is used for data sovereignty in CRPF security contexts. Neo4j provides native graph traversal for director and cartel network analysis. XGBoost is chosen for the delivery risk model due to its performance on tabular data with missing features and interpretable feature importance. MCA21, GSTN, VAHAN, CPPP, and EPFO are authoritative government APIs producing legally defensible evidence. Google Cloud Vision and Tesseract 5.x handle OCR — GCV online, Tesseract offline for air-gapped CRPF environments. Hyperledger Fabric is a permissioned blockchain with no public exposure of procurement data. solutions.team_name: Grove Apex - A solution that transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S saadsyed950_0be8
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Grove Apex - A solution that transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 12:05 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
1. Problem Understanding
Government tender evaluation is currently a manual, time-intensive, and inconsistent process. Procurement officers are required to analyze large tender documents and verify bidder submissions across multiple formats such as PDFs, scanned documents, images, and structured files.This process presents several challenges:



Unstructured and heterogeneous data: Tender requirements and bidder documents vary significantly in format and structure.

Manual verification: Officers must cross-check each criterion (e.g., turnover, certifications) against multiple documents.

Inconsistency and human error: Different evaluators may interpret the same data differently.

Lack of auditability: Decisions are difficult to trace back to specific evidence.
As a result, the process is slow, error-prone, and difficult to scale. There is a need for an automated, explainable, and auditable system that assists decision-making while keeping humans in control.

2. Proposed Solution
We propose an AI-powered tender evaluation system that automates the extraction, analysis, and evaluation of tender and bidder documents.

End-to-End Flow:

Upload tender document and bidder submissions

Extract eligibility criteria from tender

Process bidder documents (OCR + parsing)

Map criteria to supporting evidence

Evaluate eligibility (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review)

Generate explainable results with audit trail

Enable human review for ambiguous cases

The system acts as a decision-support tool, not a replacement, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and scalability.

3. System Architecture
The system is designed as a modular pipeline:


Document Ingestion Layer: Handles upload and storage of tender and bidder files

OCR & Parsing Engine: Extracts text from PDFs, scanned images, and documents

LLM Processing Layer: Extracts structured criteria and key values

Retrieval (RAG) Layer: Maps extracted criteria to relevant document evidence

Evaluation Engine: Applies rules to determine eligibility

Explainability Layer: Generates human-readable justifications

Review Dashboard: Enables human-in-the-loop validation

Audit Layer: Logs all actions and decisions


This modular design ensures scalability, maintainability, and flexibility.

4. Criteria Extraction Approach
The system uses LLMs with structured prompting to extract eligibility criteria from tender documents.

Extracted Information:

Financial: Turnover, Net Worth

Compliance: GST, ISO certifications

Technical: Experience, project requirements

Classification:

Mandatory criteria

Optional criteria
Output is stored in structured format (e.g., JSON), enabling downstream processing.

5. Document Understanding Approach
The system processes bidder documents using a combination of:


OCR (Optical Character Recognition) for scanned and image-based inputs

Text parsing for structured documents

Extracted Data:

Numerical values (turnover, financials)

Dates (validity of certificates)

Identifiers (GSTIN, certification IDs)
To handle variability, the system uses semantic embeddings for flexible matching across formats.

6. Evaluation Logic
The evaluation engine compares extracted bidder data against tender criteria.

Output Categories:

Eligible → All mandatory criteria satisfied

Not Eligible → One or more mandatory criteria failed

Needs Review → Ambiguous or uncertain data

Example:

Required: ₹5 Cr turnover

Found: ₹6.2 Cr → Eligible

7. Explainability
Every decision is fully explainable and traceable.For each criterion, the system provides:



Criterion description

Source document reference

Extracted value

Reason for decision

Example:
“Turnover requirement ₹5 Cr. Found ₹6.2 Cr in Balance Sheet (Page 10). Hence Eligible.”This ensures trust and usability in real-world procurement workflows.


8. Handling Ambiguity
The system explicitly detects and handles ambiguous cases, such as:


Missing data

Low-confidence OCR extraction

Conflicting values across documents
Instead of making assumptions, the system:


Flags such cases as “Needs Review”

Provides reasons for uncertainty
This prevents incorrect decisions and ensures reliability.

9. Human-in-the-loop Design
A review dashboard allows procurement officers to:


Inspect extracted data and decisions

View supporting evidence

Approve, reject, or modify results
Reviewer actions are recorded and can be used to improve system performance over time.

10. Auditability
The system maintains a complete audit trail of:


Extracted data

Decision logic

Source documents

User actions
This ensures:


Transparency

Accountability

Compliance with government standards

11. Handling Non-Negotiables
The solution adheres to all constraints:


Works with synthetic or redacted data

Avoids hallucination by grounding decisions in document evidence

Ensures explainability for every decision

Never silently rejects case, ambiguous cases are flagged for review

12. Technology Stack

Backend: FastAPI for AI and Express for Business Logic

Frontend: React.js (vite)

OCR: Gemini Vision

AI/LLM: Gemini

Database: MongoDB

Vector Database: Chroma

Cloud: Railway

13. Risks & Trade-offs

OCR inaccuracies → Mitigated via confidence scoring and review

Ambiguity in documents → Handled via human-in-the-loop

LLM hallucination → Prevented via evidence grounding

Performance vs accuracy → Balanced using hybrid rule-based + AI approach
This solution transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.

Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:03 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
KarnaNyaya+ is an advanced AI-driven framework designed to bridge the critical gap between court judgments and their real-world execution in India’s judicial ecosystem. While existing systems such as CIS and CCMS effectively provide case information and status updates, they lack mechanisms to ensure timely compliance after a judgment is delivered. KarnaNyaya+ addresses this limitation by transforming unstructured legal documents into structured, actionable insights using technologies like OCR, Named Entity Recognition (NER), and rhetorical role labeling. It extracts key directives, identifies responsible departments, and organizes cases based on legal context.A key innovation is its “Comply or Appeal” decision intelligence system, which enables officials to make faster and more informed legal decisions. It also incorporates automated deadline tracking, workflow assignment, and escalation mechanisms to minimize delays and legal risks. Human-in-the-loop verification ensures transparency, accountability, and accuracy in every step. Additionally, dashboards and analytics provide real-time insights into compliance rates and administrative efficiency. Overall, KarnaNyaya+ transforms the judiciary from a passive information system into an active, execution-driven ecosystem, enabling faster, smarter, and more accountable justice delivery across India.

solutions.team_name: CertiProcure AI: Blockchain-Anchored Multi-Modal Intelligence for Automated Tender Compliance
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Aim-0->1
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CertiProcure AI: Blockchain-Anchored Multi-Modal Intelligence for Automated Tender Compliance
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Core ConceptCertiProcure AI is a secure, end-to-end platform designed for the CRPF to eliminate manual evaluation fatigue. It combines Agentic AI for document reasoning with a Permissioned Blockchain Ledger to ensure that every eligibility decision is transparent, immutable, and legally defensible.2. Technical Architecture & Blockchain IntegrationOur architecture is built on a "Decoupled Trust" model:The Intelligence Layer (AI): Uses a hybrid of LayoutLMv3 (for spatial document understanding) and GPT-4o/Claude 3.5 (for complex legal reasoning). It handles typed PDFs, scans, and mobile photos by normalizing them into structured JSON data.The Integrity Layer (Blockchain): We integrate a Private Ethereum Sidechain or Hyperledger Fabric. This serves as the system's "Black Box" recorder.Data Anchoring: Instead of storing heavy files on-chain, we store SHA-256 Hashes of the original tender and bidder documents.Immutable Logs: Every automated verdict (Eligible/Ineligible) and human override is recorded as a "Transaction," creating a permanent audit trail that cannot be deleted or altered by any administrator.3. Step-by-Step WorkflowTender Ingestion: AI extracts criteria (e.g., Turnover > ₹5 Cr). These rules are "anchored" on the blockchain as the baseline for the evaluation.Multi-Modal Bidder Analysis: The OCR engine processes diverse file types. If a document is a blurry photograph, the AI flags it for "Manual Review" rather than rejecting it.Automated Comparison: The AI matches bidder evidence against tender rules.Verification & Logging: Each decision is timestamped and written to the Blockchain. This ensures that if a bidder is disqualified, the specific reason and the version of the document used are locked in the ledger.4. Addressing Non-NegotiablesExplainable Verdicts: The system produces a "Decision Trace." For example: "Criterion: GST Registration; Result: Pass; Evidence: Page 3 of 'Tax_Docs.jpg'; Hash: [abc123...]"Human-in-the-Loop: A dedicated dashboard surfaces "Ambiguous" cases (e.g., low-confidence OCR results) for an officer’s sign-off. The officer's decision is also logged on-chain to ensure accountability.Auditability: The blockchain provides a Consolidated Audit Report. In case of a legal challenge, the CRPF can prove the exact state of the evidence at the time of the decision.5. Key TechnologiesOCR/Vision: LayoutLMv3, Tesseract (Pre-processing), and TrOCR.LLM Reasoning: Fine-tuned Llama-3 or Claude for administrative rule extraction.Blockchain: Hyperledger Fabric (for permissioned, high-speed transaction logging).Storage: Encrypted AWS S3 for documents with hashes stored on-chain.6. Implementation PlanPhase 1: Build the Vision Pipeline to handle noisy scans/photos.Phase 2: Implement the Smart Contract for logging evaluation verdicts.Phase 3: Develop the Human-in-the-loop dashboard for final CRPF officer verification.

solutions.solution_text:

P pavankumar21_896c
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TirpuKaryaSetu AI: From Court Judgments to Verified Government Actions
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TirpuKaryaSetu AI is a secure decision-intelligence system that understands court judgments and converts them into verified, accountable government actions — not just summaries. ತೀರ್ಪು ಕಾರ್ಯ ಸೇತು: a bridge from court judgment to government action.﻿THE PROBLEMGovernment departments receive court judgments as long, complex PDF documents where critical directions — what must be done, by when, and by which department — are buried inside dense legal text. This creates delays, confusion, file movement inefficiencies, and risk of non-compliance or contempt of court.﻿TirpuKaryaSetu AI transforms these judgments into structured, explainable, and officer-verified action workflows that fit real government processes.﻿HOW IT WORKSProcesses court judgment PDFs (scanned or digital) using OCR and text extraction.Uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to identify orders, directions, deadlines, responsible authorities, and conditions from the original judgment text.Converts the judgment into structured outputs: compliance actions, appeal options, timelines, and department routing.Applies policy and rule logic such as appeal windows, compliance deadlines, and urgency/risk flags.Sends output into a human verification workflow where officers can Approve, Edit, Reject, assign ownership, and add notes before final action.Maintains full traceability by linking each action item back to the exact source sentence in the judgment.EXAMPLE WORKFLOWInput (from judgment): "The respondent department is directed to release pension benefits within 8 weeks from the date of receipt of this order."﻿System output inside TirpuKaryaSetu AI:Action: Release pension benefits to the petitioner.Responsible Department: Finance / Pension Cell.Deadline: 8 weeks from date of receipt of order.Risk: Contempt risk if delayed beyond deadline.Recommendation: No appeal; proceed with compliance and track proof of action.Evidence Link: Exact sentence from the court order shown for officer review and verification.KANNADA + ENGLISH BILINGUAL SUPPORTTirpuKaryaSetu AI supports bilingual workflows for Karnataka departments, allowing officers to read and act on judgment-derived actions in both English and Kannada for faster understanding and execution.﻿Example1:English: "Release pension benefits within 8 weeks."ಕನ್ನಡ: "8 ವಾರಗಳೊಳಗೆ ಪಿಂಚಣಿ ಸೌಲಭ್ಯಗಳನ್ನು ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಿ.Example2: English: "Reinstate the terminated employee to service within 4 weeks from the date of receipt of this order."ಕನ್ನಡ: "ಈ ಆದೇಶದ ಸ್ವೀಕೃತಿ ದಿನಾಂಕದಿಂದ 4 ವಾರಗಳೊಳಗೆ ವಜಾಗೊಂಡ ನೌಕರರನ್ನು ಸೇವೆಗೆ ಮರಳಿ ನೇಮಿಸಿ.""DASHBOARD AND VERIFICATION WORKFLOWThe system shows a clean dashboard with verified actions, pending reviews, approaching deadlines, department-wise workload, and completed compliance cases.﻿Each case moves through a simple workflow:Judgment Received -> Extracted by AI -> Under Officer Review -> Verified & Approved -> Action In Progress -> Proof of Compliance Attached -> Case Closed﻿Dashboard shows:Pending actions with countdown timers to deadlines.Contempt-risk flags for cases approaching SLA breach.Department-wise pending and completed case counts.Full audit trail showing who reviewed, edited, approved each action.Proof upload status for completed compliance actions.CORE FEATURESIntelligent extraction of case details, parties, court directions, and responsible authorities.Action-plan generation covering compliance steps, appeal options, deadlines, and department mapping.Human approval workflow: Approve / Edit / Reject / Assign with notes.Multilingual support: English + Kannada for both judgment reading and officer interface.Verified-action dashboard with compliance status tracking.Full tamper-evident audit trail for accountability and future audits.Risk alerts: missed deadline flags, contempt-risk warnings, appeal-window countdowns.SECURITY AND TRUSTEnd-to-end encryption and role-based access control for government officers.Private, secure AI processing without exposing sensitive legal data to public models.Detailed audit logs for every action, review, edit, and approval.Deployable on government cloud or on-premise infrastructure as per department policy.KEY INNOVATIONGoes beyond summarization: generates execution-ready, officer-verified action workflows for departments.Combines AI + RAG + rule-based reasoning + human-in-the-loop verification for trustworthy outputs.Reduces hallucination risk by grounding every recommendation in the exact judgment source text.Designed specifically for government file movement and compliance operations, not generic legal search.IMPACTReduces manual reading and note-making effort for officers handling court orders by over 80%.Speeds up accurate decision-making immediately after judgment receipt.Prevents missed deadlines and lowers legal/contempt risk for departments.Bridges the gap between court judgment and on-ground government execution with clear ownership and evidence.﻿CASE METADATA EXTRACTION (Complete) For every judgment processed, TirpuKaryaSetu AI extracts a full set of structured case metadata including: Case Number, Court Name, Bench / Judge Name, Date of Order, Date of Receipt by Department, Petitioner Name, Respondent Name / Department, Disposal Status (Disposed / Pending), and Nature of Judgment (compliance-directed / appeal-relevant). This ensures that the system captures not just the directions but also the complete administrative context required for government record-keeping and CCMS integration.﻿CONFIDENCE LEVELS AND SOURCE TRANSPARENCY Every extracted field and AI-generated recommendation is displayed alongside a confidence score (High / Medium / Low) and a direct source highlight from the original PDF. For example, if the system identifies a compliance deadline, it shows: Extracted Text: "directed to release pension benefits within 8 weeks from the date of receipt" | Confidence: High (96%) | Source: Page 4, Paragraph 2. This enables the reviewing officer to immediately validate or challenge any extraction without reading the full judgment.﻿TRUSTED DASHBOARD — VERIFIED RECORDS ONLY The decision-maker dashboard is strictly gated: only officer-approved and human-verified action records are published to the dashboard view. Records that are pending review, rejected, or under edit remain in the officer queue and are never shown as actionable items. This ensures that every item visible to a department head or senior official represents a fully validated, traceable, and accountable action — not a raw AI output. The dashboard provides: Department-wise verified action plan view, Important compliance dates and appeal deadlines with countdown timers, Risk-flagged cases (contempt risk, approaching limitation period), Audit trail of who reviewed, edited, and approved each record, Proof-of-compliance upload status for closed cases.﻿CCMS AND HIGH COURT CIS INTEGRATION TirpuKaryaSetu AI is designed to plug directly into the existing Centre for e-Governance CCMS infrastructure. Once a case is disposed of in the High Court and the judgment PDF is automatically fetched into CCMS via the Court Information System (CIS) API, TirpuKaryaSetu AI picks it up from that point and processes the PDF without disrupting the current government workflow. In addition, the system also supports secure manual PDF upload by authorized officers, so departments can process legacy judgments, district court orders, exceptional cases, or documents received outside the CCMS flow. This hybrid ingestion model ensures that TirpuKaryaSetu AI can work both as a seamless value-add layer on top of CCMS and as a practical standalone tool wherever direct integration is unavailable or still being rolled out.﻿TECHNOLOGY STACK Will be Built using Google Gemini (LLM) for language understanding and action plan generation, LangChain with a RAG pipeline for grounded retrieval from judgment text, PyMuPDF and Tesseract OCR for parsing both digital and scanned PDFs, Python FastAPI for the backend API layer, and a React-based officer dashboard for the human verification and monitoring interface. The system is stateless at the AI layer, ensuring no sensitive legal data is retained in external models.﻿GOVERNMENT DATA COMPLIANCE AND NIC READINESS The current hackathon prototype will be built using open-source libraries and freely available services (Google Gemini free tier, LangChain, PyMuPDF, Tesseract OCR, Python FastAPI, React) to demonstrate the full flow. For real government deployment, the system is designed to be self-hosted — with all AI processing, data storage, and audit logs running within the department's own infrastructure or NIC Cloud, ensuring no sensitive legal data is transmitted to external services. The architecture supports swapping Google Gemini with a fully open-source, on-premise LLM (such as Ollama with LLaMA 3 or Mistral) to achieve 100% air-gapped deployment compliant with NIC cloud guidelines and India's government data classification policy (classifying judgment data as Restricted). Role-based access control, encrypted storage, and tamper-evident audit trails are implemented from day one in the prototype.

solutions.team_name: Explainable AI for CRPF Tender Evaluation: Automated Eligibility Analysis with Human-in-the-Loop Auditability
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Arpan_Ari_arpancodec
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Explainable AI for CRPF Tender Evaluation: Automated Eligibility Analysis with Human-in-the-Loop Auditability
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 21, 2026 12:52 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem SummaryGovernment organisations such as the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) issue tenders containing detailed eligibility criteria spanning financial thresholds, technical requirements, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications. These criteria are embedded within lengthy, legally structured documents, making them difficult to interpret and extract in a structured manner.Bidders respond with supporting documents in highly heterogeneous formats, including typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word files, tables, and images. The same information—such as turnover, certifications, or project experience—may appear in different formats and locations across submissions.Currently, the evaluation process is manual, requiring procurement officers to cross-check each bidder’s documents against each eligibility criterion. This process is time-consuming, inconsistent, and prone to human error. Variations in interpretation, missing data, and ambiguous documentation further increase the risk of incorrect decisions, which can lead to legal disputes, procurement delays, and financial losses.The fundamental challenge is to build a system that can reliably interpret complex tender requirements, extract relevant evidence from unstructured bidder documents, and perform consistent, explainable, and auditable eligibility evaluation under real-world uncertainty.﻿Proposed SolutionWe propose an AI-assisted, explainable, and auditable decision-support platform for automated tender evaluation.Automatically extracts eligibility criteria from tender documents and converts them into structured, machine-readable representations. Processes bidder submissions across multiple formats, including scanned documents and images, and extracts relevant evidence mapped to each criterion. Uses a deterministic rule-based evaluation engine to compare extracted evidence against criteria, ensuring consistent and non-ambiguous decision logic. Generates criterion-level verdicts — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Need Manual Review — with clear justifications. Ensures that no bidder is silently disqualified; all uncertain or low-confidence cases are explicitly flagged for human review. Maintains a complete audit trail of every decision, including extracted values, source documents, and applied rules, making the system suitable for legal and regulatory scrutiny. ﻿Key CapabilitiesTender Understanding The system identifies and extracts eligibility criteria from complex tender documents, classifying them into financial, technical, compliance, and documentation requirements, while distinguishing mandatory from optional conditions. Robust Document Processing It supports heterogeneous inputs including PDFs, scanned documents, and images, using OCR and layout-aware parsing to preserve structure such as tables and sections. Accurate Evidence Extraction The system extracts key information such as financial values, dates, certification details, and project experience using a combination of NLP, entity extraction, and pattern-based methods. Deterministic Evaluation Engine A rule-based engine evaluates each criterion against extracted evidence, ensuring consistent, transparent, and reproducible decisions without reliance on black-box models. Explainability by Design Every decision is accompanied by a clear explanation specifying the criterion evaluated, the source document and location, the extracted value, and the rule applied. Human-in-the-Loop Workflow Ambiguous, incomplete, or low-confidence cases are flagged for manual review, enabling human oversight and preventing incorrect automated rejection. End-to-End Auditability All decisions are logged with traceable metadata, enabling full audit trails required for government procurement processes. ﻿Why This MattersReduces evaluation time from days to hours, even for large tenders. Ensures consistent application of criteria across all bidders. Eliminates subjective interpretation and reduces human error. Provides full transparency and traceability for every decision. Strengthens legal defensibility of procurement outcomes. Allows officers to focus on critical judgment rather than repetitive verification tasks. ﻿Core Technical ChallengesAccurately isolating eligibility criteria from complex tender documents that mix multiple types of information. Handling semantic variations in how the same requirement or evidence is expressed across documents. Extracting numerical and structured data from low-quality scanned documents and images. Identifying relevant evidence when information is distributed across multiple documents. Managing ambiguous or borderline values without making incorrect decisions. Classifying and interpreting documents that may be poorly labeled or inconsistently structured. ﻿Solution PipelineTender Criterion Extraction The system processes the tender document to identify and structure eligibility criteria, classify their types, and determine whether they are mandatory or optional. Bidder Document Intake and Normalization All submitted documents are ingested, classified, and converted into a standardized representation using OCR, text extraction, and layout-aware parsing. Evidence Extraction Layer For each criterion, the system retrieves relevant document sections and extracts required values such as financial figures, certification details, and experience records, along with confidence scores. Evaluation and Decision Engine Extracted evidence is compared against criteria using predefined rules. Each criterion is evaluated independently, and results are aggregated into a final decision. Human Review and Audit Layer Low-confidence or ambiguous cases are flagged for manual review. All decisions are logged with complete traceability, and reports are generated for final approval. Our Unique Approach (What Makes This Solution Different)OUR UNIQUE APPROACH Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on end-to-end black-box models, our system is designed as a hybrid, audit-first AI framework that prioritizes reliability, explainability, and legal defensibility over blind automation.Audit-First, Not Automation-First Most solutions attempt to fully automate decision-making. In contrast, our system is designed to maximize trust and auditability, ensuring every decision can be traced, verified, and justified before acceptance. The system acts as an intelligent assistant, not an opaque decision-maker. Deterministic Rule Engine over Black-Box Decisions Instead of relying on large language models for final eligibility decisions, we use a rule-based evaluation engine. This guarantees consistent, reproducible outcomes and eliminates risks of hallucination or unpredictable behavior, which is critical in government procurement. Confidence-Aware Decision Making Every extracted value is associated with a confidence score. Rather than forcing a binary decision, the system introduces a third state — “Need Manual Review” — as a first-class outcome, ensuring that uncertainty is handled explicitly and safely. Criterion-Centric Processing (Not Document-Centric) Traditional systems process documents as a whole. Our approach reverses the paradigm by evaluating each criterion independently, retrieving only the most relevant evidence across all documents. This improves accuracy, reduces noise, and aligns directly with how human evaluators think. Multi-Source Evidence Aggregation The system does not assume that required information exists in a single document. It intelligently aggregates evidence across multiple sources (e.g., financial statements, CA certificates, company profiles), enabling robust evaluation even when data is fragmented. Explainability at the Core, Not as an Add-On Explainability is built into every layer of the system. Each verdict includes: The exact criterion evaluated The document and page referenced The extracted value The rule applied This ensures full transparency and makes the output directly usable in official reports. Graceful Handling of Real-World Noise The system is explicitly designed for real-world conditions: Poor-quality scans Inconsistent formats Approximate values Missing or ambiguous data Instead of failing silently, it flags and escalates such cases, ensuring robustness in practical deployment. Separation of Understanding and Decision Layers We clearly separate: AI components (for extraction and understanding) Rule-based components (for decision-making) This modular design improves maintainability, reduces risk, and allows independent improvement of each layer. Legally Defensible Audit Trail Every action in the pipeline is logged with structured metadata, enabling: Full traceability of decisions Reproducibility of results Compliance with procurement audit standards This makes the system suitable not just for assistance, but for formal adoption in government workflows.

solutions.team_name: Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Binary Brains ITM
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 10:10 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Veridict — Evidence-Based Decision Intelligence for Government Tender EvaluationA CRPF procurement committee evaluating 20 bidders manually spends 4 to 6 days cross-checking hundreds of pages of documents. Two evaluators reviewing the same submission often reach different conclusions. A single missed criterion can result in an incorrect award, and an untraceable decision can lead to audit failure or legal disputes.Veridict is designed to eliminate both inconsistency and untraceability.Veridict is not just an AI extraction system. It is a decision intelligence engine that reads tender documents, understands eligibility criteria, evaluates bidder submissions across multiple formats, and produces fully explainable, evidence-backed decisions with built-in consistency validation across all bidders.The system processes typed PDFs, scanned documents, images, and Word files. Using OCR and layout-aware models such as LayoutLMv3, it converts unstructured inputs into structured representations including tables, financial values, and key entities. Image preprocessing ensures robustness against low-quality scans.The evaluation pipeline is built on three core engines.CriteriaLens converts the tender document into a structured rule schema. Each criterion is extracted with its type, threshold, time constraints, mandatory status, and acceptable evidence sources. Legal indicators such as “shall”, “must”, and “essential” are used to detect mandatory conditions. Before evaluation begins, Veridict introduces a validation step where unclear or incomplete criteria (for example, vague terms like “adequate experience” or missing thresholds) are flagged for officer confirmation. This ensures that the system never evaluates against ambiguous rules.DocProbe processes bidder submissions using a hybrid extraction pipeline. For digital documents, it performs direct parsing. For scanned inputs, it applies preprocessing followed by OCR. Instead of extracting text blindly, the system uses retrieval-based grounding to locate only the most relevant sections for each criterion. Semantic normalization using models such as Sentence-BERT ensures that variations like “turnover”, “revenue”, and “gross receipts” are interpreted consistently. Every extracted value is tied to a document, page number, and source snippet. If evidence is not found, the system explicitly reports it rather than inferring.VerdictCore performs structured evaluation using a deterministic reasoning engine. Unlike systems that rely purely on AI matching, Veridict applies explicit reasoning logic for different types of criteria. Numeric conditions are evaluated using rule-based comparisons, temporal conditions such as “last 5 years” are validated using time-aware logic, and count-based requirements such as “at least 3 projects” are computed through aggregation. Logical conditions (AND/OR) are resolved systematically. AI is used only for interpretation, while final decisions are deterministic, ensuring consistent results for the same input every time.Each decision is assigned a multi-factor confidence score based on OCR quality, extraction reliability, semantic alignment, cross-document consistency, and anomaly detection signals. Veridict does not hide uncertainty. If confidence is low, the system clearly explains why — whether due to poor scan quality, missing data, conflicting values, or unclear evidence — and routes the case for manual review.Veridict introduces advanced intelligence layers that improve reliability beyond standard approaches.The Evidence Graph links every decision to its source: criterion → extracted value → document → page reference. This ensures that every output is fully traceable and auditable.The Consistency Engine evaluates all bidders collectively, not in isolation. It detects anomalies such as outlier values, inconsistent document formats, or unusual patterns across submissions. For example, if most bidders provide audited financial statements while one bidder provides unsupported values, the system flags this discrepancy automatically.The Cross-Validation Layer verifies information across multiple documents within the same submission. If a financial value appears in both a balance sheet and a tax document, the system checks consistency. Conflicting evidence reduces confidence and triggers a review.The Fraud Signal Layer identifies suspicious patterns such as repeated document reuse, abnormal value distributions, or inconsistencies across submissions. These signals are surfaced to officers as indicators, not final judgments.Each criterion is classified into one of three outcomes: Eligible when requirements are clearly satisfied, Not Eligible when requirements are clearly violated, Needs Manual Review when confidence is low, evidence is missing, or inconsistencies are detected.No bidder is ever silently disqualified. Every uncertain case is surfaced with explicit reasoning.Veridict also provides comparative intelligence across bidders. Instead of evaluating submissions in isolation, the system highlights top-performing candidates, detects outliers, and enables ranking based on criteria satisfaction. This gives decision-makers a clearer view of the overall competition.Every decision is accompanied by a complete explanation including the criterion evaluated, the value extracted, the document source, and the reasoning applied. A tamper-evident audit trail records every step of the process, making the system suitable for formal government use.System performance is measured using clear metrics such as extraction accuracy, decision precision, false rejection rate (targeting zero incorrect disqualifications), and percentage of cases requiring manual review. Reviewer corrections are captured as labeled data and used to continuously improve extraction accuracy, confidence calibration, and decision quality over time.In a real-world scenario, a procurement officer uploads a tender and multiple bidder submissions. Veridict extracts criteria, validates them, evaluates all bidders using deterministic reasoning, highlights anomalies and inconsistencies, and generates a consolidated report within minutes. Clearly eligible and ineligible bidders are identified, while ambiguous cases are flagged with full context for review.Veridict directly addresses all key evaluation requirements. It demonstrates a deep understanding of the problem by targeting inconsistency, ambiguity in criteria, auditability, and real-world document complexity. Its hybrid architecture combines AI-based understanding with deterministic reasoning, ensuring both flexibility and reliability. The system is deployable as a modular layer without requiring changes to existing infrastructure and can operate in secure environments with controlled data handling.Beyond procurement, the architecture is scalable to any domain involving document-heavy eligibility decisions, including compliance verification, grant evaluation, and regulatory audits. As more data and reviewer feedback are incorporated, the system continuously improves while maintaining decision stability.Veridict transforms tender evaluation from a manual, inconsistent process into a structured, transparent, and defensible decision system where every outcome is backed by verifiable evidence, consistent reasoning, and measurable confidence.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ParakhOps
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 10:27 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
ParakhOps – AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis SystemParakhOps is designed to address inefficiencies in government tender evaluation processes, such as those followed by the CRPF. Traditional evaluation involves manual verification of complex eligibility criteria across multiple bidder documents, leading to delays, inconsistencies, and limited auditability.The system transforms this workflow into a structured, explainable, and auditable decision pipeline. It begins by extracting eligibility criteria from tender documents—including financial, technical, and compliance requirements—and structuring them into a standardized schema. These criteria are then used to evaluate bidder submissions, which may include PDFs, scanned documents, or images.How It WorksFor each bidder, ParakhOps performs criterion-level evaluation by extracting relevant values, comparing them against requirements, and assigning a status: Pass, Fail, or Needs Manual Review.Rather than producing "black-box" outputs, every decision is supported with:Clear reasoningExtracted valuesDirect document referencesAmbiguous or low-confidence cases are never auto-rejected; they are explicitly flagged for human review to ensure fairness and accuracy.Key FeaturesGranular Intelligence: Criterion-level analysis for precision.Evidence-Based Decisions: Every result is backed by specific document data.Conflict Detection: Identifies discrepancies across different bidder documents.Human-in-the-Loop: A robust review mechanism for high-stakes or ambiguous data.Audit Trail: Maintains a complete log of inputs, extracted data, results, and timestamps for total traceability.Technical ArchitectureThe architecture is built for speed and reliability:Interface: Streamlit for seamless control flow and UI.Reasoning Engine: Google Gemini 1.5 Pro for multimodal document understanding.Data Structure: JSON-based outputs to ensure consistency across the pipeline.Decision Layer: A lightweight rules engine that enforces mandatory criteria and handles ambiguity.ParakhOps significantly reduces evaluation time, improves consistency across decisions, and provides transparent, audit-ready outputs. It acts as a decision-support system rather than a replacement for human evaluators, ensuring reliability and trust in government procurement processes.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CocoMind
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 18, 2026 11:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemGovernment procurement (like CRPF tenders) is slowed by manual document verification, prone to human error, and vulnerable to legal challenges (RTIs/CAG audits). Most AI tools are "black boxes" that lack the transparency and data security required for high-stakes government contracts.The Solution CocoMind automates the transition from messy PDF bids to a Verdict Traceability Matrix (VTM). It extracts data using LLMs but verifies it using a deterministic Python Rule Engine, ensuring every Pass/Fail decision is mathematically provable and linked to source evidence. Key Technical Features Traceable Logic: Every verdict includes the raw text, page number, bounding box, and the specific rule expression used for evaluation.Indian-Specific PII Redaction: Mandatory masking of Aadhaar, PAN, and GSTIN using Microsoft Presidio before any data hits the LLM.Hybrid Multilingual RAG: Combines BGE-M3 semantic search with BM25 lexical search to handle English, Hindi, and Marathi technical specifications.Integrity-First Architecture: Uses a SHA-256 hash-chained audit log and DSC (Digital Signature) integration to ensure non-repudiation of officer decisions.CRPF-Specific Compliance: Automated checkers for CVC debarment lists, EMD bank guarantee validity, and near-relation affidavits.The Impact:CocoMind slashes evaluation time by 90% while providing a "bulletproof" audit trail. It moves AI from a "creative assistant" to a formal verification tool that can survive a CAG audit and simplify RTI compliance.

solutions.team_name: ProcureCheck — AI-Assisted Tender Eligibility Evaluation for Government Procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S siddharthreddygogula123_8143
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ProcureCheck — AI-Assisted Tender Eligibility Evaluation for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 10:46 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemTender evaluation committees in government bodies like CRPF spend 7–15 working days manually cross-checking 30–50 documents per bidder across 10–20 bidders. One officer misses what another catches. Two evaluators reach different conclusions from the same file. Bidders describing the same qualification in different words confuse tired readers. When rejected bidders file RTI queries, committees often cannot produce a clean answer — because the process never created one.ProcureCheck does not replace the officer. It does all the reading and checking so the officer can focus entirely on judgment. Every decision stays human.﻿System FlowTender Upload |Tender Understanding EngineRule-based pattern extraction + LLM structured extractionClassify criteria: Financial / Technical / ComplianceFlag each condition: Mandatory or Optional |Officer Criteria Review LayerOfficer edits and confirms the extracted criteria listNo evaluation starts before officer approval |Bidder Document IngestionTyped PDFs / Word files / Scanned PDFs / Photographs |Document Processing Pipelinepdfplumber for typed PDFspython-docx for Word filesOpenCV preprocessing for scans and photos (deskew, denoise, contrast enhancement)Google Vision OCR for scanned textSeparate table extraction for financial figures |Evidence Extraction LayerExtract: turnover, project counts, GST, ISO, EPF/ESI, dates, document IDsEach field linked to: document name, page number, source passage |Evidence Reliability Scoring EngineOCR confidence of source pageDocument type trust level (audited balance sheet vs self-declaration)Consistency of the same value across multiple documentsStructured field vs free text |Retrieval and Matching EngineBM25 for exact values (amounts, numbers, dates)Semantic search for phrasing variationMulti-document aggregation when evidence is splitContradiction detection when documents disagreeMissing and unclear evidence detection |Rule-Aware Evaluation EngineNumeric threshold checksCount checksBoolean checksDocument validity checks |Verdict EngineELIGIBLENOT ELIGIBLEOFFICER REVIEW REQUIRED |Officer Review WorkflowLow-confidence and ambiguous cases surfaced to officerConflicting evidence shown side by sideManual override with mandatory written justification |Audit and Compliance LayerImmutable PostgreSQL audit logStores: extracted criteria, evidence used, reliability scores, verdicts, officer overrides, timestamps, officer IDs |Dashboard and Reporting LayerBidder x Criteria evaluation grid, colour-coded by verdictDrill-down evidence view per cellOfficer review queueExport: Tender Committee Minutes format under GFR 2017RTI-ready final signed report﻿How Each Stage WorksStage 1-3 — Reading the TenderThe officer uploads the tender. The system uses rule-based pattern matching combined with LLM extraction to pull out every eligibility condition. Mandatory conditions are spotted by phrases like "the bidder must" or "shall be rejected if." Optional ones say "preference will be given to." Where the language is genuinely ambiguous, the condition is flagged for the officer to classify. The officer sees the full extracted list, edits freely, and confirms. Evaluation only runs against what the officer approves.Stage 4-5 — Processing Bidder DocumentsGoogle Vision is used over Tesseract because it handles degraded government scans — blurry stamps, faded ink, phone camera photos of certificates — far more reliably, and returns per-word confidence scores. Pages below the confidence threshold are flagged and never used to generate a verdict on their own. Financial tables are parsed separately so rupee figures stay linked to correct row labels.Stage 6-7 — Evidence Reliability ScoringBefore any matching begins, every extracted piece of evidence gets a reliability score. Low-reliability evidence never drives an automatic verdict — it goes straight to the officer review queue with the score and reason shown clearly. The system does not just extract. It knows how much to trust what it extracted, and is honest about it.Stage 8-10 — Matching and Verdict GenerationKeyword search (BM25) finds exact values — rupee amounts, registration numbers, project counts. Semantic search finds meaning regardless of wording — "three works of similar nature costing not less than forty percent of estimated cost" and "three comparable contracts above the minimum threshold" correctly match the same condition. The engine aggregates evidence across documents, detects missing or contradicting information, and routes partial matches to the officer rather than auto-rejecting.Every bidder gets exactly one verdict per condition:ELIGIBLE — clearly met. Shows document name, page, extracted value, exact passage, and reliability score.NOT ELIGIBLE — clearly not met. Shows what was found and why it fails in plain language.OFFICER REVIEW REQUIRED — triggered by low reliability, ambiguous language, missing documents, contradictions, or evidence needing human judgment. The officer sees the exact reason and all evidence gathered so far.The system never silently disqualifies a bidder. Any doubt goes to the officer — always.Stage 11-13 — Officer Review, Audit, and ReportA grid shows every bidder against every condition, colour-coded by verdict. Clicking any cell opens the full evidence with reliability scores. Flagged cases appear in a review queue — the officer reads the source document, makes the call, and writes a short reason. Every override in either direction is permanently logged with timestamp and officer ID. On sign-off, ProcureCheck generates an evaluation report in the Tender Committee Minutes format under GFR 2017 — RTI-ready by default.﻿Technology ChoicesGoogle Vision API over Tesseract -> Handles degraded government scans far better. Returns per-word confidence scores which directly power the reliability scoring system.BM25 + Semantic Search together, not either alone -> BM25 catches exact values like rupee thresholds and registration numbers. Semantic embeddings catch phrasing variation — the same condition written in different legal language by different bidders. Both are needed.Qdrant Vector Store -> Open source, self-hostable on NIC servers. Sensitive bid documents never leave the local environment.Structured LLM Output -> Forces the model to return machine-readable verdicts with evidence references. No free-form text to parse or interpret.PostgreSQL Immutable Audit Log -> Append-only event log. Nothing can be deleted or edited after the fact — a hard requirement for government procurement accountability and RTI compliance.﻿Risks and How We Handle ThemPoor scan quality -> OCR confidence gate flags the page before any verdict runs from it.Ambiguous mandatory/optional conditions in the tender -> Officer confirms and classifies the full criteria list before evaluation begins.Evidence split across multiple documents -> Multi-document aggregation runs before verdict generation.Missing documents entirely -> Treated as unverified, not automatically failed — goes to the officer review queue.Contradictions within a single bidder's submission -> Both conflicting passages are shown to the officer side by side.LLM or API failure mid-evaluation -> All processing results are cached. Officer can enter verdicts manually through the dashboard.﻿Why This Will Actually Be UsedEvery existing tool — Minaions, ContraVault, TenderIntelligence — is built for bidders checking their own eligibility. Not one is built for the officer who must evaluate all bidders fairly and sign a legally defensible record.ProcureCheck is built for that officer. Evaluation time drops from 15 days to 2. Every rejection becomes documentable and defensible. The platform scales from CRPF to all central and state procurement — bringing consistency and auditability to India's ₹50–70 lakh crore annual procurement market.

SATYA (Systemic Automated Tender Yield Analyzer) is a multi-layer AI system that extracts tender criteria, evaluates bidder eligibility with explainable evidence, detects fraud signals, and ensures every procurement decision is audit-ready and RTI-compliant.The system operates as a 4-module pipeline:Criteria Intelligence Engine — Extracts and classifies all eligibility criteria from tender documents into a structured schema (financial, technical, compliance).Multi-Modal Document Parser — Processes all bidder submission formats including clean PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, and photographs using PaddleOCR and Vision AI.Explainable Evaluation Engine — Produces per-criterion verdicts (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review) with exact evidence citations — document name, page number, and extracted value.Cross-Bidder Risk & Fraud Signal Detector — Analyzes all bidders collectively to detect cartel patterns, duplicate GST numbers, and document similarity — invisible to manual review.Unlike traditional systems, SATYA never silently disqualifies a bidder. solutions.team_name: TenderKit — AI-powered eligibility evaluation for government procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T tenderkit
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderKit — AI-powered eligibility evaluation for government procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 01:48 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A document-intelligence platform that extracts tender requirements, parses bidder submissions in any format, evaluates eligibility with explainable evidence, detects fraud signals across bidders, and produces a fully auditable, RTI-ready procurement report — without silently disqualifying anyone.THE PROBLEMWhen a government body like CRPF issues a tender, evaluating whether each bidder actually meets the eligibility criteria is entirely manual. A committee reads through hundreds of pages per bidder — financial statements, tax certificates, experience letters, scanned copies of ISO certificates — and cross-checks each one against the tender requirements. It takes days, it is inconsistent across evaluators, and there is no structured audit trail. Two officers reviewing the same submission can reach different conclusions. Ambiguous cases either get passed through without scrutiny or rejected without explanation. And beyond individual bidder evaluation, there is a deeper problem that manual review cannot catch at all: coordinated fraud, cartel behaviour, duplicate registrations, and suspiciously similar documents submitted by bidders who appear unrelated. Neither inconsistency nor invisible fraud is acceptable in a formal procurement context.THE IDEATenderKit is built as a four-stage pipeline: extract and structure the tender criteria, parse every bidder document regardless of format, evaluate each bidder criterion by criterion with full evidence, and then analyse all bidders collectively to surface fraud signals that only become visible when you look across the entire submission pool at once.The process starts when the procurement officer uploads the tender document. TenderKit parses it — handling PDFs, Word files, and scanned copies through OCR — and passes the full text through an LLM to extract every eligibility criterion. Each criterion is tagged as mandatory or optional and classified by type: financial threshold, technical qualification, compliance document, or certification requirement. These structured criteria are stored in ChromaDB as vector embeddings, keyed to a unique tender ID, and serve as the ground truth for every evaluation that follows.When bidder submissions arrive, TenderKit handles every file format without treating any of them as a special case. Typed PDFs go through pdfplumber or pymupdf4llm, Word files through python-docx, and scanned images or photographs of physical certificates through PaddleOCR or AWS Textract. The output in every case is clean, readable text that the rest of the pipeline can work with. We then query ChromaDB using each bidder's extracted content to retrieve the most relevant criteria. Because this is vector-based semantic search rather than keyword matching, it handles natural variation in phrasing — a bidder writing "yearly revenue above five crore" and the tender saying "minimum annual turnover of ₹5 crore" correctly map to the same requirement.EVALUATION AND VERDICTSFor each criterion, the LLM receives the requirement from the tender alongside the relevant section from the bidder's documents. It returns one of three verdicts — eligible, not eligible, or needs human review — along with a confidence score and a short explanation citing the specific document, page number, and extracted value that drove the decision.When a bidder clearly meets a criterion — the turnover figure is present, readable, and above the threshold — the verdict is eligible with a confidence close to 1. When a required document is missing or a value clearly falls short, the verdict is not eligible, again with high confidence. The third case is the most important one: when the LLM cannot be sure — because a scanned certificate is too blurry to read reliably, because a figure is present but the context is ambiguous, or because a document only partially addresses the requirement — it returns a needs human review verdict with a low confidence score and an explicit explanation of what is uncertain. That case is never silently dropped. It is flagged, reasoned, and handed to the procurement officer with full context.This is the core design principle of TenderKit: the system is allowed to be uncertain, but it is never allowed to hide that uncertainty. Every borderline case surfaces with a reason.CROSS-BIDDER FRAUD AND RISK DETECTIONOnce individual evaluations are complete, TenderKit runs a second layer of analysis that looks across all bidders together — something a manual reviewer simply cannot do at scale. This layer checks for duplicate or shared GST numbers across different company submissions, unusually high document similarity between bidders who should be independent, pricing patterns consistent with cartel behaviour, and other signals that suggest coordination or fraud. These patterns are invisible when you review each submission in isolation but become clear the moment you compare them systematically.Any bidder or group of bidders that triggers a risk signal is flagged in the report with a clear description of what was detected and why it warrants attention. The system does not make accusations — it surfaces evidence and leaves the judgement to the officer.THE OUTPUTOnce all evaluations and risk checks are complete, TenderKit generates a consolidated Word report. It covers every bidder and every criterion — listing the verdict, the source document, the page number, the extracted value, and the LLM's reasoning for each one. Bidders flagged for human review are highlighted with the specific reason. Documents that were expected but not submitted are listed explicitly. Risk and fraud signals detected across bidders are summarised in a dedicated section. The report is structured to be RTI-compliant and ready to sign off — every automated decision traces back to something TenderKit actually read, and nothing in it is a black box. TenderKit does not replace the procurement officer. It makes every decision they take fully defensible.WHY THIS WORKS FOR GOVERNMENT PROCUREMENT SPECIFICALLYMost document automation tools assume clean, digital inputs. Real government procurement is the opposite — documents arrive scanned at odd angles, typed in inconsistent formats, and written in legal language that varies across bidders and regions. TenderKit is designed from the ground up for this messiness. The OCR layer handles poor quality scans, the semantic search layer handles language variation, the LLM is explicitly instructed to flag uncertainty rather than push through it, and the cross-bidder analysis layer catches coordinated fraud that no individual document review would ever surface. The audit trail is built in at every step — every verdict, every flag, and every risk signal is tied to a source, a page, and a value, making the entire procurement decision traceable and defensible long after the report is signed.TECH STACKChromaDBpdfplumber / pymupdf4llmPaddleOCR / AWS Textractpython-docxLLM — verdict and confidence scoringcross-bidder fraud signal detectionFastAPIRTI-compliant Word report generationFrontend- React

Pramaan AI
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 02:28 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Pramaan AI (प्रमाण AI) — Bringing Trust, Transparency, and Determinism to Government Tender EvaluationGovernment procurement systems like CRPF face a critical bottleneck: manual verification of tender compliance. Officers must sift through dense legal documents and inconsistent bidder submissions—ranging from clean PDFs to blurry scanned images. This process is slow, error-prone, and lacks auditability.While Generative AI promises automation, it introduces a dangerous tradeoff: hallucinations and black-box decisions are unacceptable in legal workflows.Pramaan AI solves this by redefining AI’s role—from decision-maker to verifiable compliance assistant.What We BuiltPramaan AI is a deterministic, explainable AI-powered compliance engine that transforms tender evaluation into a structured, transparent, and auditable workflow. 1. Intelligent Tender UnderstandingParses complex tender documents into structured JSON criteriaClassifies requirements into mandatory vs optionalEliminates ambiguity in interpretation 2. Multi-Format Document IntelligenceHandles PDFs, scans, and images seamlesslyUses Vision-Language Models (VLMs) instead of unreliable OCRAccurately extracts even tables, stamps, and low-quality scans3. Deterministic Evaluation EngineAI extracts values → Rule engine evaluates complianceExample: Extracts “₹6 Crore turnover” → compares against “≥ ₹5 Crore”Ensures zero hallucinated approvals/rejections4. Explain ability & Auditability (Game-Changer)Every decision is backed by visual proofHighlights exact evidence in documents using bounding boxesGenerates tamper-proof audit logs (SHA-256 hashed)Key InnovationsOCR-Free Visual RAG (ColPali) Reads documents as visual patches → works even on distorted scansZero Silent Rejections Confidence-based system: If confidence < 85% → “Needs Manual Review”Visual Grounding UI Officers don’t just see results—they see the exact proof instantlyWhy This MattersReduces tender evaluation time from days → minutesEliminates human bias & inconsistencyEnsures legal-grade transparency and trustKeeps humans in control, AI as an assistant—not a black boxScalable & Production-Ready ArchitectureFrontend: React + Tailwind (intuitive split-screen review UI)Backend: Node.js + Express (secure API & logging)AI Engine: Python + FastAPI + LangGraphLLMs: Gemini 1.5 Pro (multimodal reasoning)Database: PostgreSQL + pgvectorRisk-Aware by DesignNo blind AI decisions → rule-based verificationPoor-quality inputs → auto-flagged for human reviewEvery action → fully auditable and traceableVisionPramaan AI is not just a tool—it is a trust infrastructure for public procurement. By combining AI + determinism + visual proof, we aim to make government tendering:✔ Faster ✔ Fairer ✔ Fully transparentWhy Pramaan AI Stands Out InnovationMoves beyond generic LLM automation to a deterministic AI + rule-engine hybrid, eliminating hallucination risks in legal workflows Introduces OCR-free Visual RAG (ColPali) to extract information from complex, low-quality documents where traditional systems fail Implements visual grounding with bounding boxes, turning AI decisions into verifiable evidence instead of opaque outputs First-principles design: AI extracts → Code decides, ensuring reliability in high-stakes governance Not just smarter AI—safer AI for real-world decision systems. ImpactReduces tender evaluation time by 80–90%, accelerating public procurement cycles Minimizes human bias, clerical errors, and corruption risks through consistent rule-based evaluation Enables audit-ready governance with cryptographic logs—critical for disputes and compliance Scalable across all government departments (not just CRPF) and even private procurement systems Transforms procurement from a bottleneck into a transparent, high-trust pipeline.FeasibilityBuilt using production-ready, widely adopted technologies (FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Gemini) Modular microservice architecture ensures easy scaling and integration with existing systems Clear implementation roadmap (4-week phased plan already defined) Human-in-the-loop design ensures practical deployability without regulatory resistance Not a concept—this is deployable within real government workflows.“While others use AI to automate decisions, Pramaan AI makes decisions provable.”In high-stakes systems like government procurement, accuracy is not enough—trust is mandatory. Pramaan AI is designed not just to be intelligent, but to be provably correct, auditable, and accountable.

S sneha7259
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderMind AI
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 05:26 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemKarnataka's government procurement involves thousands of tenders annually across departments — police, infrastructure, health, IT, and more. Every tender evaluation is governed by the KTPP Act, 1999 and GFR 2017. Yet the core of this process — "evaluating bidder eligibility" — is still done manually, by overwhelmed committees, on paper.A single tender can attract 40 bidders, each submitting 45+ pages of documents. That's 1,800+ pages for a committee to read, cross-check, score, and record — within 30–45 days. The result is chronic failure across six dimensions: 6 Critical Problems in Government Tender Work1. Evaluation Inconsistency — Two officers reading the same documents reach different verdicts. One accepts a CA-certified turnover letter; another demands an audited balance sheet. No standardization exists, and decisions depend on who's in the room, not what the law says.2. Document Overload — 1,000+ pages per tender, evaluated manually under strict legal deadlines that are routinely missed — triggering grievance challenges and project delays.3. Silent Disqualification — Bidders are rejected without documented, criterion-level reasons. When challenged at the Karnataka High Court, the government cannot defend its decisions. The outcome: costly legal reversals and mandatory re-tendering.4. Corruption & Bid Rigging — Manual evaluation enables criteria manipulation, document forgery, L1 manipulation, and insider leaks. Because evaluation records are narrative and unstructured, these manipulations leave no detectable trace.5. CAG Audit Failures — Every procurement decision must be traceable to a criterion, a document, and an officer. Manual narrative reports cannot meet this standard. Karnataka departments face repeated CAG audit objections for missing criterion-level evaluation records.
Solution: Upload a tender. Upload bidder documents. Get a ranked, scored, criterion-by-criterion evaluation report in minutes — not weeks.TenderMind AI is a purpose-built, AI-powered evaluation platform that understands the legal, technical, and financial language of Indian government tenders.Upload a tender. Upload bidder documents. Get a ranked, scored, criterion-by-criterion evaluation report in minutes — not weeks.How it works:Officer uploads the NIT/RFP (any format — PDF, Word, scanned)AI auto-extracts all eligibility criteria with mandatory/optional flagsOfficer confirms or edits criteria before evaluation beginsBidders upload documents; AI classifies each document on uploadOCR + LLM pipeline reads all documents — typed or scanned — and extracts criterion-relevant valuesAI scores every bidder against every criterion, flags ambiguous cases for human reviewGenerates a ranked bidder list and audit-ready report with source references and L1 identificationThree-verdict system per criterion: ELIGIBLE (AI confidence ≥85%) → auto-scored | MANUAL REVIEW (70–84%) → flagged to officer with evidence | NOT ELIGIBLE (≥85%, criterion failed) → auto-rejection report generated."Zero silent disqualifications". Every rejected bidder gets a structured report: criterion failed + document evaluated + value found vs. value required. This pre-empts litigation and satisfies KTPP Act transparency requirements.﻿Why AI — and Why Nothing Else WorksKarnataka's e-Procurement portal already digitizes bid submission. But digitization ≠ intelligence. The portal cannot read a scanned GST certificate and verify it matches the bidder's PAN. It cannot calculate whether a project value meets the minimum turnover threshold. It cannot cross-reference figures across two documents. Only AI can do this at scale.ProblemTenderMind AI SolutionInconsistent evaluationSame logic applied to every bidder, every timeDocument overload60-page submission processed in under 30 secondsSilent disqualificationAuto-generated criterion-level rejection reportsCorruption riskDeterministic scoring with tamper-evident audit logsCAG audit failuresEvery decision traceable to criterion + document + timestampGovernance delaysWeeks of evaluation compressed to hours﻿Tech StackBuilt on microservices, deployable on KSDC / NIC Cloud:Ingestion: FastAPI + MinIO + ClamAVDocument Intelligence: Tesseract OCR + Azure AI Doc Intelligence + IndicBERT (Kannada/Hindi/English)AI Reasoning: Fine-tuned LLM + LangChain + RAG pipelineEvaluation Engine: Python rules engine + PostgreSQL + RedisAudit Layer: React.js dashboard + DSC-signed PDF export + immutable audit logImpact and SecurityTenderMind AI doesn't just speed up procurement — it structurally fixes it. Evaluation time drops from weeks to hours. Litigation risk drops to near-zero. CAG compliance becomes automatic. Corruption loses its entry points. And public services get delivered on time. This is Smart Government Procurement for Karnataka — purpose-built, legally compliant, and ready to prototype. How the Public Can Trust TenderMind AI: Every evaluation decision is locked into an immutable, tamper-proof audit log — no one can alter it after the fact, not even system administrators. Every rejected bidder receives a written, criterion-level reason — no silent disqualifications, ever. All override decisions by officers are dual-approved and permanently recorded. CAG and Lokayukta auditors have independent read-only access to the entire evaluation trail at any time. And every final report is digitally signed, making it mathematically verifiable in court. The system doesn't ask the public to trust people — it makes the process itself trustworthy.
