solutions.team_name: Pramana AI: Evidence-Grounded Eligibility Evaluator
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B Boys
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Pramana AI: Evidence-Grounded Eligibility Evaluator
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 05:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Core Bottleneck & Security ImperativeGovernment procurement in defense and paramilitary organizations (CRPF) is a high-stakes, legally binding process. Procurement officers are tasked with evaluating massive volumes of heterogeneous bidder submissions—ranging from structured digital PDFs to blurry photographs of physical certificates—against strict tender criteria.The primary challenge is twofold: First, manual evaluation is prone to human fatigue, leading to inconsistencies and oversight. Second, deploying standard, cloud-based AI to automate this process introduces unacceptable data sovereignty risks (by transmitting sensitive financial/PII data to third-party servers) and legal risks (through "black-box" AI hallucinations). A compliant solution must be entirely air-gapped, explainable, and deterministic.2. Proposed Innovation: The "Extraction vs. Evaluation" ArchitecturePramana AI (derived from the Kannada word for Proof/Evidence) solves the hallucination problem by strictly separating text extraction from mathematical evaluation. Large Language Models are excellent at reading text but terrible at deterministic math.The AI Extracts: A locally hosted, open-weight LLM reads the unstructured bidder documents to extract raw values (e.g., retrieving the string "₹4.5 Crore" from a scanned tax document).The Code Evaluates: Hard-coded, deterministic Python logic handles the actual threshold comparisons (e.g., verifying if 4.5 >= 5.0).By ensuring the AI never calculates the final verdict itself, Pramana AI guarantees 100% mathematical accuracy and legal compliance, eliminating the risk of a model hallucinating a passing grade for an unqualified bidder.3. End-to-End WorkflowThe pipeline operates in four strict stages designed to process "dirty data" without relying on external APIs:Phase 1: Tender Deconstruction: The system ingests the authoritative CRPF tender document. It uses NLP to extract and categorize all eligibility criteria into a strict JSON schema (Technical, Financial, Compliance), explicitly flagging rules as Mandatory or Optional. A procurement officer confirms this baseline schema.Phase 2: Multi-Modal Bidder Ingestion: Bidder documents are dynamically routed based on format. Digital PDFs undergo direct text extraction (PyPDF), while physical scans, images, and tables are processed through local Optical Character Recognition (Tesseract OCR) to normalize the text.Phase 3: RAG-Grounded Evidence Matching: The system creates a local FAISS vector database for each bidder submission. When evaluating a criterion, it mathematically retrieves only the most relevant paragraphs. The LLM is forced to extract answers strictly from these retrieved chunks, ensuring all data is explicitly grounded in the bidder's own submission.Phase 4: The Explainability Dashboard: The backend generates a strict JSON array of verdicts ("Eligible," "Not Eligible," or "Needs Review") which is pushed to an interactive frontend UI.4. Non-Negotiables: Human-in-the-Loop & AuditabilityPramana AI acts as an evidence-gathering "Digital Co-Pilot," not an autonomous operator.No Silent Disqualification: If a scanned document is too blurry (OCR confidence Hyperlinked Audit Trails: Every automated "Eligible/Not Eligible" verdict is hyperlinked directly to the source document. When an officer clicks a verdict, the UI displays a side-by-side view highlighting the exact page, paragraph, and extracted text used to make that decision, ensuring complete administrative transparency.5. Technology Stack (Air-Gapped & Zero-Trust)This stack is specifically chosen to run entirely on local hardware, meeting strict government data privacy requirements:AI/LLM Engine: Llama 3.1 (8B) running locally via Ollama.Orchestration: LangChain for RAG workflows and Pydantic for strict JSON output parsing.Vector Store & Embeddings: FAISS and sentence-transformers for high-speed local semantic search.Document Processing: Tesseract OCR (Scans/Images) and PyPDF (Digital Text).Frontend UI: Streamlit for deploying the interactive Procurement Officer Dashboard and immutable audit reports.

solutions.team_name: NirnayAI : AI-powered tender evaluation for fast, fair, and auditable government decisions
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


V VSquare
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NirnayAI : AI-powered tender evaluation for fast, fair, and auditable government decisions
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 10:41 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Statement and Need Government procurement, such as tenders issued by organizations like Central Reserve Police Force, is heavily document-driven, where eligibility criteria are deeply embedded in lengthy, unstructured legal text. Evaluators must manually interpret these criteria—financial, experiential, and compliance-based—and verify them against highly inconsistent bidder submissions (scanned images, PDFs, varied formats), making the process slow and cognitively demanding. The lack of standardization forces subjective judgment calls, especially in ambiguous cases (e.g., unclear scans or mismatched financial formats), leading to inconsistencies across evaluators. This not only delays evaluations (often 3–5 days per tender) but also weakens auditability, increasing risks of disputes, complaints, and allegations of bias or corruption. At its core, the task is structured reasoning over unstructured data—reading, extracting, comparing, and justifying decisions—which is currently done manually despite being well-suited for systematic, traceable automation.

Solution Overview NirnayAI structures the evaluation process into a three-stage intelligent pipeline—understanding the tender, interpreting bidder submissions, and generating explainable decisions—each directly addressing a core inefficiency in traditional procurement workflows. Stage 1 — Tender Intelligence: The system uses an LLM to read the tender contextually and extract a structured criteria registry, classifying conditions (financial, technical, compliance, documentation) and distinguishing mandatory vs. optional based on legal language cues. Each criterion is stored with source text, category, and a formal rule, with a human-in-the-loop review to ensure control over rule definition. Stage 2 — Bidder Document Parsing: A multi-format ingestion pipeline processes PDFs, scans, and Word files using OCR and layout-aware parsing, followed by an AI layer that builds an evidence map linking extracted values to exact document locations. This enables consistent interpretation despite variations in format or presentation. Stage 3 — Criterion-Level Evaluation and Reporting: The system evaluates each bidder against every criterion, producing explainable outputs with evidence, extracted values, and reasoning. It supports three outcomes—Eligible, Not Eligible, and Needs Manual Review—to explicitly handle ambiguity, ensuring human oversight where needed while maintaining a complete, auditable decision trail.

Comprehensive Feature Set 1. Automated Criteria Extraction — structured registry, automatically converts unstructured tender text into clearly defined, editable eligibility rules with categories and thresholds. 2. Multi-Format Document Ingestion — all formats, processes PDFs, scans, images, Word, and Excel files seamlessly through format-specific pipelines. 3. OCR with Confidence Scoring — accuracy flags, assigns confidence levels to extracted text and flags low-quality or uncertain data for review. 4. Evidence Mapping — source traceability, links every extracted value to its exact document source (file, page, section) for easy verification. 5. Three-Tier Verdict System — clear outcomes, classifies results as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review to handle ambiguity safely. 6. Criterion-Level Explainability — granular insights, provides detailed reasoning for each criterion rather than just overall bidder results. 7. Human-in-the-Loop Review Interface — manual validation, allows officers to review flagged cases with full context and override decisions when needed. 8. Immutable Audit Log — full traceability, records every action and decision in a tamper-proof, append-only system for accountability. 9. Consolidated Procurement Report — exportable summary, generates a complete, structured, and audit-ready report for official use. 10. Role-Based Access Control — user roles, ensures secure access by assigning permissions based on user responsibilities.

System Architecture and Workflow Tier 1 — Document Ingestion Layer: Uploaded files are routed by format — typed PDFs through text extraction, scanned documents and photos through confidence-scored OCR, and Word/Excel files through structural parsers — with every output stored alongside its source file, page number, and confidence score. Tier 2 — Tender Intelligence Layer: The tender text is processed by an LLM that extracts a structured criteria registry, tagging each criterion by type, mandatory/optional status, threshold value, and source sentence, which an officer reviews and confirms before evaluation begins. Tier 3 — Bidder Evidence Extraction Layer: For each bidder, the LLM reads across all submitted documents and builds an evidence map recording what value was found for each criterion, in which document, on which page, and with what confidence — handling variation in language and presentation automatically. Tier 4 — Evaluation and Verdict Engine: Each criterion is matched against the bidder's evidence map to produce a three-tier verdict — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — with full reasoning recorded and any low-confidence, borderline, or contradictory cases automatically escalated rather than silently decided. Tier 5 — Human Review and Override Layer: Flagged cases are presented to the officer with the criterion, extracted evidence, confidence level, and source document side by side; every officer decision is recorded with timestamp and user identity as an immutable audit entry. Tier 6 — Reporting and Audit Layer: The consolidated report is assembled with all verdicts, evidence references, and officer decisions, backed by an append-only audit database that records every automated and human action and cannot be modified at any level.

Technology Stack 1. Document Ingestion & OCR (Tesseract OCR, AWS Textract / Google Vision) — layout-aware extraction, high accuracy. 2. Core AI / Language Understanding (Claude API / GPT-4 via Azure OpenAI) — contextual reasoning, legal text. 3. Backend (Python + FastAPI) — async, scalable APIs. 4. Document Processing Pipeline (LangChain, PyMuPDF, python-docx) — structured parsing, orchestration. 5. Database (PostgreSQL) — relational, audit integrity. 6. Frontend (React + TypeScript) — maintainable, structured UI. 7. Infrastructure (Docker, NIC MeghRaj / AWS GovCloud) — secure, portable deployment.

Unique Differentiators 1. Language-Aware Understanding — interprets meaning beyond rigid templates. 2. Three-Tier Verdict System — avoids risky binary-only automated decisions. 3. Built-in Immutable Audit Trail — tamper-proof logging at database level. 4. India-Specific Document Handling — optimized for local formats and scripts. 5. Transparent Explainability — shows reasoning, not black-box outputs.

Challenges and Impact Challenge 1 — Poor Quality Scanned Documents Strategy — Confidence-based OCR with manual escalation. Impact — Prevents errors and ensures reliable evaluations. Challenge 2 — Ambiguous Tender Language Strategy — Pre-evaluation human clarification of criteria. Impact — Improves consistency and reduces misinterpretation. Challenge 3 — Data Privacy & Security Strategy — On-premise or configurable AI deployment. Impact — Ensures data sovereignty and regulatory compliance. Challenge 4 — Resistance to Adoption Strategy — Human-in-the-loop decision support design. Impact — Builds trust and enhances officer productivity. Challenge 5 — Legal Admissibility Strategy — AI outputs as recommendations with audit logs. Impact — Enables legally defensible and transparent decisions.

Scenario After Project Adoption Before NirnayAI, tender evaluation involved multiple officers manually reviewing lengthy documents, leading to 3–5 day timelines, inconsistent judgments, repeated reviews, and weak audit trails with limited reasoning. After adoption, the process becomes streamlined—criteria are auto-extracted, bidder documents are analyzed within hours, and officers review only flagged cases with full evidence on a dashboard. Decision-making shifts from manual reading to structured validation, reducing effort to a few hours over 1–2 days. The system ensures consistency by evaluating all bidders uniformly against every criterion, eliminating missed checks and subjective variation. It produces audit-ready, explainable reports instantly, making decisions legally defensible. Overall, NirnayAI delivers faster evaluations, higher accuracy, scalability, and significantly improved transparency in procurement.

For More Understanding of the Idea and Visual Representation : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1didG6NjIW679qalxrmlF0kqkCHbykQEm/view A simple prototype to demonstrate the proposed solution : https://nirnay-clarity-aid.lovable.app
"From paper trails to proof trails — NirnayAI turns tender decisions into decisions you can defend."

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


R r9427173766_af96
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderLens — AI-Powered Tender Eligibility Evaluation & Audit Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 19, 2026 11:49 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemGovernment procurement evaluation is manual, inconsistent, and slow. For a single tender, a committee spends days cross-checking hundreds of pages of bidder documents against eligibility criteria — and two evaluators can reach different conclusions from the same submission. Scanned certificates, varied document formats, and legally dense language make the problem worse. There is no audit trail, no consistency guarantee, and no scalable path forward.﻿Our SolutionTenderLens is an AI-powered platform that ingests a tender document and a set of bidder submissions, automatically extracts eligibility criteria, evaluates each bidder against those criteria, and produces an explainable, auditable evaluation report — ready for a procurement officer to review and sign off.﻿How It WorksStep 1 — Tender Understanding The platform parses the tender document using an LLM-based extraction pipeline. It identifies and structures every eligibility criterion — financial thresholds (e.g. minimum turnover), technical requirements (e.g. past project experience), compliance conditions (e.g. GST registration), and certification requirements (e.g. ISO 9001). Each criterion is tagged as Mandatory or Optional and stored in a structured schema that drives the downstream evaluation.Step 2 — Bidder Document Ingestion Bidder submissions arrive in any format — typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word documents, tables, or photographs of physical certificates. The platform runs each document through an OCR layer (for scanned/image inputs) followed by a document understanding model that extracts the values and evidence relevant to each criterion. Variation in how bidders present the same information is handled through semantic matching rather than rigid templates.Step 3 — Criterion-Level Evaluation For each bidder, the platform evaluates every criterion independently. Each verdict — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — is backed by a structured evidence record: which criterion was checked, which document was used, what value was extracted, and why the verdict was reached. No bidder is silently disqualified. Any case where confidence falls below a defined threshold is automatically routed to a human reviewer with the specific reason flagged.Step 4 — Consolidated Report & Audit Trail The platform generates a consolidated evaluation report listing all bidders, their per-criterion verdicts, and the evidence behind each decision. Every automated decision is logged with a timestamp, confidence score, and source reference — making the full evaluation auditable end-to-end and suitable for formal government procurement decisions﻿What Makes This DifferentMost document AI tools extract information. TenderLens goes further — it evaluates, explains, and escalates. The human reviewer is not a fallback; they are a first-class participant. Every ambiguous case is surfaced with context, not buried. The output is not a data dump — it is a decision-ready report that a procurement officer can trust and defend.

TenderSaarthi AI: An AI-Powered Tender Eligibility and Evaluation Assistant for Smarter Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 01:08 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderSaarthi AI is an AI-based platform built to make government tenders easier to understand, evaluate, and act on. Many businesses, especially MSMEs and small enterprises, struggle with tender documents because they are long, technical, and full of complex eligibility and compliance conditions. As a result, even capable businesses may miss good opportunities simply because the process is confusing and time consuming.Our idea is to build a smart assistant that can read tender documents, extract the important requirements, and help businesses quickly know whether they are eligible to apply. Instead of manually going through every clause, the user can upload a tender document and get a clear breakdown of key details such as eligibility criteria, turnover requirements, prior experience, technical conditions, certifications, EMD details, deadlines, and possible disqualification factors.TenderSaarthi AI will then compare these requirements with the business profile of the applicant and generate a simple eligibility analysis. It can highlight missing documents, flag risky clauses, summarize difficult sections in simpler language, and help the user understand whether they should proceed with the tender or not. This saves time, reduces mistakes, and makes the bidding process much more accessible.The main goal of the platform is not just automation, but better decision-making. It helps businesses avoid wasting effort on tenders they are unlikely to qualify for, while also helping them prepare better for tenders that match their profile. For smaller companies that do not have legal experts or dedicated bid teams, this can make a major difference.To make the solution more inclusive, TenderSaarthi AI can also provide simplified explanations and multilingual support, so that tender information becomes easier to understand for a wider range of users. In this way, the platform can improve participation, reduce confusion, and support a more transparent and efficient procurement ecosystem.Overall, TenderSaarthi AI is a practical AI solution for a real government procurement problem. It combines document intelligence, eligibility analysis, and user friendly guidance to help businesses make faster, smarter, and more confident bidding decisions.

solutions.team_name: TenderVerify AI: Agentic Multi-Modal Framework for Explainable Procurement Audit
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


J Jai_Hind
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderVerify AI: Agentic Multi-Modal Framework for Explainable Procurement Audit
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 01:31 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding: The "Gov-Procure" RealityIn the context of the CRPF, tender evaluation is not just a data-matching task; it is a legal and administrative safeguard. The primary challenges are:Semantic Complexity: Eligibility criteria use "Legalese" where mandatory (shall/must) vs. directory (may/should) clauses are critical.Data Heterogeneity: Bidders often provide non-standardized evidence (e.g., a handwritten experience letter vs. a digital GST certificate).Accountability: An AI "black box" is unacceptable. Every decision must have a "paper trail" to prevent litigation and ensure transparency in government spending.2. Proposed Architecture: The Agentic WorkflowWe propose a multi-agent system built on a Vision-Language RAG pipeline. Instead of one large model doing everything, we use specialized AI agents.Agent A (The Analyst): Extracts and structures eligibility criteria into a dynamic JSON schema.Agent B (The Visionary): Uses Multi-modal LLMs to parse scanned images/PDFs and extract relevant entities.Agent C (The Auditor): Compares Agent B’s findings against Agent A’s criteria and flags discrepancies.3. Technical ApproachA. Tender Intelligence (Understanding the Rulebook)We use Hierarchical PDF Parsing to maintain the document structure (headings, sub-clauses).Model: Gemini 1.5 Pro or GPT-4o (due to high context windows).Logic: The system classifies criteria into:Technical: (e.g., "3 years experience in construction").Financial: (e.g., "Turnover > ₹5Cr").Compliance: (e.g., "Valid ISO 9001").Output: A Structured Criteria Map where each requirement is assigned a "Mandatory" flag based on linguistic cues.B. Heterogeneous Document Processing (The OCR+VLM Layer)To handle "photographs and scans," we bypass traditional OCR-to-text (which loses formatting) and use Layout-Aware Extraction.Tech Stack: DocTR (Document Text Recognition) or Azure Document Intelligence for spatial anchoring.VLM Integration: We use Vision-Language Models to "see" certificates. If a GST certificate is a photo, the VLM identifies the GSTIN and the validity date directly from the image pixels.C. Explainable Evaluation & Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)Evidence Mapping: For every "Eligible" verdict, the system generates a Source Citation. (e.g., "Criterion 4.1 met. Reference: 'Experience_Letter.pdf', Page 2, Paragraph 3").Ambiguity Trigger: We use Confidence Scoring. If the VLM's confidence in reading a blurry scanned figure is below 85%, the system does not disqualify. It generates a "Flag for Manual Review" with a reason: "Inconclusive reading of turnover figures due to document blur."4. Auditability and Risk ManagementEnd-to-End Audit Trail: Every step—from extraction to the final verdict—is logged in a version-controlled database. Any human override is recorded with a mandatory justification field.Risk Mitigation:Hallucination Risk: We use Self-Correction loops where Agent C cross-checks Agent B's extracted values against the raw text chunks to ensure factual alignment.Format Inconsistency: Using Few-shot prompting with diverse Indian government document templates (GST, ITR, MSME certificates) to improve extraction accuracy.5. Round 2 Implementation PlanPhase 1 (Data Pre-processing): Build a pipeline using PyMuPDF and EasyOCR/Tesseract for initial text/image segregation.Phase 2 (Schema Definition): Develop a system to convert "CRPF Tender Language" into a standardized JSON Eligibility Template.Phase 3 (Agent Development): Implement the multi-agent framework using LangGraph or CrewAI to manage the flow between extraction and verification.Phase 4 (UI/Dashboard): Create a Streamlit/React dashboard for procurement officers to view the "Consolidated Evaluation Report" with side-by-side document comparisons.6. Key Technology ChoicesLLM/VLM: Gemini 1.5 Pro (Native multi-modality for scans).Vector Database: ChromaDB or Pinecone for storing document embeddings for RAG.Framework: LangChain for orchestration.Document Analysis: LayoutLMv3 for understanding the spatial relationship in tables and forms.

B BidLens
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
BidLens
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 08:34 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
BidLens is an AI-native procurement evaluation platform designed to eliminate fraud, inconsistency, and human error from government tender evaluations. Built specifically for the CRPF and Government of Karnataka procurement use case, BidLens replaces weeks of manual document review with an intelligent, multi-stage verification pipeline.The platform features a stateful AI agent the Tender Agent, that reads tender documents, extracts eligibility criteria, parses bidder submissions of any format (typed PDFs, scanned documents, photographs), cross-verifies claims against public government portals, and produces explainable criterion-level verdicts before handing off to human reviewers.A three-tier human review process (AI → L1 Technical Evaluator → L2 Senior Officer) ensures every automated decision is human-validated before a tender is allocated or rejected. An immutable audit trail underpins every decision for government compliance.Key Metrics BidLens Targets80%+ reduction in manual evaluation time per tender100% criterion coverage no eligibility condition silently skipped0 silent disqualifications every rejection traceable to source document + criterion3-tier review ensuring no tender allocated without senior human sign-offFull audit trail exportable as signed PDF report per tender

M mharikumar2007_54a4
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TEVA 2.0
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 10:35 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TEVA 2.0 ---> Truth-First Intelligent Vendor Shortlisting for CRPF ProcurementTheme 3 — AI-Based Tender Evaluation & Eligibility AnalysisPROTYPE LINK :    https://teva2.netlify.app/  LINK HERE ﻿﻿1. ProblemCRPF procurement committees manually review bids — slow, inconsistent, and easily manipulated. Forged certificates pass undetected. Cartel bids go unnoticed. Every competing team will build:PDF → OCR → NLP → Pass/Fail (This solves the wrong problem)﻿2. Core Insight“The PDF is not the answer. It is the KEY to an independent investigation.”TEVA extracts identifiers (CIN, GST No., project locations, equipment registration) from submitted documents, then uses these as keys to independently verify reality from sources the bidder cannot control or fabricate.﻿3. Truth Stack Architecture — Four Evidence Layers﻿LayerNameWeightData SourcesLayer 1 — Most TrustedGround Truth25%Satellite imagery · IoT/FASTag · Public recordsLayer 2Behavioural30%Financial DNA · Narrative intelligence · PatternsLayer 3Registry30%MCA21 · GSTN · VAHAN · CPPP · ROC · EPFOLayer 4. — Least TrustedDocuments15%PDFs · Scans · Photos · Word files﻿﻿4. System FlowStepAction1Upload Tender PDF → GPT-4o extracts mandatory & desirable criteria2Upload Bidder Submissions (any format: typed PDF, scan, photo, Word)3Extract identifiers: CIN, GST No., Project Sites, Equipment Reg. Numbers4Truth Stack Agents fire in parallel across all 4 layers per bidder5Neo4j Cartel Detection — director network mapped across ALL bidders6Composite Score 0–100 per bidder → Ranked Shortlist generated7Officer Dashboard: review flags, acknowledge risks, sign-off8Decision anchored to Hyperledger Fabric — immutable blockchain audit﻿﻿5. Five Unique Innovations5.1 Reverse Verification EngineBidder claims a highway project (Rajasthan, 2022). TEVA queries Google Earth Engine + ISRO Bhuvan — before/after satellite imagery confirms whether construction actually occurred. A forged letter cannot fake a satellite image.5.2 Financial DNA FingerprintingClaimed turnover vs actual GST e-invoice volume. TDS deductions reveal real payroll. ROC filing gaps signal stress. Example: Bidder claims Rs.7.2 Cr; GST invoices show Rs.2.1 Cr → Claim-Reality Ratio 3.4x → FRAUD FLAG.5.3 Cartel & Fraud Graph DetectionMCA21 API extracts all directors for every bidder. Neo4j maps the complete ownership network across all bidders simultaneously. Shared director + shared address + shared CA firm = Cartel Flag raised automatically.5.4 IoT Asset VerificationBidder claims 10 JCBs. VAHAN confirms registration. FASTag transaction history confirms whether those machines actually moved on Indian highways in the last 24 months. Paper ownership ≠ operational capacity.5.5 Predictive Delivery Risk ModelXGBoost classifier trained on historical GeM contract outcomes. Predicts delivery failure before contract is awarded. Features: company age, project/company size ratio, simultaneous contract load, geographic distance, past penalty clauses﻿﻿8. TEVA 2.0 vs Other ApproachesManual procurement takes 3–5 days per tender and outputs a pass/fail decision based entirely on submitted paperwork — no fraud detection, no delivery prediction, no cartel awareness. Every other AI team will produce the same outcome faster using OCR and NLP. TEVA 2.0 is categorically different: it delivers a ranked composite score for every bidder, validates turnover via GST DNA instead of reading a balance sheet, confirms project existence via satellite instead of accepting an experience letter, detects cartel manipulation via a director ownership graph, predicts delivery risk via an ML model trained on real contract outcomes, scores human factors via EPF and Glassdoor, and produces a blockchain-anchored audit trail. Time per tender: minutes.9. Technology StackGPT-4o with function calling handles structured criteria extraction and grounded explanation — function calling eliminates parsing errors and grounding checks prevent hallucination. Google Earth Engine and ISRO Bhuvan provide satellite imagery with change detection; ISRO is used for data sovereignty in CRPF security contexts. Neo4j provides native graph traversal for director and cartel network analysis. XGBoost is chosen for the delivery risk model due to its performance on tabular data with missing features and interpretable feature importance. MCA21, GSTN, VAHAN, CPPP, and EPFO are authoritative government APIs producing legally defensible evidence. Google Cloud Vision and Tesseract 5.x handle OCR — GCV online, Tesseract offline for air-gapped CRPF environments. Hyperledger Fabric is a permissioned blockchain with no public exposure of procurement data. FastAPI with Celery and Redis runs asynchronous parallel Truth Stack agents with real-time progress tracking.10. SDG AlignmentSDG 16 (Strong Institutions): Blockchain-anchored, explainable shortlisting with independently verifiable evidence reduces corruption in CRPF procurement. SDG 9 (Innovation & Infrastructure): Satellite-verified experience ensures infrastructure contracts reach vendors with proven delivery capacity. SDG 8 (Decent Work): The Human Factor Engine rewards EPF-compliant vendors and flags those with worker exploitation signals. SDG 17 (Partnerships): Integration across MCA21, GSTN, VAHAN, EPFO, and CPPP creates a replicable public-data model scalable to all government procurement across India.Core Principle“Every other team reads the documents bidders give them. TEVA 2.0 uses those documents as keys to independently investigate reality — then ranks, scores, and shortlists the most genuinely capable vendors with evidence that satellites, government APIs, and ground truth independently confirm

solutions.team_name: Grove Apex - A solution that transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S saadsyed950_0be8
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Grove Apex - A solution that transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 12:05 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
1. Problem Understanding
Government tender evaluation is currently a manual, time-intensive, and inconsistent process. Procurement officers are required to analyze large tender documents and verify bidder submissions across multiple formats such as PDFs, scanned documents, images, and structured files.This process presents several challenges:



Unstructured and heterogeneous data: Tender requirements and bidder documents vary significantly in format and structure.

Manual verification: Officers must cross-check each criterion (e.g., turnover, certifications) against multiple documents.

Inconsistency and human error: Different evaluators may interpret the same data differently.

Lack of auditability: Decisions are difficult to trace back to specific evidence.
As a result, the process is slow, error-prone, and difficult to scale. There is a need for an automated, explainable, and auditable system that assists decision-making while keeping humans in control.

2. Proposed Solution
We propose an AI-powered tender evaluation system that automates the extraction, analysis, and evaluation of tender and bidder documents.

End-to-End Flow:

Upload tender document and bidder submissions

Extract eligibility criteria from tender

Process bidder documents (OCR + parsing)

Map criteria to supporting evidence

Evaluate eligibility (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review)

Generate explainable results with audit trail

Enable human review for ambiguous cases

The system acts as a decision-support tool, not a replacement, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and scalability.

3. System Architecture
The system is designed as a modular pipeline:


Document Ingestion Layer: Handles upload and storage of tender and bidder files

OCR & Parsing Engine: Extracts text from PDFs, scanned images, and documents

LLM Processing Layer: Extracts structured criteria and key values

Retrieval (RAG) Layer: Maps extracted criteria to relevant document evidence

Evaluation Engine: Applies rules to determine eligibility

Explainability Layer: Generates human-readable justifications

Review Dashboard: Enables human-in-the-loop validation

Audit Layer: Logs all actions and decisions


This modular design ensures scalability, maintainability, and flexibility.

4. Criteria Extraction Approach
The system uses LLMs with structured prompting to extract eligibility criteria from tender documents.

Extracted Information:

Financial: Turnover, Net Worth

Compliance: GST, ISO certifications

Technical: Experience, project requirements

Classification:

Mandatory criteria

Optional criteria
Output is stored in structured format (e.g., JSON), enabling downstream processing.

5. Document Understanding Approach
The system processes bidder documents using a combination of:


OCR (Optical Character Recognition) for scanned and image-based inputs

Text parsing for structured documents

Extracted Data:

Numerical values (turnover, financials)

Dates (validity of certificates)

Identifiers (GSTIN, certification IDs)
To handle variability, the system uses semantic embeddings for flexible matching across formats.

6. Evaluation Logic
The evaluation engine compares extracted bidder data against tender criteria.

Output Categories:

Eligible → All mandatory criteria satisfied

Not Eligible → One or more mandatory criteria failed

Needs Review → Ambiguous or uncertain data

Example:

Required: ₹5 Cr turnover

Found: ₹6.2 Cr → Eligible

7. Explainability
Every decision is fully explainable and traceable.For each criterion, the system provides:



Criterion description

Source document reference

Extracted value

Reason for decision

Example:
“Turnover requirement ₹5 Cr. Found ₹6.2 Cr in Balance Sheet (Page 10). Hence Eligible.”This ensures trust and usability in real-world procurement workflows.


8. Handling Ambiguity
The system explicitly detects and handles ambiguous cases, such as:


Missing data

Low-confidence OCR extraction

Conflicting values across documents
Instead of making assumptions, the system:


Flags such cases as “Needs Review”

Provides reasons for uncertainty
This prevents incorrect decisions and ensures reliability.

9. Human-in-the-loop Design
A review dashboard allows procurement officers to:


Inspect extracted data and decisions

View supporting evidence

Approve, reject, or modify results
Reviewer actions are recorded and can be used to improve system performance over time.

10. Auditability
The system maintains a complete audit trail of:


Extracted data

Decision logic

Source documents

User actions
This ensures:


Transparency

Accountability

Compliance with government standards

11. Handling Non-Negotiables
The solution adheres to all constraints:


Works with synthetic or redacted data

Avoids hallucination by grounding decisions in document evidence

Ensures explainability for every decision

Never silently rejects case, ambiguous cases are flagged for review

12. Technology Stack

Backend: FastAPI for AI and Express for Business Logic

Frontend: React.js (vite)

OCR: Gemini Vision

AI/LLM: Gemini

Database: MongoDB

Vector Database: Chroma

Cloud: Railway

13. Risks & Trade-offs

OCR inaccuracies → Mitigated via confidence scoring and review

Ambiguity in documents → Handled via human-in-the-loop

LLM hallucination → Prevented via evidence grounding

Performance vs accuracy → Balanced using hybrid rule-based + AI approach
This solution transforms tender evaluation from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, explainable, and auditable system, enabling faster and more reliable procurement decisions.

Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:03 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
KarnaNyaya+ is an advanced AI-driven framework designed to bridge the critical gap between court judgments and their real-world execution in India’s judicial ecosystem. While existing systems such as CIS and CCMS effectively provide case information and status updates, they lack mechanisms to ensure timely compliance after a judgment is delivered. KarnaNyaya+ addresses this limitation by transforming unstructured legal documents into structured, actionable insights using technologies like OCR, Named Entity Recognition (NER), and rhetorical role labeling. It extracts key directives, identifies responsible departments, and organizes cases based on legal context.A key innovation is its “Comply or Appeal” decision intelligence system, which enables officials to make faster and more informed legal decisions. It also incorporates automated deadline tracking, workflow assignment, and escalation mechanisms to minimize delays and legal risks. Human-in-the-loop verification ensures transparency, accountability, and accuracy in every step. Additionally, dashboards and analytics provide real-time insights into compliance rates and administrative efficiency. Overall, KarnaNyaya+ transforms the judiciary from a passive information system into an active, execution-driven ecosystem, enabling faster, smarter, and more accountable justice delivery across India.

solutions.team_name: CertiProcure AI: Blockchain-Anchored Multi-Modal Intelligence for Automated Tender Compliance
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Aim-0->1
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CertiProcure AI: Blockchain-Anchored Multi-Modal Intelligence for Automated Tender Compliance
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Core ConceptCertiProcure AI is a secure, end-to-end platform designed for the CRPF to eliminate manual evaluation fatigue. It combines Agentic AI for document reasoning with a Permissioned Blockchain Ledger to ensure that every eligibility decision is transparent, immutable, and legally defensible.2. Technical Architecture & Blockchain IntegrationOur architecture is built on a "Decoupled Trust" model:The Intelligence Layer (AI): Uses a hybrid of LayoutLMv3 (for spatial document understanding) and GPT-4o/Claude 3.5 (for complex legal reasoning). It handles typed PDFs, scans, and mobile photos by normalizing them into structured JSON data.The Integrity Layer (Blockchain): We integrate a Private Ethereum Sidechain or Hyperledger Fabric. This serves as the system's "Black Box" recorder.Data Anchoring: Instead of storing heavy files on-chain, we store SHA-256 Hashes of the original tender and bidder documents.Immutable Logs: Every automated verdict (Eligible/Ineligible) and human override is recorded as a "Transaction," creating a permanent audit trail that cannot be deleted or altered by any administrator.3. Step-by-Step WorkflowTender Ingestion: AI extracts criteria (e.g., Turnover > ₹5 Cr). These rules are "anchored" on the blockchain as the baseline for the evaluation.Multi-Modal Bidder Analysis: The OCR engine processes diverse file types. If a document is a blurry photograph, the AI flags it for "Manual Review" rather than rejecting it.Automated Comparison: The AI matches bidder evidence against tender rules.Verification & Logging: Each decision is timestamped and written to the Blockchain. This ensures that if a bidder is disqualified, the specific reason and the version of the document used are locked in the ledger.4. Addressing Non-NegotiablesExplainable Verdicts: The system produces a "Decision Trace." For example: "Criterion: GST Registration; Result: Pass; Evidence: Page 3 of 'Tax_Docs.jpg'; Hash: [abc123...]"Human-in-the-Loop: A dedicated dashboard surfaces "Ambiguous" cases (e.g., low-confidence OCR results) for an officer’s sign-off. The officer's decision is also logged on-chain to ensure accountability.Auditability: The blockchain provides a Consolidated Audit Report. In case of a legal challenge, the CRPF can prove the exact state of the evidence at the time of the decision.5. Key TechnologiesOCR/Vision: LayoutLMv3, Tesseract (Pre-processing), and TrOCR.LLM Reasoning: Fine-tuned Llama-3 or Claude for administrative rule extraction.Blockchain: Hyperledger Fabric (for permissioned, high-speed transaction logging).Storage: Encrypted AWS S3 for documents with hashes stored on-chain.6. Implementation PlanPhase 1: Build the Vision Pipeline to handle noisy scans/photos.Phase 2: Implement the Smart Contract for logging evaluation verdicts.Phase 3: Develop the Human-in-the-loop dashboard for final CRPF officer verification.

solutions.team_name: TirpuKaryaSetu AI: From Court Judgments to Verified Government Actions
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


P pavankumar21_896c
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TirpuKaryaSetu AI: From Court Judgments to Verified Government Actions
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 20, 2026 11:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TirpuKaryaSetu AI is a secure decision-intelligence system that understands court judgments and converts them into verified, accountable government actions — not just summaries. ತೀರ್ಪು ಕಾರ್ಯ ಸೇತು: a bridge from court judgment to government action.﻿THE PROBLEMGovernment departments receive court judgments as long, complex PDF documents where critical directions — what must be done, by when, and by which department — are buried inside dense legal text. This creates delays, confusion, file movement inefficiencies, and risk of non-compliance or contempt of court.﻿TirpuKaryaSetu AI transforms these judgments into structured, explainable, and officer-verified action workflows that fit real government processes.﻿HOW IT WORKSProcesses court judgment PDFs (scanned or digital) using OCR and text extraction.Uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to identify orders, directions, deadlines, responsible authorities, and conditions from the original judgment text.Converts the judgment into structured outputs: compliance actions, appeal options, timelines, and department routing.Applies policy and rule logic such as appeal windows, compliance deadlines, and urgency/risk flags.Sends output into a human verification workflow where officers can Approve, Edit, Reject, assign ownership, and add notes before final action.Maintains full traceability by linking each action item back to the exact source sentence in the judgment.EXAMPLE WORKFLOWInput (from judgment): "The respondent department is directed to release pension benefits within 8 weeks from the date of receipt of this order."﻿System output inside TirpuKaryaSetu AI:Action: Release pension benefits to the petitioner.Responsible Department: Finance / Pension Cell.Deadline: 8 weeks from date of receipt of order.Risk: Contempt risk if delayed beyond deadline.Recommendation: No appeal; proceed with compliance and track proof of action.Evidence Link: Exact sentence from the court order shown for officer review and verification.KANNADA + ENGLISH BILINGUAL SUPPORTTirpuKaryaSetu AI supports bilingual workflows for Karnataka departments, allowing officers to read and act on judgment-derived actions in both English and Kannada for faster understanding and execution.﻿Example1:English: "Release pension benefits within 8 weeks."ಕನ್ನಡ: "8 ವಾರಗಳೊಳಗೆ ಪಿಂಚಣಿ ಸೌಲಭ್ಯಗಳನ್ನು ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಿ.Example2: English: "Reinstate the terminated employee to service within 4 weeks from the date of receipt of this order."ಕನ್ನಡ: "ಈ ಆದೇಶದ ಸ್ವೀಕೃತಿ ದಿನಾಂಕದಿಂದ 4 ವಾರಗಳೊಳಗೆ ವಜಾಗೊಂಡ ನೌಕರರನ್ನು ಸೇವೆಗೆ ಮರಳಿ ನೇಮಿಸಿ.""DASHBOARD AND VERIFICATION WORKFLOWThe system shows a clean dashboard with verified actions, pending reviews, approaching deadlines, department-wise workload, and completed compliance cases.﻿Each case moves through a simple workflow:Judgment Received -> Extracted by AI -> Under Officer Review -> Verified & Approved -> Action In Progress -> Proof of Compliance Attached -> Case Closed﻿Dashboard shows:Pending actions with countdown timers to deadlines.Contempt-risk flags for cases approaching SLA breach.Department-wise pending and completed case counts.Full audit trail showing who reviewed, edited, approved each action.Proof upload status for completed compliance actions.CORE FEATURESIntelligent extraction of case details, parties, court directions, and responsible authorities.Action-plan generation covering compliance steps, appeal options, deadlines, and department mapping.Human approval workflow: Approve / Edit / Reject / Assign with notes.Multilingual support: English + Kannada for both judgment reading and officer interface.Verified-action dashboard with compliance status tracking.Full tamper-evident audit trail for accountability and future audits.Risk alerts: missed deadline flags, contempt-risk warnings, appeal-window countdowns.SECURITY AND TRUSTEnd-to-end encryption and role-based access control for government officers.Private, secure AI processing without exposing sensitive legal data to public models.Detailed audit logs for every action, review, edit, and approval.Deployable on government cloud or on-premise infrastructure as per department policy.KEY INNOVATIONGoes beyond summarization: generates execution-ready, officer-verified action workflows for departments.Combines AI + RAG + rule-based reasoning + human-in-the-loop verification for trustworthy outputs.Reduces hallucination risk by grounding every recommendation in the exact judgment source text.Designed specifically for government file movement and compliance operations, not generic legal search.IMPACTReduces manual reading and note-making effort for officers handling court orders by over 80%.Speeds up accurate decision-making immediately after judgment receipt.Prevents missed deadlines and lowers legal/contempt risk for departments.Bridges the gap between court judgment and on-ground government execution with clear ownership and evidence.﻿CASE METADATA EXTRACTION (Complete) For every judgment processed, TirpuKaryaSetu AI extracts a full set of structured case metadata including: Case Number, Court Name, Bench / Judge Name, Date of Order, Date of Receipt by Department, Petitioner Name, Respondent Name / Department, Disposal Status (Disposed / Pending), and Nature of Judgment (compliance-directed / appeal-relevant). This ensures that the system captures not just the directions but also the complete administrative context required for government record-keeping and CCMS integration.﻿CONFIDENCE LEVELS AND SOURCE TRANSPARENCY Every extracted field and AI-generated recommendation is displayed alongside a confidence score (High / Medium / Low) and a direct source highlight from the original PDF. For example, if the system identifies a compliance deadline, it shows: Extracted Text: "directed to release pension benefits within 8 weeks from the date of receipt" | Confidence: High (96%) | Source: Page 4, Paragraph 2. This enables the reviewing officer to immediately validate or challenge any extraction without reading the full judgment.﻿TRUSTED DASHBOARD — VERIFIED RECORDS ONLY The decision-maker dashboard is strictly gated: only officer-approved and human-verified action records are published to the dashboard view. Records that are pending review, rejected, or under edit remain in the officer queue and are never shown as actionable items. This ensures that every item visible to a department head or senior official represents a fully validated, traceable, and accountable action — not a raw AI output. The dashboard provides: Department-wise verified action plan view, Important compliance dates and appeal deadlines with countdown timers, Risk-flagged cases (contempt risk, approaching limitation period), Audit trail of who reviewed, edited, and approved each record, Proof-of-compliance upload status for closed cases.﻿CCMS AND HIGH COURT CIS INTEGRATION TirpuKaryaSetu AI is designed to plug directly into the existing Centre for e-Governance CCMS infrastructure. Once a case is disposed of in the High Court and the judgment PDF is automatically fetched into CCMS via the Court Information System (CIS) API, TirpuKaryaSetu AI picks it up from that point and processes the PDF without disrupting the current government workflow. In addition, the system also supports secure manual PDF upload by authorized officers, so departments can process legacy judgments, district court orders, exceptional cases, or documents received outside the CCMS flow. This hybrid ingestion model ensures that TirpuKaryaSetu AI can work both as a seamless value-add layer on top of CCMS and as a practical standalone tool wherever direct integration is unavailable or still being rolled out.﻿TECHNOLOGY STACK Will be Built using Google Gemini (LLM) for language understanding and action plan generation, LangChain with a RAG pipeline for grounded retrieval from judgment text, PyMuPDF and Tesseract OCR for parsing both digital and scanned PDFs, Python FastAPI for the backend API layer, and a React-based officer dashboard for the human verification and monitoring interface. The system is stateless at the AI layer, ensuring no sensitive legal data is retained in external models.﻿GOVERNMENT DATA COMPLIANCE AND NIC READINESS The current hackathon prototype will be built using open-source libraries and freely available services (Google Gemini free tier, LangChain, PyMuPDF, Tesseract OCR, Python FastAPI, React) to demonstrate the full flow. For real government deployment, the system is designed to be self-hosted — with all AI processing, data storage, and audit logs running within the department's own infrastructure or NIC Cloud, ensuring no sensitive legal data is transmitted to external services. The architecture supports swapping Google Gemini with a fully open-source, on-premise LLM (such as Ollama with LLaMA 3 or Mistral) to achieve 100% air-gapped deployment compliant with NIC cloud guidelines and India's government data classification policy (classifying judgment data as Restricted). Role-based access control, encrypted storage, and tamper-evident audit trails are implemented from day one in the prototype.

TenderIQ: Explainable AI System for Smart Government Tender EvaluationThis solution focuses on improving transparency, efficiency, and fairness in government procurement systems.Problem StatementGovernment tender evaluation is currently:Manual and time-consuming Prone to human bias and errors Difficult due to large unstructured documents (PDFs, technical bids, financial data) This leads to inefficiency, lack of transparency, and delayed decision-making.Proposed SolutionTenderIQ is an AI-powered platform that automates tender evaluation using:Natural Language Processing (NLP) for document understanding Machine Learning (ML) for scoring and ranking Explainable AI (XAI) for transparent decision-making The system extracts, analyzes, and evaluates bidder data to generate objective and explainable results.How It WorksStepProcessUploadUpload tender & bidder documentsOCR & ParsingExtract text from PDFs and scanned documentsCriteria ExtractionIdentify eligibility rulesEvaluationScore biddersExplanationShow reasons for decisions﻿Key FeaturesAutomated document parsing & data extraction AI-based eligibility analysis and scoring Explainable AI for transparency Real-time bidder comparison dashboard Advanced CapabilitiesSupports scanned documents using OCR Flags ambiguous cases for human review Maintains complete audit trail Ensures criterion-level explainability ImpactReduces evaluation time Minimizes bias Improves transparency Enables data-driven decisions

solutions.team_name: Explainable AI for CRPF Tender Evaluation: Automated Eligibility Analysis with Human-in-the-Loop Auditability
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Arpan_Ari_arpancodec
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Explainable AI for CRPF Tender Evaluation: Automated Eligibility Analysis with Human-in-the-Loop Auditability
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 21, 2026 12:52 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem SummaryGovernment organisations such as the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) issue tenders containing detailed eligibility criteria spanning financial thresholds, technical requirements, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications. These criteria are embedded within lengthy, legally structured documents, making them difficult to interpret and extract in a structured manner.Bidders respond with supporting documents in highly heterogeneous formats, including typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word files, tables, and images. The same information—such as turnover, certifications, or project experience—may appear in different formats and locations across submissions.Currently, the evaluation process is manual, requiring procurement officers to cross-check each bidder’s documents against each eligibility criterion. This process is time-consuming, inconsistent, and prone to human error. Variations in interpretation, missing data, and ambiguous documentation further increase the risk of incorrect decisions, which can lead to legal disputes, procurement delays, and financial losses.The fundamental challenge is to build a system that can reliably interpret complex tender requirements, extract relevant evidence from unstructured bidder documents, and perform consistent, explainable, and auditable eligibility evaluation under real-world uncertainty.﻿Proposed SolutionWe propose an AI-assisted, explainable, and auditable decision-support platform for automated tender evaluation.Automatically extracts eligibility criteria from tender documents and converts them into structured, machine-readable representations. Processes bidder submissions across multiple formats, including scanned documents and images, and extracts relevant evidence mapped to each criterion. Uses a deterministic rule-based evaluation engine to compare extracted evidence against criteria, ensuring consistent and non-ambiguous decision logic. Generates criterion-level verdicts — Eligible, Not Eligible, or Need Manual Review — with clear justifications. Ensures that no bidder is silently disqualified; all uncertain or low-confidence cases are explicitly flagged for human review. Maintains a complete audit trail of every decision, including extracted values, source documents, and applied rules, making the system suitable for legal and regulatory scrutiny. ﻿Key CapabilitiesTender Understanding The system identifies and extracts eligibility criteria from complex tender documents, classifying them into financial, technical, compliance, and documentation requirements, while distinguishing mandatory from optional conditions. Robust Document Processing It supports heterogeneous inputs including PDFs, scanned documents, and images, using OCR and layout-aware parsing to preserve structure such as tables and sections. Accurate Evidence Extraction The system extracts key information such as financial values, dates, certification details, and project experience using a combination of NLP, entity extraction, and pattern-based methods. Deterministic Evaluation Engine A rule-based engine evaluates each criterion against extracted evidence, ensuring consistent, transparent, and reproducible decisions without reliance on black-box models. Explainability by Design Every decision is accompanied by a clear explanation specifying the criterion evaluated, the source document and location, the extracted value, and the rule applied. Human-in-the-Loop Workflow Ambiguous, incomplete, or low-confidence cases are flagged for manual review, enabling human oversight and preventing incorrect automated rejection. End-to-End Auditability All decisions are logged with traceable metadata, enabling full audit trails required for government procurement processes. ﻿Why This MattersReduces evaluation time from days to hours, even for large tenders. Ensures consistent application of criteria across all bidders. Eliminates subjective interpretation and reduces human error. Provides full transparency and traceability for every decision. Strengthens legal defensibility of procurement outcomes. Allows officers to focus on critical judgment rather than repetitive verification tasks. ﻿Core Technical ChallengesAccurately isolating eligibility criteria from complex tender documents that mix multiple types of information. Handling semantic variations in how the same requirement or evidence is expressed across documents. Extracting numerical and structured data from low-quality scanned documents and images. Identifying relevant evidence when information is distributed across multiple documents. Managing ambiguous or borderline values without making incorrect decisions. Classifying and interpreting documents that may be poorly labeled or inconsistently structured. ﻿Solution PipelineTender Criterion Extraction The system processes the tender document to identify and structure eligibility criteria, classify their types, and determine whether they are mandatory or optional. Bidder Document Intake and Normalization All submitted documents are ingested, classified, and converted into a standardized representation using OCR, text extraction, and layout-aware parsing. Evidence Extraction Layer For each criterion, the system retrieves relevant document sections and extracts required values such as financial figures, certification details, and experience records, along with confidence scores. Evaluation and Decision Engine Extracted evidence is compared against criteria using predefined rules. Each criterion is evaluated independently, and results are aggregated into a final decision. Human Review and Audit Layer Low-confidence or ambiguous cases are flagged for manual review. All decisions are logged with complete traceability, and reports are generated for final approval. Our Unique Approach (What Makes This Solution Different)OUR UNIQUE APPROACH Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on end-to-end black-box models, our system is designed as a hybrid, audit-first AI framework that prioritizes reliability, explainability, and legal defensibility over blind automation.Audit-First, Not Automation-First Most solutions attempt to fully automate decision-making. In contrast, our system is designed to maximize trust and auditability, ensuring every decision can be traced, verified, and justified before acceptance. The system acts as an intelligent assistant, not an opaque decision-maker. Deterministic Rule Engine over Black-Box Decisions Instead of relying on large language models for final eligibility decisions, we use a rule-based evaluation engine. This guarantees consistent, reproducible outcomes and eliminates risks of hallucination or unpredictable behavior, which is critical in government procurement. Confidence-Aware Decision Making Every extracted value is associated with a confidence score. Rather than forcing a binary decision, the system introduces a third state — “Need Manual Review” — as a first-class outcome, ensuring that uncertainty is handled explicitly and safely. Criterion-Centric Processing (Not Document-Centric) Traditional systems process documents as a whole. Our approach reverses the paradigm by evaluating each criterion independently, retrieving only the most relevant evidence across all documents. This improves accuracy, reduces noise, and aligns directly with how human evaluators think. Multi-Source Evidence Aggregation The system does not assume that required information exists in a single document. It intelligently aggregates evidence across multiple sources (e.g., financial statements, CA certificates, company profiles), enabling robust evaluation even when data is fragmented. Explainability at the Core, Not as an Add-On Explainability is built into every layer of the system. Each verdict includes: The exact criterion evaluated The document and page referenced The extracted value The rule applied This ensures full transparency and makes the output directly usable in official reports. Graceful Handling of Real-World Noise The system is explicitly designed for real-world conditions: Poor-quality scans Inconsistent formats Approximate values Missing or ambiguous data Instead of failing silently, it flags and escalates such cases, ensuring robustness in practical deployment. Separation of Understanding and Decision Layers We clearly separate: AI components (for extraction and understanding) Rule-based components (for decision-making) This modular design improves maintainability, reduces risk, and allows independent improvement of each layer. Legally Defensible Audit Trail Every action in the pipeline is logged with structured metadata, enabling: Full traceability of decisions Reproducibility of results Compliance with procurement audit standards This makes the system suitable not just for assistance, but for formal adoption in government workflows.

solutions.team_name: TenderLens: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Code Storm
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderLens: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 21, 2026 10:57 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem UnderstandingGovernment procurement evaluation is slow, inconsistent, and difficult to audit. A single tender can attract dozens of bidders each submitting hundreds of pages - typed PDFs, scanned certificates, Word files, and photographs. Two evaluators reading the same documents often reach different conclusions. TenderLens automates this process end-to-end while keeping a human officer in control of every uncertain decision.﻿Four hard constraints shape a usable solution:• Document chaos: submissions arrive in every format, with OCR errors, stamps over text, and mixed-language content.• Language ambiguity: tender criteria use legally hedged language with compound conditions, date arithmetic, and domain-specific terms.• No silent disqualification: a wrongly disqualified bidder is a legal liability. Uncertain cases must go to a human, not be auto-rejected.• Auditability: every verdict must trace to a source document, page, and extracted value suitable for formal sign-off.﻿2. Eligibility Criteria ExtractionThe tender document is parsed through a five-stage pipeline: section classification, individual criterion extraction (using a local LLM with JSON schema output), criterion typing (Financial / Technical / Compliance / Document), mandatory vs. optional detection (linguistic cues: 'shall', 'must', 'desirable'), and condition normalisation into structured logic objects.﻿Compound criteria are decomposed into atomic sub-criteria with explicit AND / OR connectors. For example:﻿"3 similar works >= Rs. 2 Cr each in last 5 years, OR 2 similar works >= Rs. 3 Cr each" → stored as: OR( count(similar_works, val>=2Cr, years=5) >= 3, count(similar_works, val>=3Cr, years=5) >= 2 )﻿Output is a Criteria Registry - a structured JSON of all criteria with condition logic and required evidence types. A procurement officer approves this registry before evaluation begins, catching mis-extractions before they affect any bidder.﻿3. Bidder Submission ParsingEach uploaded file is routed to the appropriate processing chain:﻿Document typeProcessingNative text PDFDirect text extraction via PyMuPDF; table layout analysisScanned PDF / photographDeskew, binarise, then OCR (Tesseract 5 + PaddleOCR). Confidence score retained per block.Word (.docx)Direct XML extraction preserving table structureMixed pagesPer-page routing: text layer if available, OCR fallback﻿A second LLM pass extracts criterion-relevant values per bidder: turnover figures, project completion dates, certificate numbers. Every extracted value carries a confidence score (0.0–1.0) from three factors: OCR confidence, LLM extraction confidence, and cross-document consistency. Low-confidence values are flagged for review, never silently used.﻿4. Criterion Matching and EvaluationA deterministic rule engine (not an LLM) evaluates each criterion's condition logic against extracted values - numeric comparisons with currency normalisation, count conditions with date-range filtering, document presence and expiry checks. Each criterion for each bidder receives one of three verdicts:﻿VerdictConditionReview?ELIGIBLEAll mandatory criteria met, confidence >= 0.85NoNOT ELIGIBLEMandatory criterion definitively not met, confidence >= 0.85No - officer can overrideNEEDS MANUAL REVIEWConfidence < 0.85, conflicting signals, or unresolvable ambiguityAlways﻿The system is biased towards review over rejection. A partially unreadable ISO certificate returns NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW, not NOT ELIGIBLE. Conflicting turnover figures from two documents surface both values for the officer rather than picking one.﻿5. Explainability and Human ReviewEvery verdict carries four elements: which criterion was evaluated, which document and page were used, what value was extracted, and why the verdict was reached in plain language (e.g., 'Turnover Rs. 6.2 Cr >= threshold Rs. 5 Cr: PASS').﻿NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW cases enter a structured officer queue showing the criterion text, the extracted evidence with a page image highlight, and the reason for uncertainty. The officer selects ELIGIBLE or NOT ELIGIBLE and must enter a free-text justification. All decisions are logged with timestamp and user ID and are irrevocable in the audit log (overrides create a chain, not an erasure). Corrections feed back as labelled training examples to reduce future escalation rates.﻿6. Architecture and TechnologyComponentTechnology & RationaleOCRTesseract 5 + PaddleOCR (on-premises, open source, Devanagari support). Vision model fallback (LLaVA) for low-quality images.LLM extractionLocally-hosted Mistral 7B / Llama-3 8B (quantised). No PII sent to external APIs. Fine-tuned on government document vocabulary.Condition evaluatorPython rule engine on structured JSON criteria - deterministic, testable, auditable.Backend / storageFastAPI + PostgreSQL + MinIO. All on-premises deployable.Audit logKafka append-only event stream mirrored to immutable object storage. Signed PDF export with SHA-256 manifest.﻿7. Risks and MitigationsRiskMitigationPoor OCR on low-quality scansPre-processing pipeline + per-block confidence; bidder can re-submit cleaner copy via review UICriteria mis-extractionOfficer approves Criteria Registry before any bidder is evaluatedLegal challenge to verdictEvery NOT ELIGIBLE verdict traces to source document, page, and extracted value. Audit log exportable.Domain language shiftModular per-tender-type prompts; feedback loop captures errors for retrainingData privacyAll processing on-premises; role-based access control on bidder submissions﻿8. Round 2 Implementation PlanWeekMilestones1Ingestion pipeline, OCR integration, per-page routing. Validate on sandbox scanned documents.2Criteria Engine with local LLM. Extraction prompts for all criterion types. Officer approval UI for Criteria Registry.3Extraction Engine, Evaluation Engine, condition logic evaluator, review queue and officer UI.4Report Service, audit log export, end-to-end testing, demo preparation.﻿9. ConclusionTenderLens delivers automation that procurement officers can trust. Robust document parsing handles every format bidders submit. Deterministic rule-based evaluation ensures consistent, auditable verdicts. A human-in-the-loop workflow guarantees no bidder is silently disqualified. An immutable audit trail makes every decision defensible in a formal government procurement context.Built for the real world - consistent, explainable, and auditable at every step.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Company Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 21, 2026 11:26 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Statement : Government departments release tenders for different works. Many companies apply for these tenders by submitting their documents such as cost details, experience, certifications, and technical proposals. Officers must manually read all submitted documents and compare them with tender conditions to select the best company. This process is difficult and time-consuming because there are many applications and each document is long and in different formats. It is also easy to miss important eligibility criteria while checking manually. Therefore, there is a need for an AI system that can automatically analyze tender conditions and compare them with company submissions.﻿SOLUTION:We build an AI-based tender evaluation system. The system reads the government tender document and extracts important conditions. Then it reads the documents submitted by companies and compares them with the tender requirements. The system checks eligibility, gives score to each company, and ranks them. Finally, it shows the best suitable companies for the government to review. Techniques Used: PDF Text Extraction – to read tender and company documents NLP (Natural Language Processing) – to understand tender conditions Keyword Matching – to compare requirements with company details Rule-Based Scoring – to assign score based on eligibility Ranking Algorithm – to select best company Explainable Output – shows why a company is selected

solutions.team_name: TenderMind AI — Intelligent Procurement Evaluation Platform
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


M madukalmanoj_e400
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
TenderMind AI — Intelligent Procurement Evaluation Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 21, 2026 12:28 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Picture a CRPF procurement officer on a Monday morning. She has a 60-page tender document, ten bidder submissions totalling 800 pages, three colleagues helping her evaluate, and a deadline on Friday. By Wednesday, two of her colleagues have marked the same bidder as eligible and ineligible respectively — neither is wrong, they just read different pages. By Thursday, nobody can remember which document proved which criterion. On Friday, the report goes out. Two months later, a rejected bidder files a legal challenge, and nobody can reconstruct the paper trail.This is not a rare failure. This is how government procurement evaluation works across India today.We built TenderMind AI because we believe a procurement officer's job should be making judgment calls — not hunting through 800 pages to find a turnover certificate that may or may not have been photographed sideways on someone's phone.﻿What TenderMind AI actually does:You upload the tender document. Within 90 seconds, the system has read every clause and built a structured checklist — each eligibility criterion with its exact threshold, the time window it applies to, what kind of document satisfies it, and the page and clause it came from. Before anything else happens, the officer reviews this checklist and approves it. If the system misread something, she fixes it here — not after 10 bidders have been wrongly evaluated.Then the bidder packages go in. Typed PDFs, scanned certificates, Word documents, phone photographs of ISO certificates — the system handles all of it. It knows the difference between a clean typed P&L and a rubber-stamped scanned certificate that needs deskewing and noise removal before any OCR will work on it. It normalises "Rs. 62 lakhs" and "INR 6.2 Cr." and "₹ 6,20,00,000" into the same number before comparing against any threshold, because that inconsistency alone breaks most naive implementations.Before a single bidder document is evaluated, the system quietly does three things most procurement tools completely ignore: it checks every bidder's GST and PAN against the CPPP debarred vendor registry; it checks whether any bidder director's name or registration number matches anyone on the evaluation committee; and it flags if a previously disqualified vendor appears to have re-registered under a new name. None of this is glamorous. All of it matters.For the actual evaluation, the system splits criteria into two types. Numeric criteria — turnover, project count, certificate validity — are handled by a rule engine. The value is extracted, normalised, compared. Either it passes or it doesn't, and the evidence is right there: page 14, the number that was read, the threshold it was compared against. Qualitative criteria — "three similar projects", "relevant construction experience" — are handled by an LLM that reads the bidder's project descriptions and reasons about similarity. These never auto-commit. A human always reviews them.The confidence system is the part we're most proud of. If the system is 85% or more confident in an extraction, it commits the verdict and logs the full evidence chain. If it's between 65–84%, it suggests a verdict but asks the officer to confirm. Below 65% — typically phone photographs or heavily stamped documents — it emits no verdict at all. It shows the officer the raw image, what it tried to read, and asks her to make the call. A system that auto-rejects when uncertain is a liability. We built the opposite.When the tender itself changes — and real tenders get amended two or three times before the submission deadline — the system detects the corrigendum, diffs it against the approved criteria list, and brings the changed criteria back to the officer for re-approval before any evaluation proceeds. Evaluating ten bidders against outdated criteria is a procurement nightmare we've seen happen repeatedly.For consortium and joint venture bids — which make up a large share of infrastructure tenders — the system extracts the JV structure, identifies each partner's role, and aggregates eligibility across them according to the tender's own JV rules. It does not treat a consortium bid like a single-entity submission.The review dashboard is where the officer actually spends her time. Every bidder, every criterion, every verdict — laid out clearly. For each flagged case, she sees the suggested verdict, the extracted value, and the actual source document rendered on screen with the relevant region highlighted in yellow. She confirms, overrides, or escalates. She cannot bulk-approve. Each flagged case is individual, because rubber-stamping defeats the whole point. Every action she takes is logged with her ID and timestamp.We also added something that no current procurement tool offers: a criteria anomaly detector. It compares the extracted criteria against similar past tenders in the database and flags statistically unusual thresholds — the kind that seem written to let exactly one vendor through. It does not make accusations. It flags for a senior officer's attention. Procurement integrity requires that someone is paying attention to the criteria themselves, not just the evaluation.After eligibility is established, the platform runs a bid price reasonableness check on submitted financial bids — comparing L1 against cost benchmarks and historical bid distributions to catch suspiciously low bids before the contract is awarded. And over time, the historical bid intelligence layer surfaces patterns: which vendors consistently win, which criteria are most commonly failed, which document types cause the most extraction problems. Institutional memory that currently lives nowhere.The final report is a cryptographically signed PDF. Every verdict traceable to a source document and page. Every reviewer action logged. If a rejected bidder challenges the decision six months later, the procurement officer has a complete, unalterable record of exactly what was found, where, and who reviewed it.The whole process — for a 60-page tender and ten bidders — takes about 38 minutes instead of three days. But the time saving is almost secondary. The real value is that for the first time, the evaluation is consistent, documented, and defensible.﻿Tenders are issued in Hindi, Kannada, Telugu, and other regional languages across India. Our OCR and extraction pipeline handles multilingual documents, because a procurement platform that only works in English is not a procurement platform for India.For officers reviewing in the field on tablets and phones with unreliable connectivity, the interface is a Progressive Web App — it works offline and syncs when the connection returns. Government technology consistently fails at field deployment. We designed for it from the start.Every reviewer correction feeds back into model retraining. The system gets measurably more accurate with each tender cycle — not just more familiar, but provably better on the document types that caused the most errors.﻿Stack: FastAPI · PyMuPDF · OpenCV · Tesseract 5 · Azure Doc Intelligence · LayoutLMv3 · spaCy · Claude Sonnet (de-identified inputs only) · LangChain + pgvector · React · PostgreSQL · Docker → NIC CloudRound 2 sprint plan: Hours 0–20: Tender ingestion, criteria extraction, corrigendum diff, officer approval gate Hours 20–40: Bidder extraction, debarment and COI checks, verdict engine with confidence gating Hours 40–60: Review dashboard with live PDF viewer and bounding-box highlights Hours 60–72: Report generator and full end-to-end demo — 4 criteria, 10 bidders, 1 scanned certificate, 1 JV bid, 1 corrigendumWe are not building a tool that replaces procurement officers. We are building one that finally gives them what they deserve: a process they can stand behind.
