solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A abhikrao1301_453f
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ClearBid — AI-Based Tender Evaluation for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 01:10 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
ClearBid is an AI system that helps government procurement officers evaluate tender submissions faster, more consistently, and in a way that can be audited and defended after the fact.The ProblemAnyone who has sat on a tender evaluation committee knows how it actually works. You get a stack of bidder submissions in different formats, a list of eligibility criteria written in legal language, and a deadline. Two officers reading the same documents often reach different conclusions. When those decisions are challenged later, there is rarely a clean trail showing exactly why a bidder was accepted or rejected and what evidence supported that call.The documents make this worse. Bidders submit typed PDFs alongside scanned certificates, photographs of physical stamps, CA-attested balance sheets with handwritten notes, and Udyam certificates with mixed Hindi-English fields. No standard format, no consistent structure.ClearBid is built specifically for this reality.What Makes It DifferentMost AI tools applied to procurement are document Q&A systems. They answer questions like "What does this document say about turnover?" That is not the same as answering "does this bidder satisfy criterion FIN_01, and what is the documentary evidence for that verdict?"ClearBid converts the tender document into a structured set of machine-evaluable rules, then evaluates each bidder against those rules one criterion at a time. Every verdict comes with a confidence score and a direct reference to the source document and page number. The system never auto-rejects a bidder silently. If evidence is ambiguous or confidence is low, the case goes to a human officer with a plain-language explanation of what was found and what was uncertain.How It WorksThe pipeline has five stages.First, the tender document is parsed by GPT-4o to extract eligibility criteria and convert them into structured rules. Financial thresholds like "5 Cr", "Rs. 5,00,00,000", and "50 lakh" are all normalized to the same integer. Time references like "last 3 financial years" are resolved to explicit date ranges. Hindi-language clauses are translated via AI4Bharat's IndicTrans2 before parsing.Second, bidder submissions go through a multi-engine document understanding stack. AWS Textract handles structured text and tables. Tesseract serves as the fallback for low-quality scans. GPT-4o Vision handles semantic understanding of certificates and photographs, extracting amounts, dates, organization names, and certificate IDs with their bounding box locations.Third, evaluation happens through an RAG architecture. Rather than sending full documents to the LLM, we embed both the criteria and the bidder document chunks into a FAISS vector index. For each criterion, we retrieve only the most relevant bidder document chunks and the LLM reasons over that retrieved evidence alone. This directly prevents hallucination. If no relevant evidence is found above the similarity threshold, the criterion is flagged for human review rather than guessed.Fourth, every verdict is explained at the criterion level: which criterion was checked, what value was found, which document it came from, and why the bidder passed, failed, or needs review. A sample output looks like this:Turnover above Rs. 5 Cr: ELIGIBLE, confidence 0.92, Rs. 6.2 Cr found in Balance Sheet FY2022, Page 3. ISO 9001 certification: NEEDS REVIEW, confidence 0.58, certificate scan partially legible, expiry date unclear, Page 7. Active GSTIN: NOT ELIGIBLE, confidence 0.96, GSTIN status Canceled per GST portal verification.Fifth, a human review interface shows flagged cases with the source document highlighted. Officers approve, edit,t or override. Every action is logged in an append-only audit trail suitable for post-award scrutiny.Confidence CalibrationConfidence scores are calibrated using Platt Scaling and Isotonic Regression against a validation dataset, so the numbers are meaningful rather than arbitrary. Cases above 0.85 are auto-evaluated with full evidence. Cases between 0.60 and 0.85 are recommended, with officer confirmation required. Cases below 0.60 go to mandatory human review with no system verdict issued. Thresholds are set conservatively and will be tuned in Round 2 based on precision-recall tradeoffs. We prioritize minimizing false negatives because approving an ineligible bidder carries significantly higher compliance risk than flagging an eligible one for review.India-Specific DesignThe system is built for GFR 2017 compliance (Rules 160-175), designed for deployment on NIC infrastructure and MeghRaj GovCloud, and natively handles the Indian document ecosystem, em including GST Registration Certificates, PAN cards, Udyam and MSME registrations, CA-attested financials, government work orders, and DigiLocker or MCA21 signed documents. GSTIN is cross-verified against the public GST portal or through a GSP-licensed API, subject to GSTN data-sharing agreements.The system also handles fraud signals through metadata consistency checks, statistical image analysis for compression artifacts, font anomaly detection, and digital signature chain validation. No anomaly results in automatic disqualification. Everything is surfaced to the officer with an explanation.SecurityEach tender is an isolated workspace. Role-based access control separates Procurement Officers, Reviewers, and Auditors. Data is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.3. Audit logs are append-only and tamper-evident.Round 2 Demo PlanWe will build a working pipeline around a synthetic CRPF tender with five synthetic bidders: two clearly eligible, one clearly ineligible with a verifiable reason, and two deliberately ambiguous with realistic problem documents. The demo will show live criteria extraction, the evaluation dashboard, click-through evidence highlighting, the officer review queue, and a one-page exportable audit report.TeamWe are undergraduate engineering students with hands-on experience in machine learning, NLP, and backend development through coursework and personal projects. We have worked with LLM APIs, document parsing, and retrieval-based systems at a project level. This is our first time applying those skills to a problem with real institutional stakes, which is exactly why we chose Theme 3.

Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 09:16 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project - Our proposal describes an AI-powered platform the Smart Tender Evaluation System (STES) that automates the extraction of eligibility criteria from tender documents and the evaluation of each bidder's submission against those criteria.Solution - STES uses a pipeline of document parsing, large language model (LLM)-based information extraction, rule-based matching, and a human-in-the-loop verification layer to produce explainable, criterion-level verdicts. Every decision is traceable to a specific source document and value making output suitable for government.Government procurement failures cost time , money and public trust. STES reduces evaluation time from days to hours , eliminates inconsistency across evaluators , and procedures an audit trail that can withstand scrutiny. The technology is mature , the problem is well-defined and the impact is immediate.Idea - Government tenders are legally complex documents that can span 80–120 pages with dozens of eligibility conditions across technical, financial, and compliance domains. Bidder responses are equally voluminous a single tender may attract 10–20 bidders, each submitting 50–200 pages of supporting material. Evaluating all of this manually is slow, inconsistent, and difficult to audit.The current process suffers from four core problems that is Inconsistency that is two evaluators reading the same documents may reach different conclusions due to ambiguous wording or subjective interpretation , Oversight Risk that is critical criteria buried in annexures or footnotes are frequently missed under time pressure , Auditability Gaps that is verbal deliberations leave no traceable record of why a bidder was accepted or rejected and Speed that is a committee may spend 3–5 working days on a single evaluation, delaying procurement timelines.Our proposal model uses document AI, large language models, deterministic matching, and a structured human-in-the-loop review layer, the platform delivers consistent, explainable, and fully auditable eligibility verdicts at a fraction of the time required by manual evaluation.The design prioritises three principles above all others that is no bidder is ever silently disqualified , every verdict is traceable to a specific document and value and the system supports, rather than replaces the procurement officer's judgement and suitable for government and by using new technology.

Nexus: A Secure AI Command Layer for Cross-System Interoperability and Controlled Execution
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 12:30 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Nexus : Secure AI Command Layer for Cross-System InteroperabilityWhat if your AI agent deletes your repo or sends a Slack message without asking? Nexus ensures every action happens with your explicit permission.OverviewNexus is a secure AI-powered interoperability layer that connects multiple services like Gmail, GitHub, Slack, and Discord through natural language commands without ever exposing user credentials.Instead of blindly executing actions, Nexus introduces a controlled execution model where every AI action is evaluated, verified, and audited before it happens.The ProblemAI agents today require deep access to user accounts, but:Credentials are often stored in insecure ways Actions execute without clear visibility No control over what the agent can or cannot do No audit trail to verify behavior This makes AI powerful but risky.The SolutionNexus introduces a security-first orchestration layer:No raw credentials exposed — All tokens are securely managed via Auth0 Token Vault Central Risk Engine — Every action is classified and evaluated before execution Step-up Authentication — Sensitive actions require explicit user approval Full Audit Trail — Every action is logged with context and decision How It WorksUser gives a command in natural language AI interprets required services and actions Each action passes through a risk engine Based on risk level: Low → Auto execute Medium → Ask for approval High → Require re-authentication or block Action executes via APIs Full audit log is recorded Key FeaturesMulti-system orchestration across independent platforms Policy-based execution control using risk classification Human-in-the-loop approvals for sensitive operations Explainable decisions with transparent logs Secure token handling with zero credential exposure Tech StackFramework: Next.js 16 (App Router, TypeScript) Authentication: Auth0 Token Vault + Auth0 AI SDK AI: Google Gemini via Vercel AI SDK Security Layer: Custom Risk Engine (EXECUTE / STEP-UP / RE-AUTH / BLOCK) UI: Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion Audit System: Persistent logging Why This MattersAs AI agents become more autonomous, trust becomes the biggest barrier.Nexus solves this by ensuring:Every action is visible Every decision is controlled Every operation is auditable This enables safe adoption of AI agents in real-world systems without compromising security.

solutions.team_name: Nyayasetu - AI-Powered Judgment Intelligence Platform for Government Compliance
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


K knpknp599_e1b5
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Nyayasetu - AI-Powered Judgment Intelligence Platform for Government Compliance
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 03:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government departments in Karnataka receive High Court judgment PDFs automatically through CCMS, but everything after that is manual. An officer has to read through 20–60 pages of dense legal language, figure out what the court actually directed, who is responsible, what the deadlines are, and whether to comply or appeal. This is slow, inconsistent, and risky — a missed deadline can mean contempt of court, a missed appeal window forecloses legal options entirely.NyayaSetu sits as an intelligent layer on top of CCMS. When a judgment arrives, it automatically extracts case details, key directions, parties involved, and timelines. It then generates a structured action plan — whether to comply or consider appeal, what specific actions are needed, who is responsible, and absolute calendar deadlines (resolving relative periods like "within 8 weeks" against the order date). Every extraction is source-linked, meaning the system shows exactly which page and paragraph it pulled each piece of information from.Before anything reaches the dashboard, a human reviewer must verify it. The verification interface shows extracted fields side by side with the PDF, with the relevant text highlighted. Each field has a confidence score — low confidence items require mandatory review. The reviewer can approve, edit, or reject any field. Only after full approval does the action plan enter the department dashboard. This gating is enforced at the database level, not just in the UI.The dashboard shows only verified action plans, sorted by deadline urgency. Items due within 7 days are pinned and highlighted. Department-wise filtering ensures each team sees only what is assigned to them. Automated reminders go out 7 days, 3 days, and on the day of each deadline.Tech Stack: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL with append-only audit log, PyMuPDF + Tesseract OCR for document parsing, Google Gemini 1.5 Pro (1M context window — handles full judgment in one pass), LangChain for multi-step extraction pipeline, React + PDF.js for verification UI and dashboard, Docker + Celery for background processing.Key design decisions: Multi-step extraction (segment first, then targeted extraction per segment) produces far more consistent results than single-prompt extraction on long legal documents. Gemini 1.5 Pro's large context window means no chunking — the model sees the entire judgment and can resolve cross-references correctly. An ensemble consistency check (two independent extraction passes compared) catches high-confidence hallucinations before they reach the reviewer.What makes this different from a simple PDF parser: The system distinguishes operative directions from background narration, infers responsible departments even when not explicitly named, resolves relative deadlines to absolute dates, and generates a comply-vs-appeal recommendation with reasoning — all with source citations a reviewer can verify in seconds rather than minutes.NyayaSetu does not automate government decisions. It ensures the right information reaches the right officer at the right time, so the government can act on court directions reliably and on time.

solutions.team_name: JudgeAI: Intelligent Court Judgment Analyzer & Compliance Action Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


J jubileesharma14_d815
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
JudgeAI: Intelligent Court Judgment Analyzer & Compliance Action Engine
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 05:01 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemEvery day, government departments receive court judgments in dense, multi-page PDF format. Officials must manually read through complex legal language to identify what actions to take, by when, and who is responsible. This leads to missed deadlines, delayed compliance, and increased legal risk.The challenge is not just extracting information, but correctly interpreting legal intent and ensuring that critical directives are not overlooked or misinterpreted.﻿Our SolutionJudgeAI is an end-to-end AI-powered platform designed to integrate directly with the CCMS–CIS API pipeline, processing court judgment PDFs automatically as cases are disposed in the High Court; transforming unstructured legal documents into verified, structured, and decision-ready action plans.Unlike traditional document processing systems, JudgeAI focuses on understanding legal intent, handling ambiguity, and ensuring that all outputs are explainable, auditable, and human-verified before use.﻿How It Works1. Extract (Adaptive Document Processing)Ingests court judgment PDFs (typed, scanned, or partially handwritten) using a hybrid pipeline combining PDF parsing and OCR. Extracts case details, key directives, parties involved, and relevant timelines. Every extracted field is linked back to its exact source line in the document with a confidence score.2. Directive Intelligence & Action Plan Generation (Key Differentiator)Instead of directly summarizing text, JudgeAI uses a Directive Intelligence Layer to interpret legal intent.Detects directive patterns such as:"is directed to" → Mandatory Compliance"set aside" → Review / Appeal Consideration"dismissed" → No Action RequiredClassifies each directive into a structured action containing:Action requiredType (Compliance / Appeal / Review)Responsible department (inferred where necessary)Deadline (explicit or inferred using legal norms)Risk level (based on urgency and legal impact)3. Confidence-Aware Decision Layer (Innovation)JudgeAI does not treat all outputs equally. Every extracted directive is assigned a confidence score. Low-confidence or ambiguous cases are automatically flagged and routed for deeper human review; the system never makes unsafe assumptions silently.4. Uncertainty & Conflict Handling (Real-World Readiness)Detects conflicting or overlapping directives within the same judgment and flags inconsistencies explicitly instead of resolving them silently. Ensures no incorrect or risky decision is auto-generated.5. Explainability Layer (Trust & Transparency)Every action is traceable to its exact source text in the judgment. The system displays the reasoning behind each classification decision, enabling reviewers to understand why a decision was made; not just what was extracted.6. Human Verification (Mandatory Gate)Provides a side-by-side view of the original PDF and extracted structured data. Reviewers can Approve, Edit, or Reject each field individually. Only verified records move forward. Every action is logged in a complete audit trail; no unverified data ever reaches the dashboard.7. Trusted DashboardDisplays only human-approved action plans with department-wise filtered views, deadline-based prioritization, a calendar view of upcoming compliance deadlines, and case-level tracking of compliance status and risk levels.﻿Key DifferentiatorsDirective-based legal understanding instead of basic summarizationConfidence-aware pipeline that avoids unsafe automationDetection of ambiguity and conflicting legal instructionsDeadline inference using legal norms with full transparencyFull traceability from every output back to its source textHuman-in-the-loop validation ensures legal reliability and accountabilityNative integration with the CCMS–CIS API pipelineDesigned for decision support, not black-box automation﻿Tech StackFrontend: React + Tailwind CSSBackend: FastAPI (Python)AI/LLM: API for legal extraction, directive classification, and action plan generationPDF Processing: PyMuPDF + pdfplumberOCR: Tesseract (for scanned/low-quality documents)Database: PostgreSQLAuthentication: JWT-based role separation (Reviewer vs Viewer)﻿ImpactA typical court judgment spans 15+ pages of dense legal text. JudgeAI extracts and structures all critical actions in under 30 seconds, routes them through a mandatory human verification gate, and surfaces only trusted records on a clean decision-ready dashboard.By combining AI-driven legal reasoning, confidence-aware explainability, and controlled human oversight, JudgeAI enables faster, safer, and more reliable decision-making for government departments; transforming days of manual effort into a streamlined, same-day workflow.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaBot — AI Copilot for Court Judgment Compliance
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 05:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NyayaBot is an AI copilot for court judgment compliance. Karnataka’s CCMS auto-fetches judgment PDFs, but critical directives like compliance requirements, appeal deadlines, and departmental responsibilities are buried in 20–50 pages of dense legal text, forcing manual reading and causing delays, missed deadlines, and contempt risks. NyayaBot solves this by converting judgments into structured, human-verified action plans: Extract — AI pulls case details, order date, key directives, parties, and timelines with exact page references | Action Plan — for each directive: comply or appeal, deadline, responsible department, required action | Human Verification — officers review side-by-side with the source PDF, see confidence scores, and approve/edit/reject (only verified data proceeds) | Dashboard — department-wise view of actions, deadlines, and compliance status. Built using self-hosted Llama 3.1 (Ollama), PyMuPDF + Tesseract OCR, FastAPI, Next.js, and PostgreSQL — fully open-source and on-premise. Impact: reduces processing time from days to minutes, ensures accountability via audit trails, integrates with CCMS without changes, and scales across departments. | GitHub Repo Link :  https://github.com/rajrasane/nyayabot

C choubeyanisha620_e663
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaSetu: Turning Court Judgments into Clear, Verified Action Plans for Government Departments
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 08:27 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem: Government departments already receive court judgments through systems like CCMS, but the issue is not access — it is what happens after that. A typical judgment is a long PDF where the actual directive is buried and written in complex legal language. In practice, officers have to manually read entire documents, deadlines are unclear or calculated manually, there is confusion between compliance and appeal, and when officers are transferred, pending work often gets lost. In many cases, problems only surface as contempt notices. There is currently no system that clearly answers what needs to be done, who should do it, and by when. >Solution: NyayaSetu addresses this gap by converting court judgments into clear, structured, and verified action plans. It identifies key directives, defines actions, assigns responsibility based on designation (not individual), and tracks timelines. This ensures continuity even when officers change. All outputs are verified by humans before being used, ensuring reliability. > How it works: (1) Reading the judgment — The system processes both digital and scanned PDFs using OCR (including regional language support) and extracts key details such as case information, order date, and directives. Each extracted field includes a confidence score and is linked to the exact source in the document. (2) Converting into actions — The system determines what needs to be done, who is responsible (role-based), and by when. It also calculates appeal deadlines and flags unclear timelines like “at the earliest” for human confirmation. (3) Human verification — A reviewer validates extracted data against the original PDF, with the ability to approve, edit, or reject. Only verified data is used further. (4) Dashboard — Officials see verified information including pending actions, deadlines, and status. When a new officer takes charge, they immediately see all pending responsibilities linked to their role. > Technical approach: The system uses OCR for scanned documents, language models for directive extraction, and rule-based logic for deadline calculations. It includes page-level PDF handling, field-level confidence scoring, source highlighting for transparency, and a complete audit trail of changes. It is designed to integrate with existing systems like CCMS rather than replace them. > Why this is practical: The system fits into existing government workflows, reduces manual effort instead of adding new steps, keeps humans in control of decisions, and works effectively even with unstructured and inconsistent documents. >Impact: NyayaSetu reduces missed deadlines, lowers legal risk, improves accountability, and ensures continuity of responsibilities even during officer transfers. >What makes it different: Unlike existing tools built for courts or lawyers, NyayaSetu is designed for government departments that must act on judgments. It focuses on execution rather than just document analysis and converts judgments into actionable tasks. >Additional capabilities (planned): Conflict detection across judgments, risk-based prioritization, multi-step action tracking, department-level compliance insights, and similar case detection.

solutions.team_name: GeM AI: Sovereign Multi-Agent Procurement Engine with Multimodal OCR & Immutable Audit
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S Saurav Thakur
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
GeM AI: Sovereign Multi-Agent Procurement Engine with Multimodal OCR & Immutable Audit
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 08:39 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
WHAT MAKES THIS DIFFERENT Not a single AI model — an autonomous network of 8 specialized agents covering the complete procurement lifecycle: Tender → Bids → Fraud Check → Compliance → Decision → Audit. No other system combines multimodal OCR, cryptographic audit-chaining, fraud detection, and sovereign local inference under one roof. 01. THE PROBLEM India processes ₹5 Lakh Crore in procurement annually across 1.6 Lakh+ buyer organizations — yet it runs on spreadsheets and institutional memory. Impact Metrics (Traditional Process vs GeM AI System):
Evaluation Time: 5–7 days (manual) vs Minutes–Hours (AI-automated)

Fraud Detection: <10% vs 70–85% auto-flagged

Audit Preparation: 2–3 weeks vs Same-day via API

Cost Savings: 2–3% vs 8–12% (4×) 50× basis: Traditional 5-day cycle (≈40 hrs) vs. ~1hr end-to-end AI evaluation on tested hardware.

Root Causes:
Officers manually parse 50–200 page tenders — error-prone, impossible to scale.

Vendors submit blurry GSTIN/PAN photos that standard OCR fails entirely.

~40% of bids carry cartelization/shell-company signals invisible to humans.

Audit logs are fully editable at DB level — post-facto tampering is trivial.

02. DOCUMENT OCR & PARSINGAgent 01 — The Spec Reader: Chunked RAG + pgvector pipeline handles 50–200 page tenders with no context window limits. Extracts Technical Specs, Financial Thresholds, GFR 2017/CVC Compliance Rules into a validated Master Digital Checklist (MDC). Pydantic v2 strict schemas + self-healing retry loop (3 attempts) eliminate hallucinations. Agent 02 — The Visionary: Dual-engine for the "Bharat Data Problem" — Local LLaVA 1.6 vision model + MinerU OCR fallback with confidence scoring (0–100). Multi-page documents processed page-by-page via sequential inference — no page or file size restriction. Supports PDF, image, scanned docs.
vision_confidence_score < 70 → MinerU OCR fallback

Score 70–89 → LOW CONFIDENCE, surfaced to officer

Cross-validates extracted vs. vendor-typed values → FORGERY FLAGS on mismatch

Zero data egress — runs entirely inside Docker

03. EXPLAINABILITY & HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP THE CORE GUARANTEE: "AI Recommends. Human Officer Decides. Always."
Every verdict is clause-cited — zero silent rejections (min. 20 chars reasoning, Pydantic-enforced)

All <90% confidence outputs → Manual Review Queue — never a black-box exclusion

Semantic Ambiguity Resolution: "50 Million" = "5 Crore" = "₹50,000,000" (auto-normalised)

Sample AI Reasoning: "FAILURE — GFR 2017 Rule 144(xi): ₹5Cr turnover required. Balance Sheet (Page 4): FY22=₹2.8Cr, FY23=₹3.1Cr, FY24=₹3.4Cr — avg ₹3.1Cr. Shortfall ₹1.9Cr. Confidence: 92%. Action: REJECT. Human override available with mandatory written justification." 5-Factor Weighted Scoring — Agent 03 (replaces manipulation-prone L1 mandate):
Technical Specification Match: 30%

Price Reasonableness (±15% Fair Market): 25%

Vendor Credibility (GeM rating, history): 20%

GFR 2017 + Make In India Compliance: 15%

Delivery Feasibility: 10% (Action: ≥ 80 → Shortlist | ≥ 60 → Manual Review | < 60 → Recommend Rejection - officer approval required)

04. AUDITABILITY: GFR 2017 + CVC COMPLIANCEAgent 06 — The Ledger:
SHA-256 hash-chained immutable audit log — disk-level alteration breaks chain, halts system, alerts all admins

PL/pgSQL triggers block UPDATE/DELETE even from DBA superusers

GFR 2017, CVC, Make In India — clause-level reasoning at every step

RTI & CAG Export API (same-day): GET /api/v1/audit/export?format=csv&tender_id=TND-2024-0055 | GET /api/v1/audit/verify-chain → {"chain_valid": true, "total_records": 1847}

05. ARCHITECTURE: 8-AGENT SOVEREIGN WORKFORCEAgent Roles:
01 The Spec Reader: RAG + pgvector → validated MDC with GFR clause references

02 The Visionary: LLaVA 1.6 + MinerU OCR → forgery detection via cross-validation

03 The Analyst: 5-factor weighted scoring → beyond manipulation-prone L1 mandate

04 The Watchdog: Cartelization, shared IPs, shell company detection → 70–85% fraud rate

05 The Policy Enforcer: GFR 2017 + CVC + Make In India — every decision legally defensible

06 The Ledger: SHA-256 immutable audit log → same-day RTI/CAG export

07 The Coordinator: Vendor-officer communication logged to audit chain

08 The Final Brain: Aggregates all 7 outputs → shortlist with risk levels + cost efficiency

Tech Stack:
Interface: Next.js 14 · React 19 · Tailwind CSS · Framer Motion

Core Engine: FastAPI (Python 3.12) · Pydantic v2 · SQLAlchemy 2.0 Async

Local AI Hub: One-click model install — no terminal required. Llama, Qwen, Gemma, Mistral, Granite. ZERO DATA EGRESS — fully local Docker inference.

Cloud Fallback: OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Groq — unlimited failover keys. Activated only under memory pressure.

Mobile Gateway: OpenClaw (Telegram/WhatsApp) — full procurement lifecycle from smartphone.

Data Layer: PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector · Redis 7

DevOps: 5-container Docker Compose — deploys in under 15 min on 16GB+ RAM

06. RISKS & MITIGATIONS
Compute Latency: Groq API fallback (~0.3s/completion) + Redis caching (30-min TTL)

AI Hallucinations: Pydantic v2 schemas + self-healing retry. All <90% confidence → Manual Review

Vision Accuracy: LLaVA 1.6 sequential inference. Below 70 → MinerU fallback. No page/size limit

Fraud False Positives: Zero auto-rejection — human officers make all final calls

Audit Tampering: PL/pgSQL blocks DB edits. SHA-256 chain breaks on any disk-level alteration

07. ROUND 2 DEMO PLAN Fully operational 5-container Docker stack — deployable in under 15 minutes.
Tender Ingestion: Upload 50+ page CRPF tender → real-time MDC extraction with GFR clause references

Heterogeneous Bids: 10+ mock bids — clean PDFs, blurry GSTIN photos, currency variations, shared-IP bids

Evaluation Matrix: Rank all 10 bids on 5-factor model with per-factor scores + GFR disqualification citations

Manual Review Queue: Low-confidence + fraud-flagged bid in split-panel interface with mandatory acknowledgement

Immutable Audit: verify-chain → {"chain_valid": true} live + CSV export for same-day RTI submission

OpenClaw Demo: "/draft 100 bulletproof vests for CRPF battalion" via Telegram → returned in 30 seconds

Success Metrics: Parsing ≥95% | OCR ≥92% | 100% seeded fraud flagged | chain_valid: true

08. CLOSING IMPACT GeM AI is not a procurement assistant — it is an autonomous procurement department. An incorruptible, 24/7 digital workforce with algorithmic precision, GFR 2017 + CVC compliance, cryptographic auditability, and human verification at every decision point. Impact Results:
Speed: 50× Faster — 5–7 days → ~1hr AI-automated

Fraud Detection: 70–85% auto-flagged (vs. <10% manually)

Audit Traceability: Same-day (vs. 2–3 weeks)

Data Security: Zero Data Leakage — fully local inference

Transparency: Zero Silent Rejections — every verdict clause-cited

Budget Savings: 8–12% (4× better than traditional)

09. REFERENCES
Live Prototype: https://gem-ai-agent.vercel.app

Demo Video: https://youtu.be/nbRa2PNhb5U

GitHub: https://github.com/sauravthakurq/GeM-AI.git

Pitch Deck: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DZurpUfsdN7-xQiA9pAfVJs0TQsIShpd/view?usp=sharing

M madhusudhan2315_a9ba
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderTrace: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Audit Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 09:40 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Problem Government organization like the Central Reserve Police Force manually evaluate hundreds of pages of bidder documents for every tender. A single tender with 10 bidders can involve 500+ pages, requiring days of effort, producing inconsistent results, and leaving no structured audit trail. Two evaluators reviewing the same documents can reach different conclusions.
Proposed SolutionAn AI-powered platform that automates the entire tender evaluation process end-to-end:Tender Understanding - The system reads the tender document and extracts all eligibility criteria (financial thresholds, technical requirements, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications) into a structured checklist. It also distinguishes mandatory from optional criteria using legal language cues such as “shall,” “must,” and “mandatory.”Bidder Document Parsing - The system processes bidder submissions across all formats — typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, and photographs. Digital PDFs are parsed using Apache PDFBox Scanned documents and images are processed using Google Vision OCR (DOCUMENT_TEXT_DETECTION), capable of handling tables, stamps, and handwritten contentEvaluation Engine - For each bidder, the system evaluates every criterion and assigns one of three verdicts: ELIGIBLE — Evidence found and threshold satisfied (with source cited) NOT ELIGIBLE — Evidence found but threshold not satisfied (with value cited) NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — Evidence is unclear, ambiguous, or not found with sufficient confidence.Explainability - Every verdict is fully explainable. The system clearly references: The criterion being evaluated The source document used The extracted value The reason for the decision There are no black-box outputs.Audit Trail Every document is hashed (SHA-256) upon upload Every processing step is timestamped and logged Evaluation records are immutable The final report includes a complete audit appendix suitable for formal government use.﻿Non-Negotiables We AddressThe system never silently disqualifies a bidder — ambiguous cases are always flagged for manual reviewAll decisions are explainable at the criterion levelSupports scanned documents and photographs, not just digital textGenerates a downloadable, audit-ready PDF report for procurement officers ﻿Technology StackBackend: Java 8, Spring Boot (REST API)AI/LLM: Google Gemini 1.5 Flash (criteria extraction and evaluation)OCR: Google Vision API (scanned documents and images)Document Processing: Apache PDFBox (PDF), Apache POI (Word)Report Generation: iText 7 (PDF output)Database: H2 (embedded)Frontend: Single-page HTML with vanilla JavaScript﻿What the Officer/ Owner Experience Looks LikeUpload the tender document → criteria extracted automaticallyUpload bidder submissions → all documents parsedClick Evaluate → criterion-by-criterion results generatedDownload report → colour-coded (green/red/amber), ready for review and sign-off

solutions.team_name: NyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment Intelligence & Verified Action Planning for Karnataka Government
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


2 2006gdivya_3669
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
NyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment Intelligence & Verified Action Planning for Karnataka Government
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 22, 2026 10:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PROBLEMKarnataka's CCMS system automatically fetches High Court judgment PDFs. But what happens next is entirely manual. A government officer opens a 50–150 page legal document to find one critical directive buried inside — what action is needed, who is responsible, and by when. They do this for dozens of judgments every week with no systematic support.The consequences are real: missed deadlines, contempt of court notices, reopened cases, summoned officials. Not because officers are negligent — but because the system gives them zero intelligent support after the PDF arrives.OUR SOLUTION — NYAYASETUNyayaSetu ("Bridge to Justice") is an AI-powered compliance platform that sits on top of CCMS without modifying it. It transforms any court judgment PDF into a verified, actionable plan in under 60 seconds.HOW IT WORKSStep 1 — Intelligent Extraction NyayaSetu ingests the judgment PDF using PyMuPDF for structural parsing (isolating the operative clause from background facts), followed by a local LLM for semantic extraction of nine fields: case number, order date, parties, court directions, responsible department, explicit deadlines, implicit deadlines, comply/appeal recommendation, and monetary orders. Every field carries a confidence score and a source pointer — exact page, paragraph, and line in the original PDF.Step 2 — Deadline Intelligence Engine Court judgments rarely give absolute dates. They say "within 30 days", "before the next hearing", "within the limitation period under Section 116A." NyayaSetu converts every such phrase into a real calendar date using the order date as anchor, statutory limitation periods, and court vacation calendars. Calculation logic is shown transparently so officers can verify and override.Step 3 — Human Verification (Mandatory) The verification screen shows the Action Plan on the left and the original PDF on the right, with every extracted field highlighted at its exact source location. Officers approve, edit, or reject each field individually. Nothing reaches the live dashboard without explicit human sign-off. Every edit, approval, and rejection is permanently logged with officer identity and timestamp.Step 4 — Verified Action Dashboard Only human-approved plans appear on the dashboard. Section officers see a prioritised queue colour-coded by urgency: green (14+ days), amber (7–14 days), red (under 7 days), black (overdue). Senior officers see department-wide compliance overview. One-click export generates formal office notes and compliance reports.WHAT MAKES NYAYASETU UNIQUEEvery existing legal AI tool in India — SUPACE, LegRAA, Adalat AI — is built for judges and lawyers doing pre-judgment research. None address what happens after a judgment arrives at a government department. NyayaSetu is the first system built specifically for post-judgment administrative compliance.Three innovations no existing system has:Source-linked confidence scoring — every extracted field traces back to the exact PDF location. Officers verify evidence, not just outputs.Multi-modal deadline intelligence — statutory, relative, court-calendar, and limitation-period deadlines all resolved to hard calendar dates with transparent logic.Department-aware action routing — when a judgment names multiple respondent departments, NyayaSetu splits and routes action items to each department's queue automatically.TECH STACKBackend: Python, FastAPI, PyMuPDF, local LLM via Ollama — all inference on-premises, zero judgment content leaves government servers. Frontend: React with PDF.js for split-screen verification with inline annotations. Database: PostgreSQL with append-only audit log. Deployment: Entirely within Karnataka government infrastructure.NON-NEGOTIABLES RESPECTEDCCMS is not modified — NyayaSetu is a read-only downstream consumer. No raw case data sent to external APIs. Every decision is explainable via source pointers, auditable via append-only logs, and reversible via the verification workflow. Human approval is architecturally enforced.﻿IMPACTKarnataka High Court disposes tens of thousands of cases annually. Every judgment with a government respondent creates compliance obligations tracked today by manual file reading. NyayaSetu makes missed court deadlines structurally impossible — not by replacing human judgment, but by ensuring humans always have the right information at the right time.When the court speaks, the government must listen — immediately, accurately, and verifiably. NyayaSetu makes that happen.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T Team Cipher
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
PRAMAN : Evidence-Based AI Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform for CRPF
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:13 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PRAMAN is an evidence-first, human-in-the-loop procurement decision platform for CRPF tender evaluation. It converts tender documents into structured eligibility rules, parses bidder submissions in any format, matches extracted evidence against those rules, and produces criterion-level PASS / FAIL / REVIEW verdicts with exact evidence, traceable reasoning, and immutable audit logs.
This directly addresses the Theme 3 requirement for explainable, auditable, criterion-by-criterion evaluation of heterogeneous tender and bidder documents.
Problem Context
Government tender evaluation is not a generic document-QA task.
It is a high-stakes procurement workflow where the system must understand:
Technical specifications

Financial thresholds

Compliance rules

Mandatory certifications

Document checklists written in formal legal language

Bidder evidence may arrive in multiple formats:
Typed PDFs

Scanned pages

Tables

Word files

Phone photographs

Stamps

Handwritten notes

Rotated pages

Multilingual documents

PRAMAN is designed specifically for this reality.
It does not replace officers — instead, it provides a defensible decision system that is:
Faster

More consistent

Easier to audit

Core Method
PRAMAN follows a strict principle: AI extracts facts, rules make decisions, humans resolve uncertainty
1. Tender Understanding

Tender is ingested and segmented into:

Clauses

Annexures

Forms

Schedules

Eligibility sections

Each criterion is extracted as an atomic rule

Classified into:

Financial

Technical

Compliance

Document-check

This distinction is critical for final eligibility decisions

2. Bidder Document Understanding

Each bidder submission goes through a multi-modal pipeline:
Multi-OCR for typed text, scans, and photos

Layout analysis for tables and multi-column documents

Handwriting/stamp/signature-aware detection

Language identification + multilingual OCR

✔ Uses primary + fallback OCR engines ✔ Low-confidence pages → sent for review (not rejected)
3. RAG and Chat with PDF

Documents are chunked with:

Page metadata

Bounding boxes

Document tags

Indexed into a vector store

Supports queries like:
“Show me the turnover proof”

“Where is the ISO certificate?”

? RAG is used for retrieval and explanation, NOT decision-making
4. Deterministic Decision Engine

Extracted values are evaluated via rule engine

Verdict logic:
PASS → Evidence satisfies criterion

FAIL → Evidence does not satisfy

REVIEW / ABSTAIN → Missing / conflicting / low-confidence

✔ Ensures no silent disqualification ✔ Ambiguity always goes to human review

Key Differentiating Features
1. Real-Time Tender Monitoring
Scrapes public portals

Categorizes tenders

Builds searchable procurement intelligence system

2. Multi-Government / Multi-Tender Support
Works across departments

Uses reusable templates & criteria libraries

3. Multi-Language Support
Page-level language detection

Preserves original + translated text

4. Notifications System
Event-driven alerts via:
Email

SMS

In-app

Triggers:
New tenders

Missing documents

Review requests

Final approvals

5. Bidder Rating & Leaderboard
Mandatory criteria = hard filters

Optional criteria = weighted scoring

Generates ranked bidder list

6. Similar Bidder Detection
Detects suspicious similarity using:
Document fingerprinting

Layout similarity

Shared metadata

Repeated details

Graph-based analysis

7. False Certificate Detection
Checks for:
OCR inconsistencies

Invalid dates

Fake issuer patterns

Signature anomalies

Duplicate certificates

8. Signature Detection
Detects stamp/signature regions

Flags missing or altered signatures

9. Immutable Audit & Hashing

Every action is hashed

Append-only logs

Tamper-evident system

10. Live Process Tracking

Tracks states like:
Pending

Reviewed

Accepted

Rejected

11. Bidder Profiles

Maintains history of:
Past tenders

Compliance record

Behavior patterns

Explainability & Auditability

Each verdict includes an evidence card:
Criterion ID, Source clause, Document reference, Page number, Extracted value, Confidence score, Verdict Reason

✔ Generates full evaluation report
Human-in-the-Loop Workflow
Ambiguous cases → review queue

Officers can:

Compare evidence

Override decisions

Add justification

✔ Every action is logged in audit trail
Architecture Overview
Tender ingestion layer

Criteria extraction layer

Mandatory/optional classification

Bidder parsing layer

Multi-OCR + layout analysis

RAG retrieval layer

Rule engine

Explainability layer

Audit & hashing

Dashboard + reports

Notification system

Technology Stack
Backend: FastAPI + Pydantic OCR: PaddleOCR, Docling, fallback engines Retrieval: Embeddings + vector DB Decision Engine: Deterministic rules Chat: RAG-based PDF querying Audit: Hashing + append-only logs Frontend: React dashboard Deployment: Docker / On-prem / VM

Round 2 Implementation Plan
Phase 1: OCR + ingestion + viewer

Phase 2: Criteria extraction

Phase 3: Bidder parsing + RAG

Phase 4: Rule engine

Phase 5: Audit + reporting

Phase 6: Dashboard + advanced features

Why PRAMAN Stands Out

PRAMAN is not a generic AI tool.
It is a legally defensible, evidence-grounded procurement co-pilot designed for:
Heterogeneous documents

Ambiguous rules

Human validation

Auditability

Real-world government use

✔ Goes beyond basics with:
Tender scraping

Multi-language support

Fraud detection

Bidder intelligence

Workflow tracking

Final Vision : We are building an AI copilot for procurement officers, not a replacement for their judgment.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


2 22je0718_5189
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderProof — AI Tender Evaluation with Criterion-Level Explainability and Immutable Audit Trail
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:25 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A procurement committee should spend its time on judgment — not on counting pages, chasing scanned certificates, or second-guessing what a bidder meant to say. TenderProof handles everything else.TenderProof is an AI-powered platform that takes a government tender document and a set of bidder submissions as input, and produces a structured, evidence-backed, criterion-level evaluation report — complete with auditable decision records and a prioritised human review queue — in minutes rather than days.﻿The ProblemFor organisations like CRPF, evaluating a competitive tender is a slow, inconsistent, and high-risk manual process. A committee may spend several days cross-checking hundreds of pages of bidder documents against a list of eligibility criteria. Two officers reviewing the same file often reach different conclusions. Scanned certificates and photographs of physical licenses are treated as a burden rather than valid evidence. And when a procurement decision is later challenged, there is frequently no structured, evidence-backed record to defend it.The problem is not just speed. It is consistency, explainability, and institutional trust.﻿Our ApproachMost AI tools ask the model to judge a bidder. TenderProof does not.We separate the problem into two strictly distinct tasks:LLM as Parser — Extract values, evidence, and document references from submissions, regardless of format.Deterministic Rule Engine as Judge — Evaluate extracted values against typed criteria using pure logic, not model inference.This separation is the foundational architectural decision. A deterministic engine produces the same verdict for the same input, every time. It is reproducible, auditable, and legally defensible. The LLM never decides eligibility. It only reads documents.﻿How It WorksStep 1 — Compile the Tender into a Typed Evaluation SchemaThe tender document is processed by an LLM that extracts every eligibility criterion into a structured, typed schema — specifying each criterion's data type (currency threshold, certificate boolean, date range, count), whether it is mandatory or optional, what evidence is acceptable, and the fallback rule if evidence is ambiguous. An officer reviews and approves this schema before evaluation begins. This one-time review takes minutes. Errors caught here prevent errors across every subsequent bidder verdict. Step 2 — Extract Bidder Evidence Without OCREvery bidder document — typed PDF, scanned certificate, Word file, or photograph of a physical licence — is converted to page images and read directly by a multimodal vision model. No traditional OCR is used. This eliminates the significant accuracy degradation that standard text-extraction pipelines suffer on scanned inputs. Each extracted value is tagged with: source document name, page number, raw extracted text, and a self-reported confidence score. Step 3 — Evaluate DeterministicallyThe rule engine compares each bidder's extracted profile against the approved schema. annual_turnover_crore >= 5.0 is a typed Boolean comparison — not a model judgment call. High-confidence extractions produce automatic verdicts. Low-confidence extractions are automatically routed to a human review queue and never silently discarded. Step 4 — Cross-Bidder Integrity AnalysisAfter individual evaluation, TenderProof runs a cross-bidder analysis layer that standard procurement tools do not provide:Document fingerprinting — Detects near-duplicate submission documents across competing bidders, a common indicator of coordinated bid rigging.Entity relationship mapping — Shared director names, GST registration numbers, registered addresses, or PAN numbers across competing bids are flagged as potential collusion signals and visualised as a relationship graph.Certificate cross-check — Identical ISO, GST, or licence certificate numbers appearing under multiple different company names are flagged as credential fraud indicators.All flags are surfaced for human review with supporting evidence. None result in automatic disqualification. Step 5 — Consolidated Report and Audit LogThe system produces a criterion-by-criterion evaluation card for every bidder, with source citations at the document and page level. A consolidated summary table shows the overall picture across all bidders. Every automated decision — input value, rule applied, and output verdict — is written to an immutable, hash-chained audit log that an officer can sign and attach to the formal procurement record.﻿Key InnovationsSchema compilation before evaluation — The tender is compiled into a typed contract that the officer verifies once, rather than re-interpreted by AI on each bidder. Reproducibility is guaranteed by design.Vision-first document extraction — Page images go directly to a multimodal model. Scanned certificates and photographs are first-class inputs, not edge cases. The OCR accuracy bottleneck is eliminated entirely.Deterministic verdict engine — The LLM sits entirely outside the verdict path. Eligibility decisions are produced by typed comparisons, not model inference. Hallucination risk in the final decision is zero.Confidence-routed human review — Ambiguous cases surface with the specific reason for ambiguity, the source document, and a suggested verdict. Officers make informed overrides, not blind ones. All overrides are logged and feed future calibration.Cross-bidder collusion and fraud detection — The only procurement evaluation platform built to actively detect bid rigging and shared credential fraud, from the evaluator's perspective rather than the supplier's.﻿Non-Negotiables: Fully AddressedRequirementHow TenderProof Addresses ItEvery verdict explainable at criterion levelRule engine logs: criterion ID → source document → page number → extracted value → rule applied → verdictNever silently disqualify a bidderConfidence below threshold → auto-routes to human review queue with reason and suggested verdictScanned documents and photographs supportedVision-first extraction; no OCR dependency at any stageAuditable end-to-end for formal procurementImmutable, hash-chained decision log; officer sign-off is a required workflow step before report exportAmbiguous cases surfaced, not suppressedHuman review queue is ranked by criterion importance × uncertainty — officers review what matters most first﻿Technology StackComponentTechnologyTender schema compilationLLM with structured output (Pydantic typed schema)Document extractionClaude 3.5 Sonnet Vision / GPT-4o Vision — page-image basedVerdict enginePython — deterministic typed comparisons, zero LLM inferenceCross-bidder analysisMinHash document similarity, NetworkX entity relationship graphBackendFastAPI + PostgreSQLOfficer dashboard and review UINext.jsAudit logAppend-only, hash-chained PostgreSQL table﻿Round 2 Implementation PlanWeekMilestoneWeek 1Tender schema compiler + officer schema verification UIWeek 2Vision-first bidder extraction pipeline + confidence scoringWeek 3Deterministic rule engine + confidence-routed human review queueWeek 4Cross-bidder integrity analysis + consolidated report generationWeek 5Immutable audit log, officer sign-off workflow, end-to-end testing on sandbox data

solutions.solution_text:

P patilyash23745_34a0
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Sentinel AI – AI-Assisted Deepfake Forensics and Victim Support Platform
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:38 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Sentinel AI

AI-Assisted Deepfake Forensics & Rapid Response System Sentinel AI is a real-time forensic and response platform designed to protect individuals from the escalating threat of AI-generated misinformation, blackmail, and identity theft. Unlike traditional tools that only offer detection, Sentinel AI provides a comprehensive pipeline from identification to legal action.

The Problem: The "Detection Dead-End"

Deepfakes are being weaponized across India for harassment and financial fraud. Current solutions are insufficient because: Passive Detection: Tools tell you a video is fake but offer no "next steps." Evidence Loss: Victims often lose or mishandle digital evidence required for prosecution. Legal Barriers: Most users do not know how to draft a formal cybercrime complaint. Viral Velocity: By the time a victim reacts, the damage is often irreversible.

The Solution: Forensic-to-Action Pipeline

Sentinel AI bridges the gap between AI forensics and legal empowerment. It transforms raw, suspicious media into a structured legal case in seconds.

Core Capabilities

Advanced Forensic Detection: Performs pixel-level analysis using CNN-based models to identify blending artifacts, lighting inconsistencies, and frequency domain anomalies. Instant Risk Verdicts: Provides clear labels (REAL, SUSPICIOUS, or FAKE) with a high-precision confidence score. Automated Evidence Structuring: Generates a unique Digital Case ID and a tamper-evident record of the forensic findings. One-Click Legal Complaint: An AI-driven engine converts user input and forensic data into a professionally structured cybercrime complaint, ready for submission to authorities.

Technical Backbone

The system is built for speed, accuracy, and modularity: Backend: High-concurrency FastAPI framework for real-time inference. Computer Vision: OpenCV for intelligent frame sampling and artifact visualization. Deep Learning: A modular pipeline utilizing state-of-the-art CNN-based deepfake detectors and Vision Transformers (ViT). Forensics: Frame-by-frame aggregation to ensure consistency across video content.

Why This Matters for Bharat

India is currently a primary target for AI-driven identity misuse. Sentinel AI is specifically engineered for the Indian context: Accessibility: Lightweight architecture deployable on standard smartphones and low-bandwidth environments. Scalability: Designed to handle the massive scale of Indian social media traffic. Actionable Impact: It shifts the focus from theoretical AI research to practical victim support.

Future Expansion

Multi-Lingual Support: Generating legal complaints in regional Indian languages. Direct API Integration: Linking directly with the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal. Social Media Monitoring: Real-time browser extensions to flag deepfakes as users browse. Blockchain Evidence: Securing case IDs on a ledger to ensure the chain of custody for digital evidence.

"Sentinel AI is not just a tool—it is a digital shield for a safer Bharat."

M meghasyam reddy
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaSetu: AI-Powered Bridge from Court Judgments to Verified Government Action Plans
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:52 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project Description – NyayaSetu: AI Bridge from Court Judgments to Verified Action PlansNyayaSetu is an AI-powered governance solution that converts complex court judgments into structured, verified, and actionable plans for government departments. Public agencies receive a large volume of legal documents daily, which are often lengthy, unstructured, and difficult to interpret. This results in delayed compliance, missed appeal deadlines, and increased legal risk.NyayaSetu addresses this problem through an end-to-end intelligent pipeline. The system ingests court judgment PDFs (both digital and scanned), extracts key legal information using OCR and Natural Language Processing, and transforms it into structured data including case details, directives, responsible departments, and critical timelines.Using advanced AI models, the platform generates clear action plans such as whether to comply with the order, file an appeal, or escalate the matter. A core feature of the system is intelligent deadline tracking, which automatically identifies limitation periods and provides timely alerts to prevent missed legal actions.To ensure trust and reliability, NyayaSetu integrates a human-in-the-loop verification mechanism where officials can review, edit, and approve AI-generated outputs before execution. The platform also provides a centralized dashboard that offers real-time visibility into pending cases, compliance status, and department-wise workload.Additional features include automated notifications (email/WhatsApp alerts), batch processing of multiple judgments, multilingual support, audit trails for accountability, compliance heatmaps, and similar case recommendations for informed decision-making.By bridging the gap between legal complexity and administrative execution, NyayaSetu improves efficiency, enhances accountability, and enables faster, data-driven governance. The system is scalable, practical, and designed to align with real-world government workflows, making it a powerful tool for modern judicial compliance management.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE

LEADERBOARD


C coders
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
JudgmentAction CCMS
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:54 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem UnderstandingGovernment departments often receive court judgment PDFs that contain important directions, deadlines, and administrative implications, but these documents are long, legally dense, and difficult to process quickly. Officials must manually read through them to determine what action is required, whether an appeal should be considered, which department is responsible, and what deadlines apply. This creates delays, increases the risk of missing important directives, and makes the workflow hard to scale.Our solution addresses this by building an AI-assisted judgment understanding system that extracts key details, generates structured action plans, highlights supporting evidence from the source PDF, and requires human verification before any output is shown in a trusted dashboard.Our SolutionWe propose JudgmentAction CCMS, a legal document understanding and decision-support platform that converts court judgment PDFs into verified action plans. The system combines OCR and layout-aware extraction, directive and timeline identification, action-plan generation, source evidence highlighting, and mandatory reviewer approval.The core design principle is that the system does not merely summarize a judgment. Instead, it produces a structured administrative output that helps departments decide what needs to be done next, who should do it, and by when, while keeping humans in control of the final approved record.How It WorksIngest judgment PDFRun OCR and layout-aware parsing for scanned or digital documentsExtract case details, parties, order date, directions, and timelinesConvert extracted information into a structured action planHighlight source evidence directly from the PDFShow confidence scores for extracted fieldsRequire reviewer approval, edit, or rejectionDisplay only verified action plans in the dashboardExtraction EngineThe document understanding layer extracts information such as:case detailsparties involveddate of orderkey directions or ordersrelevant timelines and limitation-related dates where possibleThe system is designed to handle both digital and scanned PDFs, which is important because court judgments may arrive in inconsistent formats.Action Plan GenerationThe main differentiator of the platform is the action-plan generation layer. Based on the extracted judgment content, the system structures outputs such as:whether the matter appears to require compliancewhether the matter may require consideration for appealresponsible department or authorityimportant timelinesnext action ownernature of action requiredThis makes the output directly useful for decision-making rather than only for document reading.Human Verification and Trust LayerHuman verification is mandatory in this theme, so the platform is built around a reviewer workflow. Before any extracted data is treated as trusted:the extracted record is shown with PDF highlightseach key field carries a confidence scorereviewers can approve, edit, or rejectonly approved records move to the dashboardThis creates a clean separation between machine-generated suggestions and trusted administrative action records.Why This Approach Fits the ProblemOur approach is strong for Round 1 because it matches the stated workflow almost exactly:extract information from judgment PDFsgenerate structured action planshighlight evidence from source documentsenforce human reviewdisplay only verified records in the dashboardIt is especially strong because it focuses on the required administrative output, not just on summarization.ArchitectureThe proposed architecture includes:Judgment ingestion and OCR layerLayout-aware legal extraction engineAction-plan generation moduleConfidence scoring and PDF evidence highlightingReviewer approval / edit / reject interfaceVerified dashboard for trusted action plansTechnology StackOCR + layout parser for scanned and digital PDFsLocal legal-text extraction model for structured understandingRule layer for timeline interpretation and appeal/compliance hintsDashboard with PDF evidence pane for human verification and trusted displayRound 2 Prototype PlanIf shortlisted, we will build a working prototype with:3–5 sample judgment PDFsextraction of key information with highlightsstructured action-plan generationreviewer correction and approval workflowverified dashboard cards for approved casesExpected OutcomeThe final system will help government departments move faster from receiving a court judgment to understanding the required action, while ensuring that every important field remains explainable, evidence-backed, and human-verified before it is used operationally.

solutions.team_name: SyncBridge Karnataka
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C coders
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
SyncBridge Karnataka
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 12:55 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem UnderstandingKarnataka’s Single Window System (SWS) is intended to become the State’s forward-looking business-facing entry point, while multiple department systems continue to function as the authoritative systems for existing business records. Since these systems were built independently, they use different schemas, identifiers, APIs, and update mechanisms. As a result, the same service request raised on one side often does not propagate to the other, causing state drift, duplicate effort, and inconsistent records across systems.Our solution addresses this split-brain problem by building an interoperability layer that works with existing systems as they are, without requiring any modifications to SWS or department platforms.Our SolutionWe propose SyncBridge Karnataka, a UBID-first interoperability layer that enables service requests and record updates to propagate reliably in both directions between the Single Window System and legacy department systems.The platform uses UBID as the canonical join key and converts every incoming update into a common event format. Once normalized, the event is translated into the target system’s schema using department-specific adapters, written using idempotent operations, and tracked through a complete audit trail. The system also handles retry logic, detects conflicts when multiple updates arrive close together, and applies explainable conflict-resolution policies or routes high-risk cases for manual review.How It WorksCapture updates originating from SWS or a department systemConvert each update into a canonical event formatUse UBID to identify the same business across systemsTranslate the canonical event into each target system’s schemaPerform idempotent writes to avoid duplicatesRetry safely through a durable queue when failures occurDetect conflicting updates arriving within a short windowResolve conflicts using configurable policies or escalate for manual reviewRecord every propagation step in an immutable audit logCanonical Event ModelEvery incoming update is transformed into a standard event structure containing:UBIDsource systementity typechanged fieldstimestampversion / hashThis common representation ensures that updates from heterogeneous systems can be processed uniformly before being translated and written to target systems.Two-Way Interoperability DesignFrom SWS to Department SystemsWhen a business raises or updates a service request in SWS, the platform identifies all corresponding department records linked through the same UBID. It then translates the SWS data into each department’s schema and writes the update to all applicable target systems.From Department Systems to SWSWhen a change is initiated in a department system, the platform detects it using the best available integration surface:webhookAPI pollingsnapshot comparison / diffingThe detected update is then converted into the canonical event format, joined using UBID, translated into the SWS schema, and written back into SWS.Conflict Detection and ResolutionA major challenge in two-way interoperability is handling near-simultaneous updates to the same UBID from different systems. SyncBridge Karnataka addresses this through a configurable conflict-resolution engine.Conflict policies include:field-level source prioritylatest verified write wins within a defined policy windowmanual review for high-risk conflicting fieldsThis ensures that the final system state is not silently overwritten and that every resolution remains explainable and reversible.Idempotency, Retries, and ReliabilityTo guarantee robust propagation under failures:outbound writes use idempotency keys to prevent duplicate updatesretries are managed through a durable queuereconciliation logic ensures that failed writes are surfaced and reprocessed safelyall propagation attempts are tracked end-to-endThis design supports at-least-once delivery while preventing unintended duplication and lost updates.Audit TrailEvery propagation creates an immutable audit entry that records:where the change originatedwhat fields changedhow the event was translatedwhere it was writtenwhether a conflict occurredwhat resolution policy was appliedwhether retry or review was requiredFor example, an audit trace may read:Factories → detected signatory change → mapped to canonical schema → conflict with newer SWS value → sent to reviewer / resolved by policyThis makes the system fully traceable and suitable for administrative and operational oversight.Why This Approach Fits the ProblemOur approach is strong for Round 1 because it focuses on the real engineering challenge: heterogeneous live systems that cannot be migrated or modified. Instead of proposing a big-bang replacement, we build a practical interoperability layer that works around differences in APIs, schemas, and event capabilities.It directly respects the non-negotiables:no changes to SWS or department systemsUBID used as the common join keyidempotent and fully auditable propagationcompatible with sandbox, synthetic, or scrambled dataexplainable conflict handling with human review where requiredArchitectureThe proposed architecture includes:Connector layer for SWS and department integrationsCanonical event bus for standardized change propagationSchema adapter registry for department-specific translationsConflict resolution engine for competing updatesDurable queue and retry manager for reliable processingImmutable event store and audit logOperations dashboard for monitoring, review, and traceabilityTechnology StackFastAPI or Spring Boot for backend orchestration servicesKafka or RabbitMQ for event-driven propagation and retriesPostgreSQL as the event store and audit databaseAdapter registry for per-department schema translationReact dashboard for operational monitoring, audit review, and conflict handlingRound 2 Prototype PlanIf shortlisted, we will build a working sandbox prototype with:mock SWS and 2 department APIsone polling-based connectorone webhook-based connectorcanonical event conversion and UBID-based routingidempotent propagation with retry supportconflict detection and resolution demoend-to-end audit viewer for all propagated changesExpected OutcomeThe final system will allow Karnataka’s Single Window System and legacy department systems to stay in sync without requiring system replacement or schema modification. It will ensure that updates propagate reliably in both directions, conflicts are handled transparently, and every action remains fully auditable, explainable, and operationally trustworthy.

document was used, what value was found, and why the bidder was marked Eligible, Not Eligible, or Manual Review.How It WorksParse the tender document and identify all eligibility conditionsExtract solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A Arjuna
TEAM SUBMISSION [9]
TenderSetu: A Trust-Anchored AI Platform for Explainable Tender Evaluation in Indian Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 01:04 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Documentation ﻿Government procurement in India is the invisible machinery behind every road laid, every uniform stitched, and every piece of equipment that reaches a jawan posted at a forward base. For forces like the CRPF, procurement is not an administrative formality — it is an operational lifeline. Yet the process of evaluating tender bids remains stuck in an era of manila folders, highlighter pens, and committee rooms where officers spend fourteen-hour days cross-checking hundreds of pages against dozens of eligibility clauses. Two officers looking at the same stack of documents frequently arrive at different conclusions. A scanned turnover certificate with a faded stamp can delay an entire procurement cycle by weeks. A bidder who genuinely qualifies can be rejected because their evidence was buried on page 87 of a 200-page submission. The cost of this inefficiency is not just time — it is trust, transparency, and ultimately the readiness of the forces that depend on timely procurement.TenderSetu is our answer to this problem. The name combines "Tender" with the Hindi word "Setu" (bridge) — because what government procurement actually needs is not another black-box AI that spits out verdicts, but a bridge between the precision demanded by legal compliance and the messiness of real-world bidder documentation. Our platform ingests a tender document and any number of bidder submissions, and produces a criterion-by-criterion evaluation report where every single verdict is traceable to the exact clause it tested, the exact document it examined, the exact value it extracted, and the exact reasoning it applied. No silent rejections. No unexplained approvals. No AI hallucinations hiding behind confident language.What makes TenderSetu fundamentally different from a conventional document-processing pipeline is our Evidence Provenance Graph — a structured, queryable knowledge layer that links every extracted fact to its source coordinates (document ID, page number, bounding box, OCR confidence, extraction method) and to the specific eligibility criterion it addresses. This is not metadata bolted on afterwards; it is the backbone of the system. When a procurement officer asks "why was Bidder 7 marked as Needs Manual Review for the turnover criterion?", the system does not generate a post-hoc explanation — it replays the exact chain of evidence that led to that verdict, including the OCR confidence score on the scanned balance sheet, the ambiguity in the fiscal year mentioned, and the specific legal threshold from clause 4.2.1 of the tender. This provenance graph is what makes the system genuinely auditable under CVC guidelines and GFR 2017 compliance norms, not merely "AI-assisted."Our technical architecture combines three complementary intelligence layers. The Criterion Extraction Layer uses a fine-tuned legal-domain language model to parse tender documents and extract eligibility conditions, classifying each into technical, financial, or compliance categories and tagging mandatory versus optional status through dependency parsing of statutory verbs ("shall," "must," "may," "should"). The Multi-Modal Bid Ingestion Layer handles the genuine chaos of Indian bid submissions — typed PDFs, scanned photocopies, phone photographs of physical certificates, tables with merged cells, regional-language stamps, and handwritten annotations. We use a hybrid pipeline combining PaddleOCR for Indic script resilience, LayoutLMv3 for document structure understanding, and a custom table-transformer for financial statement parsing. The Reasoning and Verdict Layer performs criterion-to-evidence matching using a retrieval-augmented approach with explicit confidence calibration, routing low-confidence cases to human reviewers with a structured doubt-articulation template rather than forcing a binary decision.Three features set TenderSetu apart from anything currently in this space. First, our Adversarial Forgery Detection Module uses a vision transformer trained on synthetic forged certificates to flag documents where stamps, signatures, or figures show statistical inconsistencies with genuine government-issued templates — a critical capability given the documented prevalence of forged experience certificates and turnover statements in Indian tenders. Second, our Criterion Drift Detector compares the tender's eligibility language against a corpus of past CRPF tenders and flags clauses that deviate significantly from standard procurement language, helping officers catch both typos and potentially tailored specifications that might indicate favoritism. Third, our Doubt Ledger is a structured, immutable log of every case the AI routed for manual review, creating an institutional memory that both trains future model versions and serves as evidence during post-award audits or CAG reviews.TenderSetu is designed from day one for the realities of government deployment. It runs on-premise within CRPF's existing infrastructure with no data leaving the sandbox, supports air-gapped operation for sensitive procurements, integrates with the existing GeM and CPPP workflows through standardized export formats, and produces evaluation reports in the exact format that procurement committees already sign off on. Every model we use is either open-source or trainable on government hardware — no vendor lock-in, no API dependencies on foreign servers, no mystery costs. The system is not a replacement for procurement officers; it is a force multiplier that turns their fourteen-hour evaluation days into two-hour review sessions, while making every decision more defensible than before. In a country that spent over ₹14 lakh crore on public procurement last year, a platform that makes this process faster, fairer, and fully auditable is not a convenience — it is infrastructure for a more trustworthy state.
