A anjanak72_tech03
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Veracta - Turning pages of law into paths of action.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 04:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Veracta is an AI-powered system built to help government departments handle court judgments more easily and efficiently. Today, most court orders are available as long and complex PDF documents. Officials need to read the entire document to understand what action is required, whether they need to comply with the order or file an appeal, and what deadlines they must follow. This manual process takes time and can lead to delays or missed actions. Veracta solves this problem by converting court judgments into simple, clear, and verified action plans.﻿The system works by first accepting court judgment PDFs, whether they are scanned or digital. It extracts text from these documents using tools like Tesseract OCR for scanned files and pdf-parse for digital PDFs. Once the text is extracted, it is processed using advanced AI models from OpenAI, managed through LangChain. These tools help the system understand the legal content and identify important information such as case details, involved parties, judgment date, key directions, and timelines.﻿After extracting the information, Veracta generates an action plan. This is the most important part of the system and directly aligns with the theme “From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans.” Instead of just showing raw legal text, the system converts it into clear steps, such as whether the department needs to take action, consider an appeal, or follow a specific deadline. It also suggests the responsible department and priority level, making it easier for officials to act quickly and correctly.﻿To ensure accuracy and trust, Veracta includes a human verification step. The extracted data and action plan are shown alongside the original PDF using react-pdf, with highlighted sections that show where each piece of information came from. Users can review the results, make changes if needed, and approve or reject them. This ensures that only correct and verified information is used, which is very important in government workflows.﻿Once verified, the final data is displayed on a simple dashboard built using Next.js and powered by Node.js. All data is stored in MongoDB, allowing easy access and management. The dashboard shows important actions, deadlines, and department-wise tasks in a clean and organized way, helping decision-makers take quick and informed actions.﻿Veracta’s features include intelligent document reading, AI-based data extraction, automatic action plan generation, human verification with source highlighting, and a clean dashboard for trusted decision-making. All these features work together to reduce manual effort, improve accuracy, and speed up government processes.﻿In simple terms, Veracta helps transform complex legal documents into clear, verified actions. It connects court judgments directly to real-world decisions, making governance faster, smarter, and more reliable.

Design a system to detect phishing, scams, or suspicious activities in real-time using AI or rule-based approaches.

Project Description
Problem & Proposed Solution Problem Description :- ﻿The Statistic: 1 email out of every 99 is a phishing scam.•The Development: Cyber-criminals now employ advanced AI algorithms for social engineering which appear 100 percent professional and have gone past broken English.•The Weakness: Mobile users are thrice as vulnerable to a “smishing” message on their phones due to smaller screens.•Hyper-Scale Threats: Phishing attacks have increased by 300% annually, with millions of new malicious URLs created every month.﻿Proposed Solution :-﻿•The Mission: A hyper-scale, real-time cybersecurity engine that bridges the gap between massive data and human-centric accuracy.•Hybrid Intelligence: The first of its kind to fuse Deep Machine Learning (trained on 5.3M+ samples) with Contextual Heuristics to understand "who" the user is.﻿List of Key Features offered by the Solution :-﻿•Massive Memory: The AI has studied over 5 Million examples of scams to learn exactly how hackers think.•Double-Check System: It uses two different "brains"—one for checking websites and one for checking text messages.•Understand Work Emails: It knows the difference between a real internship offer and a fake job scam.•Instant Results: You get a safety verdict in less than half a second—no waiting required.•Easy to Read: Uses simple Green, Yellow, and Red colors to tell you if something is safe or dangerous.•Total Privacy: The AI never saves your messages. It reads them once to check for danger and then forgets them instantly.•Stops SMS Scams: Specialized in catching "Smishing" (fake texts) about bank accounts or package deliveries.•Always Available: Since it lives in the "Cloud," you can use it anywhere in the world, 24/7.﻿Use Cases & Target Audience 1. Individual Users: Protection against everyday Smishing (SMS scams) and fake delivery links.2. Students & Professionals: Ensuring legitimate internship and job opportunities aren't lost in spam folders.3. Senior Citizen Safety: Preventing "Grandparent Scams" and bank fraud via SMS.4. Corporate Security: A lightweight secondary check for HR and Finance departments who handle high volumes of links.5. Enterprise Integration: Potential for lightweight API integration into corporate email gateways for an extra layer of "Human-Logic" verification.

solutions.team_name: ProcureShield AI - Explainable Tender Eligibility Evaluation Platform for Government Procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


P Playing cards and I'm missing a queen
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
ProcureShield AI - Explainable Tender Eligibility Evaluation Platform for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 05:47 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding﻿Government procurement bodies like CRPF issue tenders with dozens of eligibility criteria spread across lengthy legal documents. Evaluating bid submissions manually is slow, inconsistent, and prone to human error. Two evaluators looking at the same documents often reach different conclusions. A single tender can take a committee days to evaluate. There is no audit trail, and silently disqualifying ambiguous bidders creates legal risk.﻿ The real challenge is not just reading PDFs, it is extracting structured eligibility criteria from formal legal language, parsing heterogeneous bidder documents (typed PDFs, scanned certificates, photographs, Word files), matching evidence to criteria consistently, and surfacing every uncertain case for human review with a clear explanation.﻿﻿Proposed Solution: ProcureShield AI is a three-stage platform that transforms the procurement evaluation workflow: automated criterion extraction from tender documents, evidence-matching against bidder submissions, a human-in-the-loop verification and audit layer, powered by a large language model (LLM) as the core AI reasoning engine.﻿Stage 1 :- Tender Intelligence Engine﻿Using an LLM's document understanding and long-context capabilities, the system ingests the tender document and extracts all eligibility criteria in structured JSON form: financial thresholds (e.g., minimum annual turnover Rs. 5 crore), technical requirements (e.g., 3+ similar projects in 5 years), compliance conditions (e.g., valid GST registration), and certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001). Each criterion is tagged as mandatory or optional, assigned a criterion ID, and stored in a criteria registry for that tender.﻿Stage 2 :- Bidder Document Parser and Evaluator﻿For each bidder submission, the system uses a multi-modal extraction pipeline. Digital PDFs and Word files are parsed directly via the LLM's document understanding. Scanned documents and photographs are processed through an OCR layer (Tesseract / Google Document AI) before passing to the LLM for structured extraction. For each criterion, the system extracts the relevant value from the bidder's documents and returns a verdict: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review, along with the specific document, page, and extracted value that drove the verdict. No bidder is silently disqualified; every ambiguous case (low-confidence OCR, missing document, unclear language) is flagged for human review with a detailed reason.﻿Stage 3 :- Human-in-the-Loop Verification and Audit﻿A procurement officer reviews all automated verdicts through a structured review interface. For each criterion, the officer sees: the criterion text, the extracted value, the source document reference, the AI confidence score, and the AI verdict. The officer can approve, override, or escalate each verdict. All reviewer decisions are logged with timestamps and user IDs. The system exports a final consolidated evaluation report that carries a full audit trail of every automated decision and every human override, suitable for formal government procurement records.﻿﻿Architecture Overview﻿The platform is built on a modular pipeline: Document Ingestion Layer (PDF/DOCX/image upload, OCR pre-processing for scans) → Criterion Extraction Module (LLM-powered structured extraction from tender documents) → Bidder Evidence Extraction Module (per-criterion evidence extraction from heterogeneous documents) → Eligibility Scoring Engine (criterion-level verdict with confidence scoring) → Human Review Interface (React/Next.js web app with source-highlighting PDF viewer) → Audit and Export Module (tamper-evident audit log, PDF/Excel report generation). A large language model with long-context support serves as the AI backbone for both criterion extraction and bidder evidence parsing, enabling it to handle multi-hundred-page tender documents without chunking artifacts.﻿﻿Key Technology Choices﻿Large Language Model API (personally trained)Core AI for legal document understanding, criterion extraction, and evidence matching. Chosen for superior long-document comprehension, instruction-following precision, and explainability. Tesseract OCR / Google DocumentScanned document and photograph digitization. PostgreSQL - structured storage of criteria, extracted values, verdicts, and audit logs. Next.js + PDF.jsWeb interface with inline PDF source-highlighting for reviewer workflow.FastAPI (Python)Backend orchestration layer.Handling Edge CasesScanned certificates with low-resolution text: confidence score below threshold routed to manual review with the raw OCR output shown. Ambiguous financial figures (different fiscal year bases): Flagged with a note explaining the ambiguity.Missing documents: Explicitly surfaced as "Document not found cannot evaluate" rather than a silent fail. Contradictory values across documents: Conflict detected and surfaced with both values for human decision.Language variation in legal text (e.g., "aggregate annual receipts" vs "total annual turnover")The LLM's semantic understanding handles equivalent phrasings.﻿Real-World DeployabilityNo source system changes are required. The platform integrates via document upload APIs. All AI decisions run on submitted documents no raw PII is sent to external services beyond what the procurement officer already handles. The audit trail meets the evidentiary standards required for government procurement challenges. The human-in-the-loop path ensures no automated verdict is final without officer sign-off.﻿﻿Scalability: The LLM API will supports concurrent requests; the evaluation pipeline is stateless and horizontally scalable.

solutions.team_name: NyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment Analysis and Verified Action Plan Generator
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


P Playing cards and I'm missing a queen
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment Analysis and Verified Action Plan Generator
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 06:01 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding﻿The Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) used by the Centre for e-Governance fetches court judgment PDFs automatically from the High Court's CIS via API. However, once a judgment lands in CCMS, the heavy lifting is entirely manual: officials must read dense, multi-page legal documents, identify critical directives, determine whether to comply or appeal, identify the responsible department, and track limitation periods. This is time-consuming, error-prone, and creates real risk of missing legally binding directives.﻿The core challenge is twofold: (1) extracting structured, reliable information from complex, inconsistent legal text — whether digital or scanned — and (2) converting that raw information into a clear, actionable plan that a government officer can trust, verify, and act on. Simply summarising a judgment is not enough; the system must produce verified, department-attributed action plans with human sign-off before they enter any workflow.﻿Proposed Solution: NyayaSetu﻿NyayaSetu ("justice bridge" in Sanskrit) is a four-stage pipeline that transforms court judgment PDFs into human-verified, department-attributed action plans displayed on a government-grade dashboard. The AI layer handles extraction and structuring; a mandatory human review layer ensures every piece of data is verified before it influences any decision.﻿Stage 1 — Judgment Ingestion and Pre-Processing﻿When CCMS fetches a new judgment PDF via the High Court API, NyayaSetu receives it automatically. Digital PDFs are processed directly by an LLM with long-context document understanding. Scanned PDFs (common with older judgments) pass through an OCR pipeline (Tesseract with layout analysis, or Google Document AI for complex layouts) before text is sent to the LLM. The system stores both the original PDF and the extracted text, maintaining a permanent, tamper-evident link between extracted data and its source document.﻿Stage 2 — Structured Extraction﻿An LLM extracts the following fields in structured JSON form: Case number and title, Date of order, Court and bench details, Parties involved (petitioner, respondent, and their departments), Key directions and orders (each direction extracted as a separate item), Explicit timelines and deadlines (e.g., "within 8 weeks"), Nature of the order (compliance required, contempt risk, interim stay, final disposal), and Subject matter tags (land, service, infrastructure, etc.). Each extracted field is linked to the specific page and paragraph from the source PDF so reviewers can instantly verify any value. Confidence scores accompany each extraction, and low-confidence fields are flagged automatically for reviewer attention.﻿Stage 3 — AI-Assisted Action Plan Generation﻿Based on the extracted information, the LLM generates a draft structured action plan covering: What is required (comply, file appeal, file review petition, obtain stay), Key timelines (explicit deadlines from the judgment, plus inferred deadlines based on statutory limitation periods for appeals under the relevant rules), Responsible department(s) (inferred from the subject matter and parties involved), Nature and priority of action (urgent compliance, routine compliance, legal strategy decision), and a plain-language summary of the judgment for non-legal officers. The action plan is presented as a draft — clearly marked as AI-generated and unverified — until a human reviewer approves it.﻿Stage 4 — Human-in-the-Loop Verification (Mandatory)﻿Before any extracted data or action plan is used, a designated reviewer (a legal officer or section officer) must go through the verification workflow. The interface presents: each extracted field alongside a highlighted excerpt from the source PDF, the AI confidence score for each field, and the draft action plan. The reviewer can approve, edit, or reject each item individually. A field edited by a reviewer is marked as "Human Verified — Corrected." Only after full review does a record move to the verified dashboard. This is not an optional step — unverified records cannot enter any downstream workflow.﻿Stage 5 — Dashboard for Decision-Makers﻿The verified dashboard shows only approved action plans. It provides a department-wise view of pending actions, filtering by urgency, deadline, department, and action type. Each record shows: case reference, key directions, action required, deadline, responsible department, and compliance status. Color-coded deadline tracking flags cases approaching limitation periods. The dashboard is designed for government use — clean, readable, and actionable without requiring legal expertise.﻿Architecture Overview﻿The system is built as a modular pipeline: PDF Ingestion Layer (API listener for CCMS webhook, OCR pre-processing for scanned documents) → Extraction Module (LLM-powered structured field extraction with source citation) → Action Plan Generator (LLM drafts action plan from extracted structured data) → Human Review Interface (Next.js web app with side-by-side PDF viewer and field-by-field approval) → Verified Data Store (PostgreSQL with audit log of all extractions, edits, approvals) → Dashboard (React-based department-wise action plan view with deadline tracking).﻿The LLM is used for both extraction and action plan drafting. Its long-context capability is essential for handling multi-hundred-page High Court judgments. The human review interface uses PDF.js to highlight the specific passage that justifies each extracted value, making reviewer verification fast and reliable.﻿Key Technology Choices﻿Large Language Model (LLM) — core AI for legal document understanding, structured extraction, and action plan drafting. Chosen for its ability to handle complex legal language, infer implicit timelines, and attribute actions to the correct parties. Tesseract OCR / Google Document AI — for scanned and low-quality PDF digitization. PostgreSQL — structured storage of extracted records, verification status, reviewer actions, and audit logs. Next.js + PDF.js — web interface with inline passage-highlighting for reviewer verification workflow. FastAPI (Python) — backend orchestration and CCMS integration layer.﻿Handling Edge Cases﻿Scanned judgments with degraded text: OCR confidence score gates routing — poor-quality extractions are flagged for manual entry rather than passed to the LLM. Multi-party judgments with complex direction structures: the LLM extracts each direction as an independent item to avoid conflation. Implicit deadlines (e.g., "within a reasonable time"): flagged as "Deadline not explicit — review required" rather than guessing. Judgments in regional languages or mixed language: language detection applied at ingestion; non-English sections flagged for human reviewer. Conflicting extracted values: both values surfaced with their source citations so the reviewer can decide.﻿Real-World Deployability﻿NyayaSetu integrates with CCMS via the existing PDF-fetching API — no modifications to CCMS or the High Court CIS are required. The platform processes PDFs on secure infrastructure; no judgment text leaves the government's environment. The verified audit trail provides legal defensibility for every action plan. The human-in-the-loop architecture ensures the system is a decision-support tool, not an autonomous actor.﻿Implementation Plan for Round 2﻿Week 1: Set up PDF ingestion pipeline, OCR integration, and LLM-based structured extraction on sample High Court judgment PDFs. Week 2: Build action plan generation module and confidence scoring. Week 3: Develop human review interface with PDF.js source-highlighting and PostgreSQL audit logging. Week 4: End-to-end integration, verified dashboard, and demo on a set of 10 representative synthetic judgment documents.﻿Risks and Mitigations﻿LLM hallucination on legal text: mitigated by requiring source citations for every extracted field and by mandatory human review before any record moves to the dashboard. OCR errors on old scanned judgments: mitigated by confidence-gated routing — poor OCR triggers manual review rather than propagating errors. Reviewer bottleneck: mitigated by AI pre-filling all fields so reviewers only need to validate, not re-enter data; estimated review time per judgment is under 5 minutes. Legal language variation across judges and courts: the LLM is prompted to extract semantics, not surface patterns, making it robust to stylistic variation.﻿Why This Wins﻿NyayaSetu directly solves every problem in the CCMS pain statement: it handles both digital and scanned PDFs, extracts all critical fields including implicit timelines, generates structured action plans with department attribution, enforces human verification before any data enters the workflow, and presents a clean dashboard that non-legal government officers can use confidently. The architecture is deployable as a CCMS plugin with zero changes to existing systems.

TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
LegalAI Resolver
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 06:26 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
We are building an AI agentic pipeline that automates pre-litigation dispute resolution end to end. A user describes their dispute in plain English, and five specialized AI agents run sequentially — classifying the case, mapping applicable Indian statutes, calculating win probability and optimal settlement range, generating court-ready legal documents, and mediating between both parties across up to three structured rounds. If both parties reach agreement, a verified Settlement Agreement is auto-generated and delivered. If negotiation fails after three rounds, a complete court filing package is automatically prepared with all evidence, legal citations, and filing steps. What takes lawyers months and costs Rs. 50,000 minimum, our platform resolves in 48 hours at near zero cost.

J jainangel020_0856
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NividaAI — AI-Powered Explainable Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis Platform for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 03:07 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Title NividaAI, AI-Powered Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis Platform for Government Procurement﻿DescriptionGovernment tender evaluation in India is entirely manual today. Officers spend days reading hundreds of pages of vendor documents, two evaluators reading the same file often reach different conclusions, and there is no structured audit trail behind any decision. NividaAI fixes this.NividaAI is a dual-interface web platform built for both government officers and vendors. Officers log in as Administrator to upload tenders and run evaluations. Vendors log in to submit their documents directly. The AI engine running behind the scenes is completely hidden from vendors.Here is what the system does. The officer uploads a tender document. The AI reads it, extracts every eligibility criterion, and generates a structured summary separating mandatory from optional requirements. The officer reviews and confirms this list. Vendors then upload their bid documents in any format, whether typed PDFs, scanned files, Word documents, or phone photographs. The AI reads every document, highlights the key figures and values relevant to each criterion, and evaluates each vendor automatically.Every verdict is one of three outcomes. Pass means the requirement is clearly met. Fail comes with a mandatory warning prompting the officer to manually verify before the decision is treated as final. Needs Review means the system was not confident enough and escalates the case to the officer with a full explanation. No vendor is ever silently disqualified.Every decision cites exactly which criterion was checked, which document was used, what value was found, and why the verdict was given. Fraud patterns such as duplicate submissions and suspiciously identical figures across vendors are flagged automatically. At the end, a signed, exportable PDF report is generated that is ready for filing, archiving, or producing in court.The platform supports Hindi, English, and Kannada, is built on React, FastAPI, Claude API, and PostgreSQL, and is deployable on free-tier cloud infrastructure making it immediately accessible without heavy setup costs.NividaAI turns days of manual document checking into minutes of consistent, explainable, and fully auditable evaluation.﻿﻿ https://1drv.ms/p/c/6675de0ad7bb9725/IQBtp8gJR69TRIgybvhDMd3JATr6WaKOQQPRATGoR8to8ZY?e=RgrIvL ﻿Edit link

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T Team Astra
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
BidOptic: Layout-Aware Parsing and Rule-Bound Evaluation for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 03:52 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tenders operate under tight legal constraints, and a single evaluation error can derail the entire process. Bidders submit a complete mess of file formats—from clean digital PDFs to blurry, scanned financial tables. To handle this safely, BidOptic completely separates the AI data extraction phase from the actual decision-making logic.Instead of letting an AI model "guess" the outcome and risk hallucinated results, we use LayoutLMv3 to understand the physical structure of the documents and Flan-T5 to pull out the exact text. Once those values are extracted, a hard-coded Python rule engine runs the actual Pass/Fail checks. This guarantees the math is perfectly accurate and ensures no bidder is ever silently disqualified.For text-based requirements, we use DeBERTa to check if the bidder's language matches the tender's rules. If a scan is too low-quality or the wording is ambiguous, the system stops and immediately flags it to a human-in-the-loop React dashboard. To make the entire process auditable, BidOptic links every extracted data point to its exact pixel coordinates on the original file. This gives CRPF officers a side-by-side visual reference so they can verify decisions quickly, speeding up the evaluation without risking legal compliance.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A anjanak72_tech03
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Veracta - Turning pages of law into paths of action.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 04:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Veracta is an AI-powered system built to help government departments handle court judgments more easily and efficiently. Today, most court orders are available as long and complex PDF documents. Officials need to read the entire document to understand what action is required, whether they need to comply with the order or file an appeal, and what deadlines they must follow. This manual process takes time and can lead to delays or missed actions. Veracta solves this problem by converting court judgments into simple, clear, and verified action plans.﻿The system works by first accepting court judgment PDFs, whether they are scanned or digital. It extracts text from these documents using tools like Tesseract OCR for scanned files and pdf-parse for digital PDFs. Once the text is extracted, it is processed using advanced AI models from OpenAI, managed through LangChain. These tools help the system understand the legal content and identify important information such as case details, involved parties, judgment date, key directions, and timelines.﻿After extracting the information, Veracta generates an action plan. This is the most important part of the system and directly aligns with the theme “From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans.” Instead of just showing raw legal text, the system converts it into clear steps, such as whether the department needs to take action, consider an appeal, or follow a specific deadline. It also suggests the responsible department and priority level, making it easier for officials to act quickly and correctly.﻿To ensure accuracy and trust, Veracta includes a human verification step. The extracted data and action plan are shown alongside the original PDF using react-pdf, with highlighted sections that show where each piece of information came from. Users can review the results, make changes if needed, and approve or reject them. This ensures that only correct and verified information is used, which is very important in government workflows.﻿Once verified, the final data is displayed on a simple dashboard built using Next.js and powered by Node.js. All data is stored in MongoDB, allowing easy access and management. The dashboard shows important actions, deadlines, and department-wise tasks in a clean and organized way, helping decision-makers take quick and informed actions.﻿Veracta’s features include intelligent document reading, AI-based data extraction, automatic action plan generation, human verification with source highlighting, and a clean dashboard for trusted decision-making. All these features work together to reduce manual effort, improve accuracy, and speed up government processes.﻿In simple terms, Veracta helps transform complex legal documents into clear, verified actions. It connects court judgments directly to real-world decisions, making governance faster, smarter, and more reliable.

solutions.team_name: ProcureShield AI - Explainable Tender Eligibility Evaluation Platform for Government Procurement
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


P Playing cards and I'm missing a queen
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
ProcureShield AI - Explainable Tender Eligibility Evaluation Platform for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 05:47 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding﻿Government procurement bodies like CRPF issue tenders with dozens of eligibility criteria spread across lengthy legal documents. Evaluating bid submissions manually is slow, inconsistent, and prone to human error. Two evaluators looking at the same documents often reach different conclusions. A single tender can take a committee days to evaluate. There is no audit trail, and silently disqualifying ambiguous bidders creates legal risk.﻿ The real challenge is not just reading PDFs, it is extracting structured eligibility criteria from formal legal language, parsing heterogeneous bidder documents (typed PDFs, scanned certificates, photographs, Word files), matching evidence to criteria consistently, and surfacing every uncertain case for human review with a clear explanation.﻿﻿Proposed Solution: ProcureShield AI is a three-stage platform that transforms the procurement evaluation workflow: automated criterion extraction from tender documents, evidence-matching against bidder submissions, a human-in-the-loop verification and audit layer, powered by a large language model (LLM) as the core AI reasoning engine.﻿Stage 1 :- Tender Intelligence Engine﻿Using an LLM's document understanding and long-context capabilities, the system ingests the tender document and extracts all eligibility criteria in structured JSON form: financial thresholds (e.g., minimum annual turnover Rs. 5 crore), technical requirements (e.g., 3+ similar projects in 5 years), compliance conditions (e.g., valid GST registration), and certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001). Each criterion is tagged as mandatory or optional, assigned a criterion ID, and stored in a criteria registry for that tender.﻿Stage 2 :- Bidder Document Parser and Evaluator﻿For each bidder submission, the system uses a multi-modal extraction pipeline. Digital PDFs and Word files are parsed directly via the LLM's document understanding. Scanned documents and photographs are processed through an OCR layer (Tesseract / Google Document AI) before passing to the LLM for structured extraction. For each criterion, the system extracts the relevant value from the bidder's documents and returns a verdict: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review, along with the specific document, page, and extracted value that drove the verdict. No bidder is silently disqualified; every ambiguous case (low-confidence OCR, missing document, unclear language) is flagged for human review with a detailed reason.﻿Stage 3 :- Human-in-the-Loop Verification and Audit﻿A procurement officer reviews all automated verdicts through a structured review interface. For each criterion, the officer sees: the criterion text, the extracted value, the source document reference, the AI confidence score, and the AI verdict. The officer can approve, override, or escalate each verdict. All reviewer decisions are logged with timestamps and user IDs. The system exports a final consolidated evaluation report that carries a full audit trail of every automated decision and every human override, suitable for formal government procurement records.﻿﻿Architecture Overview﻿The platform is built on a modular pipeline: Document Ingestion Layer (PDF/DOCX/image upload, OCR pre-processing for scans) → Criterion Extraction Module (LLM-powered structured extraction from tender documents) → Bidder Evidence Extraction Module (per-criterion evidence extraction from heterogeneous documents) → Eligibility Scoring Engine (criterion-level verdict with confidence scoring) → Human Review Interface (React/Next.js web app with source-highlighting PDF viewer) → Audit and Export Module (tamper-evident audit log, PDF/Excel report generation). A large language model with long-context support serves as the AI backbone for both criterion extraction and bidder evidence parsing, enabling it to handle multi-hundred-page tender documents without chunking artifacts.﻿﻿Key Technology Choices﻿Large Language Model API (personally trained)Core AI for legal document understanding, criterion extraction, and evidence matching. Chosen for superior long-document comprehension, instruction-following precision, and explainability. Tesseract OCR / Google DocumentScanned document and photograph digitization. PostgreSQL - structured storage of criteria, extracted values, verdicts, and audit logs. Next.js + PDF.jsWeb interface with inline PDF source-highlighting for reviewer workflow.FastAPI (Python)Backend orchestration layer.Handling Edge CasesScanned certificates with low-resolution text: confidence score below threshold routed to manual review with the raw OCR output shown. Ambiguous financial figures (different fiscal year bases): Flagged with a note explaining the ambiguity.Missing documents: Explicitly surfaced as "Document not found cannot evaluate" rather than a silent fail. Contradictory values across documents: Conflict detected and surfaced with both values for human decision.Language variation in legal text (e.g., "aggregate annual receipts" vs "total annual turnover")The LLM's semantic understanding handles equivalent phrasings.﻿Real-World DeployabilityNo source system changes are required. The platform integrates via document upload APIs. All AI decisions run on submitted documents no raw PII is sent to external services beyond what the procurement officer already handles. The audit trail meets the evidentiary standards required for government procurement challenges. The human-in-the-loop path ensures no automated verdict is final without officer sign-off.﻿﻿Scalability: The LLM API will supports concurrent requests; the evaluation pipeline is stateless and horizontally scalable.

Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 06:01 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding﻿The Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) used by the Centre for e-Governance fetches court judgment PDFs automatically from the High Court's CIS via API. However, once a judgment lands in CCMS, the heavy lifting is entirely manual: officials must read dense, multi-page legal documents, identify critical directives, determine whether to comply or appeal, identify the responsible department, and track limitation periods. This is time-consuming, error-prone, and creates real risk of missing legally binding directives.﻿The core challenge is twofold: (1) extracting structured, reliable information from complex, inconsistent legal text — whether digital or scanned — and (2) converting that raw information into a clear, actionable plan that a government officer can trust, verify, and act on. Simply summarising a judgment is not enough; the system must produce verified, department-attributed action plans with human sign-off before they enter any workflow.﻿Proposed Solution: NyayaSetu﻿NyayaSetu ("justice bridge" in Sanskrit) is a four-stage pipeline that transforms court judgment PDFs into human-verified, department-attributed action plans displayed on a government-grade dashboard. The AI layer handles extraction and structuring; a mandatory human review layer ensures every piece of data is verified before it influences any decision.﻿Stage 1 — Judgment Ingestion and Pre-Processing﻿When CCMS fetches a new judgment PDF via the High Court API, NyayaSetu receives it automatically. Digital PDFs are processed directly by an LLM with long-context document understanding. Scanned PDFs (common with older judgments) pass through an OCR pipeline (Tesseract with layout analysis, or Google Document AI for complex layouts) before text is sent to the LLM. The system stores both the original PDF and the extracted text, maintaining a permanent, tamper-evident link between extracted data and its source document.﻿Stage 2 — Structured Extraction﻿An LLM extracts the following fields in structured JSON form: Case number and title, Date of order, Court and bench details, Parties involved (petitioner, respondent, and their departments), Key directions and orders (each direction extracted as a separate item), Explicit timelines and deadlines (e.g., "within 8 weeks"), Nature of the order (compliance required, contempt risk, interim stay, final disposal), and Subject matter tags (land, service, infrastructure, etc.). Each extracted field is linked to the specific page and paragraph from the source PDF so reviewers can instantly verify any value. Confidence scores accompany each extraction, and low-confidence fields are flagged automatically for reviewer attention.﻿Stage 3 — AI-Assisted Action Plan Generation﻿Based on the extracted information, the LLM generates a draft structured action plan covering: What is required (comply, file appeal, file review petition, obtain stay), Key timelines (explicit deadlines from the judgment, plus inferred deadlines based on statutory limitation periods for appeals under the relevant rules), Responsible department(s) (inferred from the subject matter and parties involved), Nature and priority of action (urgent compliance, routine compliance, legal strategy decision), and a plain-language summary of the judgment for non-legal officers. The action plan is presented as a draft — clearly marked as AI-generated and unverified — until a human reviewer approves it.﻿Stage 4 — Human-in-the-Loop Verification (Mandatory)﻿Before any extracted data or action plan is used, a designated reviewer (a legal officer or section officer) must go through the verification workflow. The interface presents: each extracted field alongside a highlighted excerpt from the source PDF, the AI confidence score for each field, and the draft action plan. The reviewer can approve, edit, or reject each item individually. A field edited by a reviewer is marked as "Human Verified — Corrected." Only after full review does a record move to the verified dashboard. This is not an optional step — unverified records cannot enter any downstream workflow.﻿Stage 5 — Dashboard for Decision-Makers﻿The verified dashboard shows only approved action plans. It provides a department-wise view of pending actions, filtering by urgency, deadline, department, and action type. Each record shows: case reference, key directions, action required, deadline, responsible department, and compliance status. Color-coded deadline tracking flags cases approaching limitation periods. The dashboard is designed for government use — clean, readable, and actionable without requiring legal expertise.﻿Architecture Overview﻿The system is built as a modular pipeline: PDF Ingestion Layer (API listener for CCMS webhook, OCR pre-processing for scanned documents) → Extraction Module (LLM-powered structured field extraction with source citation) → Action Plan Generator (LLM drafts action plan from extracted structured data) → Human Review Interface (Next.js web app with side-by-side PDF viewer and field-by-field approval) → Verified Data Store (PostgreSQL with audit log of all extractions, edits, approvals) → Dashboard (React-based department-wise action plan view with deadline tracking).﻿The LLM is used for both extraction and action plan drafting. Its long-context capability is essential for handling multi-hundred-page High Court judgments. The human review interface uses PDF.js to highlight the specific passage that justifies each extracted value, making reviewer verification fast and reliable.﻿Key Technology Choices﻿Large Language Model (LLM) — core AI for legal document understanding, structured extraction, and action plan drafting. Chosen for its ability to handle complex legal language, infer implicit timelines, and attribute actions to the correct parties. Tesseract OCR / Google Document AI — for scanned and low-quality PDF digitization. PostgreSQL — structured storage of extracted records, verification status, reviewer actions, and audit logs. Next.js + PDF.js — web interface with inline passage-highlighting for reviewer verification workflow. FastAPI (Python) — backend orchestration and CCMS integration layer.﻿Handling Edge Cases﻿Scanned judgments with degraded text: OCR confidence score gates routing — poor-quality extractions are flagged for manual entry rather than passed to the LLM. Multi-party judgments with complex direction structures: the LLM extracts each direction as an independent item to avoid conflation. Implicit deadlines (e.g., "within a reasonable time"): flagged as "Deadline not explicit — review required" rather than guessing. Judgments in regional languages or mixed language: language detection applied at ingestion; non-English sections flagged for human reviewer. Conflicting extracted values: both values surfaced with their source citations so the reviewer can decide.﻿Real-World Deployability﻿NyayaSetu integrates with CCMS via the existing PDF-fetching API — no modifications to CCMS or the High Court CIS are required. The platform processes PDFs on secure infrastructure; no judgment text leaves the government's environment. The verified audit trail provides legal defensibility for every action plan. The human-in-the-loop architecture ensures the system is a decision-support tool, not an autonomous actor.﻿Implementation Plan for Round 2﻿Week 1: Set up PDF ingestion pipeline, OCR integration, and LLM-based structured extraction on sample High Court judgment PDFs. Week 2: Build action plan generation module and confidence scoring. Week 3: Develop human review interface with PDF.js source-highlighting and PostgreSQL audit logging. Week 4: End-to-end integration, verified dashboard, and demo on a set of 10 representative synthetic judgment documents.﻿Risks and Mitigations﻿LLM hallucination on legal text: mitigated by requiring source citations for every extracted field and by mandatory human review before any record moves to the dashboard. OCR errors on old scanned judgments: mitigated by confidence-gated routing — poor OCR triggers manual review rather than propagating errors. Reviewer bottleneck: mitigated by AI pre-filling all fields so reviewers only need to validate, not re-enter data; estimated review time per judgment is under 5 minutes. Legal language variation across judges and courts: the LLM is prompted to extract semantics, not surface patterns, making it robust to stylistic variation.﻿Why This Wins﻿NyayaSetu directly solves every problem in the CCMS pain statement: it handles both digital and scanned PDFs, extracts all critical fields including implicit timelines, generates structured action plans with department attribution, enforces human verification before any data enters the workflow, and presents a clean dashboard that non-legal government officers can use confidently. The architecture is deployable as a CCMS plugin with zero changes to existing systems.

TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
LegalAI Resolver
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 06:26 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
We are building an AI agentic pipeline that automates pre-litigation dispute resolution end to end. A user describes their dispute in plain English, and five specialized AI agents run sequentially — classifying the case, mapping applicable Indian statutes, calculating win probability and optimal settlement range, generating court-ready legal documents, and mediating between both parties across up to three structured rounds. If both parties reach agreement, a verified Settlement Agreement is auto-generated and delivered. If negotiation fails after three rounds, a complete court filing package is automatically prepared with all evidence, legal citations, and filing steps. What takes lawyers months and costs Rs. 50,000 minimum, our platform resolves in 48 hours at near zero cost.

Project Description
Problem Context Government procurement through tenders involves evaluating complex eligibility criteria across hundreds of pages of documents submitted by multiple bidders. These documents are highly unstructured, inconsistent in format (PDFs, scans, images), and written in formal legal language. Today, evaluation is manual, time-intensive, and subjective, often leading to: Delays in procurement decisions Inconsistent evaluations across committees Risk of oversight or human error Lack of transparency and auditability There is a critical need for a system that can bring speed, consistency, and explainability into this process. Proposed Solution We propose an Explainable AI-powered platform that automates tender understanding, bidder analysis, and eligibility evaluation — while keeping humans in the loop for critical decisions. ? Core CapabilitiesTender Intelligence Engine Automatically parses tender documents using NLP/LLMs Extracts: Technical requirements Financial thresholds Compliance conditions Mandatory certifications Converts them into structured, machine-readable criteria Classifies rules as Mandatory vs OptionalMulti-Format Document Understanding Processes bidder submissions in: PDFs (text & scanned) Images / photographs Word documents Uses OCR + document parsing to extract: Financial data (turnover, net worth) Certifications (ISO, GST, etc.) Experience records Handles variation in format and languageAI + Rule-Based Eligibility Engine Maps extracted bidder data to tender criteria Applies deterministic rule evaluation for consistency Outputs: ✅ Eligible ❌ Not Eligible ⚠️ Needs Manual ReviewExplainability & Audit Layer (Key Differentiator) Every decision is fully traceable: Criterion evaluated Extracted value Source document + page reference Reason for decision ? Ensures: No silent disqualification High trust for government usage Complete audit trailHuman-in-the-Loop Decision System Flags ambiguous or low-confidence cases Allows procurement officers to review AI suggestions Prevents incorrect automated rejectionSmart Evaluation Dashboard Bidder-wise and criterion-wise comparison Highlights risks, missing documents, and mismatches Generates exportable audit-ready reportsProposed Tech Stack Backend: FastAPI (Python) AI/NLP: LLMs + spaCy + Transformers OCR: PaddleOCR / Tesseract Database: PostgreSQL Frontend: React + TypeScript ? Impact ⏱️ Reduce evaluation time from days to minutes ? Improve consistency across evaluators ? Increase transparency and trust ? Enable audit-ready procurement decisions ⚖️ Ensure fair and unbiased bidder evaluation ? Future Scope Multilingual tender processing (Hindi, regional languages) Integration with e-procurement systems AI-based fraud/anomaly detection in bids Continuous learning from past tender decisions

B bhawya214_e8cf
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI-Powered Tender Intelligence System for Automated Eligibility and Compliance Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 08:07 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
OverviewGovernment procurement processes involve evaluating multiple bidders against a set of predefined eligibility criteria. In practice, this process is highly document-intensive and requires manual analysis of tender clauses and bidder submissions. The proposed solution is an AI-assisted decision-support system that transforms unstructured tender and bidder documents into structured, explainable, and auditable evaluation results.Instead of replacing human decision-makers, the system is designed to assist procurement officers by automating repetitive analysis while keeping critical decisions under human control.﻿Problem StatementIn real-world procurement environments such as those followed by CRPF and other government bodies, tender evaluation faces several operational challenges:Large volumes of documents create cognitive overloadTender conditions are written in complex legal languageBidder submissions come in multiple formats (PDFs, scans, images, certificates)Manual evaluation leads to inconsistency and delaysDecisions are often hard to audit and justify laterAs seen in the worked examples (pages 11–15 of the document), even a single unclear value (e.g., unreadable turnover in a scanned certificate) can lead to incorrect rejection if handled improperly .Fully automating this process is risky due to ambiguity and data quality issues. Therefore, the core problem is:How can we improve efficiency and consistency in tender evaluation while ensuring fairness, transparency, and auditability?﻿Proposed SolutionWe propose a hybrid AI + rule-based system that converts unstructured documents into structured evidence and applies deterministic evaluation logic.The system performs the following:Extracts eligibility criteria from tender documentsParses bidder submissions into structured dataMatches evidence against criteria using rule-based logicProduces clear outcomes: PASS / FAIL / REVIEWGenerates an explainable and auditable reportA key design principle is:The system never silently disqualifies a bidder — uncertain cases are flagged for human review.﻿Core WorkflowThe system follows a step-by-step pipeline:Document Ingestion Upload tender and bidder documents with traceable IDsFile Classification Detect file types (PDF, scanned, image, etc.)Text ExtractionDigital docs → direct extractionScanned docs → OCR with confidence scoringTender Understanding AI extracts structured criteria (thresholds, operators, mandatory flags)Bidder Parsing Extract key fields such as:TurnoverCertificationsWork experienceRegistrationsMatching Engine Deterministic rules compare extracted values with criteriaDecision Generation Each criterion is labeled:PASS → requirement metFAIL → requirement not metREVIEW → ambiguous or low confidenceReport Generation Outputs a complete evaluation report with:Evidence referencesPage numbersConfidence scoresDecision reasoning﻿Key Features1. Structured Criteria ExtractionConverts legal tender text into machine-readable rulesIdentifies mandatory vs optional requirements2. Robust Document HandlingSupports PDFs, images, scanned documents, tablesNormalizes extracted data (currency, dates, units)3. Deterministic Decision EngineAvoids unreliable AI-only decisionsEnsures consistent and repeatable results4. ExplainabilityEach decision includes:Extracted valueSource document + pageComparison logicReason for outcomeExample:“Turnover ₹6.2 Cr ≥ ₹5 Cr → PASS”5. Human-in-the-Loop SafetyLow-confidence or ambiguous cases → REVIEWPrevents wrongful disqualification6. Full Audit TrailLogs every step (extraction → decision → review)Enables compliance with government audit standards﻿Innovation & DifferentiationUnlike generic AI solutions, this system focuses on practical deployment in regulated environments:Combines AI extraction + rule-based validationAvoids black-box decision makingEnsures traceability at every stepDesigned for real government workflowsThe worked example (pages 12–16) clearly shows how different bidders are evaluated with traceable outcomes such as ELIGIBLE, NOT ELIGIBLE, and MANUAL REVIEW .﻿Technology ApproachBackend: FastAPI / django Processing: OCR + NLP + LLM (structured extraction only) Decision Layer: Rule-based engine Storage: PostgreSQL + Object Storage Queue: Async processing (Celery/Redis)AI is used only for understanding documents, while final decisions remain deterministic.﻿Risk HandlingChallengeApproachPoor scan qualityConfidence scoring + REVIEWAmbiguous languageHuman review fallbackAI hallucinationStructured outputs + rule validationFormat variabilityLayout-aware parsing﻿Security & ComplianceRole-based access (Uploader, Evaluator, Auditor)Encrypted storage and secure APIsComplete logging for audit complianceCompatible with government infrastructure (e.g., MeghRaj)﻿Expected Impact⏱️ Reduce evaluation time significantly? Improve consistency across decisions? Enhance transparency and auditability⚖️ Prevent unfair rejection of valid bidders?‍? solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


V vijayraju346_6a05
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) Framework: A Neuro-Symbolic and Multimodal System for Automated Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 08:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) FrameworkAI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis System﻿1. IntroductionGovernment procurement is a critical process that ensures transparency, fairness, and efficient utilization of public funds. Organizations such as the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) rely on tender-based systems to procure goods and services. However, the current evaluation process is largely manual, time-consuming, and prone to inconsistencies.The Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) Framework is proposed as an advanced AI-based system that automates tender evaluation while ensuring accuracy, transparency, and auditability. The system acts as a smart auditor that understands tender requirements, analyzes bidder submissions, and provides explainable decisions.﻿2. Problem StatementTender documents contain complex eligibility criteria including technical specifications, financial requirements, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications. These are written in legal language and distributed across multiple pages.Bidders submit supporting documents such as financial statements, certificates, and company profiles in various formats including PDFs, scanned images, Word files, and photographs.The key challenge is to accurately determine whether each bidder satisfies all eligibility criteria while handling unstructured data and ensuring consistency in evaluation.﻿3. Challenges in Existing Systems3.1 Manual System ChallengesEvaluation process takes several days per tender High cognitive load leads to human errors Inconsistent decisions across evaluators Lack of standardized evaluation procedures Difficult to audit decisions Limited scalability with increasing data ﻿3.2 Existing AI System ChallengesPoor accuracy on scanned or low-quality documents Limited understanding of legal and technical language Lack of explainability in decision-making Inability to distinguish mandatory and optional criteria Weak cross-document analysis Difficulty in handling diverse document formats No mechanism to detect fraud or inconsistencies ﻿4. Proposed SolutionThe CPI Framework is designed to address these challenges by combining advanced AI techniques with logical reasoning mechanisms. The system operates as a multi-layer intelligent pipeline that mimics human expertise while ensuring consistency and transparency.The key objectives of the system are:Automate extraction of eligibility criteria from tender documents Analyze bidder submissions across multiple formats Perform accurate and consistent evaluation Provide explainable and auditable decisions Identify ambiguous cases and flag them for manual review ﻿5. System ArchitectureThe CPI Framework consists of the following layers:Perception Layer Extraction Layer Reasoning Layer Validation Layer Explanation Layer Each layer contributes to transforming raw documents into structured insights and final decisions.﻿6. Detailed Working Mechanism6.1 Perception LayerThis layer is responsible for understanding the structure and content of documents.Key functionalities:Identifies layout elements such as headings, tables, and sections Processes scanned and image-based documents Applies preprocessing techniques such as noise removal and alignment correction Recognizes text and handwritten elements This ensures that even low-quality or complex documents can be processed effectively.﻿6.2 Extraction LayerThe extraction layer focuses on retrieving relevant data from documents.Key functionalities:Extracts financial data such as turnover and net worth Identifies certifications, registration numbers, and validity dates Parses tables and structured data Handles variations in how information is presented The extracted data is converted into a structured format for further processing.﻿6.3 Reasoning LayerThis layer performs logical evaluation based on predefined rules.Key functionalities:Matches extracted data with tender criteria Applies rule-based logic for eligibility determination Differentiates between mandatory and optional conditions Ensures consistent decision-making across all bidders This layer combines machine learning with rule-based systems to ensure both flexibility and accuracy.﻿6.4 Validation LayerThe validation layer ensures the correctness of extracted data.Key functionalities:Cross-verifies information across multiple documents Detects inconsistencies and mismatches Validates document authenticity and expiry dates Flags suspicious or incomplete data This layer reduces errors and improves the reliability of the system.﻿6.5 Explanation LayerThe explanation layer ensures transparency in decision-making.Key functionalities:Provides detailed reasoning for each decision Links decisions to specific criteria and documents Displays extracted values used in evaluation Generates a complete audit trail This makes the system suitable for government use where accountability is critical.﻿7. Key InnovationsThe CPI Framework introduces several innovative features:Multi-layer validation system for cross-document verification Explainable AI for transparent decision-making Intelligent document understanding beyond keyword matching Detection of hidden inconsistencies and potential fraud Integration of rule-based logic with machine learning ﻿8. Human-in-the-Loop MechanismThe system incorporates a human-in-the-loop approach to handle uncertainty.Cases with low confidence are flagged for manual review Officers are provided with detailed explanations Human feedback can be used to improve the system This ensures that no bidder is unfairly disqualified.﻿9. Output and ReportingThe system generates comprehensive evaluation reports including:Criterion-wise evaluation for each bidder Final eligibility status Supporting evidence for each decision Summary of all bidders Exportable reports for official use ﻿10. Scalability and PerformanceThe CPI Framework is designed to handle large-scale data efficiently.Can process multiple tenders simultaneously Reduces evaluation time from days to minutes Scales easily with increasing number of bidders ﻿11. Impact and BenefitsThe system provides significant advantages:Reduces evaluation time drastically Improves accuracy and consistency Enhances transparency and accountability Minimizes human errors Detects fraud and inconsistencies Supports better decision-making ﻿12. Risks and MitigationPotential risks include:OCR inaccuracies in low-quality documents Ambiguity in legal language Incomplete or inconsistent data Mitigation strategies:Use of preprocessing techniques Human-in-the-loop review system Multi-layer validation ﻿13. Implementation RoadmapPhase 1:Develop basic document processing and extraction systemPhase 2:Integrate advanced AI models and reasoning mechanismsPhase 3:Enhance validation, scalability, and performance﻿14. ConclusionThe Cognitive Procurement Intelligence Framework transforms the traditional tender evaluation process into an intelligent, automated, and transparent system. By combining document understanding, data extraction, logical reasoning, and explainability, the system ensures accurate and fair evaluation of bidders.This solution not only improves efficiency but also strengthens trust and accountability in government procurement processes.

1 1dt23ca043_11e2
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AdhikarAI — Court Judgment to Verified Government Plan
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:27 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
India's High Courts dispose thousands of judgments every year. Each judgment contains critical directives — who must act, by when, and what happens if they don't. Today, these directives are buried in lengthy PDF documents, manually read by officials, and frequently missed or misinterpreted — leading to missed deadlines, fragmented accountability, and even contempt of court risks.
AdhikarAI doesn't just read judgments — it ensures they are executed.
AdhikarAI is an AI-powered Court Case Monitoring System that transforms unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, human-verified government action plans.
HOW IT WORKS:
EXTRACT — Upload any court judgment PDF (scanned or digital). Our pipeline uses PyMuPDF + OCR to parse the document, then an LLM extracts key information: case details, date of order, directions/orders, parties involved, responsible entities, deadlines, and timelines — each with a confidence score.

GENERATE ACTION PLAN — For every extracted directive, the system generates a structured action plan detailing required actions, responsible departments, timelines, and compliance steps.

HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP VERIFICATION (MANDATORY) — A legal reviewer validates extracted data side-by-side with the original PDF using source highlights. Records can be Approved, Edited, or Rejected. Only verified data moves forward — ensuring explainability and accountability.

TRUSTED DASHBOARD — A role-based dashboard presents verified action plans, assigned responsibilities, deadlines, and compliance status — giving decision-makers a real-time view of execution.

CORE PROBLEM SOLVED: Judgment directives are no longer lost in PDFs. Every order is extracted, verified, converted into actionable tasks, and tracked to completion — enabling timely and accountable government action.
INPUT: Multi-page court judgment PDFs (unstructured, scanned, or digital)
OUTPUT: Structured data → AI-generated action plans → Human-verified records → Real-time compliance dashboard
Built with: FastAPI, Next.js, PostgreSQL, PDF.js, PyMuPDF, Tesseract OCR

H harizeeraman_ae89
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
JusticeTrack — AI-Powered Court Judgment Analysis and Verified Compliance Action System
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Karnataka’s Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) already solves document delivery by automatically receiving judgment PDFs from the High Court’s Court Information System (CIS). However, delivery is where automation stops.What follows is entirely manual: officials must read lengthy legal documents, interpret directions, identify responsible departments, calculate appeal windows, and decide actions — all while statutory deadlines silently progress.This creates not just inefficiency, but legal risk. Missing a compliance deadline can trigger contempt of court. Missing an appeal window permanently forfeits the State’s right to challenge a judgment. Current systems provide no visibility into urgency — cases exist as flat lists with no indication of which ones require immediate action.JusticeTrack addresses this gap. It is a human-verified, deadline-aware compliance intelligence system designed specifically for how government legal workflows function.Key Design PrinciplesNo upstream changes: Integrates with existing CCMS APIs; no modification to CCMS/CIS required Human-in-the-loop: All outputs require mandatory human verification before use Audit-first architecture: Every AI output, confidence score, and human decision is logged and traceable Privacy-safe development: Uses synthetic/redacted judgments for training and testing Format-agnostic ingestion: Handles both digital and scanned PDFs via OCRHow JusticeTrack Works1. Judgment ClassificationIncoming judgments are automatically classified into five actionable categories:Compliance Direction Status Quo Order Appeal-Worthy Judgment Cost Imposition Contempt Risk Order Each category determines urgency, routing, and action templates.2. Structured Extraction with Confidence ScoringThe system extracts:Case details, parties, departments Key directions and timelines Required actions and appeal signals Each field is assigned an individual confidence score and linked to its exact source location in the document (page and paragraph). Low-confidence fields are automatically flagged for mandatory review.3. Legal Deadline EngineJusticeTrack embeds statutory timelines (e.g., Limitation Act rules) to compute:Appeal deadlines (e.g., 30 or 90 days) Compliance deadlines (explicit or inferred) Days remaining from current date Each case is assigned a Compliance Risk Level:CRITICAL: Deadline ≤ 7 days or contempt indicated HIGH: 8–21 days or strong directive language MEDIUM: 22–45 days LOW: Monitoring scenarios Cases are automatically prioritized based on risk — not filing order.4. Human Verification InterfaceA structured review system ensures reliability:Side-by-side PDF and extracted data view Click-to-highlight source traceability Field-level actions: Approve, Edit, Reject Mandatory review for low-confidence fields All actions are logged with user identity, timestamps, and change history.5. Department Routing & EscalationVerified cases are routed using a Department Registry that standardizes naming variations.Automated escalation triggers:21 days remaining — reminder 7 days — escalation to senior अधिकारी 3 days — critical alert6. Trusted DashboardOnly fully verified cases appear. Features include:Risk-prioritized case list Department-specific views Deadline calendar Action status tracking Exportable compliance reports Designed for rapid decision-making by senior officials.ImpactJusticeTrack transforms legal document handling from passive storage to proactive risk management. It ensures:No missed appeal deadlines Reduced contempt risk Faster inter-department coordination Full accountability and auditabilityImplementation PhasesCore extraction & verification prototype Deadline engine & risk scoring Routing, escalation & dashboardJusticeTrack — Because every judgment starts a clock. This system makes sure the government never misses it.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


K kumarpushpam17_0293
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderSaarthi: Explainable Multilingual AI for Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tender evaluation is still a slow, document-heavy, and high-risk manual process. Procurement teams must read lengthy tender documents, identify eligibility criteria, and verify those conditions across bidder submissions that arrive in many formats such as typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, tables, certificates, and even photographs. This leads to delays, inconsistency across evaluators, and difficulty in maintaining an audit trail.We propose TenderSaarthi, an AI-assisted platform for tender evaluation and eligibility analysis designed specifically for formal government procurement workflows.The platform works in five stages: Tender Understanding: TenderSaarthi ingests the tender document and extracts eligibility criteria across technical, financial, compliance, certification, and document-checklist categories. It identifies whether a criterion is mandatory or optional and converts it into a structured rule that can later be matched against bidder submissions. Multiformat and Multilingual Document Understanding: The platform parses bidder documents across typed PDFs, scanned files, images, tables, and certificates. In addition, it is designed to support multilingual tender and bidder documents, including cases where tender clauses or supporting documents are submitted in regional languages. Using multilingual OCR plus translation-assisted extraction, the system can interpret and normalize information from English, Hindi, Kannada, and other Indian-language documents into a common structured representation for evaluation. Criteria-to-Evidence Matching: For each bidder, the system maps extracted evidence to each tender criterion. For example, turnover values, project experience, GST registration, certification validity, and compliance records are matched against the corresponding tender requirements. The engine combines rule-based checks for explicit thresholds with semantic matching for legal and technical language variations.Explainable Verdicts with Human-in-the-Loop Review: For every criterion, the system produces one of three outcomes: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review. Every verdict is fully explainable:which criterion was checked which document was usedwhat value or evidence was foundwhy the bidder passed, failed, or was flaggedIf the extracted evidence is incomplete, low-confidence, ambiguous, or poorly scanned, the system never silently disqualifies the bidder. Instead, it routes the case to a human reviewer with highlighted evidence and the reason for uncertainty.Audit-Ready Reporting: All automated findings and manual interventions are logged with document references, page-level evidence, confidence scores, reviewer edits, and final outcomes. The result is a consolidated evaluation report that procurement officers can review, export, and use as the basis for decision-making.Why this solution is practical TenderSaarthi is not a black-box AI tool. It is a decision-support platform built around transparency, auditability, and officer control. It aligns with real procurement requirements where every decision must be explainable and reviewable. It also supports a scalable future state where the same core engine can be reused across departments, tender types, and larger bidder volumes.﻿Architecture Diagram: View TenderSaarthi system flowRound 2 vision In the sandbox phase, we would demonstrate end-to-end tender ingestion, multilingual document processing, criterion extraction, bidder-wise evaluation, manual review workflows, and exportable audit-ready reports using mock or redacted tender data.

solutions.team_name: NyayaLoop: AI-Powered Court Judgment to Verified Action Plan Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hehe something
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NyayaLoop: AI-Powered Court Judgment to Verified Action Plan Engine
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:41 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemKarnataka's High Court disposes thousands of cases every year. Each judgment PDF lands in the Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) and then the process breaks down. A government officer receives a 30-60 page legal document, must read it entirely, mentally extract the directives buried inside, figure out which department owns each action, calculate deadlines, and log it manually. Directives get missed. Limitation periods lapse silently. The same court order comes back four years later because no one tracked whether it was ever executed.This is not a failure of intent. It is a failure of infrastructure.What NyayaLoop DoesNyayaLoop is an AI layer that sits on top of the existing CCMS. It does not replace any existing system. It makes the judgment PDF actionable in minutes instead of days.The system has four stages:Stage 1: Intelligent ExtractionWhen a judgment PDF arrives via the CCMS API, NyayaLoop processes it automatically. For scanned PDFs, it runs OCR with layout preservation. For digital PDFs, it extracts text with positional metadata. The system identifies the operative portion of the judgment (where 80% of actionable directives are concentrated) and extracts:Case number, court, bench, and date of orderPetitioner and respondent namesNature of the caseEvery directive or order, verbatim and normalizedThe responsible party for each directiveExplicit deadlinesInferred deadlines (e.g. "within eight weeks" is converted to a calculated calendar date)The limitation period for filing an appeal, calculated from the order dateWhether the case requires compliance, appeal consideration, or bothA confidence score for every extracted fieldThe exact PDF page and text span that supports every extraction (no black box outputs)Stage 2: AI-Generated Action PlanNyayaLoop converts the extracted information into a structured, decision-ready action plan with three possible tracks:Track A (Compliance Required): Plain-language summary of what must be done, which department or officer is responsible, the deadline, a suggested sequence of sub-tasks, and what documents or evidence must be gathered to prove compliance.Track B (Appeal Consideration): AI-flagged grounds for potential appeal drawn from the judgment text, the calculated limitation period, the recommended escalation path, and key legal points that may support an appeal.Track C (Both): For cases where the department must comply immediately to avoid contempt while simultaneously preserving appeal rights, NyayaLoop generates a parallel timeline covering both tracks.Every action plan also includes a Priority Score (High, Medium, or Low) based on deadline proximity, nature of directive, department involved, and contempt risk.Stage 3: Human-in-the-Loop Verification (Mandatory)This is the architectural centerpiece of NyayaLoop. The system is built on the principle that AI output is a starting point, not a final answer.The verification interface shows a split screen: the extracted data on the left, the source PDF on the right with the exact supporting text highlighted in yellow. Every field displays its confidence level (green for high, yellow for medium, red for low). Fields with low confidence are pre-flagged for mandatory human review.The reviewer can approve, edit inline, or reject each field with a reason. Every action is logged with the reviewer's name, timestamp, and the original versus edited value.Only verified records are allowed to move forward. Unverified data is quarantined and never reaches the dashboard.This creates a legally defensible chain: if a compliance action is taken based on NyayaLoop output, there is a documented human authorization at every step.Stage 4: Decision-Maker Dashboard (Verified Data Only)The dashboard shows only records that passed human verification. No raw AI output ever appears here.Views available to government officials:Department View: All pending actions for the logged-in department, sorted by deadlineCalendar View: A deadline heatmap covering the next 7, 30, and 60 daysRisk Alerts: Cases approaching the limitation period for appealAction Status Tracker: Pending, In Progress, Completed (officers update status themselves)Contempt Risk Tracker: Cases where a deadline has passed without a marked completionJudgment Explorer: Search and filter by department, date range, case type, and current statusThe dashboard also sends automated alerts via SMS and email when deadlines are approaching or when a case has been escalated.Why This is Different from Existing Legal AI ToolsAll existing legal AI in India (jhana.ai, BharatLaw, SUPACE, SUVAS) operates in the pre-judgment space. They help lawyers and judges research cases, find precedents, and draft documents. NyayaLoop operates post-judgment, on the government compliance side. It closes the loop between "order issued" and "order executed." This gap is completely unaddressed by any existing tool.The second differentiator is confidence-grounded verification UX. The AI shows its work, highlights its sources in the original PDF, quantifies its uncertainty, and hands structured control to a human reviewer. This makes the system deployable in a government context where "the AI said so" is not an acceptable answer.Integration with CCMSNyayaLoop is designed as a plug-in on top of the existing CCMS:Listens to the CCMS API webhook when a new judgment PDF is fetched from the High Court CISProcesses in the background and queues items for human verificationPushes verified action plans back to department dashboardsRequires zero modification to the CCMS coreTechnical StackOCR and PDF parsing: Azure Document Intelligence, PyMuPDF, TesseractLLM for extraction and action plan generation: Claude API / GPT-4o with structured JSON output schema and source-grounded promptingBackend: FastAPI (Python)Frontend dashboard: React with Tailwind CSSVerification UX: PDF.js for inline source highlightingDatabase: PostgreSQL for verified records, Redis for processing queueDeployment: Docker containers, deployable on-premise or on government cloud infrastructureDataset UsedKarnataka High Court judgments from the AWS Open Data Registry (Indian High Court Judgments dataset by Dattam Labs), downloaded from:  https://registry.opendata.aws/indian-high-court-judgments/  (https://registry.opendata.aws/indian-high-court-judgments/)﻿Karnataka court code: court=27_1. Judgments from 2020-2024 used for extraction testing, action plan quality evaluation, and confidence score calibration.ImpactEvery judgment that goes unexecuted is justice delayed twice: once in court, and again in administration. NyayaLoop turns every court order into a tracked, accountable work item. It makes the invisible visible. It gives IAS officers and department heads the same visibility over compliance that a project manager has over a sprint board.If it cannot work in the real world, it does not win. NyayaLoop is built for CCMS integration from day one. The data exists. The API exists. The problem is validated by the Centre for e-Governance itself. This is not a concept, it is a deployment plan.
