solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


K kumarpushpam17_0293
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderSaarthi: Explainable Multilingual AI for Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tender evaluation is still a slow, document-heavy, and high-risk manual process. Procurement teams must read lengthy tender documents, identify eligibility criteria, and verify those conditions across bidder submissions that arrive in many formats such as typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, tables, certificates, and even photographs. This leads to delays, inconsistency across evaluators, and difficulty in maintaining an audit trail.We propose TenderSaarthi, an AI-assisted platform for tender evaluation and eligibility analysis designed specifically for formal government procurement workflows.The platform works in five stages: Tender Understanding: TenderSaarthi ingests the tender document and extracts eligibility criteria across technical, financial, compliance, certification, and document-checklist categories. It identifies whether a criterion is mandatory or optional and converts it into a structured rule that can later be matched against bidder submissions. Multiformat and Multilingual Document Understanding: The platform parses bidder documents across typed PDFs, scanned files, images, tables, and certificates. In addition, it is designed to support multilingual tender and bidder documents, including cases where tender clauses or supporting documents are submitted in regional languages. Using multilingual OCR plus translation-assisted extraction, the system can interpret and normalize information from English, Hindi, Kannada, and other Indian-language documents into a common structured representation for evaluation. Criteria-to-Evidence Matching: For each bidder, the system maps extracted evidence to each tender criterion. For example, turnover values, project experience, GST registration, certification validity, and compliance records are matched against the corresponding tender requirements. The engine combines rule-based checks for explicit thresholds with semantic matching for legal and technical language variations.Explainable Verdicts with Human-in-the-Loop Review: For every criterion, the system produces one of three outcomes: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review. Every verdict is fully explainable:which criterion was checked which document was usedwhat value or evidence was foundwhy the bidder passed, failed, or was flaggedIf the extracted evidence is incomplete, low-confidence, ambiguous, or poorly scanned, the system never silently disqualifies the bidder. Instead, it routes the case to a human reviewer with highlighted evidence and the reason for uncertainty.Audit-Ready Reporting: All automated findings and manual interventions are logged with document references, page-level evidence, confidence scores, reviewer edits, and final outcomes. The result is a consolidated evaluation report that procurement officers can review, export, and use as the basis for decision-making.Why this solution is practical TenderSaarthi is not a black-box AI tool. It is a decision-support platform built around transparency, auditability, and officer control. It aligns with real procurement requirements where every decision must be explainable and reviewable. It also supports a scalable future state where the same core engine can be reused across departments, tender types, and larger bidder volumes.﻿Architecture Diagram: View TenderSaarthi system flowRound 2 vision In the sandbox phase, we would demonstrate end-to-end tender ingestion, multilingual document processing, criterion extraction, bidder-wise evaluation, manual review workflows, and exportable audit-ready reports using mock or redacted tender data.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Explainable AI Platform for Automated Tender Evaluation & Bidder Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 11:16 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement processes, such as those used by CRPF, involve evaluating complex tender documents and heterogeneous bidder submissions. This is not just a data extraction task but a high-stakes decision-making process involving legal compliance, financial verification, and accountability. Currently, this process is manual, time-consuming, inconsistent, and difficult to audit.Our solution proposes an AI-powered, explainable decision-support platform that automates tender understanding, bidder evaluation, and eligibility verification while ensuring full transparency, auditability, and human oversight.Problem Understanding: Tender documents contain detailed eligibility criteria such as financial thresholds, technical requirements, compliance rules, and certifications. Bidders submit documents in multiple formats (PDFs, scans, images, Word files), often with inconsistent structures. Evaluating these manually leads to delays, errors, and lack of standardization.Solution Overview: We design a multi-stage intelligent pipeline that converts unstructured documents into structured insights and produces criterion-level eligibility decisions with full explanations.Technical Approach:a) Tender Understanding Engine: The system ingests tender documents and uses OCR and layout-aware models (such as LayoutLM) along with LLM-based semantic parsing to extract eligibility criteria. Each criterion is structured into machine-readable format and classified as financial, technical, or compliance-based, and as mandatory or optional.b) Bidder Document Intelligence: The platform processes bidder submissions across all formats using OCR (PaddleOCR/AWS Textract) and NLP techniques. It extracts key entities such as turnover, project experience, certifications (GST, ISO), and compliance indicators while handling variations in document formats.c) Hybrid Matching & Decision Engine: A hybrid system combining rule-based validation and LLM reasoning maps bidder data to tender criteria. Deterministic checks (e.g., turnover thresholds) are handled via rules, while ambiguous language is resolved using LLMs. Each criterion is evaluated as:Eligible (Pass)Not Eligible (Fail)Needs Manual Review (Low confidence or ambiguity)d) Confidence Scoring: Each extracted value and decision is assigned a confidence score. Low-confidence cases are automatically flagged for review.Explainability & Auditability (Core Innovation): Every decision is fully transparent and traceable:Criterion being evaluatedExtracted bidder valueSource document and page referenceReasoning behind the decisionThis ensures compliance with audit requirements (CAG, RTI) and builds trust in AI-assisted decision-making.Human-in-the-Loop System: The system never silently disqualifies bidders. Ambiguous cases (e.g., poor OCR quality, missing data, unclear language) are flagged for human review with clear justification. Human overrides are supported at every stage.Government-Ready Deployment: The system is designed for real-world constraints:Works on low-quality scanned and handwritten documentsSupports secure/on-premise deploymentMaintains full audit logs for complianceEnsures role-based access and decision accountabilityScalability & Long-Term Impact: This platform can scale across all government departments, PSUs, and even private procurement systems. Over time, it can evolve into a national procurement intelligence layer enabling:Standardized tender evaluationsFraud detection across biddersData-driven procurement insights and policy improvementsKey Technologies:OCR: PaddleOCR / AWS TextractDocument AI: LayoutLM / DonutLLMs: GPT / LLaMA (for semantic understanding)Backend: FastAPIStorage: PostgreSQL + Object StorageVector Search: FAISS / PineconeKey Benefits:Reduces evaluation time from days to minutesEliminates evaluator inconsistencyProvides explainable and auditable decisionsHandles real-world unstructured documentsEnables scalable and transparent governanceWe are not replacing human decision-makers, but augmenting them with AI to ensure faster, fairer, and fully auditable procurement decisions.

solutions.team_name: SAMANVAYA: Zero-Disruption Interoperability OS
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


D dhaneshbhati2007_a6fb
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
SAMANVAYA: Zero-Disruption Interoperability OS
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 11:59 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project SAMANVAYA: Zero-Disruption Interoperability OS PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING: A business in Karnataka updates its registered address on the Single Window System and walks away thinking it is done. It is not. The Factories system still has the old address. Attempting a "big-bang" migration to force all departments into SWS causes cascading failures, as seen in the GST rollout. SAMANVAYA is a stateless, event-driven middleware layer that synchronizes SWS and 40+ legacy systems bidirectionally—without modifying a single line of code on either side. UBID is the absolute join key. DIRECTION 1 — SWS TO DEPARTMENTS: When a service request is raised in SWS, SAMANVAYA intercepts the payload, queries the UBID Registry to find matching departments, and routes the event to department-specific Adapters. These adapters translate the SWS schema into the target's required format and execute the write via robust PostgreSQL-backed retry queues. Every write is idempotent: duplicate detection via a SHA-256 composite key (UBID + event + target + payload) prevents double-writes even on retries. DIRECTION 2 — DEPARTMENTS TO SWS: Legacy systems do not all emit events. SAMANVAYA uses a three-tier detection strategy: Webhooks for modern systems, Adaptive API Polling for standard systems (throttling checks based on how active the UBID is), and Merkle-Tree Snapshot Diffing for legacy databases (comparing lightweight cryptographic hashes to find changes without downloading the whole database). Detected changes are translated to the SWS schema and written with UBID as the join key. CONFLICT RESOLUTION: When updates to the same UBID arrive from two sources within a configurable window, SAMANVAYA detects the collision and applies deterministic Policy Routing. SWS is authoritative for global profile data (Legal Name), while legacy systems own compliance data (Factory License). For operational fields, the latest timestamp wins. For high-stakes legal fields, conflicting updates are quarantined for human review. No silent overwrites. Ever. AUDIT TRAIL & STATE-SYNC UI: Every propagation generates an immutable, cryptographically chained record in PostgreSQL: source, target, UBID, old value, new value, retry count, and conflict decision. Officers access the "State-Sync Time-Travel UI" to drag a timeline slider, reconstructing the exact business state across all departments at any historical second. Any incorrect merge can be instantly reverted with a single click. ADVANCED INNOVATIONS: * Dynamic Adapter Studio (AI Copilot): Admins upload a legacy database schema (column headers only). A local Llama-3-8B model auto-generates the YAML mapping to SWS. The AI is turned off after approval, ensuring 100% deterministic, hallucination-free runtime sync. Shadow Mode: New adapters run in simulation mode for 7 days, logging what would have changed without writing to production, ensuring zero onboarding risk. Auto-Heal DLQ: Failed writes enter middleware that automatically fixes common formatting errors (e.g., padding PIN codes) before alerting humans. NON-NEGOTIABLES MET: Zero modifications to SWS or legacy systems. UBID is the only join key. Idempotency and auditability guaranteed. Local LLM used only for design-time schema mapping on non-PII column headers. TECH STACK: React, Tailwind CSS, Python (FastAPI), PostgreSQL (Message Queue, Idempotency Cache, and Audit Ledger), and local Llama-3. Optimized for rapid, AI-assisted development ("vibe coding") without sacrificing enterprise durability.﻿ROUND 2 PLAN: PostgreSQL broker and FastAPI routing skeleton. SWS inbound listener, YAML schema translator, and bidirectional propagation engine. Adaptive polling engine, Conflict Resolution logic, and Auto-Heal formatting scripts. State-Sync UI dashboard and end-to-end integration testing on scrambled synthetic data.

solutions.team_name: CourtTrack AI: NyayaLens — From Court Judgments to Verified Government Action Plans
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hackman
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CourtTrack AI: NyayaLens — From Court Judgments to Verified Government Action Plans
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 12:23 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Problem:Missed court deadlines can lead to contempt proceedings and serious administrative consequences, yet government departments still rely on manually reading long judgment PDFs to identify required actions. Through systems like CCMS, judgments are already accessible, but these documents are often 20–40 pages long and written in dense legal language where key directives—what action is required, by when, and by which department—are buried deep inside the text. Officers have to interpret legal phrasing and sometimes even calculate deadlines like “within 8 weeks from the date of pronouncement,” which makes the process slow and at times error-prone or inconsistent. As a result, there are missed deadlines, a lack of clear accountability, poor coordination across departments, and increased legal risk. The core issue is not access to judgments but the absence of a structured system that converts them into clear, trackable, and accountable actions. Our Solution — CourtTrack AI: NyayaLens is designed as a layer on top of existing systems that converts court judgment PDFs into structured, verified, and actionable outputs, effectively moving from “judgment received” to “action clearly defined and tracked.” Most solutions today only try to summarize documents, but that still leaves interpretation work for the officer. In contrast, this system creates a complete action workflow. It follows a simple pipeline—Judgment PDF to AI-based extraction to confidence scoring to human verification to an action dashboard—where it identifies required actions, relevant deadlines (explicit or inferred), and the responsible department. Each output comes with a confidence level and is verified by a human officer before use, so that the system remains both fast and reliable in real-world conditions. Key features include a Contempt Risk Indicator that visually highlights urgency based on deadlines, helping officers prioritize actions before they become critical; Evidence-Linked Extraction where every output is connected to the exact sentence in the PDF for quick verification and trust; Decision Summary Cards that convert lengthy judgments into simple, usable action items; Bilingual Support in English and Kannada for practical deployment at the district level; Voice-Based Explanation that allows officers to quickly understand the judgment without going through the entire document; and a Timeline View that clearly shows the flow from judgment to action to deadline. What makes this approach different is that instead of just summarizing documents, it focuses on structured extraction, verification, and tracking, making it usable within real government workflows rather than just as a standalone AI tool. It ensures that every output is verifiable, every decision is traceable, and human review remains central to the system, which is important for actual government adoption. In terms of impact, the system can significantly reduce the time spent per judgment while improving accuracy and accountability, help prevent compliance failures, and improve coordination between departments. It can integrate with systems like CCMS without major changes and scale from state-level deployment to a national-level system through eCourts integration. The long-term vision is to ensure that court directives are not just stored but clearly understood, assigned, tracked, and completed, so that no important court order is missed simply because it was buried inside a PDF.

Project Description
Title AdhikarAI: Intelligent System for Converting Court Judgments into Verified Government Action Plans
Problem Understanding Court judgments are lengthy and written in dense legal language, making it hard for officials to quickly identify required actions, deadlines, and responsibilities. Manual interpretation leads to delays, missed instructions, and compliance risks.
Proposed Solution AdhikarAI converts court judgments into structured, actionable workflows. It identifies what needs to be done, who is responsible, deadlines, and whether to comply or consider appeal—presented as a clear, ready-to-use action plan.
Key Features • Action Card System – Tasks with action, owner, deadline, and priority • Deadline Radar – Auto-detects deadlines and appeal windows • Decision Support Engine (Unique) – Suggests compliance, delay, or appeal based on context • Workflow Mapping – Coordinates tasks across departments • Legal Traceability – Links every output to exact source text • Priority Dashboard – Highlights urgent cases • Human-in-the-Loop – Review and correction by officials • Adaptive Learning – Improves with feedback
How It Works Upload judgment → Extract text (OCR) → Identify directives (NLP) → Generate action plan → Verify → Track in dashboard
Real-World Scenario • Submit compliance report within 30 days • Responsible department: Revenue • Priority: High • Suggested action: Comply
Officials understand and act instantly without reading the full document.
Technical Direction OCR + NLP for extraction, rule-based + AI reasoning for workflow generation, with a feedback loop for continuous improvement.
Tech Stack Python (FastAPI), Transformer-based NLP Models, Tesseract OCR, React.js Dashboard, PostgreSQL, Cloud Deployment (AWS/Azure/GCP)
Impact • Faster decisions • Reduced legal risk • Improved transparency • Lower manual workload
Deployment Scalable decision-support system that integrates with existing workflows.
Conclusion AdhikarAI turns complex court judgments into clear, explainable, and actionable workflows for efficient governance.

Title TenderLens AI: Explainable & Fraud-Aware Tender Evaluation System for Transparent Government Procurement
Problem Understanding Government tender evaluation is time-consuming and manual. Officers must review multiple bidder documents in different formats, leading to delays, inconsistencies, and difficulty in auditing decisions. There is also a risk of fraud, manipulation, and subjective interpretation.
Proposed Solution TenderLens AI automates tender evaluation by converting unstructured tender documents into structured criteria and matching them with bidder submissions. solutions.team_name: TenderSahayak: Transparent, Explainable AI for Government Tender Eligibility Assessment
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A aryansingh6584_63fe
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderSahayak: Transparent, Explainable AI for Government Tender Eligibility Assessment
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 12:57 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Imagine a procurement officer sitting down with 300 pages of documents — tender clauses, financial statements, scanned certificates, compliance annexures — and being asked to decide, fairly and accurately, which of ten companies qualifies. Now imagine doing that same job in minutes, with every decision fully explained and every step permanently recorded.That is what TenderSahayak was built to do.The Real Problem Government procurement teams work under enormous pressure. They read through dense tender documents and stacks of bidder submissions, cross‑checking eligibility conditions one by one. The process is slow. It is inconsistent — two evaluators reading the same file can reach different conclusions. And when a decision is challenged, documenting exactly why a bidder was accepted or rejected is harder than it should be.This is not a technology failure. It is a volume and complexity problem that technology can now genuinely solve.What TenderSahayak Does TenderSahayak reads a tender document and automatically identifies every eligibility condition — financial thresholds, technical qualifications, compliance requirements, certification checklists. It separates what is mandatory from what is optional, based on the actual language of each clause.It then processes every bidder's submission, regardless of format. Typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, even photographs of physical certificates — all are handled. For poor‑quality scans, image correction is applied before text is extracted, so a blurry mobile photograph of a CA certificate does not automatically disqualify a legitimate bidder.For each bidder and each criterion, the system finds the relevant value, identifies exactly which document and which passage it came from, and checks it against the tender requirement. A confidence score is assigned to every extraction. When confidence is high and the answer is clear, a verdict is given. When anything is uncertain — an unclear scan, an ambiguous figure, a missing document — the case is sent to a human officer with a full explanation of what was found and what needs a second look.No bidder is ever silently rejected.What Makes It Different Most document tools extract text. TenderSahayak makes decisions — and explains them.Every verdict references the exact rule that was applied, the document that was used, and the value that was found. If a bidder is marked ineligible, the system shows precisely which criterion they failed and where in their own documents that evidence appears. If a case is uncertain, the officer sees exactly what the system saw and why it could not decide.Beyond basic evaluation, the system also flags data inconsistencies across a bidder's own documents — for instance, a turnover figure in a CA certificate that does not match the balance sheet. These signals are surfaced for human review rather than acted on automatically, keeping officers in control of judgement calls.Every action is recorded in a tamper‑proof audit log. No entry is ever modified or deleted. Human overrides are recorded alongside the original automated decision, not instead of it. The result is an evaluation trail that holds up to scrutiny in official and legal contexts.Who Stays in Control TenderSahayak is designed to support procurement officers, not replace them. Officers approve the extracted criterion list before evaluation begins. They review all flagged cases. They sign off on the final report.The system handles the volume, the consistency and the documentation. The officer handles the judgement and the accountability.Built to Scale The architecture is designed to grow — from a single department running one tender at a time, to multiple departments processing tenders in parallel, to a shared AI evaluation layer across state and national procurement systems. The core logic — extract, match, explain, audit — is the same at every scale.TechnologyClaude 3.5 Sonnet for language understanding and reasoning across formal legal textGoogle Document AI for OCR on scanned and photographed documentsFastAPI for a high‑performance backendPostgreSQL for audit‑grade data storageReact for a clean, officer‑friendly interfaceDeployable on‑premise, so tender and bidder data never leaves a controlled environment.Why It Matters Procurement decisions affect public money, public services and public trust. When those decisions are made inconsistently or without a clear record, the system fails — not just the bidders, but the institutions that depend on fair outcomes.TenderSahayak does not promise to replace human judgement. It promises to give human judgement the information it needs, the consistency it deserves, and the audit trail it requires.Speed, fairness and accountability — built into the process from the start.

T tsanjai96_0348
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan System
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 01:09 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project nameNyayaSetu — AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan SystemProblem understandingKarnataka's CCMS system receives hundreds of High Court judgment PDFs daily. Each judgment contains critical directives — comply, appeal, timelines, responsible departments — buried in 20–100 pages of complex legal text. Officers manually read every page, causing delays, missed deadlines, and inconsistent compliance. A judgment missed can result in contempt of court. There is no tool today that reads these PDFs and tells an officer exactly what action to take and by when.Proposed solutionNyayaSetu is an AI system that: (1) accepts court judgment PDFs (scanned or digital), (2) extracts case number, order date, directives, parties, and timelines using LLM-based document understanding, (3) generates a structured action plan classifying each directive as Comply / Consider Appeal / Monitor, with inferred deadlines, (4) presents extracted data with source highlights from the original PDF for human verification — officers can approve, edit, or reject each item, (5) only verified records flow into a department-wise action dashboard showing pending actions and upcoming deadlines.Tech stackPython, Streamlit (UI), Google Gemini 1.5 Flash API (free tier — PDF understanding + action plan generation), PyMuPDF (PDF parsing), SQLite (verified records store). Deployed on Streamlit Cloud (free). No cloud costs, no external dependencies.Real-world deployabilityDesigned as an add-on layer to the existing CCMS system — no modification to current systems. Uses only the PDF output already generated by CCMS. Runs on synthetic/redacted documents in sandbox. Every AI decision is explainable, reversible, and auditable. Scalable to all 30 Karnataka districts.ImpactKarnataka High Court disposes 50,000+ cases per year. Each judgment requires action from 1–5 government departments. NyayaSetu reduces review time from hours to minutes, eliminates missed deadlines, and creates a full audit trail for every directive — directly supporting Karnataka's e-governance mission.

solutions.team_name: PRAMAN-AVA (Action and Verification Assistant) : AI System for Court Judgment Understanding and Verified Action Planning
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T Team Cipher
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
PRAMAN-AVA (Action and Verification Assistant) : AI System for Court Judgment Understanding and Verified Action Planning
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 01:30 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Understanding Government departments receive court judgments through systems such as CCMS in the form of multi-page PDF documents. These documents contain critical legal directives that require timely administrative action. However, they present several challenges: Judgments are unstructured and written in complex legal language Documents may be scanned, inconsistent, or poorly formatted Key directives are embedded within long paragraphs Important timelines and responsibilities are not explicitly structured Manual interpretation is time-consuming, error-prone, and non-scalable As a result, there is a significant gap between document availability and actionable understanding, leading to delays, inefficiencies, and potential non-compliance. The core problem is not only extracting information but converting legal text into structured, explainable, and verifiable action plans. Additionally, in real-world government workflows:Officers must quickly identify whether to comply, appeal, or escalateDeadlines and limitation periods are often missed due to lack of structured visibilityThere is no unified system to track actions after judgment interpretation PRAMAN-AVA addresses not just extraction, but the entire lifecycle from judgment to verified action execution.Proposed Solution Overview We propose PRAMAN-AVA (Action and Verification Assistant), an AI-powered system designed to: Extract structured information from court judgment PDFs Interpret legal directives and generate actionable plans Provide explainable outputs with source traceability Enable human verification with full control Present only verified data in a decision-support dashboard The system is designed as a decision-support layer, not a replacement for human judgment, ensuring reliability and compliance with government workflows. Additionally, the system ensures:No silent decision-makingNo loss of contextual legal meaningFull traceability from input document to final actionSystem Objectives The proposed solution aims to: Achieve high accuracy in extracting structured legal information Generate meaningful and legally relevant action plans Ensure complete explainability and auditability of outputs Integrate human verification as a core component Provide a clean and actionable interface for decision-makers Further objectives include:Minimizing missed directives and deadlinesStandardizing interpretation across departmentsReducing cognitive load on officersSystem Architecture 4.1 High-Level Pipeline Input PDF ? Preprocessing ? OCR ? Layout Analysis ? Information Extraction ? Legal Reasoning ? Action Plan Generation ? Confidence Scoring ? Human Verification ? Dashboard Output4.2 Component Description 4.2.1 Document Processing Layer Handles input PDFs of varying formats: Scanned documents Digitally generated PDFs Image-based documents Key processes: OCR for text extraction Layout analysis for identifying sections, headings, and structure Noise reduction and image preprocessing for better OCR accuracy4.2.2 Information Extraction Engine Extracts key structured elements: Case details (case number, court, parties) Date of judgment Key directions and orders Legal obligations Explicit timelines Additional extraction capabilities include:Identification of limitation periods for appealExtraction of conditional directivesDetection of multiple directives within a single paragraph Techniques used: Named Entity Recognition (NER) Context-aware text parsing Hybrid rule-based and ML-based extraction4.2.3 Legal Reasoning Engine Interprets extracted data to understand: Nature of directives Legal intent Required administrative response This component transforms extracted text into semantic meaning, enabling action generation. Additionally, it:Distinguishes between mandatory orders and observationsIdentifies whether a directive requires immediate action or evaluationHandles implicit instructions not explicitly written4.2.4 Action Plan Generator Generates structured outputs including: Type of action (compliance, appeal consideration, review) Responsible department or authority Timeline (explicit or inferred) Priority level Supporting evidence Extended outputs include:Suggested next steps for officersRisk classification of each directiveDependency mapping between actions This is the core differentiator, converting legal text into decision-ready intelligence.4.2.5 Confidence and Explainability Layer Each extracted and generated output includes: Confidence score Source reference Evidence highlighting Additionally:Low-confidence outputs are automatically flaggedConfidence thresholds determine routing to review This ensures transparency and enables informed human validation.4.2.6 Human Verification Module Provides a mandatory review interface where users can: Approve extracted and generated data Edit incorrect or incomplete outputs Reject unreliable results Additional features:Side-by-side view of source document and extracted dataAbility to override system decisions with justificationReviewer feedback stored for continuous learning All reviewer actions are recorded, ensuring: Full auditability Continuous system improvement4.2.7 Dashboard Layer Displays only verified information: Department-wise action items Important deadlines Case summaries Status tracking Extended capabilities:Deadline alerts and remindersAction lifecycle tracking (pending, verified, assigned, completed)Filtering by priority and risk level The dashboard is designed for clarity, usability, and quick decision-making.Detailed Workflow Step 1: Input Court judgment PDF is uploaded or fetched via API. Step 2: Extraction System processes the document and extracts structured data including case details, orders, and timelines. Step 3: Action Generation AI generates a structured action plan based on extracted information. Step 4: Verification Human reviewer validates outputs using highlighted evidence and confidence scores. Step 5: Dashboard Output Only approved and verified data is presented for decision-making.Explainability and Auditability The system ensures: Every output is linked to source text Evidence is highlighted within the document Confidence scores are provided for all decisions Reviewer actions are logged and traceable Additional guarantees:Immutable logs for every actionFull trace from input PDF to final decisionNo black-box reasoning This guarantees compliance with government standards for transparency and accountability.Handling Real-World Challenges Challenge Approach Scanned and noisy documents OCR with preprocessing and layout analysis Complex legal language Domain-adapted NLP models Missing or implicit timelines Contextual inference mechanisms Ambiguity in directives Human verification loop Inconsistent formats Hybrid rule-based and ML extraction Additional handling:Multi-language documentsOverlapping or nested directivesLarge multi-page judgmentsTechnology Stack AI/ML Transformer-based NLP models (BERT / Legal-BERT) Layout-aware models (LayoutLM) OCR Tesseract / PaddleOCR Backend Python (FastAPI / Django) Frontend React / Next.js Database PostgreSQL / MongoDB Additional components:Vector database for semantic retrieval (RAG)Caching layer for performance optimizationEvaluation Strategy The system will be evaluated based on: Accuracy of information extraction Correctness of generated action plans Effectiveness of human verification workflow Clarity and usability of dashboard Explainability and traceability of outputs Additional metrics:Reduction in manual processing timeReduction in missed deadlinesReviewer intervention rateRisks and Mitigation Risk Mitigation OCR inaccuracies Multi-stage preprocessing and validation Legal ambiguity Human-in-the-loop validation Over-reliance on automation Decision-support design only Data variability Adaptive and hybrid models Additional risks:Misinterpretation of legal context ? mitigated through verificationPerformance on large documents ? optimized processing pipelineScalability and Future Enhancements Integration with CCMS APIs Multi-language support Cross-case analytics Predictive legal insights Continuous learning from reviewer feedback Extended future scope:Integration with departmental workflow systemsAutomated notification and escalation systemsAI-assisted appeal recommendation supportImplementation Plan Phase 1 Basic document processing and extraction pipeline Phase 2 Action plan generation and reasoning engine Phase 3 Human verification interface Phase 4 Dashboard and analytics layer Phase 5 (Extended) Risk detection, timeline intelligence, and cross-case learningKey Differentiators Evidence-linked extraction and decision-making Confidence-based validation framework Legal-aware action planning (not generic summarization) Strong human-in-the-loop design Full auditability and traceability Additional differentiators:Directive-level risk classificationTimeline inference and delay detectionDepartment mapping intelligenceAction lifecycle trackingLegal ambiguity detectionCross-judgment learning systemConclusion PRAMAN-AVA provides a robust and scalable solution to transform unstructured court judgments into structured, explainable, and verified action plans. By combining AI-driven extraction with human validation, the system ensures reliability, transparency, and practical usability in government workflows. This approach enables faster decision-making, reduces manual effort, and minimizes the risk of missing critical legal directives while maintaining full control and accountability. PRAMAN-AVA acts as an AI co-pilot for government officers, supporting them in making accurate, timely, and well-informed decisions.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI-Powered Tender Intelligence System for Automated Eligibility and Compliance Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 08:07 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
OverviewGovernment procurement processes involve evaluating multiple bidders against a set of predefined eligibility criteria. In practice, this process is highly document-intensive and requires manual analysis of tender clauses and bidder submissions. The proposed solution is an AI-assisted decision-support system that transforms unstructured tender and bidder documents into structured, explainable, and auditable evaluation results.Instead of replacing human decision-makers, the system is designed to assist procurement officers by automating repetitive analysis while keeping critical decisions under human control.﻿Problem StatementIn real-world procurement environments such as those followed by CRPF and other government bodies, tender evaluation faces several operational challenges:Large volumes of documents create cognitive overloadTender conditions are written in complex legal languageBidder submissions come in multiple formats (PDFs, scans, images, certificates)Manual evaluation leads to inconsistency and delaysDecisions are often hard to audit and justify laterAs seen in the worked examples (pages 11–15 of the document), even a single unclear value (e.g., unreadable turnover in a scanned certificate) can lead to incorrect rejection if handled improperly .Fully automating this process is risky due to ambiguity and data quality issues. Therefore, the core problem is:How can we improve efficiency and consistency in tender evaluation while ensuring fairness, transparency, and auditability?﻿Proposed SolutionWe propose a hybrid AI + rule-based system that converts unstructured documents into structured evidence and applies deterministic evaluation logic.The system performs the following:Extracts eligibility criteria from tender documentsParses bidder submissions into structured dataMatches evidence against criteria using rule-based logicProduces clear outcomes: PASS / FAIL / REVIEWGenerates an explainable and auditable reportA key design principle is:The system never silently disqualifies a bidder — uncertain cases are flagged for human review.﻿Core WorkflowThe system follows a step-by-step pipeline:Document Ingestion Upload tender and bidder documents with traceable IDsFile Classification Detect file types (PDF, scanned, image, etc.)Text ExtractionDigital docs → direct extractionScanned docs → OCR with confidence scoringTender Understanding AI extracts structured criteria (thresholds, operators, mandatory flags)Bidder Parsing Extract key fields such as:TurnoverCertificationsWork experienceRegistrationsMatching Engine Deterministic rules compare extracted values with criteriaDecision Generation Each criterion is labeled:PASS → requirement metFAIL → requirement not metREVIEW → ambiguous or low confidenceReport Generation Outputs a complete evaluation report with:Evidence referencesPage numbersConfidence scoresDecision reasoning﻿Key Features1. Structured Criteria ExtractionConverts legal tender text into machine-readable rulesIdentifies mandatory vs optional requirements2. Robust Document HandlingSupports PDFs, images, scanned documents, tablesNormalizes extracted data (currency, dates, units)3. Deterministic Decision EngineAvoids unreliable AI-only decisionsEnsures consistent and repeatable results4. ExplainabilityEach decision includes:Extracted valueSource document + pageComparison logicReason for outcomeExample:“Turnover ₹6.2 Cr ≥ ₹5 Cr → PASS”5. Human-in-the-Loop SafetyLow-confidence or ambiguous cases → REVIEWPrevents wrongful disqualification6. Full Audit TrailLogs every step (extraction → decision → review)Enables compliance with government audit standards﻿Innovation & DifferentiationUnlike generic AI solutions, this system focuses on practical deployment in regulated environments:Combines AI extraction + rule-based validationAvoids black-box decision makingEnsures traceability at every stepDesigned for real government workflowsThe worked example (pages 12–16) clearly shows how different bidders are evaluated with traceable outcomes such as ELIGIBLE, NOT ELIGIBLE, and MANUAL REVIEW .﻿Technology ApproachBackend: FastAPI / django Processing: OCR + NLP + LLM (structured extraction only) Decision Layer: Rule-based engine Storage: PostgreSQL + Object Storage Queue: Async processing (Celery/Redis)AI is used only for understanding documents, while final decisions remain deterministic.﻿Risk HandlingChallengeApproachPoor scan qualityConfidence scoring + REVIEWAmbiguous languageHuman review fallbackAI hallucinationStructured outputs + rule validationFormat variabilityLayout-aware parsing﻿Security & ComplianceRole-based access (Uploader, Evaluator, Auditor)Encrypted storage and secure APIsComplete logging for audit complianceCompatible with government infrastructure (e.g., MeghRaj)﻿Expected Impact⏱️ Reduce evaluation time significantly? Improve consistency across decisions? Enhance transparency and auditability⚖️ Prevent unfair rejection of valid bidders?‍? Assist officers instead of replacing them﻿ConclusionThis solution focuses on augmenting human decision-making rather than automating it blindly. By combining structured extraction, deterministic validation, and explainable outputs, the system brings reliability and efficiency to one of the most critical government processes.The goal is not just faster decisions, but better, fairer, and fully accountable decisions.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS

V vijayraju346_6a05
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) Framework: A Neuro-Symbolic and Multimodal System for Automated Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 08:13 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) FrameworkAI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis System﻿1. IntroductionGovernment procurement is a critical process that ensures transparency, fairness, and efficient utilization of public funds. Organizations such as the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) rely on tender-based systems to procure goods and services. However, the current evaluation process is largely manual, time-consuming, and prone to inconsistencies.The Cognitive Procurement Intelligence (CPI) Framework is proposed as an advanced AI-based system that automates tender evaluation while ensuring accuracy, transparency, and auditability. The system acts as a smart auditor that understands tender requirements, analyzes bidder submissions, and provides explainable decisions.﻿2. Problem StatementTender documents contain complex eligibility criteria including technical specifications, financial requirements, compliance conditions, and mandatory certifications. These are written in legal language and distributed across multiple pages.Bidders submit supporting documents such as financial statements, certificates, and company profiles in various formats including PDFs, scanned images, Word files, and photographs.The key challenge is to accurately determine whether each bidder satisfies all eligibility criteria while handling unstructured data and ensuring consistency in evaluation.﻿3. Challenges in Existing Systems3.1 Manual System ChallengesEvaluation process takes several days per tender High cognitive load leads to human errors Inconsistent decisions across evaluators Lack of standardized evaluation procedures Difficult to audit decisions Limited scalability with increasing data ﻿3.2 Existing AI System ChallengesPoor accuracy on scanned or low-quality documents Limited understanding of legal and technical language Lack of explainability in decision-making Inability to distinguish mandatory and optional criteria Weak cross-document analysis Difficulty in handling diverse document formats No mechanism to detect fraud or inconsistencies ﻿4. Proposed SolutionThe CPI Framework is designed to address these challenges by combining advanced AI techniques with logical reasoning mechanisms. The system operates as a multi-layer intelligent pipeline that mimics human expertise while ensuring consistency and transparency.The key objectives of the system are:Automate extraction of eligibility criteria from tender documents Analyze bidder submissions across multiple formats Perform accurate and consistent evaluation Provide explainable and auditable decisions Identify ambiguous cases and flag them for manual review ﻿5. System ArchitectureThe CPI Framework consists of the following layers:Perception Layer Extraction Layer Reasoning Layer Validation Layer Explanation Layer Each layer contributes to transforming raw documents into structured insights and final decisions.﻿6. Detailed Working Mechanism6.1 Perception LayerThis layer is responsible for understanding the structure and content of documents.Key functionalities:Identifies layout elements such as headings, tables, and sections Processes scanned and image-based documents Applies preprocessing techniques such as noise removal and alignment correction Recognizes text and handwritten elements This ensures that even low-quality or complex documents can be processed effectively.﻿6.2 Extraction LayerThe extraction layer focuses on retrieving relevant data from documents.Key functionalities:Extracts financial data such as turnover and net worth Identifies certifications, registration numbers, and validity dates Parses tables and structured data Handles variations in how information is presented The extracted data is converted into a structured format for further processing.﻿6.3 Reasoning LayerThis layer performs logical evaluation based on predefined rules.Key functionalities:Matches extracted data with tender criteria Applies rule-based logic for eligibility determination Differentiates between mandatory and optional conditions Ensures consistent decision-making across all bidders This layer combines machine learning with rule-based systems to ensure both flexibility and accuracy.﻿6.4 Validation LayerThe validation layer ensures the correctness of extracted data.Key functionalities:Cross-verifies information across multiple documents Detects inconsistencies and mismatches Validates document authenticity and expiry dates Flags suspicious or incomplete data This layer reduces errors and improves the reliability of the system.﻿6.5 Explanation LayerThe explanation layer ensures transparency in decision-making.Key functionalities:Provides detailed reasoning for each decision Links decisions to specific criteria and documents Displays extracted values used in evaluation Generates a complete audit trail This makes the system suitable for government use where accountability is critical.﻿7. Key InnovationsThe CPI Framework introduces several innovative features:Multi-layer validation system for cross-document verification Explainable AI for transparent decision-making Intelligent document understanding beyond keyword matching Detection of hidden inconsistencies and potential fraud Integration of rule-based logic with machine learning ﻿8. Human-in-the-Loop MechanismThe system incorporates a human-in-the-loop approach to handle uncertainty.Cases with low confidence are flagged for manual review Officers are provided with detailed explanations Human feedback can be used to improve the system This ensures that no bidder is unfairly disqualified.﻿9. Output and ReportingThe system generates comprehensive evaluation reports including:Criterion-wise evaluation for each bidder Final eligibility status Supporting evidence for each decision Summary of all bidders Exportable reports for official use ﻿10. Scalability and PerformanceThe CPI Framework is designed to handle large-scale data efficiently.Can process multiple tenders simultaneously Reduces evaluation time from days to minutes Scales easily with increasing number of bidders ﻿11. Impact and BenefitsThe system provides significant advantages:Reduces evaluation time drastically Improves accuracy and consistency Enhances transparency and accountability Minimizes human errors Detects fraud and inconsistencies Supports better decision-making ﻿12. Risks and MitigationPotential risks include:OCR inaccuracies in low-quality documents Ambiguity in legal language Incomplete or inconsistent data Mitigation strategies:Use of preprocessing techniques Human-in-the-loop review system Multi-layer validation ﻿13. Implementation RoadmapPhase 1:Develop basic document processing and extraction systemPhase 2:Integrate advanced AI models and reasoning mechanismsPhase 3:Enhance validation, scalability, and performance﻿14. ConclusionThe Cognitive Procurement Intelligence Framework transforms the traditional tender evaluation process into an intelligent, automated, and transparent system. By combining document understanding, data extraction, logical reasoning, and explainability, the system ensures accurate and fair evaluation of bidders.This solution not only improves efficiency but also strengthens trust and accountability in government procurement processes.

1 1dt23ca043_11e2
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AdhikarAI — Court Judgment to Verified Government Plan
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:27 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
India's High Courts dispose thousands of judgments every year. Each judgment contains critical directives — who must act, by when, and what happens if they don't. Today, these directives are buried in lengthy PDF documents, manually read by officials, and frequently missed or misinterpreted — leading to missed deadlines, fragmented accountability, and even contempt of court risks.
AdhikarAI doesn't just read judgments — it ensures they are executed.
AdhikarAI is an AI-powered Court Case Monitoring System that transforms unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, human-verified government action plans.
HOW IT WORKS:
EXTRACT — Upload any court judgment PDF (scanned or digital). Our pipeline uses PyMuPDF + OCR to parse the document, then an LLM extracts key information: case details, date of order, directions/orders, parties involved, responsible entities, deadlines, and timelines — each with a confidence score.

GENERATE ACTION PLAN — For every extracted directive, the system generates a structured action plan detailing required actions, responsible departments, timelines, and compliance steps.

HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP VERIFICATION (MANDATORY) — A legal reviewer validates extracted data side-by-side with the original PDF using source highlights. Records can be Approved, Edited, or Rejected. Only verified data moves forward — ensuring explainability and accountability.

TRUSTED DASHBOARD — A role-based dashboard presents verified action plans, assigned responsibilities, deadlines, and compliance status — giving decision-makers a real-time view of execution.

CORE PROBLEM SOLVED: Judgment directives are no longer lost in PDFs. Every order is extracted, verified, converted into actionable tasks, and tracked to completion — enabling timely and accountable government action.
INPUT: Multi-page court judgment PDFs (unstructured, scanned, or digital)
OUTPUT: Structured data → AI-generated action plans → Human-verified records → Real-time compliance dashboard
Built with: FastAPI, Next.js, PostgreSQL, PDF.js, PyMuPDF, Tesseract OCR

H harizeeraman_ae89
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
JusticeTrack — AI-Powered Court Judgment Analysis and Verified Compliance Action System
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Karnataka’s Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) already solves document delivery by automatically receiving judgment PDFs from the High Court’s Court Information System (CIS). However, delivery is where automation stops.What follows is entirely manual: officials must read lengthy legal documents, interpret directions, identify responsible departments, calculate appeal windows, and decide actions — all while statutory deadlines silently progress.This creates not just inefficiency, but legal risk. Missing a compliance deadline can trigger contempt of court. Missing an appeal window permanently forfeits the State’s right to challenge a judgment. Current systems provide no visibility into urgency — cases exist as flat lists with no indication of which ones require immediate action.JusticeTrack addresses this gap. It is a human-verified, deadline-aware compliance intelligence system designed specifically for how government legal workflows function.Key Design PrinciplesNo upstream changes: Integrates with existing CCMS APIs; no modification to CCMS/CIS required Human-in-the-loop: All outputs require mandatory human verification before use Audit-first architecture: Every AI output, confidence score, and human decision is logged and traceable Privacy-safe development: Uses synthetic/redacted judgments for training and testing Format-agnostic ingestion: Handles both digital and scanned PDFs via OCRHow JusticeTrack Works1. Judgment ClassificationIncoming judgments are automatically classified into five actionable categories:Compliance Direction Status Quo Order Appeal-Worthy Judgment Cost Imposition Contempt Risk Order Each category determines urgency, routing, and action templates.2. Structured Extraction with Confidence ScoringThe system extracts:Case details, parties, departments Key directions and timelines Required actions and appeal signals Each field is assigned an individual confidence score and linked to its exact source location in the document (page and paragraph). Low-confidence fields are automatically flagged for mandatory review.3. Legal Deadline EngineJusticeTrack embeds statutory timelines (e.g., Limitation Act rules) to compute:Appeal deadlines (e.g., 30 or 90 days) Compliance deadlines (explicit or inferred) Days remaining from current date Each case is assigned a Compliance Risk Level:CRITICAL: Deadline ≤ 7 days or contempt indicated HIGH: 8–21 days or strong directive language MEDIUM: 22–45 days LOW: Monitoring scenarios Cases are automatically prioritized based on risk — not filing order.4. Human Verification InterfaceA structured review system ensures reliability:Side-by-side PDF and extracted data view Click-to-highlight source traceability Field-level actions: Approve, Edit, Reject Mandatory review for low-confidence fields All actions are logged with user identity, timestamps, and change history.5. Department Routing & EscalationVerified cases are routed using a Department Registry that standardizes naming variations.Automated escalation triggers:21 days remaining — reminder 7 days — escalation to senior अधिकारी 3 days — critical alert6. Trusted DashboardOnly fully verified cases appear. Features include:Risk-prioritized case list Department-specific views Deadline calendar Action status tracking Exportable compliance reports Designed for rapid decision-making by senior officials.ImpactJusticeTrack transforms legal document handling from passive storage to proactive risk management. It ensures:No missed appeal deadlines Reduced contempt risk Faster inter-department coordination Full accountability and auditabilityImplementation PhasesCore extraction & verification prototype Deadline engine & risk scoring Routing, escalation & dashboardJusticeTrack — Because every judgment starts a clock. This system makes sure the government never misses it.

K kumarpushpam17_0293
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderSaarthi: Explainable Multilingual AI for Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tender evaluation is still a slow, document-heavy, and high-risk manual process. Procurement teams must read lengthy tender documents, identify eligibility criteria, and verify those conditions across bidder submissions that arrive in many formats such as typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, tables, certificates, and even photographs. This leads to delays, inconsistency across evaluators, and difficulty in maintaining an audit trail.We propose TenderSaarthi, an AI-assisted platform for tender evaluation and eligibility analysis designed specifically for formal government procurement workflows.The platform works in five stages: Tender Understanding: TenderSaarthi ingests the tender document and extracts eligibility criteria across technical, financial, compliance, certification, and document-checklist categories. It identifies whether a criterion is mandatory or optional and converts it into a structured rule that can later be matched against bidder submissions. Multiformat and Multilingual Document Understanding: The platform parses bidder documents across typed PDFs, scanned files, images, tables, and certificates. In addition, it is designed to support multilingual tender and bidder documents, including cases where tender clauses or supporting documents are submitted in regional languages. Using multilingual OCR plus translation-assisted extraction, the system can interpret and normalize information from English, Hindi, Kannada, and other Indian-language documents into a common structured representation for evaluation. Criteria-to-Evidence Matching: For each bidder, the system maps extracted evidence to each tender criterion. For example, turnover values, project experience, GST registration, certification validity, and compliance records are matched against the corresponding tender requirements. The engine combines rule-based checks for explicit thresholds with semantic matching for legal and technical language variations.Explainable Verdicts with Human-in-the-Loop Review: For every criterion, the system produces one of three outcomes: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review. Every verdict is fully explainable:which criterion was checked which document was usedwhat value or evidence was foundwhy the bidder passed, failed, or was flaggedIf the extracted evidence is incomplete, low-confidence, ambiguous, or poorly scanned, the system never silently disqualifies the bidder. Instead, it routes the case to a human reviewer with highlighted evidence and the reason for uncertainty.Audit-Ready Reporting: All automated findings and manual interventions are logged with document references, page-level evidence, confidence scores, reviewer edits, and final outcomes. The result is a consolidated evaluation report that procurement officers can review, export, and use as the basis for decision-making.Why this solution is practical TenderSaarthi is not a black-box AI tool. It is a decision-support platform built around transparency, auditability, and officer control. It aligns with real procurement requirements where every decision must be explainable and reviewable. It also supports a scalable future state where the same core engine can be reused across departments, tender types, and larger bidder volumes.﻿Architecture Diagram: View TenderSaarthi system flowRound 2 vision In the sandbox phase, we would demonstrate end-to-end tender ingestion, multilingual document processing, criterion extraction, bidder-wise evaluation, manual review workflows, and exportable audit-ready reports using mock or redacted tender data.

solutions.team_name: NyayaLoop: AI-Powered Court Judgment to Verified Action Plan Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hehe something
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NyayaLoop: AI-Powered Court Judgment to Verified Action Plan Engine
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:41 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The ProblemKarnataka's High Court disposes thousands of cases every year. Each judgment PDF lands in the Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) and then the process breaks down. A government officer receives a 30-60 page legal document, must read it entirely, mentally extract the directives buried inside, figure out which department owns each action, calculate deadlines, and log it manually. Directives get missed. Limitation periods lapse silently. The same court order comes back four years later because no one tracked whether it was ever executed.This is not a failure of intent. It is a failure of infrastructure.What NyayaLoop DoesNyayaLoop is an AI layer that sits on top of the existing CCMS. It does not replace any existing system. It makes the judgment PDF actionable in minutes instead of days.The system has four stages:Stage 1: Intelligent ExtractionWhen a judgment PDF arrives via the CCMS API, NyayaLoop processes it automatically. For scanned PDFs, it runs OCR with layout preservation. For digital PDFs, it extracts text with positional metadata. The system identifies the operative portion of the judgment (where 80% of actionable directives are concentrated) and extracts:Case number, court, bench, and date of orderPetitioner and respondent namesNature of the caseEvery directive or order, verbatim and normalizedThe responsible party for each directiveExplicit deadlinesInferred deadlines (e.g. "within eight weeks" is converted to a calculated calendar date)The limitation period for filing an appeal, calculated from the order dateWhether the case requires compliance, appeal consideration, or bothA confidence score for every extracted fieldThe exact PDF page and text span that supports every extraction (no black box outputs)Stage 2: AI-Generated Action PlanNyayaLoop converts the extracted information into a structured, decision-ready action plan with three possible tracks:Track A (Compliance Required): Plain-language summary of what must be done, which department or officer is responsible, the deadline, a suggested sequence of sub-tasks, and what documents or evidence must be gathered to prove compliance.Track B (Appeal Consideration): AI-flagged grounds for potential appeal drawn from the judgment text, the calculated limitation period, the recommended escalation path, and key legal points that may support an appeal.Track C (Both): For cases where the department must comply immediately to avoid contempt while simultaneously preserving appeal rights, NyayaLoop generates a parallel timeline covering both tracks.Every action plan also includes a Priority Score (High, Medium, or Low) based on deadline proximity, nature of directive, department involved, and contempt risk.Stage 3: Human-in-the-Loop Verification (Mandatory)This is the architectural centerpiece of NyayaLoop. The system is built on the principle that AI output is a starting point, not a final answer.The verification interface shows a split screen: the extracted data on the left, the source PDF on the right with the exact supporting text highlighted in yellow. Every field displays its confidence level (green for high, yellow for medium, red for low). Fields with low confidence are pre-flagged for mandatory human review.The reviewer can approve, edit inline, or reject each field with a reason. Every action is logged with the reviewer's name, timestamp, and the original versus edited value.Only verified records are allowed to move forward. Unverified data is quarantined and never reaches the dashboard.This creates a legally defensible chain: if a compliance action is taken based on NyayaLoop output, there is a documented human authorization at every step.Stage 4: Decision-Maker Dashboard (Verified Data Only)The dashboard shows only records that passed human verification. No raw AI output ever appears here.Views available to government officials:Department View: All pending actions for the logged-in department, sorted by deadlineCalendar View: A deadline heatmap covering the next 7, 30, and 60 daysRisk Alerts: Cases approaching the limitation period for appealAction Status Tracker: Pending, In Progress, Completed (officers update status themselves)Contempt Risk Tracker: Cases where a deadline has passed without a marked completionJudgment Explorer: Search and filter by department, date range, case type, and current statusThe dashboard also sends automated alerts via SMS and email when deadlines are approaching or when a case has been escalated.Why This is Different from Existing Legal AI ToolsAll existing legal AI in India (jhana.ai, BharatLaw, SUPACE, SUVAS) operates in the pre-judgment space. They help lawyers and judges research cases, find precedents, and draft documents. NyayaLoop operates post-judgment, on the government compliance side. It closes the loop between "order issued" and "order executed." This gap is completely unaddressed by any existing tool.The second differentiator is confidence-grounded verification UX. The AI shows its work, highlights its sources in the original PDF, quantifies its uncertainty, and hands structured control to a human reviewer. This makes the system deployable in a government context where "the AI said so" is not an acceptable answer.Integration with CCMSNyayaLoop is designed as a plug-in on top of the existing CCMS:Listens to the CCMS API webhook when a new judgment PDF is fetched from the High Court CISProcesses in the background and queues items for human verificationPushes verified action plans back to department dashboardsRequires zero modification to the CCMS coreTechnical StackOCR and PDF parsing: Azure Document Intelligence, PyMuPDF, TesseractLLM for extraction and action plan generation: Claude API / GPT-4o with structured JSON output schema and source-grounded promptingBackend: FastAPI (Python)Frontend dashboard: React with Tailwind CSSVerification UX: PDF.js for inline source highlightingDatabase: PostgreSQL for verified records, Redis for processing queueDeployment: Docker containers, deployable on-premise or on government cloud infrastructureDataset UsedKarnataka High Court judgments from the AWS Open Data Registry (Indian High Court Judgments dataset by Dattam Labs), downloaded from:  https://registry.opendata.aws/indian-high-court-judgments/  (https://registry.opendata.aws/indian-high-court-judgments/)﻿Karnataka court code: court=27_1. Judgments from 2020-2024 used for extraction testing, action plan quality evaluation, and confidence score calibration.ImpactEvery judgment that goes unexecuted is justice delayed twice: once in court, and again in administration. NyayaLoop turns every court order into a tracked, accountable work item. It makes the invisible visible. It gives IAS officers and department heads the same visibility over compliance that a project manager has over a sprint board.If it cannot work in the real world, it does not win. NyayaLoop is built for CCMS integration from day one. The data exists. The API exists. The problem is validated by the Centre for e-Governance itself. This is not a concept, it is a deployment plan.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


K kumarpushpam17_0293
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TenderSaarthi: Explainable Multilingual AI for Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 10:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government tender evaluation is still a slow, document-heavy, and high-risk manual process. Procurement teams must read lengthy tender documents, identify eligibility criteria, and verify those conditions across bidder submissions that arrive in many formats such as typed PDFs, scanned documents, Word files, tables, certificates, and even photographs. This leads to delays, inconsistency across evaluators, and difficulty in maintaining an audit trail.We propose TenderSaarthi, an AI-assisted platform for tender evaluation and eligibility analysis designed specifically for formal government procurement workflows.The platform works in five stages: Tender Understanding: TenderSaarthi ingests the tender document and extracts eligibility criteria across technical, financial, compliance, certification, and document-checklist categories. It identifies whether a criterion is mandatory or optional and converts it into a structured rule that can later be matched against bidder submissions. Multiformat and Multilingual Document Understanding: The platform parses bidder documents across typed PDFs, scanned files, images, tables, and certificates. In addition, it is designed to support multilingual tender and bidder documents, including cases where tender clauses or supporting documents are submitted in regional languages. Using multilingual OCR plus translation-assisted extraction, the system can interpret and normalize information from English, Hindi, Kannada, and other Indian-language documents into a common structured representation for evaluation. Criteria-to-Evidence Matching: For each bidder, the system maps extracted evidence to each tender criterion. For example, turnover values, project experience, GST registration, certification validity, and compliance records are matched against the corresponding tender requirements. The engine combines rule-based checks for explicit thresholds with semantic matching for legal and technical language variations.Explainable Verdicts with Human-in-the-Loop Review: For every criterion, the system produces one of three outcomes: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review. Every verdict is fully explainable:which criterion was checked which document was usedwhat value or evidence was foundwhy the bidder passed, failed, or was flaggedIf the extracted evidence is incomplete, low-confidence, ambiguous, or poorly scanned, the system never silently disqualifies the bidder. Instead, it routes the case to a human reviewer with highlighted evidence and the reason for uncertainty.Audit-Ready Reporting: All automated findings and manual interventions are logged with document references, page-level evidence, confidence scores, reviewer edits, and final outcomes. The result is a consolidated evaluation report that procurement officers can review, export, and use as the basis for decision-making.Why this solution is practical TenderSaarthi is not a black-box AI tool. It is a decision-support platform built around transparency, auditability, and officer control. It aligns with real procurement requirements where every decision must be explainable and reviewable. It also supports a scalable future state where the same core engine can be reused across departments, tender types, and larger bidder volumes.﻿Architecture Diagram: View TenderSaarthi system flowRound 2 vision In the sandbox phase, we would demonstrate end-to-end tender ingestion, multilingual document processing, criterion extraction, bidder-wise evaluation, manual review workflows, and exportable audit-ready reports using mock or redacted tender data.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE

N Night Fury
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Explainable AI Platform for Automated Tender Evaluation & Bidder Eligibility Analysis
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 23, 2026 11:16 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement processes, such as those used by CRPF, involve evaluating complex tender documents and heterogeneous bidder submissions. This is not just a data extraction task but a high-stakes decision-making process involving legal compliance, financial verification, and accountability. Currently, this process is manual, time-consuming, inconsistent, and difficult to audit.Our solution proposes an AI-powered, explainable decision-support platform that automates tender understanding, bidder evaluation, and eligibility verification while ensuring full transparency, auditability, and human oversight.Problem Understanding: Tender documents contain detailed eligibility criteria such as financial thresholds, technical requirements, compliance rules, and certifications. Bidders submit documents in multiple formats (PDFs, scans, images, Word files), often with inconsistent structures. Evaluating these manually leads to delays, errors, and lack of standardization.Solution Overview: We design a multi-stage intelligent pipeline that converts unstructured documents into structured insights and produces criterion-level eligibility decisions with full explanations.Technical Approach:a) Tender Understanding Engine: The system ingests tender documents and uses OCR and layout-aware models (such as LayoutLM) along with LLM-based semantic parsing to extract eligibility criteria. Each criterion is structured into machine-readable format and classified as financial, technical, or compliance-based, and as mandatory or optional.b) Bidder Document Intelligence: The platform processes bidder submissions across all formats using OCR (PaddleOCR/AWS Textract) and NLP techniques. It extracts key entities such as turnover, project experience, certifications (GST, ISO), and compliance indicators while handling variations in document formats.c) Hybrid Matching & Decision Engine: A hybrid system combining rule-based validation and LLM reasoning maps bidder data to tender criteria. Deterministic checks (e.g., turnover thresholds) are handled via rules, while ambiguous language is resolved using LLMs. Each criterion is evaluated as:Eligible (Pass)Not Eligible (Fail)Needs Manual Review (Low confidence or ambiguity)d) Confidence Scoring: Each extracted value and decision is assigned a confidence score. Low-confidence cases are automatically flagged for review.Explainability & Auditability (Core Innovation): Every decision is fully transparent and traceable:Criterion being evaluatedExtracted bidder valueSource document and page referenceReasoning behind the decisionThis ensures compliance with audit requirements (CAG, RTI) and builds trust in AI-assisted decision-making.Human-in-the-Loop System: The system never silently disqualifies bidders. Ambiguous cases (e.g., poor OCR quality, missing data, unclear language) are flagged for human review with clear justification. Human overrides are supported at every stage.Government-Ready Deployment: The system is designed for real-world constraints:Works on low-quality scanned and handwritten documentsSupports secure/on-premise deploymentMaintains full audit logs for complianceEnsures role-based access and decision accountabilityScalability & Long-Term Impact: This platform can scale across all government departments, PSUs, and even private procurement systems. Over time, it can evolve into a national procurement intelligence layer enabling:Standardized tender evaluationsFraud detection across biddersData-driven procurement insights and policy improvementsKey Technologies:OCR: PaddleOCR / AWS TextractDocument AI: LayoutLM / DonutLLMs: GPT / LLaMA (for semantic understanding)Backend: FastAPIStorage: PostgreSQL + Object StorageVector Search: FAISS / PineconeKey Benefits:Reduces evaluation time from days to minutesEliminates evaluator inconsistencyProvides explainable and auditable decisionsHandles real-world unstructured documentsEnables scalable and transparent governanceWe are not replacing human decision-makers, but augmenting them with AI to ensure faster, fairer, and fully auditable procurement decisions.

The proposed project focuses on developing an AI-based system for tender evaluation and eligibility analysis in CRPF procurement. The idea is to automate the traditionally manual and time-consuming process of evaluating vendor bids using advanced technologies such as Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning. The system will analyze tender documents, extract relevant information such as cost, technical specifications, and vendor credentials using OCR and NLP techniques, and automatically verify eligibility criteria like past experience, certifications, and financial stability. The novelty of this project lies in integrating automated eligibility verification with intelligent tender scoring and fraud detection in a single unified system, along with explainable AI-based decision reports to ensure transparency and accountability in government procurement.
