Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Procurement Justice Platform - From Tender Creation to Project Completion
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:44 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Nyayadarsi - India's Procurement Accountability Infrastructure Team: Coding_Aghoris Theme 3 - Live Demo: nyaya-darshi.vercel.app﻿The Problem Nobody Has SolvedEvery year, India loses lakhs of crores in government procurement corruption. CAG documents it. Contractors die over it. Courts take decades to resolve it.The reason is structural: corruption is invisible at the exact moment it happens. By the time an auditor arrives, the money is gone. By the time a court decides, the evidence has vanished.Nyayadarsi changes one fundamental thing: it makes corruption visible at the moment it is attempted before any money moves.﻿Where Corruption Actually EntersMost people assume procurement corruption happens at evaluation someone picks the wrong vendor. The reality is far more systematic. It enters at five distinct points:Point 1 - The tender is written to exclude competition. A specification requiring "RCC frame construction with post-tensioned slabs in seismic zone III between 2019–2022" sounds technical. In reality, only one contractor qualifies. The tender was written for them. This is the L1 Trap - documented across Karnataka PWD, BBMP, and CRPF in CAG audits. Nobody checks the tender itself before publication.Point 2 - Fraudulent documents pass evaluation. Ten bidders means hundreds of documents across formats. A human evaluator under deadline pressure misses things. Sophisticated actors submit clean-looking fraudulent certificates knowing they will not be caught.Point 3 - Shell companies simulate competition. Three companies bid. Same registered office. Same CA firm. Bids within 1.8% of each other. This is a cartel but nobody looks at bidders together, only one at a time.Point 4 - Work never gets done. CAG audits across BBMP documented bills for 80% of contracted value cleared against 20% of actual completed work. Ghost work. Cleared payments.Point 5 - Payment becomes extortion. The Karnataka State Contractors Association documented ₹37,370 crore in pending bills across four fiscal years. Two contractors died by suicide. Bills sat pending not because work was incomplete but because clearing them required paying a commission at every stage.Nyayadarsi closes all five windows. Simultaneously. At the point of decision.﻿Three Dashboards. One AI Backbone.Dashboard 1 - Government Officer Creates a TenderAs the officer types eligibility criteria, Nyayadarsi compares each criterion in real time against 10,000+ similar tenders in the same procurement category. If a criterion is so narrow that fewer than three vendors in the market could qualify, the system raises a Nyayadarsi Integrity Alert before the tender is published.The officer must revise the criterion or override with a documented justification, which becomes part of the permanent cryptographically signed audit record. The tender cannot be published with an unreviewed alert.This is the only platform that examines the tender document itself before bidding opens. It addresses the root cause, not the symptom.﻿Dashboard 2 - Evaluation Officer Reviews BidsEvery document is processed regardless of format - typed PDFs, scanned certificates, Word files, photographs of physical documents.The ingestion engine works in four layers:Format detection routes each document to the correct processing pathPre-processing corrects skewed scans, removes noise, upscales to 300 DPI minimumLayoutLMv3 extracts values while preserving spatial relationships that give them meaning - a number without its column header is meaninglessFinancial ontology maps bidder language to tender criteria: "Net Revenue from Operations" satisfies "Minimum Annual Turnover" - but only if the system understands they are equivalent, which requires domain knowledge that generic OCR tools do not haveEvery criterion receives one of three verdicts:GREEN - evidence clearly meets the threshold. Officer reviews and signs off.
RED - document missing or value falls short. Bidder excluded with full citation trail.
YELLOW - system is uncertain. Name mismatch, blurry scan, financial inconsistency. Goes to human review queue, ranked by consequence mandatory blockers first. Every YELLOW flag explains exactly what the ambiguity is and what the officer's options are.The inviolable rule: no bidder is ever silently disqualified.Phased Qualification - Breaking the L1 TrapPhase 1: Mandatory document eligibility check - failing bidders excluded with citations. Phase 2: Technical scoring - experience, equipment, credentials. Top five advance. Phase 3: Sealed financial bids open simultaneously - but only for Phase 2 qualifiers. Phase 4: Pre-award verification and award with complete audit trail.A vendor with a fraudulent certificate who would have won on lowest price is eliminated in Phase 1 before their price is ever visible.Collusion Risk Engine - What No Other Platform Is BuildingAfter individual evaluations, Nyayadarsi runs cross-bidder analysis invisible at the per-bidder level:Shared Address Flag - multiple bidders at the same registered office, the signature of shell company networks simulating competitionCA Fingerprint Flag - independent bidders' financial documents share forensic characteristics: identical fonts, layouts, rounding patterns suggesting coordinated preparationBid Clustering Flag - statistical spread computed across all submitted bids. When bids cluster within 2% of each other just above the designated winner, Nyayadarsi calculates the probability of that occurring by chance and shows it to the officerOwnership Network Flag - cross-references MCA director data to identify direct registered links between the winning firm and the authorising officerDocument Quality Asymmetry Flag - detects when one specific document, typically the one most likely to be forged, has significantly worse image quality than everything else in the submissionNone of these flags disqualify anyone automatically. All five surface to a senior officer with full evidence for investigation.﻿Dashboard 3 - Builder Submits Daily ProgressThe contractor uploads three geotagged photographs and a 60-second video every working day from the mobile app. Nyayadarsi verifies GPS coordinates match the registered project site, confirms timestamps were not manipulated, uses YOLOv8 computer vision to estimate construction progress, and compares the daily delta against the expected rate for the current milestone.Uploads from the wrong location are rejected at submission. Milestone billing cannot be raised until AI verification and officer confirmation are complete.The 80%/20% billing fraud becomes structurally impossible. You cannot bill for work that has not been photographed, GPS-confirmed, and AI-analysed.Payment logic: Milestone completed + AI verified + Officer confirmed = automatic payment release within 72 hours. No officer has timing discretion. No commission extraction point exists in the chain.﻿The Audit Trail - Legal Protection for Honest OfficersEvery action generates an append-only log: microsecond timestamp, SHA-256 hash of source document AND evaluation output, AI model version, verbatim extracted value with page and cell citation, confidence score, and officer digital signature for every human decision.Genuinely append-only at two levels: the application database user has INSERT privileges only, and the underlying volume is WORM - deletion is physically prevented at the infrastructure level.When a disqualified bidder challenges a decision in the Central Administrative Tribunal or High Court, CRPF exports a cryptographically signed, court-admissible record of every step. Every hash verifies. The honest officer is legally untouchable.﻿Technology StackComponentTechnologyDocument layout understandingLayoutLMv3OCRTesseract + Google Document AIAI reasoningLlama 3 - on-premise only, fully data-sovereignConstruction progressYOLOv8Collusion analysisscipy + NetworkXBackendFastAPI + Celery + RedisOfficer dashboardReact + Next.jsBuilder mobile appReact NativeAudit infrastructureSHA-256, WORM volume, PostgreSQL INSERT-onlyDeploymentDocker + Kubernetes, on-premise or MeitY GovCloudGPT-4 and all cloud LLM APIs are excluded entirely. Data sovereignty for CRPF procurement is non-negotiable.﻿Honest LimitationsLLM hallucination is real Nyayadarsi operates in extractive mode only, quoting verbatim and citing sources, never generating values. The MCA director check catches direct links only - benami structures are invisible to public data and we do not overstate this. GeM integration requires NIC coordination, not a simple API call - Phase 1 testing uses publicly available CPPP tenders as proxies. Change management is the hardest problem - immutable audit trails remove deniability, and mandatory deployment requires a directive from DG CRPF or CVC level. We acknowledge this directly.﻿Scale and Long-Term ImpactThe same platform with department-specific configuration serves Karnataka PWD, BBMP, NHAI, Ministry of Defence, and every Central and State procurement authority.After twelve months across fifty departments, every processed tender feeds a National Tender Memory - identifying which criteria patterns recur in the same regions, which vendors win with disproportionate frequency, which officers' tenders consistently produce single-vendor outcomes reported automatically to CAG and CVC in real time.﻿CAG audits arrive years after the money moves. Nyayadarsi generates the evidence automatically. At the point of decision. Before funds are committed.This is not a document processing tool. This is India's procurement accountability infrastructure.न्यायदर्शी - AI that sees justice.

solutions.team_name: JURYSIGHT : AI for court judgement intelligence
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


I InnoFiesta
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
JURYSIGHT : AI for court judgement intelligence
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:56 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
JURISIGHT
AI for Court Judgment Intelligence The system that reads complex legal PDFs, extracts directives, generates structured action plans, and ensures human‑verified compliance for government departments.
Team LegalFlow Mahesh Vaswani, Software Development Engineer @ Barclays Mayuri Kolhe, Software Development Engineer @ Mastercard
THE PROBLEM
Judgments arrive in CCMS as lengthy PDFs.

Critical directives buried in complex text.

Officials must manually interpret entire documents.

Result: delays, inefficiencies, missed compliance deadlines.

WHAT JURISIGHT IS
One line: JURISIGHT extracts case details, generates action plans, verifies with human review, and delivers trusted dashboards for decision‑makers. Four steps make this possible:
Extraction: case details, dates, parties, directives, timelines.

Action Plan: compliance, appeal consideration, responsible departments, deadlines.

Verification: human‑in‑the‑loop approval, edit, reject.

Dashboard: department‑wise view of verified actions and dates.

EXTRACTION ENGINE
Handles scanned and digital PDFs.

Uses NLP tuned for legal language.

Outputs structured fields: case number, order date, directives, parties, timelines.

ACTION PLAN GENERATOR
Structures directives into clear actions.

Compliance required or appeal considered.

Responsible authority identified.

Deadlines explicit or inferred.

Nature of action clearly defined.

VERIFICATION LAYER
Mandatory human review.

Confidence scores visible.

Source highlights shown.

Only approved records move forward.

DASHBOARD
Trusted view only.

Department‑wise actions.

Key dates and deadlines.

Flags pending compliance.

Clean, reliable information for decision‑makers.

SAMPLE SCENARIO
Judgment PDF fetched via CCMS API.

Extraction: Case #123, Order Date: 12 Apr 2026, Directive: Dept must file compliance within 30 days.

Action Plan: Compliance required, Dept X responsible, deadline 12 May 2026.

Reviewer approves.

Dashboard shows verified plan.

TECH STACK
Python + NLP for extraction.

Rule‑based + ML hybrid for action plan generation.

Human‑in‑the‑loop UI for verification.

PostgreSQL + ElasticSearch for storage.

React + D3.js for dashboards.

Kubernetes for scalable deployment.

STAGES OF BUILD
Stage 1: PDF ingestion and baseline extraction.

Stage 2: Action plan generator and confidence scoring.

Stage 3: Human verification module and source highlights.

Stage 4: Dashboard integration and sandbox demo.

RISKS AND MITIGATIONS
Complex legal language → fine‑tuned legal NLP.

False extraction → mandatory human verification.

Scanned PDFs → OCR preprocessing.

User distrust → confidence scores and explainability.

WHY JURISIGHT WINS
Court‑to‑action pipeline, not just extraction.

Human‑in‑the‑loop ensures trust.

Explainable outputs with confidence and source highlights.

Dashboard clarity for departments.

Scalable and modular across cases.

Courts deliver justice. JURISIGHT delivers action. The system that extracts, structures, verifies, and informs. Built to transform Karnataka’s compliance workflow, because justice delayed is justice denied.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B bhumikachhabra23.set_acbe
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaPath AI: Judgement to action system with citizen protection.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:08 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NyayaPath AI is built for normal people like us—people who don’t understand complicated legal language and often feel confused or stressed in legal situations. When someone faces a legal issue, they usually don’t know what the judgement really means, what to do next, or whether they are being treated fairly. This system makes things simple. It takes complex legal decisions and explains them in easy, everyday language, so anyone can understand. Then, it guides the user with clear, step-by-step actions—what to do next, where to go, and how to handle the situation properly. But what makes NyayaPath AI truly useful is that it looks out for the user. If someone is about to take a wrong step—like reacting emotionally, paying more money than needed, or following incorrect advice—the system warns them and suggests a better, legal way. It also helps users stay alert in situations where they might be getting misled, such as being asked to pay extra fines or facing unnecessary delays. In simple words, NyayaPath AI is like a smart helper that stands by you, helping you understand your situation, make the right decisions, and avoid being confused or taken advantage of. It doesn’t replace lawyers, but it gives people the confidence and basic guidance they need to handle legal situations more safely and correctly.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Coding_Aghoris
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Nyayadarsi: AI-Driven Tender Evaluation and Governance Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:20 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Every other team will build a faster document parser. Nyayadarsi closes the information asymmetry gap that procurement corruption depends on to survive — across the entire procurement lifecycle, not just the evaluation step. The problem runs three layers deep: on the surface, manual tender evaluation is slow, inconsistent, and legally indefensible; beneath that, procurement committees face two simultaneous threats — accidental non-compliance by legitimate businesses and deliberate document fraud by sophisticated bad actors — and most automation tools address only one; and at the root, in documented cases confirmed by CAG Performance Audits across Karnataka PWD and BBMP, tender criteria are written before bidding opens to exclude legitimate competition entirely, with the Karnataka State Contractors Association documenting Rs 37,370 crore in pending contractor bills and two contractor deaths by suicide that Lokayukta could not prosecute because cash transactions leave no paper trail. Nyayadarsi addresses all three layers through three dashboards on one platform. The Government Officer Dashboard runs a live specificity analysis as criteria are typed — if a criterion matches fewer than three vendors in the relevant market, a Nyayadarsi Integrity Alert fires before publication; the officer must revise or override with documented justification that becomes part of a cryptographically signed permanent audit record; this is the only platform in this competition that scrutinises the tender document itself, because corruption is frequently a criteria-drafting problem, not an evaluation problem. The Evaluation Officer Dashboard breaks the L1 trap — where lowest price wins regardless of eligibility — through one design principle: price is not visible until the bidder has passed eligibility; Phase 1 checks all mandatory criteria with full citation chains (GREEN auto-eligible, YELLOW mandatory human review with structured reason and estimated resolution time, RED auto-excluded with evidence), Phase 2 scores technical capacity with the officer resolving the YELLOW queue, Phase 3 opens sealed bids simultaneously with the Collusion Risk Engine running automatically, and Phase 4 completes pre-award verification — a fraudulent ISO certificate eliminates its bidder in Phase 1 before their price is ever seen. The Builder Dashboard requires daily GPS-verified photographs and a 60-second video walkthrough from the registered project site; off-site uploads are rejected instantly; milestone billing is blocked until AI progress verification and officer confirmation are both complete; and once confirmed, payment releases automatically within 72 hours with no officer timing discretion, structurally eliminating both the 80%/20% ghost-work billing fraud and the commission extraction at every clearance stage that drove the Karnataka contractor crisis. The technical core is built on LayoutLMv3 for layout-aware extraction that preserves table row-column relationships in scanned documents; a Financial Ontology mapping acceptable equivalent field names built from actual Indian CA certificate formats; cross-document consistency analysis that flags discrepancies across CA certificate, ITR, and balance sheet to catch single-document forgeries that extract cleanly; and a Collusion Risk Engine with five flags — Shared Address (shell company networks), CA Fingerprint (forensic similarity above 87% across independent bidders' financials), Bid Clustering (CV below 5% with p-value of natural occurrence calculated), Ownership Network (director of winning firm linked to the authorising officer via MCA and HR data), and Document Quality Asymmetry (one compliance document deliberately degraded to obstruct extraction) — none triggering automatic disqualification, all surfacing to a senior officer with full statistical evidence. Every automated action generates a genuinely append-only WORM log with SHA-256 hashes of source documents and evaluation outputs, verbatim extracted values with page and cell citations, and mandatory officer Digital Signature for every human decision — so when a disqualified bidder challenges in the CAT or High Court, CRPF exports a cryptographically signed, court-admissible record and the honest officer is legally untouchable. The full stack runs on-premise and data-sovereign: LayoutLMv3, Tesseract OCR, Google Document AI for complex tables, Llama 3 8B and 70B on-premise in extractive mode only (never generating values, always citing source), YOLOv8 for construction progress estimation, FastAPI + Celery + Redis for async processing, React + Next.js for the officer dashboard, React Native for the builder mobile app, and Docker + Kubernetes for deployment within CRPF's security perimeter or MeitY GovCloud. The Round 2 roadmap runs Phase 0 (ground truth calibration from real CPPP tenders before a line of code is written) through Phase 4 (full end-to-end demonstration from tender creation to milestone payment) across six weeks. At scale, every processed tender feeds a National Tender Memory layer that identifies rigged-criteria patterns, disproportionate vendor win rates, and officers whose tenders consistently produce single-vendor outcomes — reported automatically to CAG and CVC in real time, not years after the money has moved. CAG audits arrive years after funds are committed. Lokayukta cannot prosecute what it cannot prove. Court challenges drag for decades. Nyayadarsi generates the evidence automatically — at the point of decision, before funds are committed. This is not a document processing tool. This is India's procurement accountability infrastructure. न्यायदर्शी — AI that sees justice

solutions.team_name: AI Platform for Tender Evaluation with Human-in-the-Loop Verification
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A AuditAI
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI Platform for Tender Evaluation with Human-in-the-Loop Verification
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:50 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement demands absolute fairness, transparency and legal accountability. The current tender evaluation process is manual, high-friction and error-prone. Bidders submit highly unstructured, heterogeneous documents typed PDFs, skewed scans, handwritten forms, tables and stamped certificates often in multiple languages. The challenge is not merely extracting text, but verifiably matching heterogeneous evidence against complex, legally binding criteria without ever silently disqualifying a bidder. The hardest unsolved problems in existing tools are:• LLMs misinterpreting legally ambiguous clauses even when citing correct source spans• Vision AI silently corrupting financial values from stamped or degraded documents• RAG pipelines failing on exact identifiers (e.g., ISO 9001 vs. ISO 27001)• No support for regional-language government certificates (Hindi, regional scripts)• Scalability: 50+ criteria × 100+ bidders = 5,000+ evaluation calls with no batching strategy• Audit systems that log actions but cannot reconstruct full decision provenance We propose an AI-powered, explainable tender evaluation platform that automates document understanding, eligibility verification and decision support while ensuring full transparency, auditability and human control.﻿System Workflow Upload tender document and bidder submissions Extract eligibility criteria using AI Parse bidder documents (PDFs, scans, images) Match extracted data with criteria Generate results: Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review With evidence, confidence score and reasoning Human reviewers validate uncertain cases Final audit-ready report is generated﻿Core ArchitecturePipeline 1: Tender Rules Extraction (Triple-Validated Schema Builder)﻿Converts complex legal tender documents into a structured, machine-executable schema.Maker LLM: Extracts criteria into structured JSON (type, threshold, mandatory/optional, source span) Checker LLM: Verifies exact text span Independently re-derives rule to detect misinterpretation Template Validation: Matches against known government clause patterns Human Sign-off: Ambiguous rules are reviewed and approved before use ﻿﻿Pipeline 2: Evidence Ingestion (Confidence-Scored Multi-Modal Extraction)﻿Processes messy bidder documents reliably.Supports PDFs, scanned images, multilingual documents (Hindi + others) Page-level classification (text / scanned / mixed) Every extracted field has a confidence score (0–1) Low-confidence fields are flagged before evaluation Hybrid storage using: semantic embeddings (context) BM25 (exact identifiers like ISO, GST, PAN) Re-verification anchor: system can re-read exact document region if needed ﻿﻿Pipeline 3: Agentic Evaluation Engine (Parallel + Verified)﻿Matches bidder data against criteria with full traceability.Parallel evaluation of independent criteria (~60% faster) Dependency-aware execution using lightweight DAG Re-verification loop: low-confidence data is re-read from original document Advanced anomaly detection: financial inconsistencies identifier validation (GST, PAN, CIN) unrealistic claims cross-bidder outliers Output per criterion:Status (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review) Source document + page Extracted value Confidence score Explanation ﻿﻿Pipeline 4: RBAC Dashboard & Immutable Audit System﻿Ensures governance, accountability and compliance.Role-based workflow: L1: basic validation L2: anomaly resolution L3: final authority Escalation control: prevents overload by grouping complex cases Immutable audit log: records AI decisions + human actions stores full reasoning trace enables complete reconstruction of decisions ﻿﻿Technology Decisions and Trade offs﻿Hybrid Search vs. Standard RAGPure semantic (dense) retrieval often fails on exact identifiers (e.g., ISO 9001 vs ISO 27001). Our system combines:Dense embeddings → for contextual understanding BM25 keyword search → for exact matches﻿Agentic Evaluation vs. Bulk ProcessingInstead of evaluating all criteria in a single pass, each criterion is processed independently.Ensures per-criterion traceability Prevents context mixing inside LLM responses Supports parallel execution for speed﻿Confidence Scoring vs. Binary ExtractionEvery extracted value is assigned a confidence score instead of a simple pass/fail.High confidence → automated evaluation Medium/low confidence → flagged for review﻿Enterprise Vision AI vs. Basic OCRStandard OCR struggles with:stamped documents rotated scans handwritten inputs We use advanced document intelligence systems that provide:structured extraction confidence scores﻿Append-Only Audit Log vs. Editable RecordsTraditional databases allow modification of past records, which creates compliance risks.Our system uses:append-only audit logs no deletion or modification of past events﻿Security & Data Privacy﻿For CRPF and government procurement systems, data sovereignty and security are non-negotiable. The platform is designed with strict security guarantees to ensure sensitive data is never exposed or misused.On-Premise Deployment ArchitectureAll system components including:LLM inference vector database document storage are fully containerized (Docker/Kubernetes) and can run:on-premise or within a government-approved private cloudEnd-to-End EncryptionAll data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 All inter-service communication uses mutual TLS (mTLS)Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with MFAAccess control is enforced at the API level, not just the UI Each role has least-privilege permissions Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled No user can both evaluate and approve the same caseBidder-Level Data IsolationEach bidder’s documents are stored in isolated namespaces Vector search is strictly scoped per bidder Cross-bidder data access is architecturally impossible﻿Expected Impact﻿Reduces evaluation time by 70–80%Ensures consistent and unbiased decision-makingEliminates manual document verification overheadProvides instant, audit-ready evaluation reportsImproves transparency and trust in government procurement﻿EndThis platform moves beyond basic document extraction to deliver a defensible, legally auditable, government-grade tender evaluation system built on the principle that AI must never be blindly trusted. It introduces triple-validated rule extraction to prevent misinterpretation of legal clauses, confidence-gated ingestion with re-verification to eliminate silent extraction errors and a parallel, anomaly-aware evaluation engine with full decision provenance logging to ensure scalability and traceability. With multilingual support and controlled escalation workflows, the system is designed for real-world deployment at government scale. Crucially, it preserves human-in-the-loop authority at every stage, positioning AI as a decision-support tool that provides structured, evidence-backed insights, while final decisions remain with authorized procurement officials.

solutions.team_name: JudgmentIQ — A Zero-Trust Compliance Co-Pilot for Court Directives
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hack Elite
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
JudgmentIQ — A Zero-Trust Compliance Co-Pilot for Court Directives
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:51 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
60 Seconds. 40 Pages. One Missed Deadline Away From a Contempt Notice.A single High Court judgment can contain a dozen hidden directives — each tied to a different department, a different limitation period, and a different legal consequence if missed. Today, those directives are discovered by officers reading every page by hand. When they miss one, the State loses by default, appeal windows close forever, and citizens pay the price.JudgmentIQ is the compliance co-pilot that makes missing a directive structurally impossible.﻿Why "Yet Another AI PDF Reader" Isn't EnoughAny team can wire up OCR, an LLM, and a dashboard. That's the obvious 20% of the problem. The real problem is trust:Would a senior government officer stake their career on an AI-generated action plan? Today, no. Our job is to change that answer.JudgmentIQ is engineered around a Zero-Trust AI philosophy: every AI output is treated as suspect until a human has seen it, traced it to its source, and signed off. The architecture enforces this — it is not a policy that can be skipped.﻿The Four Signature Features1. Citation Lens — No Claim Without a SourceEvery extracted field is bound to its exact origin: page, paragraph, and sentence. One click jumps the officer to the highlighted passage in the original PDF. If the AI cannot cite it, it cannot claim it.2. Confidence Ledger — Calibrated Uncertainty, Not VibesMost AI products show a meaningless "87%" next to every output. We calibrate confidence against a held-out set of real Karnataka judgments — so a 70% score actually means 7-in-10 historical accuracy. Low-confidence fields are auto-routed to a Review Queue; high-confidence ones are fast-tracked.3. Adversarial Verifier — AI That Challenges ItselfA second, independently-prompted agent re-reads the judgment and attempts to disagree with the first extraction. When the two agents diverge, the conflict is surfaced to the human reviewer as a structured disagreement — "Extractor A says deadline = Dec 15; Verifier B says Jan 30; source page 23 is ambiguous." Humans decide. This is how we catch the edge cases a single model would miss.4. Compliance Memory — Learning From Every CorrectionEvery Edit the reviewing officer makes becomes training signal. Over time, JudgmentIQ learns the Karnataka legal dialect — how this court phrases compliance orders, how this department handles appeals, which judges use "at liberty to approach" to mean what. The system gets smarter with every case.﻿The Four-Step PipelineREAD — OCR + layout-aware parsing for scanned, digital, and mixed-quality PDFsEXTRACT — Case details, parties, orders, responsible authority, timelines (explicit + inferred)PLAN — Structured action plan with compliance steps, appeal windows, and calculated deadlinesVERIFY — Mandatory Approve / Edit / Reject gate; unverified records are structurally blocked from the dashboard﻿The Metric That Matters — Court-to-Citizen Time (CCT)Everyone else will brag about extraction accuracy. We care about one number:CCT = days from judgment disposal to verified action plan published to the responsible department.Today, CCT is often weeks. Our target on demo day: under 10 minutes, end-to-end, with a human in the loop. This is the number that decides whether a citizen gets the service they were promised.﻿What the Judge Will See on Demo DayUpload a real Karnataka High Court judgment (never seen before by our system)30 seconds later: structured action plan on screen, every field citation-linked to the PDFDisagreement Panel: one field flagged where the Adversarial Verifier challenged the ExtractorOfficer clicks Edit on an ambiguous deadline — Compliance Memory updates in real timeOfficer clicks Approve — record publishes to the department-filtered, deadline-sorted dashboardStopwatch stops at under 10 minutes. CCT proven.We are not pitching a prototype. We are pitching the operating system for court-ordered government action.

solutions.team_name: Lex-Audit: Deterministic Legal Extraction & Verification Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Code Breakers
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Lex-Audit: Deterministic Legal Extraction & Verification Engine
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:01 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Executive Summary: The transition from a High Court judgment PDF to an actionable government directive is currently a manual, error-prone bottleneck. We approach this not as a generic text-summarization task, but as a rigid data-forensics operation. Lex-Audit is an AI-assisted, Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) pipeline that ingests complex legal PDFs, extracts critical compliance timelines and directives using constrained-output models, and maps them into a structured action plan. By enforcing "bounding-box explainability," we ensure that no AI-generated data enters the government dashboard without explicit human verification, eliminating the risk of legal non-compliance due to LLM hallucinations.1. Problem Understanding & The Legal Data Reality Government departments cannot act on "AI summaries"; they must act on exact judicial directives. High Court judgments are unstructured, legally dense, and often span dozens of pages. The primary bottleneck is not just reading the document, but isolating the Actionable Directives (e.g., "Compliance required within 60 days") from the judicial context. If an AI hallucinates a date or misidentifies the responsible department, the state risks contempt of court.2. Technical Implementation: Extraction & Generation Pipeline We isolate the extraction process from the generation process to maintain absolute data integrity:Stage 1 (Ingestion & Normalization): Judgments fetched via the CIS API are passed through an advanced Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and layout-parsing layer to handle unstructured, multi-page PDFs, converting them into structured markdown while retaining spatial coordinates.Stage 2 (Forensic Extraction): Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and tightly constrained JSON-output prompts, the LLM extracts specific entities: Date of Order, Petitioner/Respondent, Explicit Timelines, and Actionable Directives.Stage 3 (Action Plan Generation): The extracted data is mapped to a state-defined schema, categorizing the required action (e.g., "File Appeal" vs. "Execute Compliance") and assigning it to the inferred target department.3. The Verification Layer (Mandatory Human-in-the-Loop) This is the core differentiator of Lex-Audit. We treat the AI as an assistant, not an authority.Bounding-Box Explainability: When the system extracts a "60-day compliance timeline," it does not just present the text. The UI displays the original PDF alongside the extracted data, hyperlinking directly to the exact highlighted paragraph in the document where that timeline was found.Zero-Trust Verification: The extracted action plan sits in a "Pending Review" state. A human legal officer must click "Approve," "Edit," or "Reject" on each extracted field. Only cryptographically signed, human-verified records are permitted to move to the main database.4. The Executive Dashboard & Output Once verified, the unstructured PDF is successfully transformed into a highly structured database row. The Executive Dashboard provides department heads with a clean, trusted view:Filter by "Days until Compliance Deadline."Filter by "Department Responsible."Flag cases where an appeal limitation period is expiring within 7 days.5. Architecture, Tech Stack, & RisksTech Stack: Python backend (FastAPI) orchestrating PDF parsing (e.g., PyMuPDF/EasyOCR), a vector database for semantic search, and an isolated LLM strictly configured for JSON entity extraction. The frontend will be a React-based verification dashboard.Identified Risk & Mitigation: The primary risk is the AI missing an obscurely worded directive (False Negative). Trade-off: We tune the extraction model for high recall over high precision. The AI is designed to over-extract potential directives and let the human reviewer quickly reject the irrelevant ones, ensuring no actual court order is ever missed.

solutions.team_name: VyaparaSetu: Connecting Karnataka’s Business Systems through Event Driven Middleware
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S sakethm1122_7fec
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
VyaparaSetu: Connecting Karnataka’s Business Systems through Event Driven Middleware
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:10 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project Document / Detailed Architecture: You can view the complete solution here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Kk6pHuBQ2_mOo6pdyBVG6dZE7iGwkmNI/view?usp=sharing
VyaparaSetu is a lightweight, event-driven interoperability middleware designed to solve the “split-brain” problem in Karnataka’s business ecosystem, where the same business data exists inconsistently across the Single Window System (SWS) and 40+ legacy department systems.Currently, updates made in one system (e.g., address changes in SWS) are not reflected across other departmental systems, leading to data inconsistency, operational inefficiencies, and lack of trust. Since modifying existing government systems is not feasible, VyaparaSetu introduces a non-intrusive integration layer that connects all systems externally.
What the Solution Does:VyaparaSetu enables real-time, bidirectional data synchronization between SWS and all department systems without modifying any of them.i) Converts updates from any system into standardized events ii) Uses UBID (Unique Business ID) as the single linking key iii) Propagates updates across systems through an event-driven pipeline iv) Ensures data consistency, reliability, and traceability
Core Architecture:The system is built around a scalable, modular architecture: Adapters (per system) : Detect changes via APIs, CDC, polling, or logs and convert them into events Kafka Event Backbone: Ensures reliable, ordered, and decoupled communication Processing Engine: Validates events, detects conflicts, applies resolution rules, and routes updates AI-Assisted Schema Mapper: Handles differences in field names and formats across systems using configurable mappings Immutable Event Store: Maintains a complete audit trail of all changes Trace & Query Layer: Allows monitoring, debugging, and manual overrides
Key Features: Two-way synchronization (SWS ↔ Departments) Zero modification to existing systems Conflict detection & resolution using configurable rules Full auditability (who changed what, when, and where) AI-assisted onboarding of new departments Why This Matters:VyaparaSetu transforms fragmented government systems into a unified, consistent data ecosystem without requiring risky migrations or system redesigns.Eliminates data inconsistencies across departments Improves governance, transparency, and decision-making Reduces manual reconciliation efforts ,Provides a scalable foundation for future digital infrastructure
Impact: Instead of replacing legacy systems, VyaparaSetu connects them intelligently, acting as a bridge toward a future where SWS can become the single source of truth.

Nyay AI
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:11 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
From unreadable court PDFs to accountable government action—NYAY-AI closes the compliance gap.﻿NYAY-AI (National Judgment & Action Yield Intelligence) is an AI-powered governance system designed to bridge the critical gap between court judgments and real-world government action. Across India, court orders are delivered as lengthy, unstructured PDFs, requiring officials to manually interpret legal directives, identify responsibilities, and ensure compliance—often leading to delays, misinterpretation, and missed deadlines.NYAY-AI transforms this process through a structured 6-stage pipeline that converts complex legal documents into clear, actionable, and trackable government workflows. The system automatically extracts key directives, identifies responsible departments, generates execution plans, and enforces multi-layer human verification to ensure legal accuracy and accountability.Unlike traditional document processing tools, NYAY-AI goes beyond extraction by introducing a closed-loop execution framework. Every action is assigned to a named officer, tracked against deadlines, and verified through evidence-based compliance, supported by escalation mechanisms and real-time dashboards for decision-makers.Built on the principle that “AI assists, humans decide,” NYAY-AI does not replace legal judgment but enhances it—making non-compliance visible, measurable, and accountable. By integrating AI intelligence with administrative workflows, the system enables governments to move from passive document handling to proactive, transparent, and timely execution of court mandates.

solutions.team_name: Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Tender Evaluation and Accountability Engine
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Coding_Aghoris
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Tender Evaluation and Accountability Engine
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:32 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
 Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Tender Evaluation and Accountability Engine  (https://1-nine-gamma-97.vercel.app/)﻿1. Fulfilling the Vision of AI Tender Evaluation﻿The problem statement correctly identifies the critical need for an AI tender evaluation tool. We view this as the essential foundation. To ensure this tool delivers its maximum impact for the CRPF, we must look beyond standard document parsing to address the real-world pressures evaluation officers face.﻿When a procurement committee evaluates bidders against a complex tender, they are defending the process against two fundamentally different threats. Standard tools only assist with the first:Threat 1: Accidental Non-Compliance. A legitimate small business submits a valid certificate under a slightly different legal name. A rigid automated OCR system eliminates them. This is a failure of the tool.Threat 2: Deliberate Obfuscation. A sophisticated external bad actor buries a fraudulent financial certificate in a degraded scan, actively trying to trick a human evaluator working under deadline pressure. This is an attack on the process.﻿Nyayadarsi is a structured accountability layer that solves both threats, restores the integrity of the L1 (Lowest Bidder) mandate, and provides ironclad legal protection for honest evaluation officers against external challenges.2. The Core Solution (Our Hackathon Deliverable)To strictly address the Theme 3 mandate, we have engineered an evaluation pipeline that sequences decision-making to structurally prevent manipulation.﻿Restoring the Integrity of L1 SequencingThe lowest price should only win if the eligibility is genuinely verified. In Nyayadarsi, financial bids remain strictly masked and inaccessible until the bidder has passed the AI-driven eligibility phase.Phase 1 — Document Eligibility (AI): Incoming bids are processed through our ingestion engine. Bidders who clearly fail mandatory criteria are auto-excluded with a full citation trail.Phase 2 — The YELLOW Queue (Human + AI): The AI does not make silent legal decisions. It triages ambiguous cases (e.g., blurry scans, name mismatches) into a structured queue. The system presents the exact extracted anomaly and empowers the officer to log a digitally signed pass/fail decision.Phase 3 — Micro-Collusion Check: Before the final award, the system runs a cross-document analysis on the submitted cohort to detect shared metadata or forensic similarities indicating cartel behavior among bidders.3. Technical Architecture: Built for RealityStandard OCR converts documents to plain text and destroys table structures. A financial figure without its row label is meaningless. Nyayadarsi is built specifically for the reality of complex Indian procurement documents.Format Detection: Identifies digital PDFs, scanned images, and structured tables.Layout-Aware Extraction: We utilize LayoutLMv3 to preserve the spatial relationships of financial tables and technical matrices.The Financial Ontology: Nyayadarsi uses a curated, domain-specific mapping of Indian CA certificate formats to correctly identify valid financial equivalents without relying on exact, rigid keyword matches.﻿Evaluation Engine Confidence ScoringVerdicts are reached using an optimized LLM running purely in extractive mode to prevent hallucination:Score BandConfidenceSystem ActionGREEN85–100%Evidence verified. Auto-marks eligible.YELLOWBelow 85%Ambiguous. Triggers mandatory human review with a structured reason statement.RED85–100% (fail)Value clearly below threshold. Auto-marks ineligible with full citation chain.4. The Audit Trail — Legal Protection for OfficersHonest officers who make correct disqualification decisions frequently face legal petitions from disqualified bidders. Without a documented evidence trail, even a correct decision is vulnerable. Every automated action and human override generates an append-only log containing:Timestamp to microsecond precision.Cryptographic hash of the source document to prove it wasn't tampered with by the bidder.Verbatim extracted values cited by page and cell location.Officer Digital Signature ID for any human override.5. Risks, Limitations, and MitigationLLM Hallucination: Mitigated by restricting the LLM strictly to extractive QA. It is forbidden from inferring numbers.Sophisticated Forgery: A visually perfect, mathematically consistent forged document will pass extraction. We mitigate this through cross-document consistency checks (e.g., turnover on a CA certificate must logically match the submitted ITR).Legal Ambiguity: Criteria like "works of similar complexity" are subjective. Nyayadarsi is programmed to flag these as YELLOW rather than guess, keeping legal authority firmly with the human officer.6. Roadmap: Beyond EvaluationWhile our immediate focus for this hackathon is delivering a flawless Phase 1 and 2 evaluation pipeline for the CRPF, the underlying architecture is designed to scale:Phase 2 (Pre-Tender): An "Integrity Alert" system that analyzes drafting criteria to prevent restrictive specifications.Phase 3 (Post-Award Execution): A builder dashboard requiring daily, GPS-verified progress uploads analyzed by computer vision to authorize milestone payments.Nyayadarsi does not replace the procurement officer. It empowers them. It gives the honest officer the documentation to do their job efficiently and the undeniable evidence to defend every decision. This is India's procurement accountability infrastructure.Edit link

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderIQ: AI-Powered Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:43 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderIQ addresses one of the most consequential manual processes in public administration — government tender evaluation. Our platform automates eligibility checking across all bidder document types (digital PDFs, scanned certificates, Word files, photographs) while guaranteeing that every verdict is explainable, auditable, and reversible.Problem We're SolvingA CRPF procurement committee manually cross-checks dozens of bidders against 10–20 eligibility criteria, reading hundreds of pages across scanned certificates, audited financials, and experience letters. This takes days, is inconsistent across evaluators, and is hard to defend when challenged. The deeper issue is defensibility — not just speed.Our Approach1. Tender Ingestion & Criterion ExtractionAutomatically segments the tender PDF into sections (Eligibility, Technical, Financial, Documentation)Extracts each criterion as a structured JSON object: type, threshold, mandatory/optional flag, evidence type expected, source page referenceFive criterion types: Financial, Experience, Technical, Compliance, DocumentationProcurement officer reviews and approves the extracted criteria before any bidder evaluation begins2. Bidder Document ProcessingDigital PDFs → PyMuPDF + pdfplumberScanned documents & photographs → Tesseract OCR with OpenCV preprocessing (deskew, denoise, perspective correction)Word files → python-docxTables → Camelot (lattice + stream modes)All processing runs on-premise — no document content sent to external APIs3. Semantic Evidence RetrievalEach criterion is matched against bidder documents using sentence-transformer embeddings (local)Extracted values are normalized: lakhs/crores/absolute rupees → common unit; word-to-digit conversionCross-document consistency checks flag discrepancies (e.g., turnover figure in CA certificate vs. balance sheet >10% apart)4. Confidence-Scored VerdictsEvery (bidder × criterion) pair gets: extracted value, source document + page, comparison result, confidence scoreAbove 0.85 → Auto verdict0.40–0.85 → Human review queueBelow 0.40 → Evidence not found (visible, never silent)The system structurally cannot silently disqualify a bidder — every FAIL requires a human-readable evidence chain5. Human-in-the-Loop ReviewReviewers see: criterion text, extracted passage highlighted in context, original document image (for scanned docs), confidence breakdownActions: Confirm Pass / Confirm Fail / Request Additional DocumentAll reviewer decisions are timestamped, attributed, and loggedConfirmed extractions feed back into model fine-tuning after 50+ examples6. Consolidated Evaluation ReportSummary table: one row per bidder, one column per criterion, with confidence scores and overall status (Eligible / Not Eligible / Manual Review Pending)Per-bidder evidence page: every criterion links to exact source passageFull audit log exportable as PDF — suitable for formal procurement records and legal challengeArchitectureComponentTechnologyReasonPDF text extractionPyMuPDF + pdfplumberSpeed + table-aware parsingOCRTesseract 5 + OpenCVAccurate, fully on-premiseTable extractionCamelotHandles bordered and borderless tablesSemantic retrievalsentence-transformers (MiniLM)Fast, local, no PII exposureLLM inferenceLlama 3.1 8B (quantized, local)Meets "no hosted LLM on raw data" constraintBackendFastAPIAsync, ideal for document pipelinesFrontend/Review UIReact + TypeScriptEvidence-highlighted review interfaceDatabasePostgreSQLRelational model for criterion × bidder matrix + audit logStorageMinIO (self-hosted S3)No data leaves deployment environmentKey Edge Cases HandledPartially legible scanned figures → routed to reviewer with original image shownMissing documents → flagged at ingestion before evaluation beginsForged/inconsistent documents → cross-document consistency checksAmbiguous criterion language → officer clarifies during criterion review stepRegional language documents → multilingual OCR with language detection

solutions.team_name: Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Procurement Justice Platform - From Tender Creation to Project Completion
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Coding_Aghoris
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Nyayadarsi: AI-Powered Procurement Justice Platform - From Tender Creation to Project Completion
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:44 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Nyayadarsi - India's Procurement Accountability Infrastructure Team: Coding_Aghoris Theme 3 - Live Demo: nyaya-darshi.vercel.app﻿The Problem Nobody Has SolvedEvery year, India loses lakhs of crores in government procurement corruption. CAG documents it. Contractors die over it. Courts take decades to resolve it.The reason is structural: corruption is invisible at the exact moment it happens. By the time an auditor arrives, the money is gone. By the time a court decides, the evidence has vanished.Nyayadarsi changes one fundamental thing: it makes corruption visible at the moment it is attempted before any money moves.﻿Where Corruption Actually EntersMost people assume procurement corruption happens at evaluation someone picks the wrong vendor. The reality is far more systematic. It enters at five distinct points:Point 1 - The tender is written to exclude competition. A specification requiring "RCC frame construction with post-tensioned slabs in seismic zone III between 2019–2022" sounds technical. In reality, only one contractor qualifies. The tender was written for them. This is the L1 Trap - documented across Karnataka PWD, BBMP, and CRPF in CAG audits. Nobody checks the tender itself before publication.Point 2 - Fraudulent documents pass evaluation. Ten bidders means hundreds of documents across formats. A human evaluator under deadline pressure misses things. Sophisticated actors submit clean-looking fraudulent certificates knowing they will not be caught.Point 3 - Shell companies simulate competition. Three companies bid. Same registered office. Same CA firm. Bids within 1.8% of each other. This is a cartel but nobody looks at bidders together, only one at a time.Point 4 - Work never gets done. CAG audits across BBMP documented bills for 80% of contracted value cleared against 20% of actual completed work. Ghost work. Cleared payments.Point 5 - Payment becomes extortion. The Karnataka State Contractors Association documented ₹37,370 crore in pending bills across four fiscal years. Two contractors died by suicide. Bills sat pending not because work was incomplete but because clearing them required paying a commission at every stage.Nyayadarsi closes all five windows. Simultaneously. At the point of decision.﻿Three Dashboards. One AI Backbone.Dashboard 1 - Government Officer Creates a TenderAs the officer types eligibility criteria, Nyayadarsi compares each criterion in real time against 10,000+ similar tenders in the same procurement category. If a criterion is so narrow that fewer than three vendors in the market could qualify, the system raises a Nyayadarsi Integrity Alert before the tender is published.The officer must revise the criterion or override with a documented justification, which becomes part of the permanent cryptographically signed audit record. The tender cannot be published with an unreviewed alert.This is the only platform that examines the tender document itself before bidding opens. It addresses the root cause, not the symptom.﻿Dashboard 2 - Evaluation Officer Reviews BidsEvery document is processed regardless of format - typed PDFs, scanned certificates, Word files, photographs of physical documents.The ingestion engine works in four layers:Format detection routes each document to the correct processing pathPre-processing corrects skewed scans, removes noise, upscales to 300 DPI minimumLayoutLMv3 extracts values while preserving spatial relationships that give them meaning - a number without its column header is meaninglessFinancial ontology maps bidder language to tender criteria: "Net Revenue from Operations" satisfies "Minimum Annual Turnover" - but only if the system understands they are equivalent, which requires domain knowledge that generic OCR tools do not haveEvery criterion receives one of three verdicts:GREEN - evidence clearly meets the threshold. Officer reviews and signs off.
RED - document missing or value falls short. Bidder excluded with full citation trail.
YELLOW - system is uncertain. Name mismatch, blurry scan, financial inconsistency. Goes to human review queue, ranked by consequence mandatory blockers first. Every YELLOW flag explains exactly what the ambiguity is and what the officer's options are.The inviolable rule: no bidder is ever silently disqualified.Phased Qualification - Breaking the L1 TrapPhase 1: Mandatory document eligibility check - failing bidders excluded with citations. Phase 2: Technical scoring - experience, equipment, credentials. Top five advance. Phase 3: Sealed financial bids open simultaneously - but only for Phase 2 qualifiers. Phase 4: Pre-award verification and award with complete audit trail.A vendor with a fraudulent certificate who would have won on lowest price is eliminated in Phase 1 before their price is ever visible.Collusion Risk Engine - What No Other Platform Is BuildingAfter individual evaluations, Nyayadarsi runs cross-bidder analysis invisible at the per-bidder level:Shared Address Flag - multiple bidders at the same registered office, the signature of shell company networks simulating competitionCA Fingerprint Flag - independent bidders' financial documents share forensic characteristics: identical fonts, layouts, rounding patterns suggesting coordinated preparationBid Clustering Flag - statistical spread computed across all submitted bids. When bids cluster within 2% of each other just above the designated winner, Nyayadarsi calculates the probability of that occurring by chance and shows it to the officerOwnership Network Flag - cross-references MCA director data to identify direct registered links between the winning firm and the authorising officerDocument Quality Asymmetry Flag - detects when one specific document, typically the one most likely to be forged, has significantly worse image quality than everything else in the submissionNone of these flags disqualify anyone automatically. All five surface to a senior officer with full evidence for investigation.﻿Dashboard 3 - Builder Submits Daily ProgressThe contractor uploads three geotagged photographs and a 60-second video every working day from the mobile app. Nyayadarsi verifies GPS coordinates match the registered project site, confirms timestamps were not manipulated, uses YOLOv8 computer vision to estimate construction progress, and compares the daily delta against the expected rate for the current milestone.Uploads from the wrong location are rejected at submission. Milestone billing cannot be raised until AI verification and officer confirmation are complete.The 80%/20% billing fraud becomes structurally impossible. You cannot bill for work that has not been photographed, GPS-confirmed, and AI-analysed.Payment logic: Milestone completed + AI verified + Officer confirmed = automatic payment release within 72 hours. No officer has timing discretion. No commission extraction point exists in the chain.﻿The Audit Trail - Legal Protection for Honest OfficersEvery action generates an append-only log: microsecond timestamp, SHA-256 hash of source document AND evaluation output, AI model version, verbatim extracted value with page and cell citation, confidence score, and officer digital signature for every human decision.Genuinely append-only at two levels: the application database user has INSERT privileges only, and the underlying volume is WORM - deletion is physically prevented at the infrastructure level.When a disqualified bidder challenges a decision in the Central Administrative Tribunal or High Court, CRPF exports a cryptographically signed, court-admissible record of every step. Every hash verifies. The honest officer is legally untouchable.﻿Technology StackComponentTechnologyDocument layout understandingLayoutLMv3OCRTesseract + Google Document AIAI reasoningLlama 3 - on-premise only, fully data-sovereignConstruction progressYOLOv8Collusion analysisscipy + NetworkXBackendFastAPI + Celery + RedisOfficer dashboardReact + Next.jsBuilder mobile appReact NativeAudit infrastructureSHA-256, WORM volume, PostgreSQL INSERT-onlyDeploymentDocker + Kubernetes, on-premise or MeitY GovCloudGPT-4 and all cloud LLM APIs are excluded entirely. Data sovereignty for CRPF procurement is non-negotiable.﻿Honest LimitationsLLM hallucination is real Nyayadarsi operates in extractive mode only, quoting verbatim and citing sources, never generating values. The MCA director check catches direct links only - benami structures are invisible to public data and we do not overstate this. GeM integration requires NIC coordination, not a simple API call - Phase 1 testing uses publicly available CPPP tenders as proxies. Change management is the hardest problem - immutable audit trails remove deniability, and mandatory deployment requires a directive from DG CRPF or CVC level. We acknowledge this directly.﻿Scale and Long-Term ImpactThe same platform with department-specific configuration serves Karnataka PWD, BBMP, NHAI, Ministry of Defence, and every Central and State procurement authority.After twelve months across fifty departments, every processed tender feeds a National Tender Memory - identifying which criteria patterns recur in the same regions, which vendors win with disproportionate frequency, which officers' tenders consistently produce single-vendor outcomes reported automatically to CAG and CVC in real time.﻿CAG audits arrive years after the money moves. Nyayadarsi generates the evidence automatically. At the point of decision. Before funds are committed.This is not a document processing tool. This is India's procurement accountability infrastructure.न्यायदर्शी - AI that sees justice.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


I InnoFiesta
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
JURYSIGHT : AI for court judgement intelligence
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 09:56 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
JURISIGHT
AI for Court Judgment Intelligence The system that reads complex legal PDFs, extracts directives, generates structured action plans, and ensures human‑verified compliance for government departments.
Team LegalFlow Mahesh Vaswani, Software Development Engineer @ Barclays Mayuri Kolhe, Software Development Engineer @ Mastercard
THE PROBLEM
Judgments arrive in CCMS as lengthy PDFs.

Critical directives buried in complex text.

Officials must manually interpret entire documents.

Result: delays, inefficiencies, missed compliance deadlines.

WHAT JURISIGHT IS
One line: JURISIGHT extracts case details, generates action plans, verifies with human review, and delivers trusted dashboards for decision‑makers. Four steps make this possible:
Extraction: case details, dates, parties, directives, timelines.

Action Plan: compliance, appeal consideration, responsible departments, deadlines.

Verification: human‑in‑the‑loop approval, edit, reject.

Dashboard: department‑wise view of verified actions and dates.

EXTRACTION ENGINE
Handles scanned and digital PDFs.

Uses NLP tuned for legal language.

Outputs structured fields: case number, order date, directives, parties, timelines.

ACTION PLAN GENERATOR
Structures directives into clear actions.

Compliance required or appeal considered.

Responsible authority identified.

Deadlines explicit or inferred.

Nature of action clearly defined.

VERIFICATION LAYER
Mandatory human review.

Confidence scores visible.

Source highlights shown.

Only approved records move forward.

DASHBOARD
Trusted view only.

Department‑wise actions.

Key dates and deadlines.

Flags pending compliance.

Clean, reliable information for decision‑makers.

SAMPLE SCENARIO
Judgment PDF fetched via CCMS API.

Extraction: Case #123, Order Date: 12 Apr 2026, Directive: Dept must file compliance within 30 days.

Action Plan: Compliance required, Dept X responsible, deadline 12 May 2026.

Reviewer approves.

Dashboard shows verified plan.

TECH STACK
Python + NLP for extraction.

Rule‑based + ML hybrid for action plan generation.

Human‑in‑the‑loop UI for verification.

PostgreSQL + ElasticSearch for storage.

React + D3.js for dashboards.

Kubernetes for scalable deployment.

STAGES OF BUILD
Stage 1: PDF ingestion and baseline extraction.

Stage 2: Action plan generator and confidence scoring.

Stage 3: Human verification module and source highlights.

Stage 4: Dashboard integration and sandbox demo.

RISKS AND MITIGATIONS
Complex legal language → fine‑tuned legal NLP.

False extraction → mandatory human verification.

Scanned PDFs → OCR preprocessing.

User distrust → confidence scores and explainability.

WHY JURISIGHT WINS
Court‑to‑action pipeline, not just extraction.

Human‑in‑the‑loop ensures trust.

Explainable outputs with confidence and source highlights.

Dashboard clarity for departments.

Scalable and modular across cases.

Courts deliver justice. JURISIGHT delivers action. The system that extracts, structures, verifies, and informs. Built to transform Karnataka’s compliance workflow, because justice delayed is justice denied.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


B bhumikachhabra23.set_acbe
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaPath AI: Judgement to action system with citizen protection.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:08 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NyayaPath AI is built for normal people like us—people who don’t understand complicated legal language and often feel confused or stressed in legal situations. When someone faces a legal issue, they usually don’t know what the judgement really means, what to do next, or whether they are being treated fairly. This system makes things simple. It takes complex legal decisions and explains them in easy, everyday language, so anyone can understand. Then, it guides the user with clear, step-by-step actions—what to do next, where to go, and how to handle the situation properly. But what makes NyayaPath AI truly useful is that it looks out for the user. If someone is about to take a wrong step—like reacting emotionally, paying more money than needed, or following incorrect advice—the system warns them and suggests a better, legal way. It also helps users stay alert in situations where they might be getting misled, such as being asked to pay extra fines or facing unnecessary delays. In simple words, NyayaPath AI is like a smart helper that stands by you, helping you understand your situation, make the right decisions, and avoid being confused or taken advantage of. It doesn’t replace lawyers, but it gives people the confidence and basic guidance they need to handle legal situations more safely and correctly.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


C Coding_Aghoris
TEAM SUBMISSION [4]
Nyayadarsi: AI-Driven Tender Evaluation and Governance Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:20 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Every other team will build a faster document parser. Nyayadarsi closes the information asymmetry gap that procurement corruption depends on to survive — across the entire procurement lifecycle, not just the evaluation step. The problem runs three layers deep: on the surface, manual tender evaluation is slow, inconsistent, and legally indefensible; beneath that, procurement committees face two simultaneous threats — accidental non-compliance by legitimate businesses and deliberate document fraud by sophisticated bad actors — and most automation tools address only one; and at the root, in documented cases confirmed by CAG Performance Audits across Karnataka PWD and BBMP, tender criteria are written before bidding opens to exclude legitimate competition entirely, with the Karnataka State Contractors Association documenting Rs 37,370 crore in pending contractor bills and two contractor deaths by suicide that Lokayukta could not prosecute because cash transactions leave no paper trail. Nyayadarsi addresses all three layers through three dashboards on one platform. The Government Officer Dashboard runs a live specificity analysis as criteria are typed — if a criterion matches fewer than three vendors in the relevant market, a Nyayadarsi Integrity Alert fires before publication; the officer must revise or override with documented justification that becomes part of a cryptographically signed permanent audit record; this is the only platform in this competition that scrutinises the tender document itself, because corruption is frequently a criteria-drafting problem, not an evaluation problem. The Evaluation Officer Dashboard breaks the L1 trap — where lowest price wins regardless of eligibility — through one design principle: price is not visible until the bidder has passed eligibility; Phase 1 checks all mandatory criteria with full citation chains (GREEN auto-eligible, YELLOW mandatory human review with structured reason and estimated resolution time, RED auto-excluded with evidence), Phase 2 scores technical capacity with the officer resolving the YELLOW queue, Phase 3 opens sealed bids simultaneously with the Collusion Risk Engine running automatically, and Phase 4 completes pre-award verification — a fraudulent ISO certificate eliminates its bidder in Phase 1 before their price is ever seen. The Builder Dashboard requires daily GPS-verified photographs and a 60-second video walkthrough from the registered project site; off-site uploads are rejected instantly; milestone billing is blocked until AI progress verification and officer confirmation are both complete; and once confirmed, payment releases automatically within 72 hours with no officer timing discretion, structurally eliminating both the 80%/20% ghost-work billing fraud and the commission extraction at every clearance stage that drove the Karnataka contractor crisis. The technical core is built on LayoutLMv3 for layout-aware extraction that preserves table row-column relationships in scanned documents; a Financial Ontology mapping acceptable equivalent field names built from actual Indian CA certificate formats; cross-document consistency analysis that flags discrepancies across CA certificate, ITR, and balance sheet to catch single-document forgeries that extract cleanly; and a Collusion Risk Engine with five flags — Shared Address (shell company networks), CA Fingerprint (forensic similarity above 87% across independent bidders' financials), Bid Clustering (CV below 5% with p-value of natural occurrence calculated), Ownership Network (director of winning firm linked to the authorising officer via MCA and HR data), and Document Quality Asymmetry (one compliance document deliberately degraded to obstruct extraction) — none triggering automatic disqualification, all surfacing to a senior officer with full statistical evidence. Every automated action generates a genuinely append-only WORM log with SHA-256 hashes of source documents and evaluation outputs, verbatim extracted values with page and cell citations, and mandatory officer Digital Signature for every human decision — so when a disqualified bidder challenges in the CAT or High Court, CRPF exports a cryptographically signed, court-admissible record and the honest officer is legally untouchable. The full stack runs on-premise and data-sovereign: LayoutLMv3, Tesseract OCR, Google Document AI for complex tables, Llama 3 8B and 70B on-premise in extractive mode only (never generating values, always citing source), YOLOv8 for construction progress estimation, FastAPI + Celery + Redis for async processing, React + Next.js for the officer dashboard, React Native for the builder mobile app, and Docker + Kubernetes for deployment within CRPF's security perimeter or MeitY GovCloud. The Round 2 roadmap runs Phase 0 (ground truth calibration from real CPPP tenders before a line of code is written) through Phase 4 (full end-to-end demonstration from tender creation to milestone payment) across six weeks. At scale, every processed tender feeds a National Tender Memory layer that identifies rigged-criteria patterns, disproportionate vendor win rates, and officers whose tenders consistently produce single-vendor outcomes — reported automatically to CAG and CVC in real time, not years after the money has moved. CAG audits arrive years after funds are committed. Lokayukta cannot prosecute what it cannot prove. Court challenges drag for decades. Nyayadarsi generates the evidence automatically — at the point of decision, before funds are committed. This is not a document processing tool. This is India's procurement accountability infrastructure. न्यायदर्शी — AI that sees justice
