solutions.team_name: AI Platform for Tender Evaluation with Human-in-the-Loop Verification
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A AuditAI
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI Platform for Tender Evaluation with Human-in-the-Loop Verification
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:50 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement demands absolute fairness, transparency and legal accountability. The current tender evaluation process is manual, high-friction and error-prone. Bidders submit highly unstructured, heterogeneous documents typed PDFs, skewed scans, handwritten forms, tables and stamped certificates often in multiple languages. The challenge is not merely extracting text, but verifiably matching heterogeneous evidence against complex, legally binding criteria without ever silently disqualifying a bidder. The hardest unsolved problems in existing tools are:• LLMs misinterpreting legally ambiguous clauses even when citing correct source spans• Vision AI silently corrupting financial values from stamped or degraded documents• RAG pipelines failing on exact identifiers (e.g., ISO 9001 vs. ISO 27001)• No support for regional-language government certificates (Hindi, regional scripts)• Scalability: 50+ criteria × 100+ bidders = 5,000+ evaluation calls with no batching strategy• Audit systems that log actions but cannot reconstruct full decision provenance We propose an AI-powered, explainable tender evaluation platform that automates document understanding, eligibility verification and decision support while ensuring full transparency, auditability and human control.﻿System Workflow Upload tender document and bidder submissions Extract eligibility criteria using AI Parse bidder documents (PDFs, scans, images) Match extracted data with criteria Generate results: Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review With evidence, confidence score and reasoning Human reviewers validate uncertain cases Final audit-ready report is generated﻿Core ArchitecturePipeline 1: Tender Rules Extraction (Triple-Validated Schema Builder)﻿Converts complex legal tender documents into a structured, machine-executable schema.Maker LLM: Extracts criteria into structured JSON (type, threshold, mandatory/optional, source span) Checker LLM: Verifies exact text span Independently re-derives rule to detect misinterpretation Template Validation: Matches against known government clause patterns Human Sign-off: Ambiguous rules are reviewed and approved before use ﻿﻿Pipeline 2: Evidence Ingestion (Confidence-Scored Multi-Modal Extraction)﻿Processes messy bidder documents reliably.Supports PDFs, scanned images, multilingual documents (Hindi + others) Page-level classification (text / scanned / mixed) Every extracted field has a confidence score (0–1) Low-confidence fields are flagged before evaluation Hybrid storage using: semantic embeddings (context) BM25 (exact identifiers like ISO, GST, PAN) Re-verification anchor: system can re-read exact document region if needed ﻿﻿Pipeline 3: Agentic Evaluation Engine (Parallel + Verified)﻿Matches bidder data against criteria with full traceability.Parallel evaluation of independent criteria (~60% faster) Dependency-aware execution using lightweight DAG Re-verification loop: low-confidence data is re-read from original document Advanced anomaly detection: financial inconsistencies identifier validation (GST, PAN, CIN) unrealistic claims cross-bidder outliers Output per criterion:Status (Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review) Source document + page Extracted value Confidence score Explanation ﻿﻿Pipeline 4: RBAC Dashboard & Immutable Audit System﻿Ensures governance, accountability and compliance.Role-based workflow: L1: basic validation L2: anomaly resolution L3: final authority Escalation control: prevents overload by grouping complex cases Immutable audit log: records AI decisions + human actions stores full reasoning trace enables complete reconstruction of decisions ﻿﻿Technology Decisions and Trade offs﻿Hybrid Search vs. Standard RAGPure semantic (dense) retrieval often fails on exact identifiers (e.g., ISO 9001 vs ISO 27001). Our system combines:Dense embeddings → for contextual understanding BM25 keyword search → for exact matches﻿Agentic Evaluation vs. Bulk ProcessingInstead of evaluating all criteria in a single pass, each criterion is processed independently.Ensures per-criterion traceability Prevents context mixing inside LLM responses Supports parallel execution for speed﻿Confidence Scoring vs. Binary ExtractionEvery extracted value is assigned a confidence score instead of a simple pass/fail.High confidence → automated evaluation Medium/low confidence → flagged for review﻿Enterprise Vision AI vs. Basic OCRStandard OCR struggles with:stamped documents rotated scans handwritten inputs We use advanced document intelligence systems that provide:structured extraction confidence scores﻿Append-Only Audit Log vs. Editable RecordsTraditional databases allow modification of past records, which creates compliance risks.Our system uses:append-only audit logs no deletion or modification of past events﻿Security & Data Privacy﻿For CRPF and government procurement systems, data sovereignty and security are non-negotiable. The platform is designed with strict security guarantees to ensure sensitive data is never exposed or misused.On-Premise Deployment ArchitectureAll system components including:LLM inference vector database document storage are fully containerized (Docker/Kubernetes) and can run:on-premise or within a government-approved private cloudEnd-to-End EncryptionAll data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 All inter-service communication uses mutual TLS (mTLS)Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with MFAAccess control is enforced at the API level, not just the UI Each role has least-privilege permissions Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled No user can both evaluate and approve the same caseBidder-Level Data IsolationEach bidder’s documents are stored in isolated namespaces Vector search is strictly scoped per bidder Cross-bidder data access is architecturally impossible﻿Expected Impact﻿Reduces evaluation time by 70–80%Ensures consistent and unbiased decision-makingEliminates manual document verification overheadProvides instant, audit-ready evaluation reportsImproves transparency and trust in government procurement﻿EndThis platform moves beyond basic document extraction to deliver a defensible, legally auditable, government-grade tender evaluation system built on the principle that AI must never be blindly trusted. It introduces triple-validated rule extraction to prevent misinterpretation of legal clauses, confidence-gated ingestion with re-verification to eliminate silent extraction errors and a parallel, anomaly-aware evaluation engine with full decision provenance logging to ensure scalability and traceability. With multilingual support and controlled escalation workflows, the system is designed for real-world deployment at government scale. Crucially, it preserves human-in-the-loop authority at every stage, positioning AI as a decision-support tool that provides structured, evidence-backed insights, while final decisions remain with authorized procurement officials.

solutions.team_name: JudgmentIQ — A Zero-Trust Compliance Co-Pilot for Court Directives
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hack Elite
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
JudgmentIQ — A Zero-Trust Compliance Co-Pilot for Court Directives
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 10:51 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
60 Seconds. 40 Pages. One Missed Deadline Away From a Contempt Notice.A single High Court judgment can contain a dozen hidden directives — each tied to a different department, a different limitation period, and a different legal consequence if missed. Today, those directives are discovered by officers reading every page by hand. When they miss one, the State loses by default, appeal windows close forever, and citizens pay the price.JudgmentIQ is the compliance co-pilot that makes missing a directive structurally impossible.﻿Why "Yet Another AI PDF Reader" Isn't EnoughAny team can wire up OCR, an LLM, and a dashboard. That's the obvious 20% of the problem. The real problem is trust:Would a senior government officer stake their career on an AI-generated action plan? Today, no. Our job is to change that answer.JudgmentIQ is engineered around a Zero-Trust AI philosophy: every AI output is treated as suspect until a human has seen it, traced it to its source, and signed off. The architecture enforces this — it is not a policy that can be skipped.﻿The Four Signature Features1. Citation Lens — No Claim Without a SourceEvery extracted field is bound to its exact origin: page, paragraph, and sentence. One click jumps the officer to the highlighted passage in the original PDF. If the AI cannot cite it, it cannot claim it.2. Confidence Ledger — Calibrated Uncertainty, Not VibesMost AI products show a meaningless "87%" next to every output. We calibrate confidence against a held-out set of real Karnataka judgments — so a 70% score actually means 7-in-10 historical accuracy. Low-confidence fields are auto-routed to a Review Queue; high-confidence ones are fast-tracked.3. Adversarial Verifier — AI That Challenges ItselfA second, independently-prompted agent re-reads the judgment and attempts to disagree with the first extraction. When the two agents diverge, the conflict is surfaced to the human reviewer as a structured disagreement — "Extractor A says deadline = Dec 15; Verifier B says Jan 30; source page 23 is ambiguous." Humans decide. This is how we catch the edge cases a single model would miss.4. Compliance Memory — Learning From Every CorrectionEvery Edit the reviewing officer makes becomes training signal. Over time, JudgmentIQ learns the Karnataka legal dialect — how this court phrases compliance orders, how this department handles appeals, which judges use "at liberty to approach" to mean what. The system gets smarter with every case.﻿The Four-Step PipelineREAD — OCR + layout-aware parsing for scanned, digital, and mixed-quality PDFsEXTRACT — Case details, parties, orders, responsible authority, timelines (explicit + inferred)PLAN — Structured action plan with compliance steps, appeal windows, and calculated deadlinesVERIFY — Mandatory Approve / Edit / Reject gate; unverified records are structurally blocked from the dashboard﻿The Metric That Matters — Court-to-Citizen Time (CCT)Everyone else will brag about extraction accuracy. We care about one number:CCT = days from judgment disposal to verified action plan published to the responsible department.Today, CCT is often weeks. Our target on demo day: under 10 minutes, end-to-end, with a human in the loop. This is the number that decides whether a citizen gets the service they were promised.﻿What the Judge Will See on Demo DayUpload a real Karnataka High Court judgment (never seen before by our system)30 seconds later: structured action plan on screen, every field citation-linked to the PDFDisagreement Panel: one field flagged where the Adversarial Verifier challenged the ExtractorOfficer clicks Edit on an ambiguous deadline — Compliance Memory updates in real timeOfficer clicks Approve — record publishes to the department-filtered, deadline-sorted dashboardStopwatch stops at under 10 minutes. CCT proven.We are not pitching a prototype. We are pitching the operating system for court-ordered government action.

D deepali.24095_db93
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI-Powered Judgment-to-Action Intelligence System (JAIS)
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 11:46 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The AI-Powered Judgment-to-Action Intelligence System (JAIS) is designed to bridge the gap between complex legal judgments and actionable governance decisions. Government officials often struggle with lengthy and unstructured legal documents, leading to delays, inefficiencies, and missed directives.
JAIS addresses this problem by converting unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, explainable, and verifiable action plans. The system uses OCR to extract text from scanned or digital documents, followed by Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to identify key information such as directives, deadlines, responsible departments, and priorities.
The system workflow includes PDF input, text extraction, NLP-based processing, action plan generation, explainability mapping, human verification, and dashboard visualization. Each extracted action is linked to its original source sentence with confidence scores, ensuring transparency and trust. A human-in-the-loop mechanism allows officials to approve, edit, or reject suggested actions.
The generated action plans include key fields such as Action Required, Deadline, Department, and Priority, enabling efficient tracking and execution. A user-friendly dashboard provides department-wise insights, urgent task alerts, and filtering capabilities to support informed decision-making.
The solution is built using a modern technology stack, including React for the frontend, Python FastAPI for the backend, spaCy for NLP processing, and Tesseract for OCR. Data is managed using SQLite or Firebase.
JAIS has the potential to significantly improve administrative efficiency, reduce manual effort, and ensure timely execution of legal directives. Future enhancements include automated alerts, integration with court monitoring systems, and continuous learning from user feedback.

solutions.team_name: LexTrace - AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan System
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


G Good Team
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
LexTrace - AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan System
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:06 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem: The existing Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) successfully fetches judgment PDFs, but forces government officials to manually read and interpret lengthy, unstructured legal documents to locate critical directives. Because vital mandates are buried deep within complex legal text rather than explicitly highlighted, this purely manual review process causes severe delays, systemic operational inefficiencies, and a high risk of missing time-sensitive legal obligations.
There is a need for a highly reliable, sovereign AI system that can instantly parse these lengthy legal PDFs into clear, departmental action plans while completely eliminating data hallucinations through deterministic extraction and strict human-in-the-loop verification.

Solution: An end-to-end, sovereign AI data pipeline that ingests raw legal PDFs and strictly enforces structured, actionable JSON outputs. The system isolates extraction into a deterministic metadata track (Regex) and a semantic reasoning track (Parent-Document Retrieval RAG). Data is routed through a mandatory Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) interface for arbitration. Verified records instantly populate a role-based Executive Dashboard while simultaneously writing to an immutable audit ledger, ensuring 100% accountability.
Technical Architecture:
Phase 1: Ingestion & Text Sanitization
Dynamic Parsing: The backend accepts PDF uploads. PyMuPDF attempts native extraction. If a scanned document is detected, the system automatically routes to PyTesseract for robust OCR processing.

Noise Reduction Layer: A Python script strips OCR artifacts, normalizes whitespace, and standardizes baseline date formats, outputting a clean, continuous text string to prevent downstream pipeline failure.



Phase 2: The Dual-Track Extraction Engine
Track A: Deterministic Metadata Arbitration Objective: Extract hard facts: Case Number, Base Judgment Date, and Petitioner/Respondent. Process: A Python Regex engine scans the sanitized text. Simultaneously, a BM25 lexical search isolates the document's header chunks and passes them to the LLM for parallel extraction. Confidence Scoring: A Python arbitration layer compares outputs. A good match assigns a high confidence score. Mismatches flag the data for mandatory human review.

Track B: Semantic Action Plan Generation Objective: Extract Directives, Timelines, and Responsible Departments Process: The text is chunked and converted into embeddings using Sentence Transformers. These embeddings are stored in MongoDB Atlas using MongoDB Atlas Vector Search (Hybrid Search: Vector + BM25). Parent-Document Retrieval: The system executes a Hybrid Search (Reciprocal Rank Fusion of Vector + BM25) to find core directives (e.g., "ordered," "directed"). It retrieves the exact matching chunk but passes the entire surrounding parent paragraph to the LLM to preserve conditional legal logic.



Phase 3: State Management and Dual-Write Output
The Single-Collection Queue: All newly parsed Action Plans are inserted into a primary MongoDB Atlas collection with a status: pending_review flag.

HITL Verification Dashboard: The frontend fetches and displays only "pending" documents, rendering the AI-extracted fields alongside the source PDF. Low-confidence Track A metadata or ambiguous Track B timelines are highlighted in red.

The Dual-Write Commit: When a human reviewer corrects and approves the data, the backend executes two operations simultaneously: Updates the primary collection with the corrected text and flips the flag to status: "verified". Silently appends a record to an immutable audit_logs collection detailing exactly who changed what, when, and what the original AI output was.



Phase 4: Executive Governance Dashboard
Role-Based View: A clean, priority-driven frontend that strictly fetches documents where status: "verified".

Actionable Tracking: Department heads view actionable mandates ordered by SLA deadlines. The dashboard provides clear Kanban-style status updates (Pending, In Progress, Complied, Appealed) without exposing the underlying AI mechanics.



Competitive Advantages:
Data Sovereignty: Native integration with Sarvam AI ensures all legal reasoning is processed through an Indian-context model, aligning perfectly with government data localization mandates.

Zero-Hallucination Architecture: Offloading deterministic metadata extraction to Track A (Regex arbitration) physically prevents the LLM from fabricating case numbers and critical baseline dates.

Enterprise-Grade Auditability: The dual-write database architecture provides a mathematically provable history of human intervention, an absolute requirement for legal technology deployment.

Context Preservation: Parent-Document Retrieval ensures complex, multi-paragraph court orders are never truncated during the RAG process, yielding complete, actionable directives.



Categories
Technologies
Use Case
Frontend
React.js / Next.js, PDF.js
Interactive UI with dual-pane PDF viewing and AI-extracted data validation.
Backend
FastAPI (Python)
High-speed API orchestration for ingestion, processing, and HITL workflows.
Database & Storage
MongoDB Atlas
Central document store for parsed outputs, status tracking, and audit logs
Search & RAG
MongoDB Atlas Vector Search (Hybrid: Vector + BM25)
Semantic + lexical retrieval enabling Parent-Document RAG for directive extraction
AI Engine
Sarvam AI
Legal reasoning and strict JSON-based structured output generation
Data Processing
PyMuPDF, PyTesseract, Python (re, datetime), Sentence Transformers
PDF parsing, OCR, metadata extraction, and embedding generation
Deployment

Containerized, scalable cloud deployment and infrastructure management

How the System Works

Step 2: Read Documents

solutions.solution_text:

O omkumar_mc22a14_49_92eb
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CRPF SmartTender AI: Explainable Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:22 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project Details:
The aim of this project is to build an artificial intelligence-based system to streamline and enhance the process of tender evaluation for government contracts. For CRPF, the process of evaluating tenders consists of examining hundreds of documents provided by various bidders, such as financial reports, certifications, and experience details. These can be in various formats like PDF, scanned documents, or Word, leading to a lengthy and inconsistent evaluation process.

Our new solution employs artificial intelligence to streamline certain aspects of the evaluation. It starts by processing the tender document to extract all the eligibility criteria, including financial, technical, and compliance-related criteria. These are then organized for evaluation.

Then the system reads the bidder's response in any format and extracts information such as turnover, GST registration, qualifications, experience, etc. The system uses sophisticated document processing and OCR to deal with scanned documents and images.

The bidder information is compared to the tender criteria. Using this match, the system can then produce a definitive result for each bidder—eligible, not eligible, or needs manual review. When the extracted data is ambiguous, it is identified as a case to be reviewed by humans rather than automatically discarded.

Another key feature of this project is transparency. The system provides justification for its decision-making, including which criterion was being checked, which document was used to extract data, and which data was used to assess the criterion. This guarantees the trustworthiness of the system.

In summary, this project seeks to minimize manual work, increase consistency, and offer a quicker, more accurate, and auditable solution for the evaluation of government tenders.


Solutions:
We have built an AI-based tender assessment system that assists government officials to assess and check bidder eligibility comprehensively.

The system has three steps. First, it processes the tender document and automatically converts all eligibility conditions such as financial, technical, and compliance regulation requirements. All of these are then presented in a format for assessment.

Second, it reads the bidder document, which can be in PDF, scanned, image, or Word file format. Using OCR and document processing, it can extract the turnover, certificates, tax ID, and previous projects, even from unstructured and bad-quality documents.

Third, it compares the bidder information with the tender criteria with a rule-based and AI-based matching engine. This enables it to check the bidder's eligibility status for each criterion: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Manual Review. If the information is unclear or insufficient, the system does not automatically disqualify the bidder from the bid but flags the bidder for review.

The other key requirement of the solution is transparency. The solution provides a justification for every decision (such as the assessment criterion, document, value, and why). This ensures transparency and builds trust.

Also, the system offers an audit of the data, decisions, and actions that were taken to make it suitable for government procurement.

Overall, the system eliminates manual processes, facilitates consistency in evaluation, and enables reliable, fast and equitable evaluation of tenders.


Idea:
Our idea is to develop an AI-powered assistant that helps government organizations like CRPF in evaluating tender bids more efficiently and accurately.

In the current system, officers manually review large tender documents and bidder submissions to check whether each bidder meets the required eligibility criteria. This process is time-consuming, repetitive, and sometimes inconsistent due to human error and the complexity of documents.

Our idea is to simplify this process by using artificial intelligence to automate the understanding and comparison of documents. The system will first read the tender document and identify all the eligibility conditions, such as financial requirements, technical qualifications, and necessary certifications.

Next, it will analyze bidder submissions, which may be in different formats like PDFs, scanned images, or Word files, and extract the relevant information needed for evaluation. The system will then compare this extracted data with the tender criteria.

Based on this comparison, the system will provide clear results for each bidder, whether they are eligible, not eligible, or require manual review. It will also give proper explanations for each decision, showing which condition was checked and what evidence was found.

The main idea is not to replace human officers but to support them by reducing their workload, improving accuracy, and ensuring transparency in the evaluation process.

Overall, our idea focuses on making tender evaluation faster, more consistent, and more reliable using AI.

Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:28 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Nividia AI: Complete Solution Description
Nividia AI is a specialized web-based application designed to modernize the Technical Evaluation process of the tenders. Built as a sovereign, air-gapped platform, it automates the extraction and verification of complex eligibility criteria from tender enquiries and bidder submissions. The solution ensures that procurement decisions are fast, consistent, and legally robust by providing a clear, explainable audit trail for every verdict.


1. Problem Understanding
Government procurement involves a rigorous two-bid system where the Technical Evaluation Committee (TEC) must verify hundreds of mandatory requirements—ranging from financial turnovers to specialized technical certifications—spread across 50–200 pages of legal prose.Bidders submit highly heterogeneous documents, including scanned certificates, typed PDFs, and Word files. Evaluating these manually is slow, inconsistent, and prone to silent errors that can lead to high-stakes legal disputes or procurement delays. Nividia AI addresses this by digitizing the evaluation workflow, ensuring that every rule is applied uniformly and every decision is backed by evidence.

""
2. Solution Architecture
Nividia AI is designed as a modular web application that processes documents through a series of specialized intelligence stages.


Module
Function
Intelligent Ingestion
Accepts multiple formats (PDF, DOCX, JPG) and performs SHA-256 hashing to ensure document integrity.
Extraction Engine
Uses Layout-Aware AI to read and understand the structure of both digital and scanned documents.
Criterion Miner
Parses the Tender Enquiry to generate a machine-readable list of eligibility rules (Mandatory vs. Optional).
Semantic Matcher
Correlates bidder evidence with tender requirements using advanced natural language understanding.
Evaluation Dashboard
Computes confidence scores for each match and flags ambiguous cases for human review.
Audit Reporter
Generates a signed, criterion-level report with direct citations to the source pages.

3. Technical Stack (Software)
The platform is built using a modern, containerized stack optimized for deployment within the secure government intranet.


Primary Intelligence Engine:Gemma 3 27B (Open-Weights), selected for its high-performance reasoning on legal and financial data while remaining fully air-gap compatible.

Web Framework:FastAPI (Backend) and React + TailwindCSS (Frontend) for a responsive, high-performance user experience.

OCR & Document Parsing:PaddleOCR and pdfminer, allowing the system to handle both typed and scanned bidder submissions.

Knowledge Retrieval:ChromaDB for vector-based semantic search, enabling the AI to find evidence without needing exact keyword matches.

Database & Audit:PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security to maintain a tamper-evident log of all evaluator actions.


4. Key Features and Offerings

Zero Silent Rejection Policy
In alignment with CRPFs non-negotiables, the system never auto-rejects a bidder.If a document is difficult to read or a clause is ambiguous, Nividia AI generates a Needs Review flag with a plain-language explanation of the uncertainty, ensuring final authority remains with the human officer.
'""
Semantic Criterion Normalization
The AI understands the meaning behind requirements.It can automatically normalize financial units (e.g., converting Lakhs to Crores) and understand that different terminology (e.g., similar work vs. analogous experience) represents the same technical requirement.
""""
Granular Explainability
Every Pass or Fail verdict is accompanied by:

""""
The exact criterion being tested.

The extracted value from the bidder's submission.

The document name and page number where the evidence was found.

A side-by-side view of the original document for instant verification.

5. Uniqueness and USP

Feature
NividiaAI Value
Tamper-Evident Logs
Uses hash-chaining (similar to blockchain logic) to make audit logs immutable and suitable for RTI responses.
Multilingual Native Support
Native parsing of Hindi and English documents, essential for regional procurement within India.
Sovereign Deployment
no data ever leaves the CRPF network, ensuring total data privacy and security.
Human-Centric Design
Designed to augment the TEC officers, reducing their manual workload while increasing the accuracy of their final report.

6. Functional Requirements & Security
As a web application, Nividia AI is designed to integrate into existing government IT ecosystems.


Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Strict separation of duties between the Uploader, the Evaluator (TEC Officer), and the Final Approver.

Audit Integrity: Every interaction—from document upload to manual override—is time-stamped and recorded in a read-only filesystem.

Infrastructure Agnostic: The platform is containerized (Docker), allowing it to be hosted on any secure server infrastructure provided by the CRPF without requiring proprietary hardware.

7. Feasibility and Impact
Nividia AI is a high-feasibility solution that leverages the latest breakthroughs in open-weights AI to solve a critical administrative bottleneck.

Operational Impact:

Reduced Cycle Time: Technical evaluation that previously took days can be completed in hours.

Legal Resilience: By providing a consistent, evidence-based rationale for every decision, the department is protected from claims of arbitrary or biased disqualification.

Accuracy: The system identifies discrepancies (e.g., mismatched business names across PAN and GST) that are often missed during manual review.

8. Conclusion
Nividia AI provides a definitive path to achieve Maximum Governance through digital transformation. By combining the reasoning capabilities of Gemma 3 with a secure, web-based delivery model, it ensures that government procurement remains transparent, efficient, and fully under the control of human officers.

""

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TendorGaurd AI – High-Fidelity Automated Eligibility & Audit System
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:44 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
"Idea Pitch"
Every day, government procurement officers in India manually sift through thousands of pages of tender documents—a process ripe for error, bias, and legal challenge. TenderGuard AI replaces this bottleneck with a five-agent AI system that reads, verifies, cross-checks, and explains every decision it makes.
Unlike a simple "AI chatbot for documents," our system uses two independent LLMs to cross-verify each verdict, links every decision to a precise location in the source document, and automatically escalates uncertain cases to a human reviewer. It's not just faster—it's legally defensible and auditable.
Idea-Document

Project Title: TendorGaurd AI – High-Fidelity Automated Eligibility & Audit System
Team: Zeta (Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation)
The Problem Manual evaluation of dense government tenders (e.g., CRPF) is a massive bottleneck. The process is slowed down by complex criteria, heterogeneous document formats (blurry scans, photos), and subjective rejections that create audit risks and legal delays.
The Solution TendorGaurd AI is an "Explainable Extraction" Engine powered by a Collaborative Multi-Agent AI System that automates document evaluation while maintaining a strict audit trail.
·Architect & Vision Agents: Automatically parse complex tender rulesets and extract data from messy, multilingual (Hindi/English) bidder documents.
·Fact-Checker & Auditor Agents: Utilize Hybrid RAG to locate exact evidence, followed by a Dual-LLM Cross-Check (GPT-4o + Claude 3.5) to prevent AI hallucinations and assign a Confidence Score to the verdict.
·Human-in-the-Loop (HITL): Automatically routes low-confidence files to a review queue, ensuring AI assists procurement officers rather than replacing them.
Unique Selling Points (Why We Win)
·Source Citation Mapper: This is our most unique feature. Every decision links back to a specific page and bounding box (highlighted area) in the bidder's document.
·Confidence Heatmap: Every piece of evidence has a 0–100% confidence score, ensure the human officer spends 90% of their time on the 10% of documents that actually need attention
·Agent Message Bus: Our agents communicate via typed AgentMessage objects, making the entire pipeline debuggable and extendable for future government needs.
·MultiLingual Support: Our agent will support to scan & parse the documents in both English & Hindi formats
·Interactive Evidence Viewer: A Streamlit dashboard where officers view extracted text side-by-side with source snippets, allowing them to confidently approve/reject with mandatory audit comments.
Tech Stack FastAPI, Celery, Docling, LlamaIndex, Hybrid RAG,Redis, Qdrant (Vector DB), PostgreSQL, and Streamlit.

A akhilpandey.r15_07
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaFlow is an AI-powered legal intelligence system that transforms complex court judgment PDFs into clear, structured and human-verified action plans for government departments.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:58 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NyayaFlow: From Court Judgments to Verified Government Action Project Description: "Justice delayed is justice denied.”
Government departments receive thousands of court judgments every year, yet translating these legal documents into timely administrative action remains a major bottleneck. Judgments are long, complex and unstructured with critical directives often buried deep within dense legal language. This leads to delays, missed deadlines and increased legal risk.
NyayaFlow is an AI-powered legal intelligence system designed to bridge this gap. It converts court judgment PDFs into clear, structured and human-verified action plans, enabling faster, more accurate and accountable decision-making, way faster than the present rate.

The Problem Current workflows suffer from:
Lengthy, unstructured judgment documents that are difficult to interpret

Critical directives in dense legal language

Manual reading and interpretation causing delays

Risk of missing compliance deadlines or appeal windows

The result is inefficient governance and exposure to legal consequences.

The Solution

NyayaFlow transforms raw legal text into decision-ready intelligence through a four-stage pipeline:

1. Intelligent Judgment Understanding: The system ingests both scanned and digital PDFs. Using OCR and natural language processing, it extracts:
Case details (court, case number, parties)

Date of order

Key directives and orders

Relevant timelines and deadlines


2. AI-Generated Action Plan: NyayaFlow goes beyond extraction and generates a structured administrative response:
Required action: compliance or appeal

Responsible department

Key deadlines (explicit or inferred)

Priority and risk level

Supporting reasoning with source references

This transforms passive legal documents into actionable insights.

3. Human-in-the-Loop Verification: Recognizing the importance of trust and accountability, NyayaFlow incorporates mandatory human validation:
Extracted data is shown with highlighted source text from the PDF

Confidence scores are provided

Reviewers can approve, edit or reject outputs

Only verified records are stored and used for decision-making.

4. Decision Dashboard: A clean and reliable dashboard presents only approved information:
Department-wise action items

Upcoming deadlines and alerts

High-priority cases

Verified, structured data for decision-makers


Key Differentiators * Action plan generation not just information extraction * Explainable AI with source traceability * Mandatory human verification for reliability * End-to-end pipeline from PDF to decision * Designed specifically for government workflows
Technology Stack:
AI/NLP: Large Language Models (OpenAI / Mistral), LangChain

PDF Processing: PyMuPDF, PaddleOCR

Backend: FastAPI

Database: PostgreSQL with vector search (Qdrant)

Frontend: React or Next.js


Impact: NyayaFlow enables:
Significant reduction in manual effort and review time

Faster and more consistent decision-making

Improved compliance with court directives

Reduced risk of missed appeals or legal penalties

Scalable adoption across departments and jurisdictions

Vision: NyayaFlow is not just a document processing tool. It is a legal intelligence layer for governance.
By ensuring that every judgment is understood, verified and acted upon in time, it strengthens accountability and transforms how governments respond to the judiciary.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


R ramharsh-aidev
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Dhiyanta: A Multi-Agent AI Platform for Secure, Transparent, and Auditable Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:58 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Dhiyanta is an advanced, AI-driven platform meticulously engineered to solve the critical challenges of tender evaluation and eligibility analysis for the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF). Moving beyond simple automation, Dhiyanta introduces a secure, transparent, and auditable ecosystem that transforms the procurement lifecycle from a slow, manual, and error-prone process into a rapid, consistent, and data-driven operation. Our solution is designed to handle the complex realities of government procurement, including diverse document formats, ambiguous language, and the non-negotiable requirement for explainability and auditability.
At its core, Dhiyanta is built upon a resilient and scalable microservices architecture, ensuring a clear separation of concerns and the ability to independently manage and upgrade critical system components. This architecture supports a sophisticated multi-agent AI system, where specialized, best-in-class AI models collaborate to deconstruct, analyze, and evaluate tender submissions with unparalleled accuracy and depth of understanding.


System Architecture Overview

Dhiyanta’s architecture is structured in logical layers, ensuring a robust separation of concerns, from user interaction down to the data storage and AI models.
Architectural Layer
Component
Core Technologies / Models
Key Responsibilities
User Plane
Procurement Officer Portal
Secure Web Framework (e.g., React, Angular)
Provides a secure, intuitive interface for uploading documents, reviewing AI-generated evaluations, managing the HITL queue, and exporting final reports.
Application & Logic Plane
API Gateway
Managed Gateway Service
Manages, secures, and throttles all incoming API requests. Acts as the single entry point for the user interface.
Core Microservices
Python (FastAPI), Node.js (Express)
- Tender Processing: Manages tender document lifecycle.
Bidder Submission: Handles ingestion and pre-processing of bid documents.

Evaluation & Compliance: Orchestrates the core evaluation logic.

Auditing & Reporting: Generates reports and logs audit data.
AI & Data Processing Plane
Orchestrator Agent
Low-Latency Generative AI
Manages the workflow between different specialized AI agents, ensuring a seamless flow from criteria extraction to final explanation.
IDP Pipeline
Enterprise IDP Suite (Neural OCR & Layout Engine)
- Pre-processes images (denoising, de-skewing).

- Extracts text from scanned documents and photos.
Parses document structure (tables, forms, paragraphs).
Data & Model Plane
Specialized AI Agents
High-Reasoning & Instruction-Tuned LMMs
- Criteria Extraction: Parses tender documents to identify all eligibility criteria.

- Data Extraction: Scans bidder documents for specific data points corresponding to criteria.
- Explanation Synthesis: Generates human-readable justifications for every verdict.
Data Stores
PostgreSQL: Relational Data
Vector Database: Semantic Data

Immutable Ledger: Audit Trail
- PostgreSQL: Stores structured data like user info, tender metadata, and extracted bidder information.

Vector DB: Enables semantic search and matching for nuanced criteria.

Immutable Ledger (QLDB): Creates a tamper-proof, chronological log of every system action and decision.


End-to-End Evaluation Workflow

Tender Ingestion & Criteria Extraction:

A procurement officer securely uploads the tender document to the Dhiyanta portal.

The Criteria Extraction Agent performs a deep analysis, identifying all technical, financial, and compliance requirements. It distinguishes mandatory from optional criteria and converts them into a structured, machine-readable format.

Bidder Submission & Intelligent Document Processing (IDP):

Bidder submissions, in their varied formats (PDFs, Word files, scanned JPEGs), are uploaded.

Each document is passed through the IDP Pipeline. Advanced OCR and layout analysis convert even poor-quality scans into structured text.

The Data Extraction Agent then scans this structured text to find and extract specific pieces of information (e.g., turnover values, certification dates, project histories) that correspond to the tender criteria.

Automated Evaluation & Explainable Verdicts:

The Evaluation & Compliance Engine systematically compares the extracted bidder data against each criterion.

For every criterion, it generates one of three verdicts:

Eligible: Clear and verifiable evidence meets the requirement.

Not Eligible: Conclusive evidence fails a mandatory requirement.

Needs Manual Review: The system flags any ambiguity, such as low-confidence OCR, conflicting data, or missing information. A bidder is never silently disqualified.

The Explanation Synthesis Agent generates a clear justification for each verdict, citing the source clauses from both the tender and bid documents.

Audit Trail & Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Review:

Every step, from data extraction to the final verdict, is recorded as a permanent, unalterable entry in the Immutable Ledger.

All items marked "Needs Manual Review" are placed in a queue for the procurement officer. The officer uses a dedicated interface to make a final determination, with all relevant information presented for an efficient decision. This final judgment is also logged in the audit trail.


Key Platform Capabilities

Feature
Description
Impact on Procurement Process
Multi-Agent AI Core
Specialized AI models collaborate, each optimized for a specific task (e.g., legal text analysis, financial data extraction).
Delivers higher accuracy and deeper understanding than a single, general-purpose model.
Intelligent Document Processing
Handles a wide array of document types and qualities, including scanned certificates, photographs, and complex tables.
Eliminates manual data entry and errors from misinterpretation of unstructured documents.
Tri-State Verdict System
Categorizes each check as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review, ensuring no silent disqualifications.
Builds trust and ensures human expertise is applied where it matters most—in cases of ambiguity.
Explainable AI (XAI)
Every automated decision is accompanied by a human-readable explanation with direct references to the source documents.
Provides complete transparency and allows officers to quickly verify the system's reasoning.
Immutable Audit Trail
A cryptographically secure, unchangeable log of every action and decision made by the system and its users.
Guarantees end-to-end auditability, ensuring the process is defensible and compliant with regulations.
Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)
An intuitive interface for officers to review and resolve ambiguous cases flagged by the AI.
Empowers human experts and keeps them in ultimate control of the evaluation decision.

By integrating these advanced capabilities, Dhiyanta provides a comprehensive, secure, and highly efficient platform poised to revolutionize the CRPF's procurement process, fostering fairness, transparency, and operational excellence.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N Nothern_Star
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
UBID Sync Layer: A Bidirectional, Idempotent Interoperability Framework for SWS and Legacy Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 07:41 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The solution recommends the implementation of an interoperability layer that exists between the Single Window System (SWS) and multiple department systems. Service requests between these two layers will be able to be propagated in both directions based on the UBID being the universal join key.
The overall system architecture will operate on an event-based paradigm whereby every change in either the SWS or individual departments will trigger events which are translated into the canonical form and propagated to the intended targets. There will be a schema translation engine within the system for translating between different models used by each department.
The solution guarantees idempotence, retryability, and avoids duplicates by virtue of unique request ID generation and state management for each request. Real-world inconsistencies will be managed through the detection and resolution of conflicts where the solution checks for changes made to the same UBID simultaneously and applies predetermined rules (such as preference for one source).

Input Source
Processing Steps
Output
SWS → Departments

Map UBID → identify target systems → translate schema → push via APIs
Updated department records

Change in department system
Detect via API/webhook/polling → map to UBID → translate → push to SWS
Updated SWS record
Change Detection
Non-event systems
Polling / snapshot diff
Captured updates
Delivery Guarantee
All flows
Retry with idempotent keys
No duplicates/loss

Approach
Benefit
Canonical Model
Define standard schema (SWS format)
Uniform processing

Project Description
Problem Statement Manual evaluation of government tender documents is slow, inconsistent, and error-prone. CRPF receives hundreds of complex PDF/scan tenders with dense legal language. Key eligibility criteria (financial thresholds, technical specs, certifications) are often missed or misinterpreted, leading to disqualifications, delays, and unfair assessments. Proposed SolutionAutoTender is an end-to-end AI system that automates tender evaluation and eligibility analysis. It ingests unstructured tender documents (PDF, scans, Word), extracts and structures all eligibility rules, and performs automated vendor-vs-tender matching. Key Features
Intelligent Criterion Extraction – Uses LayoutLM + Tesseract OCR to parse scanned and native PDFs.

Vendor Eligibility Check – Compares vendor submissions against extracted rules and returns a pass/fail + confidence score. Innovation
Converts human-readable legal text into executable evaluation logic – no manual rule writing needed. Enables real-time, auditable, and bias-free procurement decisions.

AI-Powered Judgment-to-Action Intelligence System (JAIS)
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 24, 2026 11:46 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The AI-Powered Judgment-to-Action Intelligence System (JAIS) is designed to bridge the gap between complex legal judgments and actionable governance decisions. Government officials often struggle with lengthy and unstructured legal documents, leading to delays, inefficiencies, and missed directives.
JAIS addresses this problem by converting unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, explainable, and verifiable action plans. The system uses OCR to extract text from scanned or digital documents, followed by Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to identify key information such as directives, deadlines, responsible departments, and priorities.
The system workflow includes PDF input, text extraction, NLP-based processing, action plan generation, explainability mapping, human verification, and dashboard visualization. Each extracted action is linked to its original source sentence with confidence scores, ensuring transparency and trust. A human-in-the-loop mechanism allows officials to approve, edit, or reject suggested actions.
The generated action plans include key fields such as Action Required, Deadline, Department, and Priority, enabling efficient tracking and execution. A user-friendly dashboard provides department-wise insights, urgent task alerts, and filtering capabilities to support informed decision-making.
The solution is built using a modern technology stack, including React for the frontend, Python FastAPI for the backend, spaCy for NLP processing, and Tesseract for OCR. Data is managed using SQLite or Firebase.
JAIS has the potential to significantly improve administrative efficiency, reduce manual effort, and ensure timely execution of legal directives. Future enhancements include automated alerts, integration with court monitoring systems, and continuous learning from user feedback.

solutions.team_name: LexTrace - AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan System
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


G Good Team
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
LexTrace - AI-Powered Court Judgment to Action Plan System
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:06 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem: The existing Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS) successfully fetches judgment PDFs, but forces government officials to manually read and interpret lengthy, unstructured legal documents to locate critical directives. Because vital mandates are buried deep within complex legal text rather than explicitly highlighted, this purely manual review process causes severe delays, systemic operational inefficiencies, and a high risk of missing time-sensitive legal obligations.
There is a need for a highly reliable, sovereign AI system that can instantly parse these lengthy legal PDFs into clear, departmental action plans while completely eliminating data hallucinations through deterministic extraction and strict human-in-the-loop verification.

Solution: An end-to-end, sovereign AI data pipeline that ingests raw legal PDFs and strictly enforces structured, actionable JSON outputs. The system isolates extraction into a deterministic metadata track (Regex) and a semantic reasoning track (Parent-Document Retrieval RAG). Data is routed through a mandatory Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) interface for arbitration. Verified records instantly populate a role-based Executive Dashboard while simultaneously writing to an immutable audit ledger, ensuring 100% accountability.
Technical Architecture:
Phase 1: Ingestion & Text Sanitization
Dynamic Parsing: The backend accepts PDF uploads. PyMuPDF attempts native extraction. If a scanned document is detected, the system automatically routes to PyTesseract for robust OCR processing.

Noise Reduction Layer: A Python script strips OCR artifacts, normalizes whitespace, and standardizes baseline date formats, outputting a clean, continuous text string to prevent downstream pipeline failure.



Phase 2: The Dual-Track Extraction Engine
Track A: Deterministic Metadata Arbitration Objective: Extract hard facts: Case Number, Base Judgment Date, and Petitioner/Respondent. Process: A Python Regex engine scans the sanitized text. Simultaneously, a BM25 lexical search isolates the document's header chunks and passes them to the LLM for parallel extraction. Confidence Scoring: A Python arbitration layer compares outputs. A good match assigns a high confidence score. Mismatches flag the data for mandatory human review.

Track B: Semantic Action Plan Generation Objective: Extract Directives, Timelines, and Responsible Departments Process: The text is chunked and converted into embeddings using Sentence Transformers. These embeddings are stored in MongoDB Atlas using MongoDB Atlas Vector Search (Hybrid Search: Vector + BM25). Parent-Document Retrieval: The system executes a Hybrid Search (Reciprocal Rank Fusion of Vector + BM25) to find core directives (e.g., "ordered," "directed"). It retrieves the exact matching chunk but passes the entire surrounding parent paragraph to the LLM to preserve conditional legal logic.



Phase 3: State Management and Dual-Write Output
The Single-Collection Queue: All newly parsed Action Plans are inserted into a primary MongoDB Atlas collection with a status: pending_review flag.

HITL Verification Dashboard: The frontend fetches and displays only "pending" documents, rendering the AI-extracted fields alongside the source PDF. Low-confidence Track A metadata or ambiguous Track B timelines are highlighted in red.

The Dual-Write Commit: When a human reviewer corrects and approves the data, the backend executes two operations simultaneously: Updates the primary collection with the corrected text and flips the flag to status: "verified". Silently appends a record to an immutable audit_logs collection detailing exactly who changed what, when, and what the original AI output was.



Phase 4: Executive Governance Dashboard
Role-Based View: A clean, priority-driven frontend that strictly fetches documents where status: "verified".

Actionable Tracking: Department heads view actionable mandates ordered by SLA deadlines. The dashboard provides clear Kanban-style status updates (Pending, In Progress, Complied, Appealed) without exposing the underlying AI mechanics.



Competitive Advantages:
Data Sovereignty: Native integration with Sarvam AI ensures all legal reasoning is processed through an Indian-context model, aligning perfectly with government data localization mandates.

Zero-Hallucination Architecture: Offloading deterministic metadata extraction to Track A (Regex arbitration) physically prevents the LLM from fabricating case numbers and critical baseline dates.

Enterprise-Grade Auditability: The dual-write database architecture provides a mathematically provable history of human intervention, an absolute requirement for legal technology deployment.

Context Preservation: Parent-Document Retrieval ensures complex, multi-paragraph court orders are never truncated during the RAG process, yielding complete, actionable directives.



Categories
Technologies
Use Case
Frontend
React.js / Next.js, PDF.js
Interactive UI with dual-pane PDF viewing and AI-extracted data validation.
Backend
FastAPI (Python)
High-speed API orchestration for ingestion, processing, and HITL workflows.
Database & Storage
MongoDB Atlas
Central document store for parsed outputs, status tracking, and audit logs
Search & RAG
MongoDB Atlas Vector Search (Hybrid: Vector + BM25)
Semantic + lexical retrieval enabling Parent-Document RAG for directive extraction
AI Engine

Legal reasoning and strict JSON-based structured output generation
Data Processing

Deployment

O omkumar_mc22a14_49_92eb
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CRPF SmartTender AI: Explainable Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:22 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Project Details:
The aim of this project is to build an artificial intelligence-based system to streamline and enhance the process of tender evaluation for government contracts. For CRPF, the process of evaluating tenders consists of examining hundreds of documents provided by various bidders, such as financial reports, certifications, and experience details. These can be in various formats like PDF, scanned documents, or Word, leading to a lengthy and inconsistent evaluation process.

Our new solution employs artificial intelligence to streamline certain aspects of the evaluation. It starts by processing the tender document to extract all the eligibility criteria, including financial, technical, and compliance-related criteria. These are then organized for evaluation.

Then the system reads the bidder's response in any format and extracts information such as turnover, GST registration, qualifications, experience, etc. The system uses sophisticated document processing and OCR to deal with scanned documents and images.

The bidder information is compared to the tender criteria. Using this match, the system can then produce a definitive result for each bidder—eligible, not eligible, or needs manual review. When the extracted data is ambiguous, it is identified as a case to be reviewed by humans rather than automatically discarded.

Another key feature of this project is transparency. The system provides justification for its decision-making, including which criterion was being checked, which document was used to extract data, and which data was used to assess the criterion. This guarantees the trustworthiness of the system.

In summary, this project seeks to minimize manual work, increase consistency, and offer a quicker, more accurate, and auditable solution for the evaluation of government tenders.


Solutions:
We have built an AI-based tender assessment system that assists government officials to assess and check bidder eligibility comprehensively.

The system has three steps. First, it processes the tender document and automatically converts all eligibility conditions such as financial, technical, and compliance regulation requirements. All of these are then presented in a format for assessment.

Second, it reads the bidder document, which can be in PDF, scanned, image, or Word file format. Using OCR and document processing, it can extract the turnover, certificates, tax ID, and previous projects, even from unstructured and bad-quality documents.

Third, it compares the bidder information with the tender criteria with a rule-based and AI-based matching engine. This enables it to check the bidder's eligibility status for each criterion: Eligible, Not Eligible, or Manual Review. If the information is unclear or insufficient, the system does not automatically disqualify the bidder from the bid but flags the bidder for review.

The other key requirement of the solution is transparency. The solution provides a justification for every decision (such as the assessment criterion, document, value, and why). This ensures transparency and builds trust.

Also, the system offers an audit of the data, decisions, and actions that were taken to make it suitable for government procurement.

Overall, the system eliminates manual processes, facilitates consistency in evaluation, and enables reliable, fast and equitable evaluation of tenders.


Idea:
Our idea is to develop an AI-powered assistant that helps government organizations like CRPF in evaluating tender bids more efficiently and accurately.

In the current system, officers manually review large tender documents and bidder submissions to check whether each bidder meets the required eligibility criteria. This process is time-consuming, repetitive, and sometimes inconsistent due to human error and the complexity of documents.

Our idea is to simplify this process by using artificial intelligence to automate the understanding and comparison of documents. The system will first read the tender document and identify all the eligibility conditions, such as financial requirements, technical qualifications, and necessary certifications.

Next, it will analyze bidder submissions, which may be in different formats like PDFs, scanned images, or Word files, and extract the relevant information needed for evaluation. The system will then compare this extracted data with the tender criteria.

Based on this comparison, the system will provide clear results for each bidder, whether they are eligible, not eligible, or require manual review. It will also give proper explanations for each decision, showing which condition was checked and what evidence was found.

The main idea is not to replace human officers but to support them by reducing their workload, improving accuracy, and ensuring transparency in the evaluation process.

Overall, our idea focuses on making tender evaluation faster, more consistent, and more reliable using AI.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


Y Yash Khedekar
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Nividia AI: A Sovereign, Explainable Decision Support Platform for Automated Government Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:28 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Nividia AI: Complete Solution Description
Nividia AI is a specialized web-based application designed to modernize the Technical Evaluation process of the tenders. Built as a sovereign, air-gapped platform, it automates the extraction and verification of complex eligibility criteria from tender enquiries and bidder submissions. The solution ensures that procurement decisions are fast, consistent, and legally robust by providing a clear, explainable audit trail for every verdict.


1. Problem Understanding
Government procurement involves a rigorous two-bid system where the Technical Evaluation Committee (TEC) must verify hundreds of mandatory requirements—ranging from financial turnovers to specialized technical certifications—spread across 50–200 pages of legal prose.Bidders submit highly heterogeneous documents, including scanned certificates, typed PDFs, and Word files. Evaluating these manually is slow, inconsistent, and prone to silent errors that can lead to high-stakes legal disputes or procurement delays. Nividia AI addresses this by digitizing the evaluation workflow, ensuring that every rule is applied uniformly and every decision is backed by evidence.

""
2. Solution Architecture
Nividia AI is designed as a modular web application that processes documents through a series of specialized intelligence stages.


Module
Function
Intelligent Ingestion
Accepts multiple formats (PDF, DOCX, JPG) and performs SHA-256 hashing to ensure document integrity.
Extraction Engine
Uses Layout-Aware AI to read and understand the structure of both digital and scanned documents.
Criterion Miner
Parses the Tender Enquiry to generate a machine-readable list of eligibility rules (Mandatory vs. Optional).
Semantic Matcher
Correlates bidder evidence with tender requirements using advanced natural language understanding.
Evaluation Dashboard
Computes confidence scores for each match and flags ambiguous cases for human review.
Audit Reporter
Generates a signed, criterion-level report with direct citations to the source pages.

3. Technical Stack (Software)
The platform is built using a modern, containerized stack optimized for deployment within the secure government intranet.


Primary Intelligence Engine:Gemma 3 27B (Open-Weights), selected for its high-performance reasoning on legal and financial data while remaining fully air-gap compatible.

Web Framework:FastAPI (Backend) and React + TailwindCSS (Frontend) for a responsive, high-performance user experience.

OCR & Document Parsing:PaddleOCR and pdfminer, allowing the system to handle both typed and scanned bidder submissions.

Knowledge Retrieval:ChromaDB for vector-based semantic search, enabling the AI to find evidence without needing exact keyword matches.

Database & Audit:PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security to maintain a tamper-evident log of all evaluator actions.


4. Key Features and Offerings

Zero Silent Rejection Policy
In alignment with CRPFs non-negotiables, the system never auto-rejects a bidder.If a document is difficult to read or a clause is ambiguous, Nividia AI generates a Needs Review flag with a plain-language explanation of the uncertainty, ensuring final authority remains with the human officer.
'""
Semantic Criterion Normalization
The AI understands the meaning behind requirements.It can automatically normalize financial units (e.g., converting Lakhs to Crores) and understand that different terminology (e.g., similar work vs. analogous experience) represents the same technical requirement.
""""
Granular Explainability
Every Pass or Fail verdict is accompanied by:

""""
The exact criterion being tested.

The extracted value from the bidder's submission.

The document name and page number where the evidence was found.

A side-by-side view of the original document for instant verification.

5. Uniqueness and USP

Feature
NividiaAI Value
Tamper-Evident Logs
Uses hash-chaining (similar to blockchain logic) to make audit logs immutable and suitable for RTI responses.
Multilingual Native Support
Native parsing of Hindi and English documents, essential for regional procurement within India.
Sovereign Deployment
no data ever leaves the CRPF network, ensuring total data privacy and security.
Human-Centric Design
Designed to augment the TEC officers, reducing their manual workload while increasing the accuracy of their final report.

6. Functional Requirements & Security
As a web application, Nividia AI is designed to integrate into existing government IT ecosystems.


Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Strict separation of duties between the Uploader, the Evaluator (TEC Officer), and the Final Approver.

Audit Integrity: Every interaction—from document upload to manual override—is time-stamped and recorded in a read-only filesystem.

Infrastructure Agnostic: The platform is containerized (Docker), allowing it to be hosted on any secure server infrastructure provided by the CRPF without requiring proprietary hardware.

7. Feasibility and Impact
Nividia AI is a high-feasibility solution that leverages the latest breakthroughs in open-weights AI to solve a critical administrative bottleneck.

Operational Impact:

Reduced Cycle Time: Technical evaluation that previously took days can be completed in hours.

Legal Resilience: By providing a consistent, evidence-based rationale for every decision, the department is protected from claims of arbitrary or biased disqualification.

Accuracy: The system identifies discrepancies (e.g., mismatched business names across PAN and GST) that are often missed during manual review.

8. Conclusion
Nividia AI provides a definitive path to achieve Maximum Governance through digital transformation. By combining the reasoning capabilities of Gemma 3 with a secure, web-based delivery model, it ensures that government procurement remains transparent, efficient, and fully under the control of human officers.

""

solutions.solution_text:

Z Zeta
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TendorGaurd AI – High-Fidelity Automated Eligibility & Audit System
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:44 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
"Idea Pitch"
Every day, government procurement officers in India manually sift through thousands of pages of tender documents—a process ripe for error, bias, and legal challenge. TenderGuard AI replaces this bottleneck with a five-agent AI system that reads, verifies, cross-checks, and explains every decision it makes.
Unlike a simple "AI chatbot for documents," our system uses two independent LLMs to cross-verify each verdict, links every decision to a precise location in the source document, and automatically escalates uncertain cases to a human reviewer. It's not just faster—it's legally defensible and auditable.
Idea-Document

Project Title: TendorGaurd AI – High-Fidelity Automated Eligibility & Audit System
Team: Zeta (Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation)
The Problem Manual evaluation of dense government tenders (e.g., CRPF) is a massive bottleneck. The process is slowed down by complex criteria, heterogeneous document formats (blurry scans, photos), and subjective rejections that create audit risks and legal delays.
The Solution TendorGaurd AI is an "Explainable Extraction" Engine powered by a Collaborative Multi-Agent AI System that automates document evaluation while maintaining a strict audit trail.
·Architect & Vision Agents: Automatically parse complex tender rulesets and extract data from messy, multilingual (Hindi/English) bidder documents.
·Fact-Checker & Auditor Agents: Utilize Hybrid RAG to locate exact evidence, followed by a Dual-LLM Cross-Check (GPT-4o + Claude 3.5) to prevent AI hallucinations and assign a Confidence Score to the verdict.
·Human-in-the-Loop (HITL): Automatically routes low-confidence files to a review queue, ensuring AI assists procurement officers rather than replacing them.
Unique Selling Points (Why We Win)
·Source Citation Mapper: This is our most unique feature. Every decision links back to a specific page and bounding box (highlighted area) in the bidder's document.
·Confidence Heatmap: Every piece of evidence has a 0–100% confidence score, ensure the human officer spends 90% of their time on the 10% of documents that actually need attention
·Agent Message Bus: Our agents communicate via typed AgentMessage objects, making the entire pipeline debuggable and extendable for future government needs.
·MultiLingual Support: Our agent will support to scan & parse the documents in both English & Hindi formats
·Interactive Evidence Viewer: A Streamlit dashboard where officers view extracted text side-by-side with source snippets, allowing them to confidently approve/reject with mandatory audit comments.
Tech Stack FastAPI, Celery, Docling, LlamaIndex, Hybrid RAG,Redis, Qdrant (Vector DB), PostgreSQL, and Streamlit.

Time-consuming (takes days per tender)

Extractsthe eligibility criteria from the tender documents

Matches extracted data against criteria

Generates explainable eligibility decisions

Flags ambiguous cases for human review

Auditability

Criterion checked

Human review system

A akhilpandey.r15_07
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaFlow is an AI-powered legal intelligence system that transforms complex court judgment PDFs into clear, structured and human-verified action plans for government departments.
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:58 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NyayaFlow: From Court Judgments to Verified Government Action Project Description: "Justice delayed is justice denied.”
Government departments receive thousands of court judgments every year, yet translating these legal documents into timely administrative action remains a major bottleneck. Judgments are long, complex and unstructured with critical directives often buried deep within dense legal language. This leads to delays, missed deadlines and increased legal risk.
NyayaFlow is an AI-powered legal intelligence system designed to bridge this gap. It converts court judgment PDFs into clear, structured and human-verified action plans, enabling faster, more accurate and accountable decision-making, way faster than the present rate.

The Problem Current workflows suffer from:
Lengthy, unstructured judgment documents that are difficult to interpret

Critical directives in dense legal language

Manual reading and interpretation causing delays

Risk of missing compliance deadlines or appeal windows

The result is inefficient governance and exposure to legal consequences.

The Solution

NyayaFlow transforms raw legal text into decision-ready intelligence through a four-stage pipeline:

1. Intelligent Judgment Understanding: The system ingests both scanned and digital PDFs. Using OCR and natural language processing, it extracts:
Case details (court, case number, parties)

Date of order

Key directives and orders

Relevant timelines and deadlines


2. AI-Generated Action Plan: NyayaFlow goes beyond extraction and generates a structured administrative response:
Required action: compliance or appeal

Responsible department

Key deadlines (explicit or inferred)

Priority and risk level

Supporting reasoning with source references

This transforms passive legal documents into actionable insights.

3. Human-in-the-Loop Verification: Recognizing the importance of trust and accountability, NyayaFlow incorporates mandatory human validation:
Extracted data is shown with highlighted source text from the PDF

Confidence scores are provided

Reviewers can approve, edit or reject outputs

Only verified records are stored and used for decision-making.

4. Decision Dashboard: A clean and reliable dashboard presents only approved information:
Department-wise action items

Upcoming deadlines and alerts

High-priority cases

Verified, structured data for decision-makers


Key Differentiators * Action plan generation not just information extraction * Explainable AI with source traceability * Mandatory human verification for reliability * End-to-end pipeline from PDF to decision * Designed specifically for government workflows
Technology Stack:
AI/NLP: Large Language Models (OpenAI / Mistral), LangChain

PDF Processing: PyMuPDF, PaddleOCR

Backend: FastAPI

Database: PostgreSQL with vector search (Qdrant)

Frontend: React or Next.js


Impact: NyayaFlow enables:
Significant reduction in manual effort and review time

Faster and more consistent decision-making

Improved compliance with court directives

Reduced risk of missed appeals or legal penalties

Scalable adoption across departments and jurisdictions

Vision: NyayaFlow is not just a document processing tool. It is a legal intelligence layer for governance.
By ensuring that every judgment is understood, verified and acted upon in time, it strengthens accountability and transforms how governments respond to the judiciary.
