solutions.team_name: Dhiyanta: A Multi-Agent AI Platform for Secure, Transparent, and Auditable Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


R ramharsh-aidev
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Dhiyanta: A Multi-Agent AI Platform for Secure, Transparent, and Auditable Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:58 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Dhiyanta is an advanced, AI-driven platform meticulously engineered to solve the critical challenges of tender evaluation and eligibility analysis for the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF). Moving beyond simple automation, Dhiyanta introduces a secure, transparent, and auditable ecosystem that transforms the procurement lifecycle from a slow, manual, and error-prone process into a rapid, consistent, and data-driven operation. Our solution is designed to handle the complex realities of government procurement, including diverse document formats, ambiguous language, and the non-negotiable requirement for explainability and auditability.
At its core, Dhiyanta is built upon a resilient and scalable microservices architecture, ensuring a clear separation of concerns and the ability to independently manage and upgrade critical system components. This architecture supports a sophisticated multi-agent AI system, where specialized, best-in-class AI models collaborate to deconstruct, analyze, and evaluate tender submissions with unparalleled accuracy and depth of understanding.


System Architecture Overview

Dhiyanta’s architecture is structured in logical layers, ensuring a robust separation of concerns, from user interaction down to the data storage and AI models.
Architectural Layer
Component
Core Technologies / Models
Key Responsibilities
User Plane
Procurement Officer Portal
Secure Web Framework (e.g., React, Angular)
Provides a secure, intuitive interface for uploading documents, reviewing AI-generated evaluations, managing the HITL queue, and exporting final reports.
Application & Logic Plane
API Gateway
Managed Gateway Service
Manages, secures, and throttles all incoming API requests. Acts as the single entry point for the user interface.
Core Microservices
Python (FastAPI), Node.js (Express)
- Tender Processing: Manages tender document lifecycle.
Bidder Submission: Handles ingestion and pre-processing of bid documents.

Evaluation & Compliance: Orchestrates the core evaluation logic.

Auditing & Reporting: Generates reports and logs audit data.
AI & Data Processing Plane
Orchestrator Agent
Low-Latency Generative AI
Manages the workflow between different specialized AI agents, ensuring a seamless flow from criteria extraction to final explanation.
IDP Pipeline
Enterprise IDP Suite (Neural OCR & Layout Engine)
- Pre-processes images (denoising, de-skewing).

- Extracts text from scanned documents and photos.
Parses document structure (tables, forms, paragraphs).
Data & Model Plane
Specialized AI Agents
High-Reasoning & Instruction-Tuned LMMs
- Criteria Extraction: Parses tender documents to identify all eligibility criteria.

- Data Extraction: Scans bidder documents for specific data points corresponding to criteria.
- Explanation Synthesis: Generates human-readable justifications for every verdict.
Data Stores
PostgreSQL: Relational Data
Vector Database: Semantic Data

Immutable Ledger: Audit Trail
- PostgreSQL: Stores structured data like user info, tender metadata, and extracted bidder information.

Vector DB: Enables semantic search and matching for nuanced criteria.

Immutable Ledger (QLDB): Creates a tamper-proof, chronological log of every system action and decision.


End-to-End Evaluation Workflow

Tender Ingestion & Criteria Extraction:

A procurement officer securely uploads the tender document to the Dhiyanta portal.

The Criteria Extraction Agent performs a deep analysis, identifying all technical, financial, and compliance requirements. It distinguishes mandatory from optional criteria and converts them into a structured, machine-readable format.

Bidder Submission & Intelligent Document Processing (IDP):

Bidder submissions, in their varied formats (PDFs, Word files, scanned JPEGs), are uploaded.

Each document is passed through the IDP Pipeline. Advanced OCR and layout analysis convert even poor-quality scans into structured text.

The Data Extraction Agent then scans this structured text to find and extract specific pieces of information (e.g., turnover values, certification dates, project histories) that correspond to the tender criteria.

Automated Evaluation & Explainable Verdicts:

The Evaluation & Compliance Engine systematically compares the extracted bidder data against each criterion.

For every criterion, it generates one of three verdicts:

Eligible: Clear and verifiable evidence meets the requirement.

Not Eligible: Conclusive evidence fails a mandatory requirement.

Needs Manual Review: The system flags any ambiguity, such as low-confidence OCR, conflicting data, or missing information. A bidder is never silently disqualified.

The Explanation Synthesis Agent generates a clear justification for each verdict, citing the source clauses from both the tender and bid documents.

Audit Trail & Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Review:

Every step, from data extraction to the final verdict, is recorded as a permanent, unalterable entry in the Immutable Ledger.

All items marked "Needs Manual Review" are placed in a queue for the procurement officer. The officer uses a dedicated interface to make a final determination, with all relevant information presented for an efficient decision. This final judgment is also logged in the audit trail.


Key Platform Capabilities

Feature
Description
Impact on Procurement Process
Multi-Agent AI Core
Specialized AI models collaborate, each optimized for a specific task (e.g., legal text analysis, financial data extraction).
Delivers higher accuracy and deeper understanding than a single, general-purpose model.
Intelligent Document Processing
Handles a wide array of document types and qualities, including scanned certificates, photographs, and complex tables.
Eliminates manual data entry and errors from misinterpretation of unstructured documents.
Tri-State Verdict System
Categorizes each check as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review, ensuring no silent disqualifications.
Builds trust and ensures human expertise is applied where it matters most—in cases of ambiguity.
Explainable AI (XAI)
Every automated decision is accompanied by a human-readable explanation with direct references to the source documents.
Provides complete transparency and allows officers to quickly verify the system's reasoning.
Immutable Audit Trail
A cryptographically secure, unchangeable log of every action and decision made by the system and its users.
Guarantees end-to-end auditability, ensuring the process is defensible and compliant with regulations.
Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)
An intuitive interface for officers to review and resolve ambiguous cases flagged by the AI.
Empowers human experts and keeps them in ultimate control of the evaluation decision.

By integrating these advanced capabilities, Dhiyanta provides a comprehensive, secure, and highly efficient platform poised to revolutionize the CRPF's procurement process, fostering fairness, transparency, and operational excellence.

solutions.team_name: UBID Sync Layer: A Bidirectional, Idempotent Interoperability Framework for SWS and Legacy Systems
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N Nothern_Star
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
UBID Sync Layer: A Bidirectional, Idempotent Interoperability Framework for SWS and Legacy Systems
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 07:41 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The solution recommends the implementation of an interoperability layer that exists between the Single Window System (SWS) and multiple department systems. Service requests between these two layers will be able to be propagated in both directions based on the UBID being the universal join key.
The overall system architecture will operate on an event-based paradigm whereby every change in either the SWS or individual departments will trigger events which are translated into the canonical form and propagated to the intended targets. There will be a schema translation engine within the system for translating between different models used by each department.
The solution guarantees idempotence, retryability, and avoids duplicates by virtue of unique request ID generation and state management for each request. Real-world inconsistencies will be managed through the detection and resolution of conflicts where the solution checks for changes made to the same UBID simultaneously and applies predetermined rules (such as preference for one source).
Direction
Input Source
Processing Steps
Output
SWS → Departments
Service request from SWS
Map UBID → identify target systems → translate schema → push via APIs
Updated department records
Departments → SWS
Change in department system

The proposed solution is a document intelligence platform powered by AI, which can automate tender evaluation in an entirely explainable and audit-friendly way. Understanding the Tender Elucidates tender requirements through natural language processing (NLP) techniques, and parses requirements for attributes such as criterion types (technical, financial, compliance), thresholds, and whether the requirement is mandatory or optional.

Problem Statement Manual evaluation of government tender documents is slow, inconsistent, and error-prone. CRPF receives hundreds of complex PDF/scan tenders with dense legal language. Key eligibility criteria (financial thresholds, technical specs, certifications) are often missed or misinterpreted, leading to disqualifications, delays, and unfair assessments. Proposed SolutionAutoTender is an end-to-end AI system that automates tender evaluation and eligibility analysis. It ingests unstructured tender documents (PDF, scans, Word), extracts and structures all eligibility rules, and performs automated vendor-vs-tender matching. Key Features
Intelligent Criterion Extraction – Uses LayoutLM + Tesseract OCR to parse scanned and native PDFs.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


R Rookies
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
AI-Driven Two-Way Interoperability Layer for Government Service Synchronization
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Relevance & Depth of Understanding
The core challenge arises from the coexistence of two parallel systems: the Single Window System (SWS) and multiple legacy department systems. As described in the problem context , both systems independently process service requests for businesses, but there is no mechanism to keep them synchronized. This results in a situation where the same business data can diverge across systems, leading to inconsistencies in records and confusion for both citizens and administrators. Updates made in one system are not reflected in others, causing a gradual drift in data accuracy. The difficulty is further increased by the fact that each system uses its own schema, identifiers, and APIs, and none of them can be modified. The problem therefore is not just about moving data, but about maintaining a consistent and reliable state across independent systems while handling updates from multiple sources and resolving conflicts in a controlled and transparent manner.

Technical Implementation & Innovation
The solution introduces an interoperability layer that connects SWS and department systems using UBID as the common identifier. It follows an event-driven approach where changes are detected through APIs, webhooks, or polling depending on system capabilities. A schema translation engine converts data between different formats so that requests originating in one system can be correctly interpreted by another. Each transaction is tracked using unique identifiers to ensure idempotency and prevent duplication during retries. A conflict detection mechanism identifies cases where multiple updates occur for the same business within a short time window and applies a predefined resolution strategy. All transformations and decisions are recorded, ensuring that the system remains transparent and explainable.

Real-World Deployability & Government Feasibility
The solution is designed as a non-intrusive layer that operates without modifying any existing systems. It integrates using available APIs or data access methods and can adapt to systems that do not emit real-time events. It respects the requirement of using UBID as the single join key and ensures that all updates are auditable and reversible. The system is compatible with masked or synthetic data environments and avoids reliance on external processing of sensitive data. Deployment can be carried out incrementally by onboarding one department at a time, allowing controlled adoption without disrupting current operations.

Demo Quality & Presentation
The demonstration will simulate real-world workflows where service requests originate from both SWS and department systems. It will show how a change made in one system is propagated to others, maintaining consistency across all records. The demo will also include scenarios where simultaneous updates occur, highlighting how conflicts are detected and resolved. An audit view will be presented to trace each request from origin to final state, including retries and transformations. The focus of the demo will be on clarity, showing how the system ensures synchronization and reliability across independent systems.

Scalability & Long-Term Impact
The architecture is modular and extensible, allowing additional department systems to be integrated over time without redesigning the core system. It is capable of handling increasing volumes of transactions and supports the addition of new service types and workflows. Over time, the solution can serve as a foundational integration layer for government systems, enabling consistent data across departments and improving service delivery. By ensuring reliable synchronization and traceability, it contributes to more efficient governance and better decision-making at scale.

AI-Based Tender Evaluation & Explainable Eligibility Analysis Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:55 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Relevance & Depth of Understanding
The current tender evaluation process is heavily manual, time-consuming, and inconsistent due to the unstructured nature of both tender documents and bidder submissions. As outlined in the problem context , eligibility criteria are written in complex legal language and spread across multiple pages, while bidder documents vary widely in format, including PDFs, scanned copies, images, and tables. This creates a significant challenge in extracting and comparing relevant information. Different evaluators may interpret the same documents differently, leading to inconsistencies and lack of transparency. Additionally, the process is difficult to audit because decisions are not always clearly traceable to specific pieces of evidence. The problem therefore is not just about automating document reading, but about ensuring consistent, explainable, and reliable evaluation while handling ambiguity and maintaining human oversight.

Technical Implementation & Innovation
The solution introduces an AI-driven document understanding pipeline that processes both tender documents and bidder submissions. It uses OCR to extract text from scanned and image-based documents and applies natural language processing to identify and structure eligibility criteria. Each criterion is categorized and converted into a machine-readable format that can be matched against bidder data. For bidder submissions, the system extracts relevant information such as financial values, certifications, and experience details, even when presented in different formats. A matching engine then evaluates each bidder against each criterion using rule-based logic combined with contextual understanding. The system produces a verdict for each criterion and aggregates them into an overall decision. Every decision is accompanied by a clear explanation that links the criterion, the extracted value, and the source document, ensuring full transparency.

Real-World Deployability & Government Feasibility
The system is designed to operate within existing procurement workflows without requiring changes to current processes. It supports multiple document formats, including scanned and low-quality inputs, making it suitable for real-world conditions. It ensures that no bidder is automatically disqualified without justification, and ambiguous cases are flagged for human review. All decisions are recorded with supporting evidence, making the system auditable and compliant with government requirements. The solution can work with mock or redacted data, ensuring compatibility with data-sharing constraints. It functions as a decision-support tool rather than a fully automated replacement, allowing officers to retain control over final decisions.

Demo Quality & Presentation
The demonstration will simulate the evaluation of a tender with multiple bidder submissions. It will show how the system extracts eligibility criteria from the tender document and processes each bidder’s documents to extract relevant information. The demo will highlight how each bidder is evaluated against each criterion, with clear explanations and references to source documents. It will also include cases where the system flags ambiguity and routes them for manual review. A consolidated report will be generated to show the final evaluation results in a structured and easy-to-understand format. The focus will be on clearly demonstrating how the system improves consistency, transparency, and efficiency.

Scalability & Long-Term Impact
The solution is designed to scale across multiple departments and types of tenders, regardless of domain or document complexity. Its modular architecture allows it to handle increasing volumes of documents and integrate additional data sources over time. As more evaluations are performed, the system can improve through feedback and refinement. In the long term, it can standardize procurement evaluation processes, reduce manual effort, improve fairness, and enhance trust in decision-making. By making evaluations more consistent and auditable, it supports better governance and more efficient use of resources.

solutions.team_name: ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N No Name
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System Problem Understanding: Government procurement in organizations like CRPF follows a legally rigorous process. Eligibility criteria including technical specs, financial thresholds, certifications are scattered across sections, annexures, and cross-referenced clauses. Bidders respond with heterogeneous documents: typed PDFs, scanned copies, and photographs. Evaluation is entirely manual today. Committees spend days cross-checking hundreds of pages, and two evaluators may reach different conclusions from the same documents. Our platform automates the mechanical extraction and comparison work, ensures consistency, and surfaces only genuinely ambiguous cases for human judgement while maintaining a complete audit trail.
Solution: Three-Stage PipelineStage 1 : Understanding the Tender Each page is classified as native or scanned. Native pages use PyMuPDF; scanned pages route to Google Cloud Vision OCR with confidence scores. A linear scan detects section headings via font-size, bold heuristics, and clause-number regex. Boilerplate sections are filtered out. Criteria extraction uses two LLM stages to minimize cost:
Stage 1: Only section titles (~300 tokens) sent to Gemini Flash to identify eligibility-relevant sections.

Stage 2: Full text of identified sections (~5-8k tokens) sent to Gemini Pro, extracting each criterion as structured JSON (field, type, requirement, mandatory flag, source section, unresolved references).

Cross-references (as defined in Annexure-IV) trigger targeted gap-fill LLM calls. Unresolvable references are flagged for officer review before matching begins.
Stage 2 : Understanding the Bidder Each bidders submission is OCR-processed page-by-page with confidence stored per page. Pages receive semantic labels via keyword fingerprinting (e.g., [balance_sheet | page 19]) before embedding into a per-bidder FAISS vector index ensuring retrieval results never conflate across bidders. Document handling: typed PDFs via PyMuPDF; scanned PDFs and photographs via Cloud Vision OCR with pre-processing (de-skew, contrast enhancement).
Stage 3 : Matching Evaluation For each criterion, a type-specific query retrieves the top-3 relevant pages from the bidders FAISS index. Extraction follows a regex-first strategy — rupee/lakh/crore patterns, date patterns, keyword checks. If regex confidence ≥ 0.90, LLM is skipped. Otherwise, LLM extraction triggers with explicit instructions: return null if not found, do not guess.Confidence scoring uses Geminis logprob API to score two hypotheses (meets vs. does not meet threshold), producing a calibrated probability. Final confidence combines four signals: final_confidence = extraction_confidence × ocr_confidence × retrieval_score × llm_logprob_score Verdicts: confidence ≥ 0.90 + passes → PASS; ≥ 0.90 + fails → FAIL; 0.90 → REVIEW. There is no path to silent disqualification. Cross-document validation checks company name consistency, PAN matching, turnover cross-verification (±5%), and work order vs completion certificate values (±2%). Any mismatch triggers REVIEW.
Explainability & Audit Trail Every verdict includes: criterion checked, document page used, value extracted, extraction method, full confidence breakdown, and natural-language explanation. The React dashboard provides: a color-coded comparative matrix (bidders × criteria), a REVIEW queue with source page viewer and confidence breakdown, and one-click Confirm/Override/Re-scan actions. Every officer action is logged. Auditability: immutable PostgreSQL logs, full LLM prompt/response logging, SHA-256 document fingerprinting, exportable PDF reports, and version tracking for corrigenda.
Technology Stack OCR: PyMuPDF (native) + Google Cloud Vision OCR (scanned/photos) | LLM: Google Gemini (Flash + Pro) | Vector Store: FAISS | Backend: FastAPI | Frontend: React | Database: PostgreSQL
Key Risks Mitigations
Poor scan quality → OCR confidence < 0.60 triggers REVIEW; re-upload option

Criteria across corrigenda → Later clauses take precedence

Irrelevant documents → Low retrieval scores flag "evidence not found" as REVIEW, not FAIL

Ambiguous criteria → Fuzzy-type defers to officer by default

LLM hallucination → Regex-first + null-if-not-found + logprob validation

Unit mismatches → Normalization to canonical INR

Implementation Plan (1 Week) Day 1-2: OCR pipeline + section segmentation + criteria extraction Day 3-4: Bidder processing + FAISS indexing + matching engine Day 5-6: Cross-validation + verdict generation + React dashboard Day 7: Audit trail + PDF export + end-to-end testing

Project Description
Problem Statement: Government tender documents are often lengthy, complex, and written in formal legal language, making it difficult for businesses to quickly understand eligibility requirements. This leads to significant time consumption and increases the chances of missing important criteria during manual analysis.
Project Description: TenderAI Lite is an AI-based solution designed to simplify the process of analyzing government tender documents. The system allows users to upload tender files such as PDFs and automatically extracts key eligibility criteria from them.
It identifies important details such as financial requirements, technical qualifications, certifications, and compliance conditions. The extracted information is then organized into a structured format and clearly categorized into mandatory and optional requirements, making it easier to understand.
Additionally, the system provides an eligibility checking feature where users can input their company details, such as turnover and experience. Based on the extracted criteria, the system evaluates whether the user meets the requirements and provides a clear result along with reasons.
By converting complex and unstructured documents into simple, structured insights, the system reduces manual effort, saves time, and helps users make faster and more informed decisions when applying for tenders.
Innovation: The system goes beyond basic document extraction by transforming unstructured tender data into a practical decision-making tool. It not only extracts and organizes information but also evaluates user eligibility, making the process more efficient and actionable.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE

LEADERBOARD


G GenAItics
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderTrust AI: Multimodal Automated Compliance Engine with Explainable Audit Trails for CRPF Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 02:43 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Our solution, TenderTrust AI, is a complete platform designed to automate government tender evaluations. By using Multimodal LLMs and OCR, it changes the slow and error-prone process of checking bidder eligibility into a fast, clear, and accurate digital workflow.
Technical Architecture (As per System Design)
Our system has a modular 6-layer architecture:
Input Layer: Supports bulk uploading of tender documents and bidder response files (PDF, scanned images, Word).
Document Processing: Uses Google Vision API and Tesseract OCR to extract high-quality text from low-quality scanned certificates and handwritten documents.
AI & Information Extraction: Uses GPT-4 (OpenAI) and spaCy (NLP) to identify key eligibility criteria (financial thresholds, ISO certifications, GST compliance) and extract related data from bidder documents.
Evaluation Engine: A custom rule matching engine compares extracted data against tender requirements to give "Eligible," "Not Eligible," or "Needs Review" statuses.
Explainability & Audit: Each decision has an explanation generator that cites the exact source document and page number, ensuring a fully transparent audit trail.
Output & Reporting: An easy-to-use dashboard for CRPF officers to view consolidated results and export PDF or Excel reports.
Tech Stack
Frontend: React.js, Tailwind CSS for a modern, responsive UI.
Backend: Python, FastAPI for high-performance API handling.
AI/NLP: OpenAI (GPT-4), spaCy, LangChain.
OCR: Google Cloud Vision API, Tesseract.
Database & Storage: Firebase Firestore for metadata and Firebase Storage for documents.
DevOps: Docker for containerized deployment.
Key Features & Innovation (The Winning Edge)
Multimodal Processing: Our system can process photographs of physical certificates using computer vision, unlike text-only solutions.
Explainable AI (XAI): We provide not just a "Yes/No," but also the reasoning and a confidence score for every check, which is important for government audits.
Human-in-the-loop: The "Needs Review" flag ensures that complex or borderline cases are reviewed by humans, combining AI speed with human judgment.
Data Security: Built with Firebase Authentication and role-based access to keep sensitive government procurement data safe.
Impact
Efficiency: Cuts the evaluation cycle from weeks to minutes.
Accuracy: Removes human error in checking complex financial and technical thresholds.
Transparency: Offers a tamper-proof audit trail for every procurement decision made by the CRPF.

solutions.team_name: TrustBid AI: A Sovereign, Explainable Decision Support Platform for Automated Government Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


Y Yash Khedekar
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TrustBid AI: A Sovereign, Explainable Decision Support Platform for Automated Government Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 03:47 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PROBLEM STATEMENT
Government procurement agencies like CRPF evaluate bidder submissions against tender eligibility criteria — financial thresholds, technical qualifications, certifications, compliance declarations — spread across 50–200 pages of legal documents. Bidders respond with heterogeneous evidence: typed PDFs, scanned certificates, photographs, mixed Hindi-English documents, and Word files.
Today this evaluation is entirely manual: slow, inconsistent across officers, hard to audit, and prone to both false disqualification and oversight-driven inclusion. When two officers reach different verdicts on the same document set, there is no systematic record of which evidence drove which decision. The risk is not inefficiency — it is procurement governance failure.
The core challenge is trust. A system that silently disqualifies a bidder due to a low-confidence OCR read is more dangerous than the manual process it replaces. Any AI system in this context must be explainable at the criterion level, tamper-evident, and honest about the limits of its own certainty.
PROPOSED SOLUTION
TrustBid AI is an air-gapped document intelligence platform that automates eligibility evaluation on government-controlled hardware — no external APIs, no cloud services, zero data egress. It operates as a deterministic six-stage pipeline where every stage failure escalates to human review rather than propagating corrupt state downstream.
Stage
What It Does
If It Fails
S-01 Ingestion
Classify all file types; compute SHA-256 for tamper detection
Unreadable file → quarantine + alert
S-02 OCR & Layout
Multi-engine OCR for scans/photos; table structure recovery via LayoutLMv3
Page confidence < 0.70 → flagged, not failed
S-03 Criterion Extraction
Parse tender → structured criteria with mandatory/optional tags and source clause links
Ambiguous clause → officer confirms before evaluation starts
S-04 Evidence Extraction
Retrieve and extract bidder evidence per criterion using semantic search
Evidence absent → NOT_FOUND, escalate — never auto-reject
S-05 Evaluation Engine
Compare evidence to criteria; compute confidence score; assign verdict
Confidence < 0.85 → NEEDS_REVIEW, never silent rejection
S-06 Report & Audit
Generate criterion-level report; write hash-chained audit log; sign final PDF
Always executes; records partial state

HOW IT WORKS
Criterion Extraction
–Gemma 3 27B (self-hosted) processes tender sections with chain-of-thought prompting to extract each criterion: type, threshold, required evidence documents, and mandatory vs. optional classification based on modal verb analysis (SHALL/MUST vs. SHOULD/MAY).
–Every criterion carries a source_page and verbatim source_text reference — no criterion exists without a traceable origin. Officers approve the extracted list before evaluation begins.
Confidence-Scored Evaluation
Each verdict carries a composite confidence score C built from four components:
Component
Weight
Measures
OCR Quality (Q_ocr)
25%
Word-level OCR confidence on pages supplying evidence
Extraction Confidence (Q_ext)
35%
LLM certainty on extracted value; derived from output token entropy
Match Confidence (Q_match)
30%
Explicit numeric match = 1.0; semantic/inferred match scores lower
Document Completeness (Q_doc)
10%
1.0 if all required evidence types present; 0.0 if any missing


Score
Verdict
Action
C ≥ 0.85
ELIGIBLE / NOT_ELIGIBLE
Automated — officer reviews summary
0.60 – 0.84
NEEDS_REVIEW
Named officer reviews with specific uncertainty reason
< 0.60
MANUAL_EVALUATION
System presents evidence; officer decide

Human-in-the-Loop
–Officers are engaged at defined trigger points: low-confidence verdicts, poor OCR pages, missing documents, ambiguous criteria, expired certifications, or contradictory values across documents.
–Officers cannot close a bidder evaluation until all flagged items are actioned. Each action records officer ID, timestamp, and written justification.
–Final report approval requires a second named officer — no officer approves their own determinations.
Auditability
–Every action — ingestion, OCR, extraction, verdict, human review, approval — is written to an append-only, hash-chained PostgreSQL audit table. Each record includes the SHA-256 hash of the preceding record.
–All LLM prompts and responses are logged verbatim with model version. No decision is traceable only to a black box.
The final report is signed with an on-premises RSA-2048 key. An offline integrity verification utility confirms chain integrity
TECHNOLOGY STACK
Layer
Technology
Purpose
Primary LLM
Gemma 3 27B · vLLM · 2× A100
Extraction, matching, justification — fully self-hosted
Fallback LLM
Gemma 3 12B · llama.cpp · CPU
Auto-activates on GPU failure — identical output schema
OCR
PaddleOCR 2.7 + Tesseract 5
Hindi + English; scans, photos, digital PDFs — all offline
Layout
LayoutLMv3
Table and form structure recovery from scanned documents
Semantic Search
sentence-transformers + ChromaDB
Offline criterion–evidence vector matching
Backend
FastAPI + Celery + Redis
Async pipeline; per-stage workers; retry logic
Database
PostgreSQL 16
Append-only audit log; row-level security; ACID
Storage / Auth
MinIO + Keycloak
Document versioning with checksums; LDAP/AD RBAC
Infrastructure
Docker Compose (offline bundle)
Air-gapped deployment; pre-loaded images and weights

CRITICAL RISKS AND MITIGATIONS
Risk
Mitigation
LLM misreads ambiguous legal language
Chain-of-thought prompting + Q_ext gating; all uncertain extractions escalated to officer; LLM never trusted for arithmetic
Poor scan or photograph quality
Multi-engine OCR with per-page confidence gating; officer sees original scan alongside OCR output; re-submission workflow available
GPU unavailable in government data centre
Gemma 3 12B CPU fallback activates automatically — correctness preserved, throughput degrades
LLM hallucinates absent evidence
Model instructed to return NOT_FOUND; low Q_ext on deviation triggers mandatory escalation — hallucination cannot produce a passing verdict undetected
Audit log tampered by insider
Hash-chained append-only table; UPDATE/DELETE blocked at DB role level; RSA-signed reports provide external integrity anchor

WHY TrustBid AI
Every non-negotiable in the problem brief is addressed by a specific architectural component, not a general capability claim. Explainability is enforced at the criterion level through source-linked extraction. Silent disqualification is structurally impossible — low confidence always escalates. Scanned documents and photographs are handled through a multi-engine, confidence-gated OCR pipeline. The audit trail is hash-chained, append-only, and tamper-evident by design. Gemma 3 27B and its CPU fallback run entirely on-premises — no tender data ever leaves the government network.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


D DIGI Bytes
TEAM SUBMISSION [5]
Action Bench AI : Benchmarking Compliance from Court Orders
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 04:06 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Action Bench AI is an AI-powered decision-support engine designed to bridge the gap between complex legal judgments and actionable government governance. Built as an intelligent extension to the existing Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS), it transforms lengthy, unstructured legal PDFs into clear, deadline-driven, and human-verified action plans.


The Problem




Currently, when a case is disposed of in the High Court, the resulting judgment is pushed into the CCMS as a dense, multi-page PDF. Government officials are forced to manually sift through complex legal text to determine what the court is actually ordering them to do. This manual interpretation process is painfully slow, prone to human error, and creates significant risks of missed deadlines, leading to non-compliance or missed opportunities for appeal.


Our Solution




JurisVerify acts as a digital legal assistant that processes the heavy lifting of reading and extracting, while keeping the human official firmly in the driver's seat for decision-making. We focus on decision support, not full automation, ensuring that no automated action occurs without strict human oversight. Our system is structured around four core modules: 1. Intelligent Extraction Engine
Multi-Modal Ingestion: Utilizing robust OCR (like Google Cloud Document AI or Tesseract) alongside text-extraction libraries, we process both digital and scanned, unstructured legal PDFs.

LLM Parsing: A Large Language Model (LLM) scans the digitized text to precisely extract Case Details, Dates of Order, Parties Involved, Key Directives, and explicit timelines. 2. AI-Assisted Action Plan Generation Once the raw data is extracted, the AI structures a specialized Action Plan. It evaluates the directives to determine:

Nature of Action: Does the judgment require immediate compliance (e.g., paying a settlement), or does it warrant consideration for an appeal?

Timeline Inference: It maps out explicit deadlines stated by the judge or infers implicit statutory deadlines for appeals.

Department Routing: It identifies the primary responsible department (e.g., Revenue, Public Works) tasked with executing the order. 3. Human-in-the-Loop Verification (The Trust Layer) Before any data enters the official workflow, it must be verified. We provide a transparent, dual-pane UI for the human reviewer:

Source Highlighting: Next to the extracted data, the original PDF is displayed with the exact sentences highlighted, proving exactly where the AI found its information.

Confidence Scoring: Each extracted field displays an AI confidence level (High/Medium/Low).

Gatekeeping: The reviewer must explicitly Approve, Edit, or Reject the extracted data. Only approved records proceed. 4. The Executive Dashboard Verified action plans flow into a clean, intuitive dashboard designed for government decision-makers.

Role-Based Views: Officials only see action items relevant to their specific department.

Actionable KPIs: The dashboard highlights impending deadlines, pending compliances, and required appeal decisions, transforming static legal records into an active workflow.

NyayaFlow AI is an AI-powered decision-support system that converts unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, verified, and actionable plans for government officials.It is designed not just to extract information, but to enable accountable decision-making with human verification.

solutions.team_name: CCMS-Sahayak: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T The Night's Watch
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
CCMS-Sahayak: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 05:34 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Problem
When the Karnataka High Court disposes a case involving a government department, the judgment PDF flows automatically into the Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS). But what arrives is a 30 to 50 page document of dense legal text. Buried inside are critical directives — what action is required, who is responsible, by when, and whether an appeal should be considered within the limitation period.Today, officials must read every page manually to surface these decisions. This causes three real problems: delays in compliance, missed appeal windows, and inconsistent interpretation across departments. The information is technically available, but not actionable.

Our Solution



CCMS-Sahayak is a decision-support layer that sits on top of the existing CCMS. It does not replace or modify any system. It reads the judgment PDFs that CCMS already receives, extracts the critical information, drafts a structured action plan, and presents it to a human reviewer for verification before anything reaches the dashboard.The principle is simple: AI does the heavy lifting of reading and structuring. Humans make the final call. Only verified records move forward.
How It Works



The system follows a four-stage flow:


Stage 1 — Extract: The judgment PDF is parsed using a hybrid approach. Layout-aware parsing handles both digital and scanned documents, with OCR fallback for older orders. Rule-based logic captures high-precision fields like case numbers, dates, and section references. A language model handles the harder parts — identifying directives, parties, and the nature of the order. Where the two methods disagree, the system flags the discrepancy for human review rather than silently picking one.

Stage 2 — Generate Action Plan: Extracted directives are classified into a structured taxonomy: compliance required, appeal consideration, cost order, contempt warning, quashing or setting aside, and so on. Each category maps to a template that drafts the recommended action, the likely responsible authority, and the relevant timeline including any limitation period for appeal. This is the differentiator — the system does not just summarize, it produces a working draft of what needs to happen next.

Stage 3 — Human Verification: Before any record reaches the dashboard, a reviewer sees every extracted field alongside its source. Clicking a field jumps to the exact location in the PDF where it was found, with the supporting text highlighted. Each field carries a confidence score. The reviewer can approve, edit, or reject. Edits are stored as labeled corrections, which improves the system over time.

Stage 4 — Dashboard: Only verified records appear here. The dashboard offers a department-wise view, urgency bands based on timelines, an appeal-window countdown, and clean filters for action type and responsible authority. The interface is designed for officials, not engineers — minimal, scannable, and bilingual in English and Kannada.


Handling Difficult Documents — Honest About Limits

Court records include old judgments where the PDF is a poor-quality scan, the text is faded, handwritten annotations are present, or the OCR simply cannot reconstruct the content reliably. We do not pretend the system will solve every such case automatically.When the system detects that extraction confidence is below a defined threshold — whether due to OCR failure, unreadable scans, or ambiguous structure — the document is flagged and routed to a dedicated human review queue rather than passed through with weak guesses. The reviewer sees the original PDF, the partial extraction (whatever the system could recover), and the specific reason for the flag. They complete the extraction manually, and that record then enters the same verification flow as any other.This matters for two reasons. It prevents low-quality data from contaminating the dashboard, which is what officials will actually act on. And it keeps the system honest — a flagged document is an obvious AI could not handle this, please review rather than a confidently wrong extraction that no one notices until something goes wrong.



Why This Approach Fits Government Use



Three design choices were made specifically with deployment in mind:


On-Premise Deployment: We use Indian sovereign AI models (Sarvam) deployed locally, with no judgment data leaving the government environment. This addresses the most common blocker for AI in government — data residency and confidentiality.

Non-Disruptive Layer: The system is a layer, not a replacement. CCMS continues to function exactly as it does today. CCMS-Sahayak reads from it, processes, and writes verified records back. There is no migration, no retraining of staff on new core systems, no disruption.

Full Auditability: Every output is auditable. The reviewer trail, the source highlights, the confidence scores, and the version history are all preserved. If an action plan is questioned later, the full chain of evidence is retrievable.


What Makes This Technically Strong


Reliable Hybrid Extraction: The hybrid extraction architecture is more reliable than a pure language model approach. Rules give precision where the format is consistent. The model gives flexibility where the language varies. Disagreement detection between the two acts as a built-in quality check.

Explainable Taxonomy: The action plan taxonomy is explainable by design. Every recommendation can be traced back to a directive type and a template — no black-box reasoning. This matters for legal compliance, where decisions must be defensible.

Human-in-the-Loop Core: The human verification loop is not an afterthought. It is the core of the system. The interface makes verification fast, the labeled corrections feed back into improvement, and the dashboard only ever shows trusted data. Adoption depends on officials trusting what they see, and trust comes from being able to check the source in one click.


Beyond the Pilot

The same engine that reads court judgments can read RTI responses, audit objections, tender documents, parliamentary questions, and inter-departmental orders. Every government department in India has the same underlying problem — critical decisions trapped in long PDFs. CCMS is the starting point because the integration path is clearest, but the architecture is general.A successful Karnataka pilot creates a path to other state legal cells, and the same document intelligence layer can be extended vertical by vertical across government workflows.

Deliverables for the Hackathon


A working prototype demonstrating the full pipeline on real Karnataka High Court judgment PDFs.

A reviewer interface with source highlighting and confidence scores.

A dashboard showing verified action plans with urgency bands and bilingual support.

A 5-minute video walkthrough showing extraction, verification, and dashboard flow end to end.

Complete code repository with documentation for on-premise deployment.


The Team

We are a team of student builders with hands-on experience shipping AI products. Our background spans document AI, agentic systems, FastAPI and React production stacks, and Indian-language model deployment. We have built and deployed real systems, not just hackathon prototypes — and we approach this problem the same way: as something that needs to actually work for a clerk in a government office on a Tuesday morning, not just impress a jury for five minutes.

solutions.team_name: PROCTOR: Procurement Review & OCR-Assisted Tender Intelligence Oracle  with Records
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


D Drishtikon
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
PROCTOR: Procurement Review & OCR-Assisted Tender Intelligence Oracle with Records
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 05:46 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
India processes over ₹50 lakh crore in government procurement every year. Behind that number is a system that is still largely manual, deeply inconsistent, legally fragile, and expensive to defend in court. Evaluation committees spend weeks reading hundreds of pages of vendor documents. Honest officers have no protection when their decisions are challenged. Losing bidders have no way to verify that the process was fair. PROCTOR is built to fix all three of these problems at once.
PROCTOR is a fully air-gapped artificial intelligence system that reads government tender documents, automatically scores vendor bids against evaluation criteria, flags ambiguous cases for human review, and seals every decision on an immutable blockchain audit trail. Every step of this process happens inside the government's own servers. No document, no bid amount, no vendor name, and no evaluation score ever leaves the building. This is not a feature. It is the foundation on which everything else is built.
The system introduces three capabilities that no existing procurement tool in India currently offers. The first is a Criteria Inference Engine that reads a tender document and automatically extracts the full evaluation matrix including explicit eligibility requirements and implicit criteria inferred from the scope description, eliminating the need for manual criteria entry and ensuring nothing is missed. The second is an Explainable AI Verdict Engine that scores every vendor against every criterion and provides not just a pass or fail result but the exact page and paragraph from the vendor's document that the decision is based on, the confidence level of the finding, and a plain English explanation of the reasoning. The third is an Integrity Alert Engine that analyses bid patterns across vendors to surface statistical signals of possible bid rigging or cartel behaviour including suspicious price clustering, shared document fingerprints, and overlapping director networks, giving the evaluation committee documented grounds for closer scrutiny.
Critically, PROCTOR is designed around a non-negotiable principle: artificial intelligence assists, it does not decide. Every uncertain verdict and every low-confidence finding is automatically routed to a human reviewer before it becomes part of the official record. When a reviewer approves or overrides an AI finding, that decision along with the reason is permanently written to a permissioned blockchain. The result is an evaluation process where every document is tamper-evident from the moment it is uploaded, the evaluation criteria are cryptographically locked before any bid is assessed, every AI finding and human decision is independently verifiable, and the final evaluation report can be authenticated by any court or auditor at any time.
PROCTOR is built entirely on open source local models including Llama 3.3, Surya OCR, and ChromaDB, runs on existing government hardware without requiring any new cloud subscriptions, and is compliant with the IT Act 2000 data localisation requirements. It is deployable in a fully air-gapped environment. Officers gain a tool that makes them faster and better protected. Vendors gain a process they can trust. Courts gain structured, verifiable evidence. The procurement system gains accountability.
The full technical architecture, implementation plan, technology stack, smart contract design, anomaly detection methodology, startup roadmap, and complete development guide are available in the attached document here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1yHtybOye6u6yF_vfXcUwbyNaqabZm1qk/view?usp=sharing

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


R Rookies
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
AI-Driven Two-Way Interoperability Layer for Government Service Synchronization
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:53 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Relevance & Depth of Understanding
The core challenge arises from the coexistence of two parallel systems: the Single Window System (SWS) and multiple legacy department systems. As described in the problem context , both systems independently process service requests for businesses, but there is no mechanism to keep them synchronized. This results in a situation where the same business data can diverge across systems, leading to inconsistencies in records and confusion for both citizens and administrators. Updates made in one system are not reflected in others, causing a gradual drift in data accuracy. The difficulty is further increased by the fact that each system uses its own schema, identifiers, and APIs, and none of them can be modified. The problem therefore is not just about moving data, but about maintaining a consistent and reliable state across independent systems while handling updates from multiple sources and resolving conflicts in a controlled and transparent manner.

Technical Implementation & Innovation
The solution introduces an interoperability layer that connects SWS and department systems using UBID as the common identifier. It follows an event-driven approach where changes are detected through APIs, webhooks, or polling depending on system capabilities. A schema translation engine converts data between different formats so that requests originating in one system can be correctly interpreted by another. Each transaction is tracked using unique identifiers to ensure idempotency and prevent duplication during retries. A conflict detection mechanism identifies cases where multiple updates occur for the same business within a short time window and applies a predefined resolution strategy. All transformations and decisions are recorded, ensuring that the system remains transparent and explainable.

Real-World Deployability & Government Feasibility
The solution is designed as a non-intrusive layer that operates without modifying any existing systems. It integrates using available APIs or data access methods and can adapt to systems that do not emit real-time events. It respects the requirement of using UBID as the single join key and ensures that all updates are auditable and reversible. The system is compatible with masked or synthetic data environments and avoids reliance on external processing of sensitive data. Deployment can be carried out incrementally by onboarding one department at a time, allowing controlled adoption without disrupting current operations.

Demo Quality & Presentation
The demonstration will simulate real-world workflows where service requests originate from both SWS and department systems. It will show how a change made in one system is propagated to others, maintaining consistency across all records. The demo will also include scenarios where simultaneous updates occur, highlighting how conflicts are detected and resolved. An audit view will be presented to trace each request from origin to final state, including retries and transformations. The focus of the demo will be on clarity, showing how the system ensures synchronization and reliability across independent systems.

Scalability & Long-Term Impact
The architecture is modular and extensible, allowing additional department systems to be integrated over time without redesigning the core system. It is capable of handling increasing volumes of transactions and supports the addition of new service types and workflows. Over time, the solution can serve as a foundational integration layer for government systems, enabling consistent data across departments and improving service delivery. By ensuring reliable synchronization and traceability, it contributes to more efficient governance and better decision-making at scale.

R Rookies
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
AI-Based Tender Evaluation & Explainable Eligibility Analysis Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 12:55 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem Relevance & Depth of Understanding
The current tender evaluation process is heavily manual, time-consuming, and inconsistent due to the unstructured nature of both tender documents and bidder submissions. As outlined in the problem context , eligibility criteria are written in complex legal language and spread across multiple pages, while bidder documents vary widely in format, including PDFs, scanned copies, images, and tables. This creates a significant challenge in extracting and comparing relevant information. Different evaluators may interpret the same documents differently, leading to inconsistencies and lack of transparency. Additionally, the process is difficult to audit because decisions are not always clearly traceable to specific pieces of evidence. The problem therefore is not just about automating document reading, but about ensuring consistent, explainable, and reliable evaluation while handling ambiguity and maintaining human oversight.

Technical Implementation & Innovation
The solution introduces an AI-driven document understanding pipeline that processes both tender documents and bidder submissions. It uses OCR to extract text from scanned and image-based documents and applies natural language processing to identify and structure eligibility criteria. Each criterion is categorized and converted into a machine-readable format that can be matched against bidder data. For bidder submissions, the system extracts relevant information such as financial values, certifications, and experience details, even when presented in different formats. A matching engine then evaluates each bidder against each criterion using rule-based logic combined with contextual understanding. The system produces a verdict for each criterion and aggregates them into an overall decision. Every decision is accompanied by a clear explanation that links the criterion, the extracted value, and the source document, ensuring full transparency.

Real-World Deployability & Government Feasibility
The system is designed to operate within existing procurement workflows without requiring changes to current processes. It supports multiple document formats, including scanned and low-quality inputs, making it suitable for real-world conditions. It ensures that no bidder is automatically disqualified without justification, and ambiguous cases are flagged for human review. All decisions are recorded with supporting evidence, making the system auditable and compliant with government requirements. The solution can work with mock or redacted data, ensuring compatibility with data-sharing constraints. It functions as a decision-support tool rather than a fully automated replacement, allowing officers to retain control over final decisions.

Demo Quality & Presentation
The demonstration will simulate the evaluation of a tender with multiple bidder submissions. It will show how the system extracts eligibility criteria from the tender document and processes each bidder’s documents to extract relevant information. The demo will highlight how each bidder is evaluated against each criterion, with clear explanations and references to source documents. It will also include cases where the system flags ambiguity and routes them for manual review. A consolidated report will be generated to show the final evaluation results in a structured and easy-to-understand format. The focus will be on clearly demonstrating how the system improves consistency, transparency, and efficiency.

Scalability & Long-Term Impact
The solution is designed to scale across multiple departments and types of tenders, regardless of domain or document complexity. Its modular architecture allows it to handle increasing volumes of documents and integrate additional data sources over time. As more evaluations are performed, the system can improve through feedback and refinement. In the long term, it can standardize procurement evaluation processes, reduce manual effort, improve fairness, and enhance trust in decision-making. By making evaluations more consistent and auditable, it supports better governance and more efficient use of resources.

solutions.team_name: ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


N No Name
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 01:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
ProcureCheck — Automated Tender Parsing, Bidder Matching & Compliance Audit System Problem Understanding: Government procurement in organizations like CRPF follows a legally rigorous process. Eligibility criteria including technical specs, financial thresholds, certifications are scattered across sections, annexures, and cross-referenced clauses. Bidders respond with heterogeneous documents: typed PDFs, scanned copies, and photographs. Evaluation is entirely manual today. Committees spend days cross-checking hundreds of pages, and two evaluators may reach different conclusions from the same documents. Our platform automates the mechanical extraction and comparison work, ensures consistency, and surfaces only genuinely ambiguous cases for human judgement while maintaining a complete audit trail.
Solution: Three-Stage PipelineStage 1 : Understanding the Tender Each page is classified as native or scanned. Native pages use PyMuPDF; scanned pages route to Google Cloud Vision OCR with confidence scores. A linear scan detects section headings via font-size, bold heuristics, and clause-number regex. Boilerplate sections are filtered out. Criteria extraction uses two LLM stages to minimize cost:
Stage 1: Only section titles (~300 tokens) sent to Gemini Flash to identify eligibility-relevant sections.

Stage 2: Full text of identified sections (~5-8k tokens) sent to Gemini Pro, extracting each criterion as structured JSON (field, type, requirement, mandatory flag, source section, unresolved references).

Cross-references (as defined in Annexure-IV) trigger targeted gap-fill LLM calls. Unresolvable references are flagged for officer review before matching begins.
Stage 2 : Understanding the Bidder Each bidders submission is OCR-processed page-by-page with confidence stored per page. Pages receive semantic labels via keyword fingerprinting (e.g., [balance_sheet | page 19]) before embedding into a per-bidder FAISS vector index ensuring retrieval results never conflate across bidders. Document handling: typed PDFs via PyMuPDF; scanned PDFs and photographs via Cloud Vision OCR with pre-processing (de-skew, contrast enhancement).
Stage 3 : Matching Evaluation For each criterion, a type-specific query retrieves the top-3 relevant pages from the bidders FAISS index. Extraction follows a regex-first strategy — rupee/lakh/crore patterns, date patterns, keyword checks. If regex confidence ≥ 0.90, LLM is skipped. Otherwise, LLM extraction triggers with explicit instructions: return null if not found, do not guess.Confidence scoring uses Geminis logprob API to score two hypotheses (meets vs. does not meet threshold), producing a calibrated probability. Final confidence combines four signals: final_confidence = extraction_confidence × ocr_confidence × retrieval_score × llm_logprob_score Verdicts: confidence ≥ 0.90 + passes → PASS; ≥ 0.90 + fails → FAIL; 0.90 → REVIEW. There is no path to silent disqualification. Cross-document validation checks company name consistency, PAN matching, turnover cross-verification (±5%), and work order vs completion certificate values (±2%). Any mismatch triggers REVIEW.
Explainability & Audit Trail Every verdict includes: criterion checked, document page used, value extracted, extraction method, full confidence breakdown, and natural-language explanation. The React dashboard provides: a color-coded comparative matrix (bidders × criteria), a REVIEW queue with source page viewer and confidence breakdown, and one-click Confirm/Override/Re-scan actions. Every officer action is logged. Auditability: immutable PostgreSQL logs, full LLM prompt/response logging, SHA-256 document fingerprinting, exportable PDF reports, and version tracking for corrigenda.
Technology Stack OCR: PyMuPDF (native) + Google Cloud Vision OCR (scanned/photos) | LLM: Google Gemini (Flash + Pro) | Vector Store: FAISS | Backend: FastAPI | Frontend: React | Database: PostgreSQL
Key Risks Mitigations
Poor scan quality → OCR confidence < 0.60 triggers REVIEW; re-upload option

Criteria across corrigenda → Later clauses take precedence

Irrelevant documents → Low retrieval scores flag "evidence not found" as REVIEW, not FAIL

Ambiguous criteria → Fuzzy-type defers to officer by default

LLM hallucination → Regex-first + null-if-not-found + logprob validation

Unit mismatches → Normalization to canonical INR

Implementation Plan (1 Week) Day 1-2: OCR pipeline + section segmentation + criteria extraction Day 3-4: Bidder processing + FAISS indexing + matching engine Day 5-6: Cross-validation + verdict generation + React dashboard Day 7: Audit trail + PDF export + end-to-end testing

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


G GenAItics
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderTrust AI: Multimodal Automated Compliance Engine with Explainable Audit Trails for CRPF Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 02:43 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Our solution, TenderTrust AI, is a complete platform designed to automate government tender evaluations. By using Multimodal LLMs and OCR, it changes the slow and error-prone process of checking bidder eligibility into a fast, clear, and accurate digital workflow.
Technical Architecture (As per System Design)
Our system has a modular 6-layer architecture:
Input Layer: Supports bulk uploading of tender documents and bidder response files (PDF, scanned images, Word).
Document Processing: Uses Google Vision API and Tesseract OCR to extract high-quality text from low-quality scanned certificates and handwritten documents.
AI & Information Extraction: Uses GPT-4 (OpenAI) and spaCy (NLP) to identify key eligibility criteria (financial thresholds, ISO certifications, GST compliance) and extract related data from bidder documents.
Evaluation Engine: A custom rule matching engine compares extracted data against tender requirements to give "Eligible," "Not Eligible," or "Needs Review" statuses.
Explainability & Audit: Each decision has an explanation generator that cites the exact source document and page number, ensuring a fully transparent audit trail.
Output & Reporting: An easy-to-use dashboard for CRPF officers to view consolidated results and export PDF or Excel reports.
Tech Stack
Frontend: React.js, Tailwind CSS for a modern, responsive UI.
Backend: Python, FastAPI for high-performance API handling.
AI/NLP: OpenAI (GPT-4), spaCy, LangChain.
OCR: Google Cloud Vision API, Tesseract.
Database & Storage: Firebase Firestore for metadata and Firebase Storage for documents.
DevOps: Docker for containerized deployment.
Key Features & Innovation (The Winning Edge)
Multimodal Processing: Our system can process photographs of physical certificates using computer vision, unlike text-only solutions.
Explainable AI (XAI): We provide not just a "Yes/No," but also the reasoning and a confidence score for every check, which is important for government audits.
Human-in-the-loop: The "Needs Review" flag ensures that complex or borderline cases are reviewed by humans, combining AI speed with human judgment.
Data Security: Built with Firebase Authentication and role-based access to keep sensitive government procurement data safe.
Impact
Efficiency: Cuts the evaluation cycle from weeks to minutes.
Accuracy: Removes human error in checking complex financial and technical thresholds.
Transparency: Offers a tamper-proof audit trail for every procurement decision made by the CRPF.

solutions.team_name: TrustBid AI: A Sovereign, Explainable Decision Support Platform for Automated Government Tender Evaluation
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


Y Yash Khedekar
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
TrustBid AI: A Sovereign, Explainable Decision Support Platform for Automated Government Tender Evaluation
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 03:47 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PROBLEM STATEMENT
Government procurement agencies like CRPF evaluate bidder submissions against tender eligibility criteria — financial thresholds, technical qualifications, certifications, compliance declarations — spread across 50–200 pages of legal documents. Bidders respond with heterogeneous evidence: typed PDFs, scanned certificates, photographs, mixed Hindi-English documents, and Word files.
Today this evaluation is entirely manual: slow, inconsistent across officers, hard to audit, and prone to both false disqualification and oversight-driven inclusion. When two officers reach different verdicts on the same document set, there is no systematic record of which evidence drove which decision. The risk is not inefficiency — it is procurement governance failure.
The core challenge is trust. A system that silently disqualifies a bidder due to a low-confidence OCR read is more dangerous than the manual process it replaces. Any AI system in this context must be explainable at the criterion level, tamper-evident, and honest about the limits of its own certainty.
PROPOSED SOLUTION
TrustBid AI is an air-gapped document intelligence platform that automates eligibility evaluation on government-controlled hardware — no external APIs, no cloud services, zero data egress. It operates as a deterministic six-stage pipeline where every stage failure escalates to human review rather than propagating corrupt state downstream.
Stage
What It Does
If It Fails
S-01 Ingestion
Classify all file types; compute SHA-256 for tamper detection
Unreadable file → quarantine + alert
S-02 OCR & Layout
Multi-engine OCR for scans/photos; table structure recovery via LayoutLMv3
Page confidence < 0.70 → flagged, not failed
S-03 Criterion Extraction
Parse tender → structured criteria with mandatory/optional tags and source clause links
Ambiguous clause → officer confirms before evaluation starts
S-04 Evidence Extraction
Retrieve and extract bidder evidence per criterion using semantic search
Evidence absent → NOT_FOUND, escalate — never auto-reject
S-05 Evaluation Engine
Compare evidence to criteria; compute confidence score; assign verdict
Confidence < 0.85 → NEEDS_REVIEW, never silent rejection
S-06 Report & Audit
Generate criterion-level report; write hash-chained audit log; sign final PDF
Always executes; records partial state

HOW IT WORKS
Criterion Extraction
–Gemma 3 27B (self-hosted) processes tender sections with chain-of-thought prompting to extract each criterion: type, threshold, required evidence documents, and mandatory vs. optional classification based on modal verb analysis (SHALL/MUST vs. SHOULD/MAY).
–Every criterion carries a source_page and verbatim source_text reference — no criterion exists without a traceable origin. Officers approve the extracted list before evaluation begins.
Confidence-Scored Evaluation
Each verdict carries a composite confidence score C built from four components:
Component
Weight
Measures
OCR Quality (Q_ocr)
25%
Word-level OCR confidence on pages supplying evidence
Extraction Confidence (Q_ext)
35%
LLM certainty on extracted value; derived from output token entropy
Match Confidence (Q_match)
30%
Explicit numeric match = 1.0; semantic/inferred match scores lower
Document Completeness (Q_doc)
10%
1.0 if all required evidence types present; 0.0 if any missing


Score
Verdict
Action
C ≥ 0.85
ELIGIBLE / NOT_ELIGIBLE
Automated — officer reviews summary
0.60 – 0.84
NEEDS_REVIEW
Named officer reviews with specific uncertainty reason
< 0.60
MANUAL_EVALUATION
System presents evidence; officer decide

Human-in-the-Loop
–Officers are engaged at defined trigger points: low-confidence verdicts, poor OCR pages, missing documents, ambiguous criteria, expired certifications, or contradictory values across documents.
–Officers cannot close a bidder evaluation until all flagged items are actioned. Each action records officer ID, timestamp, and written justification.
–Final report approval requires a second named officer — no officer approves their own determinations.
Auditability
–Every action — ingestion, OCR, extraction, verdict, human review, approval — is written to an append-only, hash-chained PostgreSQL audit table. Each record includes the SHA-256 hash of the preceding record.
–All LLM prompts and responses are logged verbatim with model version. No decision is traceable only to a black box.
The final report is signed with an on-premises RSA-2048 key. An offline integrity verification utility confirms chain integrity
TECHNOLOGY STACK
Layer
Technology
Purpose
Primary LLM
Gemma 3 27B · vLLM · 2× A100
Extraction, matching, justification — fully self-hosted
Fallback LLM
Gemma 3 12B · llama.cpp · CPU
Auto-activates on GPU failure — identical output schema
OCR
PaddleOCR 2.7 + Tesseract 5
Hindi + English; scans, photos, digital PDFs — all offline
Layout
LayoutLMv3
Table and form structure recovery from scanned documents
Semantic Search
sentence-transformers + ChromaDB
Offline criterion–evidence vector matching
Backend
FastAPI + Celery + Redis
Async pipeline; per-stage workers; retry logic
Database
PostgreSQL 16
Append-only audit log; row-level security; ACID
Storage / Auth
MinIO + Keycloak
Document versioning with checksums; LDAP/AD RBAC
Infrastructure
Docker Compose (offline bundle)
Air-gapped deployment; pre-loaded images and weights

CRITICAL RISKS AND MITIGATIONS
Risk
Mitigation
LLM misreads ambiguous legal language
Chain-of-thought prompting + Q_ext gating; all uncertain extractions escalated to officer; LLM never trusted for arithmetic
Poor scan or photograph quality
Multi-engine OCR with per-page confidence gating; officer sees original scan alongside OCR output; re-submission workflow available
GPU unavailable in government data centre
Gemma 3 12B CPU fallback activates automatically — correctness preserved, throughput degrades
LLM hallucinates absent evidence
Model instructed to return NOT_FOUND; low Q_ext on deviation triggers mandatory escalation — hallucination cannot produce a passing verdict undetected
Audit log tampered by insider
Hash-chained append-only table; UPDATE/DELETE blocked at DB role level; RSA-signed reports provide external integrity anchor

WHY TrustBid AI
Every non-negotiable in the problem brief is addressed by a specific architectural component, not a general capability claim. Explainability is enforced at the criterion level through source-linked extraction. Silent disqualification is structurally impossible — low confidence always escalates. Scanned documents and photographs are handled through a multi-engine, confidence-gated OCR pipeline. The audit trail is hash-chained, append-only, and tamper-evident by design. Gemma 3 27B and its CPU fallback run entirely on-premises — no tender data ever leaves the government network.

solutions.solution_text:

D DIGI Bytes
TEAM SUBMISSION [5]
Action Bench AI : Benchmarking Compliance from Court Orders
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 04:06 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Action Bench AI is an AI-powered decision-support engine designed to bridge the gap between complex legal judgments and actionable government governance. Built as an intelligent extension to the existing Court Case Monitoring System (CCMS), it transforms lengthy, unstructured legal PDFs into clear, deadline-driven, and human-verified action plans.


The Problem




Currently, when a case is disposed of in the High Court, the resulting judgment is pushed into the CCMS as a dense, multi-page PDF. Government officials are forced to manually sift through complex legal text to determine what the court is actually ordering them to do. This manual interpretation process is painfully slow, prone to human error, and creates significant risks of missed deadlines, leading to non-compliance or missed opportunities for appeal.


Our Solution




JurisVerify acts as a digital legal assistant that processes the heavy lifting of reading and extracting, while keeping the human official firmly in the driver's seat for decision-making. We focus on decision support, not full automation, ensuring that no automated action occurs without strict human oversight. Our system is structured around four core modules: 1. Intelligent Extraction Engine
Multi-Modal Ingestion: Utilizing robust OCR (like Google Cloud Document AI or Tesseract) alongside text-extraction libraries, we process both digital and scanned, unstructured legal PDFs.

LLM Parsing: A Large Language Model (LLM) scans the digitized text to precisely extract Case Details, Dates of Order, Parties Involved, Key Directives, and explicit timelines. 2. AI-Assisted Action Plan Generation Once the raw data is extracted, the AI structures a specialized Action Plan. It evaluates the directives to determine:

Nature of Action: Does the judgment require immediate compliance (e.g., paying a settlement), or does it warrant consideration for an appeal?

Timeline Inference: It maps out explicit deadlines stated by the judge or infers implicit statutory deadlines for appeals.

Department Routing: It identifies the primary responsible department (e.g., Revenue, Public Works) tasked with executing the order. 3. Human-in-the-Loop Verification (The Trust Layer) Before any data enters the official workflow, it must be verified. We provide a transparent, dual-pane UI for the human reviewer:

Source Highlighting: Next to the extracted data, the original PDF is displayed with the exact sentences highlighted, proving exactly where the AI found its information.

Confidence Scoring: Each extracted field displays an AI confidence level (High/Medium/Low).

Gatekeeping: The reviewer must explicitly Approve, Edit, or Reject the extracted data. Only approved records proceed. 4. The Executive Dashboard Verified action plans flow into a clean, intuitive dashboard designed for government decision-makers.

Role-Based Views: Officials only see action items relevant to their specific department.

Actionable KPIs: The dashboard highlights impending deadlines, pending compliances, and required appeal decisions, transforming static legal records into an active workflow.

NyayaFlow AI is an AI-powered decision-support system that converts unstructured court judgment PDFs into structured, verified, and actionable plans for government officials.It is designed not just to extract information, but to enable accountable decision-making with human verification.
