solutions.team_name: NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Integrity Platform
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A armankgp1978_a49f
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Integrity Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:37 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Intelligence Platform Bharat AI Hackathon | PanIIT | Govt. of India
THE PROBLEM
Government procurement in India is a high-stakes process where public funds are converted into infrastructure, defence capability, and essential services. A single tender may attract more than 20 bidders, each submitting 200 to 300 pages of documents across multiple formats and languages, creating thousands of data points that officers must manually verify under time pressure and strict legal accountability.
This creates five systemic failures. Inconsistent evaluation — different officers apply the same criteria differently, creating legally vulnerable outcomes. Missed criteria — eligibility conditions buried in dense documents are overlooked under pressure. Forged documents — fabricated certificates pass undetected without automated cross-verification. MSME disadvantage — smaller bidders are rejected due to document presentation quality rather than actual ineligibility. Indefensible decisions — when RTI requests or legal challenges arrive, officers cannot produce a complete, traceable justification chain.
NYAYASETU eliminates all five.
THE CORE PHILOSOPHY
NYAYASETU is built on one foundational principle: AI is never permitted to make a final eligibility decision. The system follows a neuro-symbolic architecture where AI handles document understanding, extraction, and anomaly detection, while all eligibility determinations are performed by deterministic rule-based logic that is fully auditable. AI assists. Rules decide. Humans approve. This is not a design convenience — it is a legal necessity for government-scale deployment.
COMPONENT 1 — AUTOMATED CRITERIA EXTRACTION
Tender documents are converted from unstructured natural language into a structured eligibility matrix before evaluation begins. The pipeline identifies mandatory and optional conditions, classifies them into financial, technical, compliance, and experience categories, and detects logical dependencies between criteria. Ambiguous conditions are flagged for officer clarification before any bidder document is processed. This creates a fixed, officer-validated baseline that all bidders are measured against equally. The criteria set cannot be changed after this gate is locked, eliminating any possibility of post-hoc adjustment.
COMPONENT 2 — MULTI-FORMAT DOCUMENT INTELLIGENCE
NYAYASETU processes digital PDFs, scanned files, photographed pages, Word documents, and Excel sheets. OCR and vision models handle degraded scans, handwritten annotations, and multilingual content including Hindi and regional languages. When extraction confidence falls below threshold, the specific uncertain field is routed for human verification rather than silently processed. A poorly scanned MSME document is never automatically rejected — the system requests human review of the specific field, ensuring evaluation quality does not penalise document presentation quality.
COMPONENT 3 — ZERO-HALLUCINATION EVALUATION
Each bidder is evaluated through a four-stage pipeline. Hybrid search combining keyword and semantic methods retrieves relevant document sections. AI extracts exact values such as turnover figures, certification numbers, and project dates, normalising them into standard units. Every extracted value is then verified against its source location in the original document — if the evidence chain cannot be validated, the value is automatically flagged for human review. Final eligibility determination is performed entirely by deterministic rule-based logic. AI has no role in this stage. Results are classified as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — always a deterministic outcome with full evidence, never a probability score.
COMPONENT 4 — DOCUMENT AUTHENTICITY VERIFICATION
NYAYASETU detects forged or manipulated documents through multiple layers. Digital certificate validation checks against issuing authority registries. Metadata consistency analysis examines document creation dates and modification history for signs of tampering. Cross-field coherence checks verify that numerical data across multiple submitted documents does not contradict itself. Visual anomaly detection flags documents where layout, typography, or seal patterns deviate from known authentic formats. Detected anomalies are flagged with specific evidence for officer review — never automatic disqualification.
COMPONENT 5 — EXPLAINABLE DECISIONS AND CANDIDATE CHALLENGE
Every evaluation outcome is accompanied by a complete evidence chain: the criterion as stated in the tender, the source document and page number, the exact extracted value, and the logical determination applied. For rejected bidders, a structured justification report is generated containing every specific criterion not met. This report can be produced in response to an RTI request within seconds, not days. It is legally defensible because every step is traceable and based on officer-validated criteria rather than opaque AI inference.
If a bidder formally disputes a determination, the system presents the complete evidence chain to the reviewing officer with the specific point of contention highlighted. Officers can override with documented reasoning — every such override is recorded in the audit trail.
COMPONENT 6 — HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP GOVERNANCE
Three mandatory checkpoints enforce human authority at every critical stage.
Gate 1 is Criteria Validation — the officer explicitly reviews and locks the extracted eligibility criteria before any bidder document is processed. No evaluation begins without this approval.
Gate 2 is Uncertainty Review — all flagged cases are reviewed by an officer before evaluation proceeds. No uncertain case is silently resolved.
Gate 3 is Final Approval — the complete evaluation summary is reviewed and digitally signed by the authorised officer before any result is recorded. The system cannot generate a final outcome without this signature.
No bidder is ever automatically or silently disqualified.
COMPONENT 7 — TAMPER-PROOF AUDIT AND COMPLIANCE
Every submitted document is hashed at ingestion — any subsequent modification is immediately detectable. Every system action, officer decision, AI extraction, and gate approval is recorded in an immutable append-only log with timestamp and user identity. The complete evaluation history for any tender is fully reproducible at any future point. The platform is deployed on-premise — sensitive procurement data never leaves the organisation's own infrastructure. The system is designed to comply with GFR 2017, CVC guidelines, and applicable data security standards.
TECH STACK
All components are free and open source with no proprietary API dependencies. LayoutLMv3 for visually rich document understanding. PaddleOCR for multilingual text extraction. FAISS and BM25 hybrid retrieval for semantic and keyword search. Fine-tuned language model for criteria extraction feeding into Apache Drools rule engine for all final determinations. SHA-256 document hashing and PostgreSQL append-only audit log. Docker containerisation for on-premise deployment across standard government server configurations. No data leaves the deployment environment.
FITMENT CATEGORIES
Eligible — all criteria met, evidence chain complete, no anomalies flagged. Not Eligible — one or more criteria not met, specific justification report generated. Needs Manual Review — extraction uncertainty, document anomaly, or borderline determination requiring officer decision.
DECISION SUPPORT DASHBOARD
Evaluation officers see a complete tender overview with per-bidder status, criteria coverage map, and flagged anomaly list. Every flag links directly to the specific document page and extracted value that triggered it. Rejected bidder reports are one-click exportable in formats suitable for RTI responses. The audit log is searchable by date, officer, action, and bidder for compliance reviews.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S samartha21_89e8
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderIQ- AI-Based Tender Evaluation Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:38 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderIQ: AI-Based Tender Evaluation Platform
The System would be designed to perform the following capabilities
Extract structured eligibility criteria from tender documents and Assess it

Parse and understand multi-format bidder submissions

Evaluate each bidder against all the required criterion

Generate explainable, auditable, and consistent decisions and reasoning

Flag uncertain cases for human review for enhancing scope


System Components :
NLP Engine : To understand tender and extract criterions from it

Document Processing Engine : Valuable entity extraction to assess the tender requirements say [PDFs → text extraction] and [Scanned docs/photos → OCR using Tesseract] and Normalisation

Information Mapping Layer : Maps extracted bidder data → corresponding criteria

Evaluation Engine: LLM engine to classify the bidder data as Eligible, Not Eligible , Needs Manual Review based on confidence scoring and assign to officer for uncertain cases

Human-in-the-Loop System : Officer validation for uncertain classified cases, to improve overall evaluation and improves model over time by feedback loop

Explainability Module : Agent/Bot giving overall summary/report and milestone on decisions like Criterion checked, Source document , Extracted value , Reason for verdict etc.



Feasibility
Transformer-based NLP models like BERT and OCR engines such as Tesseract OCR can reliably handle criterion extraction and document parsing with moderate customization.
Each component (NLP, OCR, evaluation, explainability) can be independently developed, tested, and scaled—well-suited for a microservices deployment.
Ambiguous cases are not auto-rejected, making the system viable for real-world government workflows where accuracy and auditability are critical.

Challenges
Scanned documents, handwritten notes, and inconsistent formats can reduce OCR accuracy and downstream extraction quality.
Tender criteria are often vaguely worded, requiring hybrid approaches (rules + LLMs) to avoid incorrect interpretations.
Advanced LLM-based decisions must still produce clear, auditable reasoning acceptable in formal procurement contexts.

S Sketch
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderEye: An Auditable, On-Premise AI Evaluation Engine for Government Tenders
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 10:01 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The solution Our workflow is built on strict governance and auditability: Standardized Ingestion: Blurry scans and nested tables are converted to text blocks with exact coordinates and OCR confidence scores. Locked Rules: An authorized officer must review, edit and mathematically lock the tender base criteria before any bidder is evaluated. Targeted Matchers: we use deterministic code for rigid rule (e.g. GSTIN checksum validation) and isolated LLM reasoning for subjective ones (e.g. matching “similar projects”).
The Core Edge Our primary differentiator is our 3-state verdict (eligible, not eligible, needs review) paired with total visual traceability. In government procurement, silent AI features are catastrophic. If OCR confidence drops below 75% or legal language ambiguous the system refuses to guess and flags needs review. Every decision is backed by an immutable hand-chained audit log in the Ui clicking a verdict opens the original source document highlighting the exact evidence in a yellow bounding box. By radically shrinking the scope to execute four criteria perfectly, we prioritize auditable precision over generic AI hype.

PPT LINK - https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1SjpTMrcv76tHp7BRf6M_9bHZ9tQPi7q8rRgkGusQkHU/edit?usp=sharing
Project DRISHTI — Document Review and Intelligent Screening for Tender Inspection — is an AI-powered procurement evaluation platform designed for government tendering processes in India.
At its core, DRISHTI addresses a critical gap: the current tender evaluation process is slow, inconsistent, and vulnerable to errors and fraud — yet it carries serious legal and regulatory consequences. DRISHTI automates the heavy lifting of document reading, criterion matching, and cross-document verification, while keeping a human officer firmly in the decision seat.
Key highlights of the solution include:
Automated tender parsing that generates a structured, machine-readable evaluation schema from any tender document

Multi-modal document ingestion — handling clean PDFs, scanned documents, and even photographs of physical certificates

Smart criterion matching across financial, technical, compliance, and documentary requirements

Cross-document contradiction detection to flag inconsistencies that are nearly impossible to catch manually

Explainable verdicts — every finding is backed by evidence, a source document, and a confidence score

An immutable, cryptographically chained audit trail for full accountability and legal defensibility

Regulatory intelligence built in — covering GFR 2017, MSME provisions, DPIIT orders, and more

The system is designed around one guiding principle: the AI reads, the officer decides — every recommendation is transparent, traceable, and overridable.

Project Description
The Solution
Veritas-Legis is a secure, Full-Stack platform designed to bridge the gap between the courtroom and the community. By combining Natural Language Processing (NLP) with Zero-Trust Security principles, the platform ensures that every legal mandate is accurately translated into a task and verified with immutable proof. Please check The Pdf for Problem Statement Solution Below

Key Features

AI Mandate Extraction: Uses LLMs to ingest complex PDF judgments and automatically extract the Operative Portion, identifying the specific "Who," "What," and "When" of the court's order.

Zero-Trust Action Tracking: Unlike standard task managers, every action is tied to a Verified Identity. Using secure authentication, only the designated official can mark a task as "In Progress" or "Complete."

Immutable Evidence Verification: To close a case, officials must upload Geo-tagged and Time-stamped evidence. The platform generates a cryptographic hash of this proof, ensuring it cannot be backdated or tampered with.

Proactive Integrity Monitoring: A dashboard for judicial and senior administrative oversight to track execution rates and identify bottlenecks in real-time.

C corsair devs
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
ABIL – Agent Based Interoperability Layer
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 10:15 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Overview Government systems in Karnataka operate in silos, where the Single Window System (SWS) and 40+ department systems independently manage business data. This leads to inconsistencies, duplicate records, and lack of a unified view.
ABIL (Agent-Based Interoperability Layer) is a middleware solution that enables bidirectional synchronization between SWS and legacy systems without modifying existing infrastructure, ensuring consistency, auditability, and reliability.
Problem Understanding This is a distributed system consistency problem caused by:
Multiple authoritative systems (SWS and department systems)

Lack of real-time communication

Schema and API heterogeneity

No reliable synchronization mechanism

Inability to modify existing systems

Resulting in:
Data inconsistencies

Conflicting updates

Lack of auditability

Proposed Solution: ABIL ABIL introduces a modular agent-based middleware layer that:
Detects changes across systems

Translates heterogeneous data formats

Synchronizes updates in both directions

Resolves conflicts using defined policies

Maintains a complete audit trail

System Architecture
Listener Agents: Detect changes via APIs or polling

Translation Agents: Map schemas across systems

Sync Agents: Handle SWS ↔ Department updates

Conflict Resolution Agent: Resolve simultaneous updates (timestamp/source-based)

Audit Agent: Log all operations for traceability

Key Features
Bidirectional synchronization

No modification of existing systems

Schema translation across APIs

Conflict detection and resolution

Idempotent and fault-tolerant processing

End-to-end auditability

Technical Approach
Backend: Node.js (microservices architecture)

Communication: Event-driven (Kafka or queue simulation)

Integration: REST APIs

Storage: MongoDB / PostgreSQL

Monitoring: Dashboard for audit and tracking

Impact
Eliminates data inconsistencies across systems

Improves transparency and trust

Enables reliable decision-making

Scales across departments and future integrations

Feasibility
Middleware-only approach (no system modification)

Works with synthetic/sandbox data

Fully compliant with constraints

Future Scope
Integration with UBID system

AI-based schema mapping and anomaly detection

Production-grade real-time event streaming

R raadheshbajoria
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderGraph Zero-Hallucination, Evidence-Anchored Evaluation Engine for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 10:41 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Evaluating government tenders is a legally critical, manual process. Standard AI solutions fail here because they hallucinate decisions and violate data privacy by sending sensitive PII to hosted cloud APIs. TenderGraph solves this by introducing a Two-Tier, privacy-first architecture. First, Local Vision-Language Models (VLMs) securely extract data from messy bidder documents (scanned certificates, photos) within an air-gapped sandbox. Second, a deterministic logic engine mathematically evaluates the extracted data against the tender's rules, guaranteeing zero hallucination. To ensure 100% auditability, TenderGraph features a "Sniper-Highlight UI" that draws visual bounding boxes directly on the original document, proving exactly where the data was found. Ambiguous documents are never silently rejected; they are safely routed to a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) review dashboard, making the procurement process fast, explainable, and legally defensible.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE

T Team Duo
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
InteropSync Karnataka — Two-Way SWS ↔ Department Middleware
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:03 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem. Karnataka's Single Window System (SWS) is the front door for new business registrations, but 40+ legacy department systems remain authoritative for businesses already on their books. Today, an address change in SWS doesn't reach Labour, Tax, or KSPCB. A change inside a department never reaches SWS. The two sides drift apart silently. Solution. InteropSync is a non-invasive bidirectional middleware:
Push engine — propagates SWS changes to all affected departments in seconds

Pull engine — webhooks where supported, snapshot-diff polling everywhere else

Schema translation — JSON-config-driven; adding a new department is config, not code

Conflict detection — side-by-side diff with configurable resolution policy (last-writer-wins / source-wins / human review)

Idempotency via payload-hash deduplication, at-least-once delivery, exponential-backoff retries

Immutable audit log — every propagation is an event-sourced record

Builds on the UBID identifier shipped in our Theme 1 submission as the common join key. Neither SWS nor any department is ever modified. Demo. 27 sync events, 6 detected conflicts (status mismatches, address mismatches), live conflict-resolution UI with side-by-side diff, full audit trail. Video walkthrough (5 min):https://github.com/sridhar7601/interopsync-karnataka/blob/main/demo/video/demo.mp4 GitHub:https://github.com/sridhar7601/interopsync-karnatakaStack. FastAPI · SQLAlchemy · APScheduler · React · TypeScript · Tailwind · event-sourced architecture Deployment. Two services (sync-engine + poll-worker) on a single VM. No production data leaves the perimeter. Pilot scope: 3 departments + Karnataka SWS, 90 days. Year-one cost ~₹35L — far cheaper than retroactively rebuilding any source system.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


T Team Duo
TEAM SUBMISSION [10]
TenderEval AI — AI-Powered Tender Evaluation for Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:04 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Problem. CRPF and other government bodies issue thousands of tenders a year. Each has detailed eligibility criteria — turnover thresholds, similar-work experience, GST registration, ISO certifications, EMD requirements. Bidder submissions arrive as typed PDFs, scanned copies, Word files, even photographs. A committee spends days cross-checking hundreds of pages, and two evaluators often reach different conclusions from the same documents. Solution. TenderEval AI is a three-stage explainable pipeline:
Extract — Docling parses the tender PDF; Claude API extracts structured eligibility criteria with verbatim quotes, page references, and mandatory/optional flags

Parse — every bidder submission, regardless of format. Typed → Docling. Scans → PaddleOCR. Photos → both

Evaluate — Claude API renders one of three verdicts per (bidder × criterion) cell: Eligible · Not Eligible · Needs Review

Every cell is criterion-level explainable with reasoning + page-level source citation. Ambiguous cases (low-confidence OCR, unclear documents) are explicitly Needs Review — never silently disqualified. Audit-ready by construction; CVC-compliant. Demo. Real CRPF "Construction of Border Outpost" tender → 8 criteria extracted automatically (Annual Turnover ≥ ₹5Cr, 3 similar projects, GST, PAN, ISO 9001, EMD, Solvency, No Blacklisting). 3 bidders × 8 criteria = 24-cell evaluation matrix with click-through reasoning per cell. Video walkthrough (5 min):https://github.com/sridhar7601/tendereval-ai/blob/main/demo/video/demo.mp4 GitHub:https://github.com/sridhar7601/tendereval-aiStack. FastAPI · SQLAlchemy · Docling · PaddleOCR · Claude API (JSON-mode) · React · TypeScript · Tailwind Deployment. Single VM + LLM endpoint. CRPF can host on internal infrastructure, route LLM calls through Azure OpenAI for data sovereignty. Pilot: 1 CRPF division, 90 days, real tender data inside their sandbox. Year-one cost ~₹40L — saves committee weeks per tender.

S Synapsis
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderSense AI: Explainable Multi-Modal AI Platform for Automated Tender Evaluation & Eligibility Analysis in Government Procurement
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:08 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
? Overview
Government procurement processes, such as those conducted by CRPF, rely on detailed tender documents and complex bidder submissions. These documents are often unstructured, spread across multiple formats (PDFs, scanned copies, images), and require extensive manual evaluation.TenderSense AI is an end-to-end, explainable AI platform that automates tender evaluation while ensuring transparency, auditability, and human oversight. It is designed specifically for government environments where every decision must be legally defensible and traceable.



⚠️ Problem Statement
Current tender evaluation systems face critical challenges:


Manual evaluation takes days and is highly resource-intensive

Bidder documents are inconsistent and unstructured across formats

Evaluations are prone to human error and subjective interpretation

Lack of auditability creates risks during RTI and legal scrutiny

No standardized mechanism to ensure consistent eligibility assessment
This results in inefficiency, delays, and reduced trust in procurement outcomes.


? Key Insight
This is not just a document processing problem — it is a trust and governance problem.A viable solution must:



Provide criterion-level explainability

Maintain full audit trails

Ensure no bidder is silently rejected

Support human-in-the-loop decision-making


?️ Proposed Solution: TenderSense AI
TenderSense AI is a modular, AI-powered platform that:


Understands Tender Documents

Extracts financial, technical, and compliance criteria

Identifies mandatory vs optional requirements

Parses Bidder Submissions

Processes PDFs, scanned documents, images, and tables

Extracts structured data such as turnover, certifications, and project experience

Evaluates Eligibility

Matches extracted data against criteria using a hybrid AI + rule engine

Outputs: Eligible / Not Eligible / Needs Review

Generates Explainable Decisions

Provides justification for every verdict

Links each decision to source document, page, and extracted value

Supports Human Oversight

Flags low-confidence or ambiguous cases

Allows officers to review, override, and annotate decisions

Ensures Auditability

Maintains tamper-proof logs of all actions and decisions

Generates audit-ready reports for compliance and legal review


⚙️ Technical Approach

OCR & Document Processing: Tesseract, Google Vision API

Layout Understanding: LayoutLM for structured extraction

NLP & Semantic Parsing: LLMs (LLaMA/GPT), BERT-based NER

Table Extraction: Camelot / Tabula

Decision System: Hybrid rule engine + AI validation

Explainability Layer: Criterion-level reasoning with traceability

Architecture: Modular microservices (deployable on secure government infrastructure)


? Real-World Impact

Reduces evaluation time by 70–80%

Ensures consistent, unbiased decision-making

Improves transparency and accountability

Minimizes legal and compliance risks

Enables scalable adoption across ministries and public sector units


? Scalability
TenderSense AI can be extended beyond CRPF to:


Defence procurement systems

Railways and infrastructure tenders

State government procurement bodies

Integration with platforms like GeM


? Why It Stands Out

Fully explainable AI (no black-box decisions)

Handles multi-format, real-world documents

Designed for government compliance and audit needs

Incorporates human-in-the-loop governance

Ready for practical deployment, not just prototype demonstration


? Conclusion
TenderSense AI transforms procurement from a manual, error-prone process into an intelligent, transparent, and auditable system — ensuring that every decision is backed by data, logic, and trust.

From manual scrutiny to intelligent, explainable governance.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A avik.chaterjee_b281
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
AI-Powered End-to-End Tender Eligibility Evaluation for Government Procurement Developed for the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF)
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:20 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PRISM (Procurement Review & Intelligent Scoring Module) is a full-stack AI platform designed to modernize government tender evaluation. It transforms a slow, inconsistent, and error-prone manual process into a fast, transparent, and legally auditable system—while ensuring that human procurement officers retain final decision authority.
The platform ingests tender documents and bidder submissions in diverse formats, including PDFs, scanned images, Word files, and photographs. Using large language models (LLMs), PRISM extracts structured eligibility criteria from complex legal text, even when criteria are scattered across multiple sections such as eligibility clauses, annexures, and scope of work.
To ensure completeness and accuracy, PRISM employs a four-pass extraction strategy: (1) identifying relevant sections, (2) extracting structured criteria into a standardized schema, (3) validating and deduplicating criteria while detecting conflicts, and (4) enabling officer review to establish a legally approved ground truth.
Document processing is handled through a robust multi-stage pipeline. Files are first classified, then enhanced using image preprocessing techniques such as deskewing, contrast adjustment, and noise removal. A five-tier OCR system ensures high accuracy across document types, combining tools like PyMuPDF, Azure OCR, Tesseract, and GPT-4 Vision, with fallback to human review when confidence is low.
PRISM’s core innovation lies in its hybrid evaluation engine. Objective criteria—such as financial thresholds, certificate validity, and document presence—are evaluated using deterministic rule-based logic, ensuring full reproducibility. Subjective criteria—such as “similar project experience”—are assessed using LLM-based semantic reasoning supported by Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which retrieves the most relevant evidence from bidder documents.
The system also handles real-world complexities including terminology variation, multi-document aggregation, borderline values, and genuine ambiguity. Cases with uncertainty are automatically routed to human reviewers, ensuring zero silent disqualifications.
Every decision in PRISM is fully explainable. Each verdict is linked to specific criteria, source documents, extracted values, and a step-by-step reasoning chain. A confidence-based routing system determines whether decisions are automated or require manual review.
For legal defensibility, PRISM maintains a tamper-proof, hash-chained audit log that records all actions, including AI outputs and human overrides. This ensures complete traceability for audits by authorities such as CAG, CVC, or courts.
By combining automation with accountability, PRISM delivers faster evaluations, consistent decision-making, and full transparency—setting a new standard for government procurement systems.

solutions.team_name: OmniBridge - An Event-Driven, Self-Healing Integration Fabric for SWS
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A anshikasaini989_e213
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
OmniBridge - An Event-Driven, Self-Healing Integration Fabric for SWS
Theme: Theme 2: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems by Karnataka Commerce & Industry Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:27 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
PPT link : https://canva.link/ad6mv8iurjxui93 Video Link : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xkB1fW6s678r4aZI6xAhtj3iW5V6Gbad/view?usp=drive_link Website Link : https://omni-bridge-nine.vercel.app/ Github Repo : https://github.com/TheAnushree25/OmniBridge
OmniBridge – Core Idea

OmniBridge is an intelligent middleware layer between SWS (Single Window System) and 40+ legacy government systems. Instead of forcing legacy systems to change, OmniBridge adapts to them and keeps data synchronized in both directions.


1. Problem & Constraints

SWS A unified portal, but backed by 40+ independent systems with different schemas, tech levels, and unsynchronized clocks. These systems cannot be modified. Why migration fails Departments are independent. Coordinating schema changes is slow and risky. New systems keep getting added. UBID necessity Universal Business ID is required to map records across departments.


2. Bidirectional Sync Without Changes

Constraint No agents, no triggers, no code changes. Only APIs are used. SWS → Departments Webhook → Ingestion Gateway → YAML mapping → Saga dispatch with idempotency. Departments → SWS Webhooks for modern systems. Virtual CDC for legacy systems using polling + change detection.


3. Schema Mapping & Onboarding

YAML Mapper Defines field mappings in readable config files. No coding needed. New department onboarding AI generates YAML → human review → shadow testing → activation. No redeployment required.


4. Change Detection in Legacy Systems

Problem No events or webhooks. Solution (Virtual CDC) Poll APIs → hash records → compare with stored values. If changed, emit event. Optimization Adaptive polling based on activity.


5. Conflict Detection & Resolution

Detection Vector clocks instead of timestamps. Resolution (CRDT rules)
Single values → Last Writer Wins

Collections → Union (no data loss)

Authority fields → SWS priority

Safety fields → Most restrictive value

Transparency All decisions are logged and reversible.


6. Reliability & Audit

Idempotency Unique keys prevent duplicate writes. Saga Orchestrator Tracks multi-step operations with retries. Partial success is allowed. Circuit Breaker Stops failed systems temporarily and resumes after recovery. Merkle Audit Ledger Hash-linked logs ensure tamper detection. Failure handling Retries, alerts, checkpoint recovery, and nightly reconciliation.


7. Architecture & Tech Stack

Core Components Gateway, Mapper, Saga Engine, CDC Poller, Conflict Engine, Audit Ledger. Stack
Django (backend + admin)

Celery + Redis (async + retries)

PostgreSQL (state + JSONB)

Redis (cache + state)

YAML (config-based mapping)

AI Usage Only for one-time YAML generation. Not used in runtime. Design Choice Lightweight alternatives to Kafka/Temporal. Fully modular and replaceable.


8. Risks & Trade-offs


Polling delay → handled by adaptive polling

Vector size → small and manageable

Wrong merge rules → mitigated via testing + audit logs

API rate limits → controlled via throttling

Temporary inconsistencies → resolved via reconciliation

Audit growth → handled via archival




Demo Scenarios


Happy path → SWS update syncs to departments with audit log

Conflict → Detected via vector clocks, resolved via rules

Failure → Circuit breaker + auto recovery

Legacy update → CDC detects and merges safely

Tampering → Merkle chain detects instantlydiately flags the inconsistency.

B bulchandanibhoomi639_ff78
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
Pramana.AI: Enterprise Tender Intelligence & Automated Auditing
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:36 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
The Problem: Government procurement is heavily bottlenecked by manual document verification. Evaluating hundreds of scanned, unstructured vendor bids against strict legal requirements (turnover, GSTINs, ISO certifications) takes days, introduces human error, and creates compliance vulnerabilities.
Our Solution: Pramana.AI is a Palantir-style document intelligence dashboard built for modern governance. It completely automates the tender evaluation pipeline. By ingesting scanned vendor documents, our deterministic AI extracts key data points and mathematically grades them against a configurable JSON rulebook, instantly outputting a "VERIFIED" or "FAILED" audit matrix.
Why it solves the problem (Explainable AI): Unlike black box LLMs that hallucinate, Pramana.AI uses strict Regex and NLP logic for its final decision making. If a government rule requires a 15-digit GSTIN, our engine uses mathematical pattern matching. This guarantees 100% auditable, transparent, and legally defensible results for IAS officers.
The Architecture & Tech Stack:
The Face (Frontend): A decoupled, high contrast React application designed for enterprise usability and speed.

The Brain (Backend): A lightning fast FastAPI server handling asynchronous processing.

The Eyes (Pipeline): A custom computer vision and Optical Character Recognition (OCR) pipeline utilizing EasyOCR and OpenCV to extract text from blurry, low-quality document scans.

The Engine (Logic): Pure Python, SpaCy, and Regex driven rule matchers to enforce financial, legal, and certification requirements. Source Code & Architecture

Explore our complete, decoupled monorepo (React Frontend + FastAPI Logic Engine + OCR Pipeline) here: https://github.com/bhoomibulchandani/Pramana.ai

solutions.team_name: NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Integrity Platform
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A armankgp1978_a49f
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Integrity Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:37 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NYAYASETU — AI-Powered Procurement Intelligence Platform Bharat AI Hackathon | PanIIT | Govt. of India
THE PROBLEM
Government procurement in India is a high-stakes process where public funds are converted into infrastructure, defence capability, and essential services. A single tender may attract more than 20 bidders, each submitting 200 to 300 pages of documents across multiple formats and languages, creating thousands of data points that officers must manually verify under time pressure and strict legal accountability.
This creates five systemic failures. Inconsistent evaluation — different officers apply the same criteria differently, creating legally vulnerable outcomes. Missed criteria — eligibility conditions buried in dense documents are overlooked under pressure. Forged documents — fabricated certificates pass undetected without automated cross-verification. MSME disadvantage — smaller bidders are rejected due to document presentation quality rather than actual ineligibility. Indefensible decisions — when RTI requests or legal challenges arrive, officers cannot produce a complete, traceable justification chain.
NYAYASETU eliminates all five.
THE CORE PHILOSOPHY
NYAYASETU is built on one foundational principle: AI is never permitted to make a final eligibility decision. The system follows a neuro-symbolic architecture where AI handles document understanding, extraction, and anomaly detection, while all eligibility determinations are performed by deterministic rule-based logic that is fully auditable. AI assists. Rules decide. Humans approve. This is not a design convenience — it is a legal necessity for government-scale deployment.
COMPONENT 1 — AUTOMATED CRITERIA EXTRACTION
Tender documents are converted from unstructured natural language into a structured eligibility matrix before evaluation begins. The pipeline identifies mandatory and optional conditions, classifies them into financial, technical, compliance, and experience categories, and detects logical dependencies between criteria. Ambiguous conditions are flagged for officer clarification before any bidder document is processed. This creates a fixed, officer-validated baseline that all bidders are measured against equally. The criteria set cannot be changed after this gate is locked, eliminating any possibility of post-hoc adjustment.
COMPONENT 2 — MULTI-FORMAT DOCUMENT INTELLIGENCE
NYAYASETU processes digital PDFs, scanned files, photographed pages, Word documents, and Excel sheets. OCR and vision models handle degraded scans, handwritten annotations, and multilingual content including Hindi and regional languages. When extraction confidence falls below threshold, the specific uncertain field is routed for human verification rather than silently processed. A poorly scanned MSME document is never automatically rejected — the system requests human review of the specific field, ensuring evaluation quality does not penalise document presentation quality.
COMPONENT 3 — ZERO-HALLUCINATION EVALUATION
Each bidder is evaluated through a four-stage pipeline. Hybrid search combining keyword and semantic methods retrieves relevant document sections. AI extracts exact values such as turnover figures, certification numbers, and project dates, normalising them into standard units. Every extracted value is then verified against its source location in the original document — if the evidence chain cannot be validated, the value is automatically flagged for human review. Final eligibility determination is performed entirely by deterministic rule-based logic. AI has no role in this stage. Results are classified as Eligible, Not Eligible, or Needs Manual Review — always a deterministic outcome with full evidence, never a probability score.
COMPONENT 4 — DOCUMENT AUTHENTICITY VERIFICATION
NYAYASETU detects forged or manipulated documents through multiple layers. Digital certificate validation checks against issuing authority registries. Metadata consistency analysis examines document creation dates and modification history for signs of tampering. Cross-field coherence checks verify that numerical data across multiple submitted documents does not contradict itself. Visual anomaly detection flags documents where layout, typography, or seal patterns deviate from known authentic formats. Detected anomalies are flagged with specific evidence for officer review — never automatic disqualification.
COMPONENT 5 — EXPLAINABLE DECISIONS AND CANDIDATE CHALLENGE
Every evaluation outcome is accompanied by a complete evidence chain: the criterion as stated in the tender, the source document and page number, the exact extracted value, and the logical determination applied. For rejected bidders, a structured justification report is generated containing every specific criterion not met. This report can be produced in response to an RTI request within seconds, not days. It is legally defensible because every step is traceable and based on officer-validated criteria rather than opaque AI inference.
If a bidder formally disputes a determination, the system presents the complete evidence chain to the reviewing officer with the specific point of contention highlighted. Officers can override with documented reasoning — every such override is recorded in the audit trail.
COMPONENT 6 — HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP GOVERNANCE
Three mandatory checkpoints enforce human authority at every critical stage.
Gate 1 is Criteria Validation — the officer explicitly reviews and locks the extracted eligibility criteria before any bidder document is processed. No evaluation begins without this approval.
Gate 2 is Uncertainty Review — all flagged cases are reviewed by an officer before evaluation proceeds. No uncertain case is silently resolved.
Gate 3 is Final Approval — the complete evaluation summary is reviewed and digitally signed by the authorised officer before any result is recorded. The system cannot generate a final outcome without this signature.
No bidder is ever automatically or silently disqualified.
COMPONENT 7 — TAMPER-PROOF AUDIT AND COMPLIANCE
Every submitted document is hashed at ingestion — any subsequent modification is immediately detectable. Every system action, officer decision, AI extraction, and gate approval is recorded in an immutable append-only log with timestamp and user identity. The complete evaluation history for any tender is fully reproducible at any future point. The platform is deployed on-premise — sensitive procurement data never leaves the organisation's own infrastructure. The system is designed to comply with GFR 2017, CVC guidelines, and applicable data security standards.
TECH STACK
All components are free and open source with no proprietary API dependencies. LayoutLMv3 for visually rich document understanding. PaddleOCR for multilingual text extraction. FAISS and BM25 hybrid retrieval for semantic and keyword search. Fine-tuned language model for criteria extraction feeding into Apache Drools rule engine for all final determinations. SHA-256 document hashing and PostgreSQL append-only audit log. Docker containerisation for on-premise deployment across standard government server configurations. No data leaves the deployment environment.
FITMENT CATEGORIES
Eligible — all criteria met, evidence chain complete, no anomalies flagged. Not Eligible — one or more criteria not met, specific justification report generated. Needs Manual Review — extraction uncertainty, document anomaly, or borderline determination requiring officer decision.
DECISION SUPPORT DASHBOARD
Evaluation officers see a complete tender overview with per-bidder status, criteria coverage map, and flagged anomaly list. Every flag links directly to the specific document page and extracted value that triggered it. Rejected bidder reports are one-click exportable in formats suitable for RTI responses. The audit log is searchable by date, officer, action, and bidder for compliance reviews.

solutions.team_name: TenderIQ- AI-Based Tender Evaluation Platform
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


S samartha21_89e8
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
TenderIQ- AI-Based Tender Evaluation Platform
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:38 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
TenderIQ: AI-Based Tender Evaluation Platform
The System would be designed to perform the following capabilities
Extract structured eligibility criteria from tender documents and Assess it

Parse and understand multi-format bidder submissions

Evaluate each bidder against all the required criterion

Generate explainable, auditable, and consistent decisions and reasoning

Flag uncertain cases for human review for enhancing scope


System Components :
NLP Engine : To understand tender and extract criterions from it

Document Processing Engine : Valuable entity extraction to assess the tender requirements say [PDFs → text extraction] and [Scanned docs/photos → OCR using Tesseract] and Normalisation

Information Mapping Layer : Maps extracted bidder data → corresponding criteria

Evaluation Engine: LLM engine to classify the bidder data as Eligible, Not Eligible , Needs Manual Review based on confidence scoring and assign to officer for uncertain cases

Human-in-the-Loop System : Officer validation for uncertain classified cases, to improve overall evaluation and improves model over time by feedback loop

Explainability Module : Agent/Bot giving overall summary/report and milestone on decisions like Criterion checked, Source document , Extracted value , Reason for verdict etc.



Feasibility
Transformer-based NLP models like BERT and OCR engines such as Tesseract OCR can reliably handle criterion extraction and document parsing with moderate customization.
Each component (NLP, OCR, evaluation, explainability) can be independently developed, tested, and scaled—well-suited for a microservices deployment.
Ambiguous cases are not auto-rejected, making the system viable for real-world government workflows where accuracy and auditability are critical.

Challenges
Scanned documents, handwritten notes, and inconsistent formats can reduce OCR accuracy and downstream extraction quality.
Tender criteria are often vaguely worded, requiring hybrid approaches (rules + LLMs) to avoid incorrect interpretations.
Advanced LLM-based decisions must still produce clear, auditable reasoning acceptable in formal procurement contexts.

Aegis is a high-assurance document processing and compliance engine designed to modernize government procurement. The system ingests dense tender specifications and vendor submissions, autonomously cross-referencing critical eligibility criteria such as ISO certifications, financial thresholds, and operational history.
Utilizing an advanced semantic parsing architecture, Aegis maps unstructured vendor data against strict regulatory frameworks, eliminating human oversight errors and drastically reducing evaluation turnaround times. The core engine is built on a fail-safe, objective evaluation protocol that flags discrepancies in real-time. Crucially, Aegis generates a transparent, tamper-proof audit trail for every vendor decision, ensuring that all CRPF procurement standards are strictly, securely, and uniformly enforced before reaching the final human-in-the-loop approval stage.

solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


V Vector Krews
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NYAI-SETU (Neural Yield for Actionable Intelligence)
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 25, 2026 11:59 PM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
NYAI-SETU ( Neural Yield for Actionable Intelligence ) ## Transforming Legal Complexity into Compliance Clarity




# Project Description
NYAI-SETU is Indias Sovereign AI-powered court judgment intelligence system that transforms legal PDFs into verified, actionable compliance plans in just few minutes






Our solution bridges the critical gap between judicial orders and government execution by automatically extracting directives, calculating deadlines, and generating department-specific action plans while maintaining complete human oversight and explainable AI transparency.

Human-Centered AI Approach: We augment, not replace. AI handles exhausting extraction work while humans provide judgment and approval.
## Key Features






Sovereign & Secure Architecture: A fully On-Premise Deployment featuring a Fine-Tuned LLM Engine specifically trained on extensive Indian Court Ruling Datasets. This ensures high-context Legal Domain Specialization while maintaining absolute Data Privacy through Offline Processing that never leaves the device.

Action Intelligence: Transforms Unstructured Judgments into Structured Action Plans using advanced NLP Extraction. By identifying Critical Directives and Judicial Holdings autonomously, the system eliminates the Manual Processing Bottleneck and ensures Litigation Readiness.

Precision & Explainability: Features a Zero-Hallucination Design with Coordinate-Level Sourcing, allowing officials to verify every extraction against the original PDF Source. This Explainable Intelligence builds trust by providing Visible AI Reasoning for every identified mandate.

Human-In-The-Loop (HITL) Workflow Designed to augment rather than replace, the system combines AI Extraction with Human Verification. It uses Deterministic Algorithms for Deadline Calculation and Confidence Scoring to prioritize reviews, ensuring a Trusted Dashboard for final administrative approval.




System Workflow






Intelligent Ingestion & OCR Processing The system ingests Court judgment PDFs and by using advanced OCR Technology, it handles multi-page, unstructured, scanned or digital or hybrid formats, preserving Page Boundaries and document structure for high-speed Intelligent Ingestion.

AI-Powered Contextual Analysis A specialized Legal Reasoning Engine parses the judgment to identify Critical Directives and timelines. This NLP Extraction phase converts unstructured text into a Structured Schema, mapping exact Source Coordinates for every identified mandate.

Intelligent Validation & Quality Assurance To ensure a Zero-Hallucination Design, the system employs Grammar Enforcement and Span Verification. Every data point undergoes Token Probability Analysis, assigning a Confidence Score to flag any fields requiring closer review.

Human-In-The-Loop (HITL) Verification A mandatory Human Verification stage provides a split-screen interface where officials compare the Extracted Intelligence against the original PDF Source. This ensures a Trusted Dashboard through manual approval, editing, or rejection of AI findings.

Predictive Action Intelligence Once verified, the system utilizes Deterministic Algorithms for Deadline Calculation and Vector Search for departmental matching. It generates Automated Alerts and compliance checklists, significantly reducing the Administrative Lifecycle compared to manual processing.




Key Features




100% Sovereign & Secure




Complete offline operation - zero internet dependency, zero external APIs

3.1GB self-contained model runs on existing government laptops

Sensitive judgment data never leaves secure infrastructure

No vendor lock-in, built on open-source foundation




Zero-Hallucination Intelligence




Four-layer validation: Grammar constraints + span verification + confidence scoring + deterministic calculations

Every extracted fact includes exact PDF coordinates (page, paragraph, character range)

Visible AI reasoning - see how the model interpreted the judgment

Sub-95% confidence extractions automatically flagged for review




Legal Domain Expertise




Fine-tuned on 500+ Indian court judgments (civil, criminal, writ)

Understands legal language patterns, procedural requirements, compliance directives

128k token context window - processes entire 100+ page judgments coherently

Trained specifically for High Court orders and administrative workflows




Mandatory Human Verification




Side-by-side interface: Original PDF with highlights + structured data

One-click approve/edit/reject for every extraction

Click extracted text → instantly jump to source in PDF

Complete audit trail of all verification actions




Intelligent Action Planning




Auto-generates compliance checklists from directives

Deterministic deadline calculation (no AI guessing dates)

Semantic department matching via vector search

Automated alert system (7/3/1 days before deadlines)




Production-Ready Performance




Processes long judgments and gives action plan in just minutes

Specifically trained to maintain very High Accuracy throughout

Runs on existing old systems

Single installer deployment (< 10 minutes setup)

Impact and Benefits

Automating the review of Lengthy PDF Judgments reduces document processing time by over 90%. This eliminates the Manual Processing Bottleneck, allowing officers to shift from tedious data entry to high-level Strategic Decision Making

By utilizing Intelligent Date Calculation and Alert Systems, the platform ensures all Statutory Time-Bars and Judicial Directives are met. This proactively mitigates the risk of Contempt of Court and missed Limitation Periods

The On-Premise Deployment ensures all sensitive legal data remains within the department’s infrastructure. This Offline Processing model provides a level of Data Security and privacy that cloud-based solutions cannot match.

The system generates Structured Action Plans and compliance checklists immediately upon judgment retrieval. This ensures the department is always prepared for Appellate Proceedings with a verified Audit Trail of all actions taken.

Designed to run on existing even on existing old devices, the solution requires no expensive server upgrades or high-end GPUs. This Edge Computing approach allows for rapid, wide-scale deployment across various judicial departments.

This system provides Coordinate-Level Sourcing, linking every extracted directive to its exact location in the Original PDF. This builds Institutional Trust by keeping the official in control through a Human-In-The-Loop workflow.

I Issue 4
TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
SAKSHYA: Supporting Transparent and Accountable Execution of Court Orders
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 26, 2026 12:00 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
SAKSHYA: System for Actionable Knowledge from Court & Hearing Yield Analysis
From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans

Welcome to an era where the Indian judicial system is handling an immense volume of pending cases, placing significant responsibility on government departments to interpret and implement court directives accurately and on time. In many instances, critical instructions are embedded within lengthy court documents. Even a single overlooked sentence in a multi-page judgment can lead to delays, compliance issues, or legal complications. Legal cells often work with large volumes of documents, making it challenging to consistently track deadlines and responsibilities across cases. SAKSHYA (System for Actionable Knowledge from Court & Hearing Yield Analysis) is designed to transform this process into a more structured, efficient, and reliable workflow, enabling departments to convert court judgments and hearing outcomes into clear, verified, and actionable plans.
The SAKSHYA Edge:
• Risk Detection & Deadline Awareness

Identifies critical timelines—such as compliance windows mentioned in orders—and provides timely reminders, helping teams stay aligned with court expectations.
• Context-Aware Understanding Interprets the intent of judicial directives, including conditional orders, dependencies, and priority of actions.


• Hearing Yield Analysis Extracts actionable outcomes not only from final judgments but also from interim hearings, orders, and proceedings—ensuring continuity across the lifecycle of a case.


• Handwritten & Stamped OCR Processes real-world judicial documents, including scanned files, handwritten notes, and official stamps commonly found in court records.


• Multilingual Capability Translates and simplifies instructions into multiple Indian languages, including Hindi and Bengali, enabling clarity at all administrative levels.


• Voice Summaries Provides concise audio summaries of key judgment and hearing points for quick review by senior officials.


• Smart Search Enables natural language queries such as “show all pending payment orders,” significantly reducing manual effort.


• Bulletproof Verification Links every extracted instruction to its exact page and line reference in the original document, ensuring transparency and trust.


Extended Capabilities


• Workflow Conversion Engine Transforms judgments and hearing outputs into structured tasks with assigned responsibilities, deadlines, and status tracking.


• Compliance Dashboard Provides a centralized view of pending, completed, and at-risk actions across departments.


• Automated Compliance Reports Generates ready-to-use summaries and reports for internal tracking and court submissions.


• Inter-Department Coordination Support Facilitates seamless task routing and follow-ups across multiple departments.


• Secure & Auditable System Implements role-based access, audit trails, and traceability to ensure accountability.


Prototype & Demonstration

A working sample of SAKSHYA with core functionalities has been deployed, demonstrating key features such as document processing, directive extraction, task generation, and basic workflow tracking. This prototype serves as a proof of concept for how the system can be scaled and integrated into real-world administrative environments.
Impact

SAKSHYA reduces manual effort in reviewing legal documents while improving clarity, coordination, and timeliness in executing court directives. By structuring complex legal information into actionable workflows, it supports more consistent and transparent compliance practices.

solutions.team_name: Tender AI
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


H Hackstreet Boys
TEAM SUBMISSION [2]
Tender AI
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 26, 2026 12:01 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Government procurement processes, especially in large organizations like CRPF, involve evaluating multiple vendor bids (tenders) against strict eligibility criteria, compliance rules, technical specifications, and financial constraints. Traditionally, this process is manual, time-consuming, and prone to human error, inconsistencies, and even bias. Evaluators must carefully read through hundreds of pages of documents, compare vendor qualifications, verify compliance, and ensure transparency. This leads to delays, inefficiencies, and sometimes incorrect vendor selection. To address these challenges, the proposed system introduces an AI-powered tender evaluation platform that automates eligibility analysis, document verification, and bid comparison using advanced natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning techniques. The core idea of this system is to build an intelligent assistant that can read, understand, and evaluate tender documents and vendor submissions, and provide a structured, explainable decision on whether a vendor is eligible and how competitive their bid is. The system ensures transparency, speed, and consistency in procurement while reducing manual workload. The working of the system begins with the data ingestion phase, where two primary inputs are provided: (1) the official tender document issued by CRPF, which includes eligibility criteria, technical requirements, and evaluation rules, and (2) vendor bid documents, which may include company profiles, compliance certificates, financial statements, and technical proposals. These documents are often in PDF or scanned formats. Therefore, the system first performs document parsing and digitization using Optical Character Recognition (OCR) tools such as Tesseract or advanced document AI models. This converts unstructured documents into machine-readable text. Once the text is extracted, the system proceeds to the document preprocessing stage. Here, irrelevant symbols, formatting noise, and inconsistencies are removed. The text is then structured into meaningful sections such as eligibility criteria, technical specifications, and financial details. Named Entity Recognition (NER) techniques are applied to identify key entities such as company names, dates, certifications, monetary values, and technical parameters. This structured representation is crucial for further analysis. The next step involves understanding the tender requirements. The system uses NLP models such as BERT or transformer-based architectures to interpret the eligibility criteria defined in the tender. For example, requirements like “minimum 3 years of experience,” “ISO certification required,” or “annual turnover above ₹5 crore” are extracted and converted into structured rules. These rules are stored in a machine-interpretable format, forming the basis for automated evaluation. Parallelly, the system analyzes the vendor bid documents. It extracts relevant information such as years of experience, certifications, financial data, and technical capabilities. Using semantic similarity and rule-matching techniques, the system compares vendor-provided information with the extracted tender requirements. For instance, if a tender requires ISO 9001 certification, the system checks whether the vendor document contains valid proof of this certification. This process forms the eligibility analysis module, which determines whether a vendor meets mandatory criteria. Following eligibility checking, the system performs technical evaluation. It compares the vendor’s technical proposal with the tender’s specifications using semantic similarity models. Instead of exact keyword matching, the system understands context, allowing it to identify whether the vendor’s solution satisfies the requirements even if phrased differently. Each technical parameter is scored, and an overall technical score is generated. In addition to technical evaluation, the system also conducts financial analysis. It extracts quoted prices, cost breakdowns, and financial terms from vendor bids. These are compared across multiple vendors to identify the most cost-effective option. The system can also flag unusually low or high bids, helping detect potential risks such as underbidding or overpricing. A critical component of the system is multicriteria decision-making and scoring. The platform combines eligibility results, technical scores, and financial analysis into a unified evaluation framework. Weighted scoring methods are used, where different aspects (eligibility, technical quality, cost) are assigned importance based on procurement rules. The system then ranks vendors and provides a clear recommendation. One of the key innovations of this system is its focus on explainable AI. Instead of providing only a final score, the system generates detailed justifications for each decision. For example, it can highlight which criteria a vendor failed to meet, which technical requirements were partially satisfied, and why a particular vendor was ranked higher. This transparency is crucial for government applications, where accountability and auditability are essential. The final output is presented through a dashboard interface designed for procurement officers. The dashboard displays vendor rankings, eligibility status, detailed score breakdowns, and highlighted document sections for quick review. Officers can also manually override decisions if needed, ensuring that the system acts as an assistive tool rather than a replacement. From a system architecture perspective, the platform consists of a frontend interface for uploading documents and viewing results, a backend server (Flask or FastAPI) for processing and model inference, and a database for storing structured data. Machine learning models handle OCR, NLP, and scoring, while APIs enable seamless integration between components. The system can be deployed on cloud infrastructure to ensure scalability and performance. For a hackathon implementation, a simplified version of this system can be developed by focusing on key components such as PDF text extraction, basic rule-based eligibility checking, semantic similarity scoring using pre-trained models, and a clean dashboard for visualization. Even a partial implementation demonstrating automated comparison between tender requirements and vendor bids can effectively showcase the concept. In conclusion, the AI-Based Tender Evaluation system transforms a traditionally manual and complex process into an intelligent, automated, and transparent workflow. By leveraging NLP, document analysis, and machine learning, the system significantly reduces evaluation time, improves accuracy, and ensures fair and consistent decision-making. This solution has strong real-world applicability in government procurement and can greatly enhance efficiency in organizations like CRPF, making it a powerful and impactful innovation.

solutions.team_name: NYAYASETU - Infrastructure for Legally Binding, Accountable, and Integrity-Driven Government Procurement.
solutions.solution_text:
PRACTICE
COMPETE
JOBS
LEADERBOARD


A armankgp1978_a49f
TEAM SUBMISSION [3]
NYAYASETU - Infrastructure for Legally Binding, Accountable, and Integrity-Driven Government Procurement.
Theme: Theme 3: AI-Based Tender Evaluation and Eligibility Analysis for Government Procurement by CRPF Submitted On: Apr 26, 2026 12:01 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
1. Problem Statement
Government procurement in India (specifically for organizations like the CRPF) is a mission-critical function involving over ₹50 lakh crore annually. The manual evaluation process is staggering: a single tender can involve 20+ bidders, 300-page submissions, and 120,000+ data points.Current Failures:



Structural Inconsistency: Human evaluators read documents differently under pressure.

Audit Gaps: Decisions are often indefensible in tribunals or RTI proceedings due to lack of traceable reasoning.

MSME Disadvantage: Smaller firms with less polished documentation are often unfairly penalized.

Security & Compliance: Solutions must strictly adhere to GFR 2017, CVC guidelines, and CERT-In data residency (no data can leave the government network).


2. Solution Overview: The Neuro-Symbolic Architecture
NYAYASETU is not just a productivity tool; it is a legally defensible decision-making infrastructure. It uses a Neuro-Symbolic design:


Neural Layer (LLMs): Handles contextual understanding, legal synonym matching, and document parsing.

Symbolic Layer (Deterministic Code): Executes threshold comparisons and rule applications via Python logic.

Principle:The LLM extracts; the Rule Engine decides. This ensures mathematical certainty in eligibility verdicts.


3. Core Technical Features

A. Intelligent Tender Extraction (The 3-Pass Architecture)

Pass 1 (Structure Mapping): Builds a Document Structure Graph to resolve cross-references (e.g., "Criteria as per Annexure B").

Pass 2 (Candidate Identification): Uses on-premise Llama 3.1 70B to identify potential eligibility passages.

Pass 3 (Normalization): Transforms prose into typed objects (Financial, Technical, Compliance, Experience) with numeric thresholds and lookback periods.

Mandatory vs. Optional: Determined by a three-tier signal hierarchy ("shall/must" vs. "desirable").

B. Heterogeneous Bidder Parsing (Vision & Multilingual)
NYAYASETU routes documents based on type:


Digital PDFs: Parsed via PyMuPDF and Camelot-py (preserving table structures).

Scanned/Photos: A 5-stage vision pipeline:

OpenCV: Preprocessing (deskewing, denoising).

Surya OCR: Open-source OCR optimized for Devanagari/Indian documents.

PaliGemma-3B: Vision model to identify stamps, seals, and complex layouts.

Grounding Verification: Uses RapidFuzz to match extracted text back to the source page, eliminating LLM hallucinations.

Multilingual: Uses EasyOCR and LaBSE embeddings for semantic matching between English criteria and regional language evidence.

C. Evaluation & Matching Pipeline

Hybrid Retrieval: Combines BM25 keyword search (for GSTIN/PAN) with dense vector search (for "similar works").

Reranking: Selects the single most relevant evidence passage.

Currency Normalization Engine: Converts all values (lakhs, crores, millions) into base units for deterministic comparison.

Live API Cross-Verification: Validates data against GST Suvidha, MCA21, DigiLocker, EPFO, and CVC blacklists to catch forged certificates or collusion.


4. Accountability & Human-in-the-Loop
The system enforces Three Mandatory Human Review Gates:


Gate 1: Officer reviews and locks the extracted criteria before evaluation begins.

Gate 2: Officer resolves "Needs Manual Review" flags (low OCR confidence or scope ambiguity).

Gate 3: Final report sign-off. The digital signature here is the legally binding act.
The Speaking Order: For every rejection, the system auto-drafts a formal legal document citing specific GFR 2017 rules, clause references, and the exact delta between the threshold and the found value.


5. Auditability & Security (CERT-In Compliant)

On-Premise Deployment: Runs entirely via Ollama on government hardware. Zero cloud dependencies.

5-Layer Audit Trail:

Provenance: SHA-256 hashes of all uploads.

Extraction Record: Logs model version, prompts, and raw outputs.

Verdict Record: Records comparison logic and confidence scores.

Intervention Log: Every human override is logged with a mandatory reason.

Merkle-Chain Linking: Each record contains the hash of the previous one; any alteration breaks the chain and triggers an alert.

Deterministic Inference: Settings (Temp=0, Seed=42) ensure that the same document always yields the same result.


6. Implementation Roadmap (14 Days)

Phase 1 (Days 1-2): Environment setup (Ollama, PostgreSQL/pgvector) and Corrigendum Resolver to handle tender amendments.

Phase 2 (Days 3-4): Vision OCR pipeline & multilingual support integration.

Phase 3 (Days 5-6): Criteria extraction logic & Gate 1 UI development.

Phase 4 (Days 7-10): Evaluation engine, consortium logic (JV rules), and Speaking Order automation.

Phase 5 (Days 11-12): Government API integrations (GST/MCA) and Merkle-Chain Audit implementation.

Phase 6 (Days 13-14): Adversarial stress-testing (50MB images, duplicate certificates) and final demo.


7. Key Metrics for Success

Extraction Recall: ≥ 95%

Silent Disqualifications: 0% (Guaranteed by human-in-the-loop flags).

Efficiency: Processing 150-page bidder packages in < 6 minutes.

Admissibility: Generates Section 65B certificates (IT Act 2000) for all electronic evidence.

Conclusion: NYAYASETU protects the evaluator from error and the bidder from bias. It transforms procurement from a paperwork nightmare into a high-integrity, automated infrastructure.

TEAM SUBMISSION [1]
NyayaSetu (ನ್ಯಾಯಸೇತು): The Moment Judgments Become Decisions
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 26, 2026 12:09 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
A court judgment arrives. 28 pages. No highlights. No clear action.
Somewhere inside, there’s a deadline ticking silently. Comply? Appeal? Who decides?

Project Description
⚖️ NyayaBid AI — Explainable Tender Evaluation & Policy Simulation Copilot

What if a single eligibility rule silently removes 3 bidders and increases cost by ₹90 lakh — without anyone noticing?
NyayaBid AI is designed to detect exactly these situations before the tender is even published.

Explore the Project
Live Demo: https://nyaya-bid.vercel.app/
GitHub Repository: https://github.com/brijeshkumar2024/NyayaBid
Flowchart : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Pwr8TTMYE0NaISdObMoeEdVp9lI1jPRyIq-m_aa4eGs/edit?usp=drivesdk
video walkthrough:https://drive.google.com/file/d/1kjlpclHSF3-liHzTpPRZJxAnpmBahICb/view?usp=drivesdk
Problem
Government procurement in India operates at a massive scale, yet tender evaluation is still largely manual.

Officers are required to:
* Go through lengthy tender documents
* Compare vendor submissions in different formats
* Justify decisions during audits

This leads to:
* Delays in evaluation
* Human errors and inconsistencies
* Difficulty in explaining and defending decisions

Solution
NyayaBid AI acts as a decision-support copilot that assists officers at every stage of the evaluation process.
Instead of replacing human judgment, it:

* Reads and understands tender requirements
* Evaluates vendor data automatically
* Provides clear explanations for every decision
* Flags uncertain cases for human review
* Distinguishes between mandatory and optional criteria

From document → decision → audit → simulation, everything is handled in one system.

Core Capabilities :
AI-Based Evaluation
* Automatically evaluates vendors against criteria
* Provides confidence scores with reasoning
* Ensures transparent and explainable decisions

Fraud & Risk Detection
* Detects GST mismatches and inconsistencies
* Flags potential collusion patterns
* Identifies unusual pricing behavior

Policy Simulation Engine (Key Innovation)
* Adjust eligibility criteria in real-time
* Instantly analyze impact on competition and cost

Example:
₹30 Cr threshold → 5 vendors → ₹4.2 Cr
₹50 Cr threshold → 2 vendors → ₹5.1 Cr

Impact: 3 bidders removed, ₹90 lakh cost increase — detected before publishing

Explainable Decisions
* Eligible: Meets all requirements with evidence and document references  
* Not Eligible: Clear reason with the exact value and source document  
* Needs Review: Uncertain cases flagged for human verification  

Audit-Ready Design
* Every action is recorded and traceable
* Reports are SHA-256 verified (tamper-proof)
* Ensures audit compliance

Technology Overview
OCR + PDF Processing: Supports both typed and scanned documents

AI Parsing: GPT/Claude-based extraction of criteria and vendor data

Evaluation Engine: Rule-based scoring with explainable results

Deployment: Fully browser-based, no backend required



How It Works
Upload Documents → Extract Data → Evaluate → Generate Report → Simulate Policy
* Supports typed PDFs, scanned documents, and varied document formats  
* Uses LLM-based extraction
* Combines AI with rule-based evaluation
* Low-confidence or unclear data is flagged for manual review instead of automatic rejection  

Why NyayaBid AI
Traditional Process vs NyayaBid AI

* Takes days → Works in seconds
* Manual comparison → Automated evaluation
* Hard to justify → Fully explainable
* No fraud detection → Built-in risk detection
* No simulation → Real-time policy testing
* Paper-based audit → Digital, verifiable reports

Impact
* Reduces evaluation time significantly
* Improves consistency and reduces bias
* Detects fraud and risks early
* Makes decisions fully auditable

Deployment Readiness
* Fully browser-based (no backend dependency)
* Easy to deploy in government environments
* Designed for real-world procurement workflows

Scalability & Future Scope
The system can be extended to integrate with platforms such as GeM and CPPP, enabling adoption across multiple departments. Future enhancements can include multilingual document support, deeper fraud analytics, and integration with national procurement databases for cross-tender intelligence.

Conclusion
NyayaBid AI ensures that procurement decisions are not just faster — but fair, explainable, and audit-ready.

JudgeTrack: An Automated System to Convert Court Judgments into Verified, Time-Bound Action Plans
Theme: Theme 11: From Court Judgments to Verified Action Plans Submitted On: Apr 26, 2026 01:04 AM IST (Asia/Kolkata)
Project Description
Court judgments in India are often rendered ineffective due to poor post-judgment enforcement — orders are passed but compliance is rarely tracked, verified, or escalated systematically. This creates a justice delivery gap where verdicts exist on paper but fail to produce real-world outcomes.
JudgeTrack is a digital platform that automatically converts court judgments into structured, verified action plans for all concerned parties — litigants, government bodies, and enforcement agencies.
Once a judgment is pronounced, the system:
Extracts key directives from the judgment using NLP and AI, identifying who must do what and by when

Generates a Verified Action Plan (VAP) — a clear, step-by-step compliance checklist assigned to the responsible party

Tracks real-time progress through a centralized dashboard accessible to judges, lawyers, and enforcement officers

Sends automated reminders and escalation alerts when deadlines are approaching or missed

Accepts digital proof of compliance — documents, photographs, or reports — at each milestone

Flags non-compliance directly to the court for contempt or further action

The result is a closed-loop judiciary enforcement system that ensures every judgment is not just delivered — but executed, verified, and closed.
